# Document 90: Collaborate with Gemini in Gmail - Computer - Gmail Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/mail/answer/14355636?hl=en
Product area: gmail
Original file: docs/collaborate-with-gemini-in-gmail-computer-gmail-help-5e84d21b.md

# Collaborate with Gemini in Gmail — Markdown Summary

**Source:** Google Gmail Help — “Collaborate with Gemini in Gmail”  
**Platform covered:** Computer  
**URL:** https://support.google.com/mail/answer/14355636?hl=en

---

## Overview

Gemini in Gmail lets eligible users use AI directly inside Gmail through the **Ask Gemini** side panel. It can help summarize emails, draft and suggest replies, search prior emails, reference Google Drive files, interact with Google Calendar, generate images, and use Gems for repetitive tasks.

---

## Feature Availability

- Requires an eligible **Google Workspace** or **Google AI** plan.
- Supported languages vary by feature.
- Google notes a change for some subscribers:

> “We're working to make it easier to use AI directly in Gmail. As such, Google AI Pro and Google AI Ultra subscribers in the US may no longer see Ask Gemini in Gmail or be able to open the Gemini in Gmail side panel. Powerful AI experiences in Gmail, such as AI Overviews and ‘Help me write,’ continue to be available directly in the product.”

---

## What Gemini in Gmail Can Do

With Gemini in Gmail, you can:

- Summarize an email thread.
- Suggest responses to an email thread.
- Draft an email.
- Find information from previous emails.
- Find information from your Google Drive files.
- Get information about Google Calendar events.
- Create events in Google Calendar.
- Generate images.
- Use Gems for repetitive or specialized tasks.

---

## Get Started with Gemini in Gmail

1. On your computer, open [Gmail](https://mail.google.com/).
2. At the top right, click **Ask Gemini**.
3. In the side panel, either:
   - Select a suggested prompt, or
   - Write your own prompt.
4. Press **Enter**.

### Use a Suggested Prompt

1. Select a suggestion from the list.
   - To see more options, select **More suggestions**.
2. At the bottom, in the prompt box, replace the example text with your own information.
3. Press **Enter**.

### Enter Your Own Prompt

1. At the bottom, in the prompt box, enter a prompt.
2. Press **Enter**.

### Clear Gemini History

To remove your history:

1. Click **More options**.
2. Select **Clear history**.

**Important tip:**

> “To avoid losing your history, copy a suggestion into your email.”

You lose your Gemini conversation history when:

- You refresh your browser.
- You close and reopen Gmail.

---

## Gemini in Gmail Controls and Actions

| Action | What it does |
|---|---|
| **Ask Gemini** | Opens Gemini. |
| **More options** | Clears recent Gemini history and asks for more suggestions. |
| **Expand** | Makes Gemini larger. |
| **Collapse** | Returns Gemini to its original size. |
| **Close** | Closes Gemini. |
| **Clear history** | Removes generated text and images not yet inserted into the document/email. |
| **More suggestions** | Shows more Gemini suggestions. |
| **Copy** | Copies a suggestion. |
| **Retry with Google Search** | Searches Google for more information about your question or request. |
| **View more** | Shows more of Gemini’s response. |
| **View less** | Minimizes Gemini’s response. |
| **Good suggestion** | Sends positive feedback about a Gemini response. |
| **Bad suggestion** | Reports an issue with a Gemini response. |
| **Gem** | Lets you chat with or create a Gem to save time on repetitive tasks. |

---

## Things You Can Ask Gemini in Gmail

### 1. Summarize an Email Thread

Gemini can summarize email conversations and extract key points or action items.

Example prompts:

> “Create a list of action items for me based on this email.”

> “Explain this email to me like I’m 5 years old.”

---

### 2. Suggest Reply Options or Draft Emails

Gemini can draft new emails or replies.

#### To get suggested replies

1. On your computer, open an email thread.
2. Click **Ask Gemini**.
3. At the bottom of the side panel, click **Suggest a reply**.

#### To draft a new email

Ask Gemini to create the draft.

Example prompts:

> “Write an announcement email for the launch of Project Clover.”

> “Create a thank you note to a colleague for their work on Project Clover.”

---

### 3. Find Information from Previous Emails

Gemini can retrieve information from emails in your inbox, including reservations, flights, packages, and similar messages.

Example prompts:

> “When is my package arriving?”

> “What time is my next flight?”

---

### 4. Search for Certain Types of Emails

Gemini can search Gmail for messages based on criteria such as unread status, date range, or sender. Results load as a Gmail search.

Example prompts:

> “Show my unread emails.”

> “Emails from [person] sent last week.”

> “Show unread emails from this week.”

---

### 5. Find Information from Google Drive Files

Gemini can reference files and documents stored in Google Drive.

Example prompts:

> “What are the main points of the weekly team meeting notes from my documents?”

> “Summarize my document for Project Clover.”

**Tip:** To check which files Gemini used in its response, click **Sources**.

-

[... summary truncated for context management ...]

---

# Document 91: Collaborate with Gemini in Gmail (Workspace Experiments) - Computer - Gmail Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/mail/answer/14199860
Product area: gmail
Original file: docs/collaborate-with-gemini-in-gmail-workspace-experiments-computer-gmail-help-a15fd461.md

# Collaborate with Gemini in Gmail (Workspace Experiments)

## Overview
Gemini in Gmail helps you:

- Summarize an email thread
- Suggest responses to an email thread
- Draft an email
- Find information from previous emails
- Find information from your Google Drive files
- Get information about Google Calendar events
- Create events in Google Calendar

**Feature availability**  
- Requires an eligible Google Workspace or Google AI plan ([learn more](https://support.google.com/drive/answer/13952129))
- Available in Google Workspace Experiments (trusted tester program for personal accounts) – check access by opening a Google Slides presentation and looking for the bottom bar ([learn more](https://support.google.com/docs/answer/13447104))
- Supported in some languages ([language details](https://support.google.com/mail/answer/13607340))

## Get Started with Gemini in Gmail

1. Open [Gmail](https://mail.google.com/) on your computer.
2. Click **Ask Gemini** in the top-right corner.
3. In the side panel, choose a suggested prompt or type your own.
   - **Use a suggestion**: Select a suggestion (click **More suggestions** for additional options), replace the placeholder text in the prompt box, and press **Enter**.
   - **Write a custom prompt**: Type your request and press **Enter**.
4. (Optional) Clear history via **More options** → **Clear history**.

> **Tip:** Conversation history is lost when you refresh the browser or close/reopen Gmail. Copy any important output into your email to avoid losing it.

## Side Panel Controls (Quick Reference)

| Control | What it does |
|---------|--------------|
| **Ask Gemini** | Open/activate the side panel |
| **More options** | Clear recent history, request more suggestions |
| **Expand / Collapse** | Resize the panel |
| **Close** | Close the side panel |
| **Clear history** | Remove all generated text/images not yet inserted |
| **More suggestions** | Load additional prompt suggestions |
| **Copy** | Copy a response to clipboard |
| **Retry with Google Search** | Search the web for your question |
| **View more / View less** | Expand or minimize the response |
| **Good suggestion / Bad suggestion** | Give positive or negative feedback |
| **Gem** | Chat with a premade or custom Gem for repetitive tasks |

## Practical Use Cases & Example Prompts

### Summarize an Email Thread
- With side panel open: click **What’s this email about?** on an email thread (more than 2 replies).
- With side panel closed: click **Summarize this email** at the top of the thread.
- Or use a prompt:
  - *“Summarize this email.”*
  - *“Create a list of action items for me based on this email.”*
  - *“Explain this email to me like I’m 5 years old.”*

### Suggest Reply Options
- Open an email thread, click **Ask Gemini**, then click **Suggest a reply** at the bottom of the side panel.
- To draft a new message:
  - *“Write an announcement email for the launch of Project Clover.”*
  - *“Create a thank you note to a colleague for their work on Project Clover.”*

### Find Information from Previous Emails
Gemini can retrieve details like reservations, flights, packages, etc.
- *“When is my package arriving?”*
- *“What time is my next flight?”*

### Search for Certain Emails
Gemini runs a Gmail search for you.
- *“Show my unread emails.”*
- *“Emails from [person] sent last week.”*
- *“Show unread emails from this week.”*

### Find Information from Your Google Drive Files
Reference documents, notes, etc.
- *“What are the main points of the weekly team meeting notes from my documents?”*
- *“Summarize my document for Project Clover.”*
- Click **Sources** to check which files were used.

### Get Help with Gmail
- *“Where can I edit my email filters?”*
- *“How can I create a label?”*

### Ask Questions from the Web
> **Important:** Include phrases like “Use Google Search” or “Using web search” in your prompt.
- *“Write a paragraph describing the weather today in Mountain View, California using web search.”*
- *“Help me write a paragraph explaining if lightning can strike the same place twice using only information from the web.”*

### Get Information About Your Calendar Events
- Works only with your **primary calendar**.
- *“What’s my first meeting tomorrow?”*
- *“When’s the next time I’ll meet with Lori?”*
- **Tip:** Add `@Calendar` at the beginning of your prompt to specify Calendar.

### Create Calendar Events
- Also limited to the primary calendar.
- *“Schedule a lunch block for tomorrow at noon.”*
- *“Create a weekly run on Fridays at 8 AM.”*
- Based on an open email: *“Add this to my calendar.”*
- **Tip:** Add `@Calendar` to the prompt. If Gemini detects event details in an email, an **Add to calendar** button appears above the email.

### Create Images with Gemini
1. Open Gemini in Gmail.
2. In the side panel, enter a prompt like: *“Create an image of a dog with glasses.”*
3. You can edit the prompt or submit feedback on the generated image.
- Images are for use only within Gmail.

### Use a Gem (Cust

[... summary truncated for context management ...]

# Document 92: Collaborate with Gemini in Google Chat - Computer - Google Chat Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/chat/answer/15345722?hl=en

# Collaborate with Gemini in Google Chat — Summary

**Source:** Google Chat Help — “Collaborate with Gemini in Google Chat”  
**Applies to:** Computer / Google Chat

## Overview

Gemini in Google Chat helps users collaborate across Chat conversations by allowing them to:

- **Find information from any conversation in Chat**
- **Generate summaries** of specific topics discussed in a space or file
- **Summarize unread messages**
- **List action items**
- **Answer questions** about previous conversations

---

## Feature Availability

Gemini in Chat is only available under certain conditions:

- Requires an **eligible Google Workspace subscription**
- Availability depends on supported languages for **Google Workspace with Gemini**
- **Alpha users** may not have access to the side panel in Google Chat
- Users with access to **Ask Gemini in Google Chat** can access Gemini collaboration tools through Ask Gemini

---

## Getting Started with Gemini in Chat

To use Gemini in Chat on a computer:

1. Go to [chat.google.com](https://chat.google.com/)
   - Or open the **Chat standalone app**
   - Or open **Chat in Gmail**
2. At the top right, click **Ask Gemini**
3. Use either:
   - A suggested prompt
   - Your own prompt in the prompt box

### Use a Suggested Prompt

1. Select a suggestion from the list
   - Select **More suggestions** to see additional options
2. Replace the example text in the prompt box with your own information
3. Press **Enter**

### Enter Your Own Prompt

1. Type a prompt in the prompt box at the bottom
2. Press **Enter**

---

## Important Tips

Gemini conversation history in Chat may be lost when:

- You refresh your browser
- You close and reopen Chat
- Your computer goes offline

To remove conversation history:

> Click **More options** → **Clear history**

---

## Gemini in Chat Controls and Actions

| Action | Purpose |
|---|---|
| **Ask Gemini** | Open Gemini |
| **More options** | Clear recent Gemini history or ask for more suggestions |
| **Expand** | Make Gemini larger |
| **Collapse** | Return Gemini to its original size |
| **Close** | Close Gemini |
| **Clear history** | Remove all generated text and images |
| **More suggestions** | Find more Gemini suggestions |
| **More image suggestions** | Find more image suggestions from Gemini |
| **Insert** | Insert generated text into your Chat |
| **Copy** | Copy a suggestion |
| **Retry with Google Search** | Search Google for more information about your question or request |
| **View more** | Show more of Gemini’s response |
| **View less** | Minimize Gemini’s response |
| **Good suggestion** | Give positive feedback on a Gemini response |
| **Bad suggestion** | Report an issue with a Gemini response |

---

## Things You Can Ask Gemini in Chat

Gemini can help with questions, summaries, files, and action items across conversations.

---

## 1. Answer Questions About Conversations

Gemini can search through your chat history to answer questions about conversations.

You can ask specific questions and may receive citations.

### Example Prompts

> “What did [leader name] announce to the team yesterday?”

> “What’s the decision on the project discussed in this conversation?"

---

## 2. Summarize a Conversation

Gemini can search through chat history and summarize conversations, unread messages, or specific topics.

### Example Prompts

> "Summarize my unread messages."

> "What are the key takeaways in this conversation?”

After receiving an initial summary, you can ask for more detail:

> "Give me a detailed summary of [topic] discussed in this space."

---

## 3. Summarize a File

Gemini can summarize files shared in a Chat conversation if certain requirements are met.

### File Summary Requirements

Gemini can summarize a file if:

- The message is **not blocked**
- The file is shared as a **Drive link** in Google Chat
- Download restrictions are **off** for the file

### Supported File Types

Gemini can summarize:

- Google Docs
- Google Sheets
- Google Slides
- `.pdf`
- `.csv`
- `.txt`
- Zip files
- Microsoft Office formats, including:
  - `.docx` — Word
  - `.xlsx` — Excel
  - `.pptx` — PowerPoint

### How to Summarize a File

1. On your computer, open [Google Chat](https://chat.google.com/) or [Gmail](https://gmail.google.com/)
   - In Gmail, click **Chat** on the left
2. Select a direct message or space that contains a file
3. Under the file, click **Summarize this file**

---

## 4. List Action Items

Gemini can identify action items from any conversation.

### Example Prompts

> "What are the action items in this conversation?”

> “Are there any action items for me?"

> “Summarize [person's] action items”

---

## Giving Feedback on Gemini Output

Google states that Workspace with Gemini is continuously improving and may not always support every request.

### Important Feedback Warning

> Because feedback may be human readable, please do not submit data that contains personal, confidential, or sensitive information.

If Gemini g

[... summary truncated for context management ...]

# Collaborate with Gemini in Google Chat — Markdown Summary

**Source:** Google Chat Help — “Collaborate with Gemini in Google Chat (Workspace Experiments)”  
**URL:** https://support.google.com/chat/answer/15395284  
**Platform covered:** Computer

---

## Overview

Gemini in Google Chat lets users collaborate with AI from within an open space or conversation. It can help users:

- **Summarize** a space or conversation.
- **Generate action items** from a space or conversation.
- **Answer specific questions** about a space or conversation.

---

## Feature Availability

Gemini in Chat is available through Google’s early access testing program:

> **“This feature is available as part of our early access testing program”**

Availability notes:

- Part of **Google Workspace Experiments**.
- Also available in **Gemini Business** and **Gemini Enterprise Alpha**.
- To turn off Workspace Experiments features, users must **exit the Workspace Experiments program**.

---

## Get Started with Gemini in Chat

### Open Gemini in Chat

1. On your computer, go to [chat.google.com](https://chat.google.com/).
   - You can also use:
     - The **Chat standalone app**
     - **Chat in Gmail**
2. At the top right, click **Ask Gemini**.
3. In the side panel:
   - Select a suggested prompt, or
   - Write your own prompt.

---

## Example Prompts

### Ask Gemini to find information from past chats

Original examples:

> `When are we launching [feature name]?`

> `What did [colleague] announce to the team yesterday?`

### Ask Gemini to summarize multiple conversations

Original examples:

> `Summarize my conversations with [colleague] from this week.`

> `Summarize my unread messages.`

---

## Using Suggested Prompts

To use a suggested prompt:

1. Select from the list of suggestions.
   - To see more, select **More suggestions**.
2. At the bottom, in the prompt box, replace the example text with the information you want.
3. Press **Enter**.

---

## Entering Your Own Prompt

To write your own prompt:

1. At the bottom of the side panel, enter a prompt in the prompt box.
2. Press **Enter**.

---

## Conversation History Tips

Gemini in Chat conversation history may be lost when:

- You refresh your browser.
- You close and reopen Chat.
- Your computer goes offline.

To remove your Gemini conversation history:

1. Click **More options**.
2. Select **Clear history**.

---

## Gemini in Chat Controls and Actions

| Action | Purpose |
|---|---|
| **Ask Gemini** | Open Gemini. |
| **More options** | Clear recent Gemini history and ask for more suggestions. |
| **Expand** | Make Gemini larger. |
| **Collapse** | Return Gemini to its original size. |
| **Close** | Close Gemini. |
| **Clear history** | Remove generated text and images that haven’t been inserted into the document yet. |
| **More suggestions** | Find more suggestions from Gemini. |
| **More image suggestions** | Find more image suggestions from Gemini. |
| **Insert** | Insert generated text into your Chat. |
| **Copy** | Copy a suggestion. |
| **Retry with Google Search** | Search Google for more information about your question or request. |
| **View more** | Show more of Gemini’s response. |
| **View less** | Minimize Gemini’s response. |
| **Good suggestion** | Give positive feedback about a Gemini response. |
| **Bad suggestion** | Report an issue with a Gemini response. |

---

# Things You Can Ask Gemini in Chat

Gemini can help with an open conversation or space by:

- Summarizing the conversation.
- Listing action items.
- Answering specific questions.
- Summarizing supported files shared in Chat.

---

## Summarize a Conversation

Gemini can summarize a conversation that is currently open in Chat.

Suggested prompts include:

> `"Summarize this conversation."`

> `"What are the key takeaways in this conversation?”`

After receiving an initial summary, you can request more detail:

> `"Give me a detailed summary of [topic] discussed in this space."`

**Important:** The relevant conversation should be open while using Gemini in Chat.

---

## Summarize a File in a Chat Conversation

Gemini can summarize a file shared within a conversation if certain conditions are met.

### Requirements

A file can be summarized if:

- The message is **not blocked**.
- The file is shared as a **Drive link** in Google Chat.
- Download restrictions are **off** for the file.

### Supported File Types

Gemini can summarize these files shared as Drive links:

- Google Docs
- Google Sheets
- Google Slides
- `.pdf`
- `.csv`
- `.txt`
- Zip files
- Microsoft Office formats, including:
  - `.docx` — Word
  - `.xlsx` — Excel
  - `.pptx` — PowerPoint

### Steps to Summarize a File

1. On your computer, open:
   - [Google Chat](https://chat.google.com/), or
   - [Gmail](https://gmail.google.com/)
2. If using Gmail:
   - On the left, click **Chat**.
3. Select a direct message or space containing a file.
4. Under the file, click **Summarize this file**.

Gemini can identi

# Collaborate with Gemini in Google Docs — Summary

**Source:** Google Docs Editors Help  
**URL:** https://support.google.com/docs/answer/14206696?hl=en  
**Platform covered:** Computer

---

## Overview

Gemini in Google Docs helps users write, edit, summarize, refine, and create content directly inside Google Docs. It can also generate images, reference other Workspace files, search selected sources, and use custom or premade Gems for repetitive tasks.

With Gemini in Google Docs, you can:

- Write and edit with Gemini
- Create personalized documents
- Summarize files from Google Drive and emails from Gmail
- Reference other files to edit or refine content
- Create images

---

## Feature Availability

**Important requirements and limitations:**

- Gemini in Docs requires an eligible **Google Workspace** or **Google AI** plan.
- Supported languages vary by feature and location.
- Gemini in Docs is also available through **Google Workspace Experiments**, a trusted tester program for personal accounts.
- To check Workspace Experiments access, open a new Google Slides presentation and look for a bottom bar.

---

## Supported Languages

You can use the **Gemini in Docs side panel** in some languages.

Google links to additional help pages for:

- Gemini feature and plan availability
- Supported languages for Google Workspace with Gemini
- Language availability for the side panel

---

## Getting Started with Gemini in Docs

### Open Gemini in a document

1. On your computer, open a document in [Google Docs](https://docs.google.com/document/u/0/).
2. At the top right, click **Ask Gemini**.
3. In the side panel:
   - Select a suggested prompt, or
   - Write your own prompt.
4. Press **Enter**.

### Use a suggested prompt

1. Select a suggestion from the list.
   - To see more options, select **More suggestions**.
2. At the bottom, replace the example text in the prompt box with your own information.
3. Press **Enter**.

### Enter your own prompt

1. At the bottom of the side panel, enter a prompt.
2. Press **Enter**.

### Clear Gemini history

To remove your recent history:

> Click **More options** → **Clear history**

**Tips:**

- To avoid losing generated content, insert Gemini output into the document.
- Gemini conversation history is lost when:
  - You refresh your browser.
  - You close and reopen the document.
  - Your computer goes offline.

---

## Gemini in Docs Controls and Actions

| Action | What it does |
|---|---|
| **Ask Gemini** | Opens Gemini. |
| **More options** | Clears recent Gemini history and asks for more suggestions. |
| **Expand** | Makes Gemini larger. |
| **Collapse** | Returns Gemini to its original size. |
| **Close** | Closes Gemini. |
| **Clear history** | Removes generated text and images not yet inserted into the document. |
| **More suggestions** | Shows more Gemini suggestions. |
| **More image suggestions** | Shows more image suggestions from Gemini. |
| **Insert** | Inserts generated text into the document. |
| **Insert image** | Inserts a generated image into the document. |
| **Copy** | Copies a suggestion. |
| **Retry** | Generates a different response. |
| **Preview** | Previews the full suggestion before insertion. |
| **Retry with Google Search** | Searches Google for more information related to your request. |
| **View more** | Expands the Gemini response. |
| **View less** | Minimizes the Gemini response. |
| **Good suggestion** | Gives positive feedback on a Gemini response. |
| **Bad suggestion** | Reports an issue with a Gemini response. |
| **Gem** | Lets you chat with or create a Gem for repetitive tasks. |

---

## Things You Can Ask Gemini in Docs

---

## Summarize Text with Gemini

Gemini can summarize an entire document or selected text.

### How to summarize a document

1. Open a Google Docs document.
2. Click **Ask Gemini** at the top right.
3. If the document contains text, Gemini may show a summary in the side panel.
4. You can also explicitly ask Gemini to summarize the document.

### Example prompts

> “Summarize this document in 3 bullet points.”

> “Explain this document like I'm 5 years old.”

### Summarize a specific section

1. Highlight the text you want summarized.
2. In the Gemini side panel, ask Gemini to summarize the selected text.

### After Gemini generates a summary

You can:

- **Add the text to your document:** Click **Insert**.
- **Generate a different version:** Click **Retry**.
- **Send feedback:** Click **Good suggestion** or **Bad suggestion**.

---

## Create Documents or New Text

Gemini can draft new documents or generate new text from a prompt.

### Steps

1. Open a document in Google Docs.
2. In the bottom bar prompt field, enter a prompt describing the document you want to create.
3. Optional:
   - Reference other files to create content.
   - Match a writing style.
   - Match a document format.
4. Click **Submit**.
5. Optional: Select **Source** to review sources used.

---

# Collaborate with Gemini in Google Drive (Workspace Experiments)

**Source:** [Google Drive Help](https://support.google.com/drive/answer/14217860?hl=en)

---

## Feature Availability
- Requires an eligible **Google Workspace** or **Google AI plan** (see [Gemini features and plans](https://support.google.com/drive/answer/13952129))
- Also available through **Google Workspace Experiments** (trusted tester program for personal accounts)
  - Check access: open a new Google Slides presentation; if you see a bottom bar, you have access. [Get started with Workspace Experiments](https://support.google.com/docs/answer/13447104).

## Supported Languages
- Gemini in Drive side panel supports a subset of languages. [Full list](https://support.google.com/drive/answer/13607340).

## What You Can Do with Gemini in Drive
- Summarize a long document or an entire folder
- Discuss a topic by synthesizing info from one or more files (across Drive or within a specific folder)
- Retrieve quick facts about a project without opening documents
- Move or create Google files and folders

---

## Get Started with Gemini in Drive

1. Go to [drive.google.com](https://drive.google.com/)
2. Click **Ask Gemini** (star icon) at the top right
3. In the side panel, choose a suggested prompt or write your own
4. Optional: to clear history, click **More options** → **Clear history**

> **Important:** Conversation history is lost when you reload the browser, close/reopen the document, or go offline.

### Prompt Examples
- *Select a suggestion* – pick from the list; choose **More suggestions** for more; replace placeholder text and press **Enter**
- *Enter your own* – type a prompt and press **Enter**

---

## Gemini in Drive Controls (Reference)

| Action | Icon / Description |
|--------|-------------------|
| Open Gemini | **Ask Gemini** ![](icon) |
| Clear history | **Clear history** – removes all generated text |
| Expand/Collapse | Make Gemini larger / return to original size |
| Close Gemini | Close the side panel |
| More suggestions | Get additional prompt suggestions |
| Copy suggestion | Copy a suggestion |
| Retry | Generate a different response |
| Retry with Google Search | Search Google for more info on your query |
| View more / less | Show full summary / minimize response |
| Show more (search) | Show full file search results |
| Good / Bad suggestion | Provide feedback |
| Gem | Chat with a Gem (pre-built or custom) |
| Export | Export response to a new Google Doc or Sheet |
| Clear selected sources | Remove previously chosen files/folders from scope |

---

## Things You Can Ask Gemini

### Search for Files
- Click **Ask Gemini** and describe the file by content (e.g., “Find the spreadsheet with Q3 revenue”)

### Ask Questions About a Specific File
- Click **Ask Gemini**, then use **Add Sources** or type **@** + file name
  - Example: `"Summarize the key points in @Sales Team Meeting Notes"`
- After response: click **Sources** to see referenced files; use **View more**, **Retry** for new text.

> **Tip:** Remove selected sources with **Clear selected sources**.

Other file-level prompts:
- “When is our contract with Vendor A expiring?”
- “Summarize this file for me in bullet points”
- “How many customer sign-ups did we get for this test application?”
- “Who wrote the Project B design doc?”

### Ask Questions About a Specific Folder
- Click **Ask Gemini**, add folder with `@` + folder name
  - Example: `"Find my files for Project Clover in @2024 Projects"`
- After response, **Sources** shows which files were used.

Folder-level prompts:
- “When is the next product review based on this folder?”
- “Tell me about @[Contract folder]”
- “What are the documents from Alice in this folder?”

### Summarize a File or Folder
- Use `@` to specify the file/folder, optionally combine with a summarize prompt.
  - Example: `"Summarize the key points in @Sales Team Meeting Notes"`
- For summaries, **Sources** indicates files used; use **Retry** for new version.

### Summarize Information Across Multiple Files
- Ask broad questions without `@` to search across all accessible files.
  - Examples:
    - “Help me understand our team’s 2024 strategy.”
    - “Summarize project X.”
- Response includes **Sources**, feedback buttons, and **Retry**.

### Move Files
- Use `@` to mention up to **5 files** and a target folder.
  - Examples:
    - “Move @Acme-Results to @Clients-Acme folder.”
    - “Move @Acme-Drafts and @Acme-Results to @Clients-Acme folder.”
- Press Enter, then click **Move** in the preview card. Permissions may change.

### Create New Files & Folders
- Prompt to create folders or files (if no name given, Gemini prompts for one)
  - “Create a folder called Design.”
  - “Create a sheet called Acme Campaign Planning.”
- Click **Create** in the preview card; new item is placed in the current folder (if open with edit access) or in My Drive.

**Exporting content** generated in the side panel:
  - Ask Gemini to create a table, summa

[... summary truncated for context management ...]

# Document 96: Collaborate with Gemini in Google Sheets - Google Docs Editors Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/docs/answer/14356410?hl=en

# Collaborate with Gemini in Google Sheets — Summary

**Source:** Google Docs Editors Help — “Collaborate with Gemini in Google Sheets”  
**URL:** https://support.google.com/docs/answer/14356410?hl=en

---

## Overview

Gemini in Google Sheets lets eligible users collaborate with AI directly inside spreadsheets. It can help create and edit spreadsheet content, analyze data, generate charts, summarize connected Workspace content, and perform spreadsheet actions through natural-language prompts.

### Gemini in Sheets can:

- Create tables.
- Create formulas.
- Generate data analysis and insights.
- Build charts and graphs.
- Summarize emails and files from Drive and Gmail.
- Perform spreadsheet actions, including:
  - Apply conditional formatting.
  - Create a pivot table.
  - Add a dropdown or checkbox.
  - Sort, apply, or clear a filter.
  - Find and replace text.
  - Set a number format.
  - Insert, delete, or freeze rows and columns.
  - Fill a range.
  - Format a table.
  - Solve optimization tasks.

---

## Feature Availability & Requirements

- Requires an eligible **Google Workspace** or **Google AI** plan.
- Supported languages vary by Gemini feature.
- Gemini works best with **native Google Sheets files**.

> **Important:** This feature works best with native Google Sheets files. To use Gemini features in an Excel file (.xlsx), select **File** → **Save as Google Sheets**.

---

## Getting Started with Gemini in Sheets

### Open Gemini

1. Open a spreadsheet in [Google Sheets](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/u/0/).
2. Click **Ask Gemini** in the top-right corner.
3. In the side panel:
   - Select a suggested prompt, or
   - Enter your own prompt.
4. Press **Enter**.

### Using Suggested Prompts

1. Select a suggestion from the list.
2. Click **More suggestions** for additional options.
3. Replace example text in the prompt box with your own request.
4. Press **Enter**.

### Entering Your Own Prompt

1. Type a prompt in the Gemini prompt box.
2. Press **Enter**.

### Optional Controls

- **Undo:** Reverse a Gemini action.
- **Clear history:** Remove recent Gemini side-panel history.
- **Select sources/tabs:** Use the dropdown in the prompt box to choose which spreadsheet tabs Gemini should focus on.
  - Use **Reset to all sources** to re-enable all available sources.

### History Tip

To avoid losing conversation history, insert generated output into the spreadsheet.

You lose Gemini side-panel conversation history when:

- You reload your browser.
- You close and reopen the spreadsheet.
- Your computer goes offline.

### Table Focus Tip

For better analysis when multiple tables exist, focus Gemini’s attention on specific tables.

---

## Gemini Side Panel Actions & Controls

| Action | Purpose |
|---|---|
| **Ask Gemini** | Open Gemini. |
| **More options** | Clear recent Gemini history or ask for more suggestions. |
| **Expand** | Make Gemini larger. |
| **Collapse** | Return Gemini to original size. |
| **Close** | Close Gemini. |
| **Clear history** | Remove generated text/images not yet inserted. |
| **More suggestions** | Get additional prompt suggestions. |
| **More image suggestions** | Get additional image suggestions. |
| **Insert** | Insert generated text into the spreadsheet. |
| **Insert image** | Insert a generated image. |
| **Copy** | Copy a suggestion. |
| **Retry** | Generate a different response. |
| **Retry with Google Search** | Search Google for more information. |
| **View more / View less** | Expand or minimize a Gemini response. |
| **Good suggestion / Bad suggestion** | Submit feedback on Gemini output. |
| **Show code / Hide code** | Show or hide code used to generate output. |
| **Gem** | Use or create a Gem for repetitive tasks. |
| **Undo** | Reverse the last Gemini action. |
| **Export to Docs** | Export Gemini output to a new Google Docs document. |

---

# Things You Can Ask Gemini in Sheets

---

## Summarize a Spreadsheet

When opening a spreadsheet with data, Gemini may show a summary in the side panel.

You can:

- Click **View more** to expand the summary.
- Use **Good suggestion** or **Bad suggestion** to provide feedback.

### Example prompts

> “Summarize this spreadsheet.”

> “Explain this spreadsheet like I'm 5 years old.”

### Keyboard shortcuts for summarizing

- **Mac:** `⌘ + Ctrl + n`
- **Windows:** `Ctrl + Alt + n`

---

## Create Tables with Gemini

Gemini can generate structured tables directly in Sheets.

### Steps

1. Open a spreadsheet.
2. Click **Ask Gemini**.
3. Select or write a prompt.
4. Press **Enter**.
5. Optionally:
   - Click **Insert** to add the table.
   - Click **Retry** for another version.
   - Add follow-up prompts to refine.
   - Submit feedback.

### Example prompts

> “Create a table for a full day team event.”

> “Create a trip plan for a vacation in a national park.”

> “Create a social media tracker for marketing.”

### Follow-up examples

> “Add another 5 rows of different activities.”

> “Add details on cost.”

# Document 97: Collaborate with Gemini in Google Sheets (Workspace Experiments) - Google Docs Editors Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/docs/answer/14218565?hl=en
Product area: sheets
Original file: docs/collaborate-with-gemini-in-google-sheets-workspace-experiments-google-docs-edito-127315f5.md

# Collaborate with Gemini in Google Sheets — Markdown Summary

**Source:** Google Docs Editors Help  
**URL:** https://support.google.com/docs/answer/14218565?hl=en  
**Topic:** Using Gemini in Google Sheets via Google Workspace Experiments and eligible Workspace/Google AI plans.

---

## 1. What Gemini in Sheets Can Do

With **Gemini in Google Sheets**, users can:

- **Create tables**
- **Create formulas**
- **Generate data analysis and insights**
- **Build charts and graphs**
- **Summarize files from Drive and emails from Gmail**
- **Generate images**
- **Get help with Google Sheets**
- **Use Google Search from prompts**
- **Use Gems**
- **Solve optimization problems**
- **Perform spreadsheet actions**, including:
  - Apply conditional formatting
  - Create a pivot table
  - Add a dropdown or checkbox
  - Sort data
  - Apply or clear filters
  - Find and replace text
  - Set number formats
  - Insert, delete, or freeze rows and columns
  - Fill a range
  - Format a table
  - Solve optimization tasks

---

## 2. Availability & Requirements

### Feature Availability

- Requires an **eligible Google Workspace or Google AI plan**.
- Gemini in Sheets is available in **Google Workspace Experiments**, a trusted tester program for users with personal accounts.
- To check whether you have Workspace Experiments access:
  - Open a new presentation in **Google Slides**
  - Look for a **bottom bar**

### Supported Languages

- Gemini in Sheets side panel is available in **some languages**.
- Language availability depends on Google Workspace with Gemini support.

### Important File Compatibility Note

> **Important:** This feature works best with native Google Sheets files. To use Gemini features in an Excel file (.xlsx), select **File** → **Save as Google Sheets**.

---

## 3. Getting Started with Gemini in Sheets

### Open Gemini

1. Open a spreadsheet in [Google Sheets](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/u/0/).
2. At the top right, click **Ask Gemini**.
3. In the side panel:
   - Select a suggested prompt, or
   - Write your own prompt.
4. Press **Enter**.

### Use Suggested Prompts

1. Select a suggestion from the list.
2. To see more options, select **More suggestions**.
3. Replace example text in the prompt box with your own information.
4. Press **Enter**.

### Enter Your Own Prompt

1. Type a prompt in the prompt box.
2. Press **Enter**.

### Optional Controls

- **Undo:** Reverse the last Gemini action.
- **Clear history:** Remove Gemini side panel history.
- **Use specific tabs in a prompt:**
  1. Check the dropdown in the prompt box.
  2. Select only the tabs Gemini should use.
  3. To restore all sources, click **Reset to all sources**.
  4. Enter your prompt.

### Conversation History Warning

To avoid losing history, insert generated output into the spreadsheet.

You lose conversation history when:

- You reload your browser.
- You close and reopen the spreadsheet.
- Your computer goes offline.

### Tip

- For more comprehensive analysis, focus Gemini on specific tables when a sheet contains multiple tables.

---

## 4. Gemini Side Panel Actions & Controls

| Action | Purpose |
|---|---|
| **Ask Gemini** | Open Gemini. |
| **More options** | Clear recent Gemini history and ask for more suggestions. |
| **Expand** | Make Gemini larger. |
| **Collapse** | Return Gemini to original size. |
| **Close** | Close Gemini. |
| **Clear history** | Remove generated text/images not yet inserted. |
| **More suggestions** | Get more text suggestions. |
| **More image suggestions** | Get more generated image suggestions. |
| **Insert** | Insert generated text into the spreadsheet. |
| **Insert image** | Insert a generated image into the spreadsheet. |
| **Copy** | Copy a suggestion. |
| **Retry** | Generate a different response. |
| **Retry with Google Search** | Search Google for more information about a question/request. |
| **View more / View less** | Expand or minimize Gemini’s response. |
| **Good suggestion / Bad suggestion** | Send positive or negative feedback. |
| **Show code / Hide code** | Show or hide code used to generate output. |
| **Gem** | Chat with or create a Gem for repetitive tasks. |
| **Undo** | Reverse the last Gemini action. |
| **Export to Docs** | Export Gemini’s response to a new Google Doc. |

---

## 5. Summarize a Spreadsheet

When opening a spreadsheet with data, Gemini may show a **summary of the spreadsheet** in the side panel.

You can:

- Click **View more** to expand the summary.
- Use **Good suggestion** or **Bad suggestion** to provide feedback.

### Example Prompts

> “Summarize this spreadsheet.”

> “Explain this spreadsheet like I'm 5 years old.”

### Keyboard Shortcuts

To summarize a spreadsheet:

- **Mac:** `⌘ + Ctrl + n`
- **Windows:** `Ctrl + Alt + n`

4. Press **Enter**.

   -

# Document 98: Collaborate with Gemini in Google Slides - Computer - Google Docs Editors Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/docs/answer/14355071?hl=en
Product area: slides
Original file: docs/collaborate-with-gemini-in-google-slides-computer-google-docs-editors-help-e5ebf5f3.md

# Collaborate with Gemini in Google Slides — Summary

**Source:** Google Docs Editors Help — “Collaborate with Gemini in Google Slides”  
**URL:** https://support.google.com/docs/answer/14355071?hl=en

---

## Overview

Gemini in Google Slides helps users create and improve presentations directly inside Slides. With Gemini, you can:

- Generate images.
- Generate new slides.
- Summarize a presentation.
- Write or rewrite content.
- Reference Google Drive files while writing or creating slides.

---

## Feature Availability

- Requires an eligible **Google Workspace** or **Google AI** plan.
- Supported languages vary by feature.
- Gems are not supported in all languages.

---

## Getting Started with Gemini in Slides

1. Open a presentation in [Google Slides](https://docs.google.com/presentation) on your computer.
2. At the top right, click **Ask Gemini**.
3. In the side panel:
   - Select a suggested prompt, or
   - Write your own prompt.
4. Press **Enter**.

### Using Suggested Prompts

1. Choose from the suggestions list.
   - **Tip:** Select **More suggestions** for more options.
2. Replace the example text in the prompt box with your own information.
3. Press **Enter**.

### Entering Your Own Prompt

1. Type your prompt in the prompt box at the bottom.
2. Press **Enter**.

### Conversation History

You can remove your Gemini history by selecting:

> **More options → Clear history**

**Important tip:** Insert a suggestion into your presentation if you don’t want to lose it. Conversation history is lost when:

- You refresh your browser.
- You close and reopen the presentation.
- Your computer goes offline.

---

## Gemini Side Panel Controls

| Action | What it does |
|---|---|
| **Ask Gemini** | Opens Gemini. |
| **More options** | Clears recent Gemini history or shows more suggestions. |
| **Expand** | Makes Gemini larger. |
| **Collapse** | Returns Gemini to its original size. |
| **Close** | Closes Gemini. |
| **Clear history** | Removes generated text/images not yet inserted. |
| **More suggestions** | Shows more Gemini suggestions. |
| **More image suggestions** | Shows more generated image options. |
| **Insert** | Inserts generated text into a slide. |
| **Insert image** | Inserts a generated image into a slide. |
| **Insert slide** | Inserts a generated slide into the presentation. |
| **Copy** | Copies a suggestion. |
| **Retry** | Generates a different response. |
| **Preview** | Shows the full suggestion before insertion. |
| **Retry with Google Search** | Searches Google for more information related to your prompt. |
| **View more / View less** | Expands or minimizes a Gemini response. |
| **Good suggestion / Bad suggestion** | Sends positive or negative feedback. |
| **Help me create a slide** | Opens Gemini for slide creation. |
| **Gem** | Opens Gems for repetitive tasks or custom workflows. |

---

## Generate Images Based on Slide Content

To create images with Gemini:

1. Open a presentation in Google Slides.
2. Click **Ask Gemini**.
3. In the side panel, click:

> **Tools → Create images**

4. Enter a prompt describing the desired image.
5. Click **Submit**.
6. After the image is generated, optionally:
   - Select **Preview** to view it.
   - Select **Generate more** for additional options.
7. In the preview window, click **Insert** to place the image in a slide.
8. To edit the generated image, enter a new prompt describing the adjustment.

**Important limitation:** Generated images are for use only within Google Slides.

---

## Generate a New Slide with Gemini

Gemini can generate new slides from prompts, current presentation context, or referenced Drive files.

### Steps

1. Open a presentation in Google Slides.
   - **Tip:** New slides use your current presentation theme.
2. Open Gemini using one of these methods:
   - Click **Ask Gemini** at the top right.
   - Click **Help me create a slide** in the toolbar.
   - Go to **Insert → Help me create a slide**.
   - Go to **Slide → Help me create a slide**.
3. Select a prompt or write your own.
4. Optionally reference Drive files using `@`.
5. Review the generated slide.

### Example Prompts

> “Create a slide about how to optimally train for a marathon.”

> “Create a slide about @Company 2023 Goals deck.”

> "Create a slide about doc @Core Team sync."

> "Create a slide proposing ideas for engaging with our customers."

### Actions Available After Slide Generation

- **Retry:** Generate a different version.
- **Insert:** Add the generated slide to your presentation.
- **Close:** Exit the generated slide preview and refine with a new prompt.
- **Good suggestion / Bad suggestion:** Send feedback.

---

## Generate Text in a Slide

Gemini can generate text for empty text boxes, including titles and body text.

**Requirement:** The slide must already contain existing content for these suggestions to appear.

### Steps

1. Open a presentation in Google Slides.
2. Select an empty text box.
3. Choose one of the options below the box:
   - *

**URL:** https://support.google.com/docs/answer/14207419?hl=en

---

## Overview

Gemini in Google Slides lets users collaborate with AI directly inside Slides to create, edit, summarize, and enhance presentations.

With Gemini in Google Slides, you can:

- Generate and edit new slides.
- Generate images.
- Use, implement, or reference the style of a current presentation.
- Reference your Drive files or Gmail emails as you write or create a new slide.
- Summarize a presentation.
- Write or rewrite content.

---

## Feature Availability

Gemini in Slides is not universally available to all users.

**Requirements and availability:**

- Requires an eligible **Google Workspace** or **Google AI plan**.
- Supported languages depend on Google Workspace with Gemini language availability.
- Available in **Google Workspace Experiments**, a trusted tester program for users with personal accounts.
- To check Workspace Experiments access:
  - Open a new presentation in Google Slides.
  - Look for a bottom bar indicating access.

---

## Getting Started with Gemini in Slides

### Open Gemini

1. On your computer, open a presentation in [Google Slides](https://docs.google.com/presentation).
2. At the top right, click **Ask Gemini**.
3. In the side panel, either:
   - Select a suggested prompt, or
   - Write your own prompt.
4. Press **Enter**.

### Use a Suggested Prompt

1. Select from the list of suggestions.
   - **Tip:** Select **More suggestions** to see additional options.
2. Replace the example text in the prompt box with your own information.
3. Press **Enter**.

### Enter Your Own Prompt

1. At the bottom of the side panel, type a prompt.
2. Press **Enter**.

### Clear Gemini History

To remove Gemini history:

1. Click **More options**.
2. Select **Clear history**.

**Important tip:**

> To avoid losing your history, insert a suggestion into your presentation.

You lose conversation history when:

- You refresh your browser.
- You close and reopen the presentation.
- Your computer goes offline.

1. Click **More options**.

# Document 100: Collaborate with Gemini in Google Slides - Google Docs Editors Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/docs/answer/14355406?hl=en
Product area: slides
Original file: docs/collaborate-with-gemini-in-google-slides-google-docs-editors-help-bc768ede.md

# Collaborate with Gemini in Google Slides

**Source:** [Google Docs Editors Help](https://support.google.com/docs/answer/14355406?hl=en)

## Overview

Gemini in Google Slides lets you:

- Generate and edit new slides
- Generate images
- Use the style of an existing presentation
- Reference Drive files or Gmail emails
- Summarize a presentation
- Write or rewrite content

## Feature Availability

- Requires an eligible Google Workspace or Google AI plan ([Learn more](https://support.google.com/drive/answer/13952129))
- Check [supported languages](https://support.google.com/docs/answer/14925782)
- Also available in Google Workspace Experiments for personal accounts (look for a bottom bar in Slides). [How to join](https://support.google.com/docs/answer/13447104)

## Get Started

1. Open a presentation in [Google Slides](https://docs.google.com/presentation).
2. Click **Ask Gemini** (top right).
3. In the side panel, either:
   - **Select a suggested prompt**: Pick one, optionally replace example text, press Enter.
   - **Enter your own prompt**: Type in the box and press Enter.
4. **Important:** Your conversation history is temporary. It’s lost when you refresh the browser, close/reopen the presentation, or go offline. Insert suggestions to keep them.

> **Tip:** To manually clear history, click More options → **Clear history**.

## Gemini Interface Buttons

| Action | Button |
|--------|--------|
| Open Gemini | Ask Gemini icon |
| Clear recent history / more suggestions | More options |
| Enlarge panel | Expand |
| Minimize panel | Collapse |
| Close panel | Close |
| Remove all uninserted text/images | Clear history |
| More prompt suggestions | More suggestions |
| More image suggestions | More image suggestions |
| Insert generated text | Insert |
| Insert generated image | Insert image |
| Insert generated slide | Insert slide |
| Copy suggestion | Copy |
| Regenerate a response | Retry |
| Preview full suggestion | Preview |
| Search Google for more info | Retry with Google Search |
| View more of a response | View more |
| Minimize response | View less |
| Positive feedback | Good suggestion |
| Report an issue | Bad suggestion |
| Open Gems (custom AI assistants) | Gem |

## Key Tasks

### Generate Images

- **Based on slide content:** “Suggest images for this slide” or choose a suggested prompt.
- **Direct prompt:** “Generate an image of a dog with glasses.”
- Click **Insert** on the desired image.
- Edit the prompt with **Edit Prompt** or generate more with **Show more**.

### Generate a Slide

1. Enter a prompt describing the slide.
2. *(Optional)* Match another presentation’s style: click **Match a presentation style** → **Add from Drive** → select file → **Add**.
3. After preview:
   - **Insert** as a new slide (click Down arrow next to Replace → Insert)
   - **Replace** the selected slide.
- You can only generate one slide at a time, and you can edit it afterwards.

### Summarize & Write Content

- The side panel automatically shows a summary of the presentation (if text exists).
- Example prompts:
  - “Summarize this presentation in 3 sentences.”
  - “Create a slide that celebrates a colleague's work anniversary.”
- Insert with **Insert** button; regenerate with **Retry**.

### Reference Drive Files

- Use `@` mentions in prompts: e.g., “Create a slide outlining the next steps from @meeting notes and @core team sync.”
- Click **Sources** at the bottom of the response to see which files were used.

### Get Help with Slides

Ask questions like:
- “How do I start a Q&A while presenting?”
- “What is the best way to share these slides with more than 100 people?”

### Ask Questions from the Web

Include phrases like *“Use Google Search”* or *“Using web search”* in the prompt.
- “Write a paragraph describing the weather today in Mountain View, California using web search.”
- “Help me write a paragraph explaining if lightning can strike the same place twice using only information from the web.”

### Use a Gem

1. Click Ask Gemini → **Gems** (in side panel).
2. Choose a premade or custom Gem.
3. Type a question to start a conversation.

> **Tip:** Create custom Gems at [gemini.google.com](http://gemini.google.com/). Learn more about [Gems in Gemini Apps](https://support.google.com/gemini/answer/15146780).

### Edit Slides with Gemini

Describe the changes you want directly, e.g.:
- “Simplify this slide with less text and add an image.”
- “Convert to two columns.”

Then follow the same Insert/Replace options. You can close the preview or refine the prompt using Ask Gemini.

## Feedback on Generated Output

- Click **Good suggestion** or **Bad suggestion** at the bottom right of the output.
- Select which data to share (prompt, context, outputs). You can uncheck items.
- For bad suggestions, click **Next** to specify the issue and add details.
- **Don’t include personal, confidential, or sensitive info** in feedback.

General feedback: **Help** → **Help Slides improve**.  
Report a legal issue: [Create a

[... summary truncated for context management ...]

# Document 101: Compare Google AI expansion add‑ons  |  Getting started  |  Google Workspace Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/a/answer/14700766?hl=en
Product area: admin-console
Original file: docs/compare-google-ai-expansion-add-ons-getting-started-google-workspace-help-1fa25b17.md

# Compare Google AI Expansion Add-ons — Google Workspace Help

**Source:** https://support.google.com/a/answer/14700766?hl=en  
**Last updated:** 2026-06-11 UTC

---

## Purpose

Google AI expansion add-ons let organizations get **higher access to advanced AI capabilities in Google Workspace**, including advanced video/image generation, Workspace Studio, NotebookLM, Google Flow, and other Gemini-powered features.

---

## Key Excerpts

> Google AI expansion add-ons enable organizations to get higher access to advanced AI capabilities in Workspace.

> Your organization's use of advanced AI features in Google Workspace is subject to usage limits. These limits are based on which Google Workspace edition you're using and any AI expansion licenses that you've assigned to users.

> Usage limits don't roll over, and they can't be shared with other people.

> **Important:** Some of the products below are not part of Google Workspace. They are additional services and are subject to their own terms of service. Interactions with these tools may be used to train models.

> \* AI Ultra Access is no longer available for purchase.

> † As of May 5, 2026, new AI Expanded Access subscriptions are not eligible to receive credits for Google Flow.

---

# Usage Limits

## Reset Cadence

AI usage limits reset either **daily** or **monthly**, depending on the feature.

- **Daily limits** reset at **12:00 a.m. PT each day**
- **Monthly limits** reset at **12:00 a.m. PT on the first day of each month**
- Limits:
  - **Do not roll over**
  - **Cannot be shared** with other users

## Actions That Count Against AI Usage Limits

Examples of usage that consumes limits include:

- Asking AI to perform work, such as:
  - Generating videos in **Google Flow** or **Google Vids**
  - Generating images with **Nano Banana** in Slides or Vids
  - Using the **AI function in Google Sheets**
  - Generating **audio overviews** in Gemini, NotebookLM, and Drive
- Creating and running flows in **Workspace Studio**

---

# Business / Enterprise: Advanced AI Feature Limits

The comparison covers:

- **Business Starter**
- **Business Standard / Business Plus**
- **Enterprise Standard / Enterprise Plus**
- **AI Expanded Access**
- **AI Ultra Access**

## Add-on Descriptions

### AI Expanded Access

Higher access to advanced AI capabilities in Workspace, including:

- Workspace Studio
- Advanced image generation
- Advanced video generation
- Other advanced AI capabilities

### AI Ultra Access

Highest access tier for advanced AI capabilities, intended for specialists working on ambitious creative and research projects.

> **Note:** AI Ultra Access is no longer available for purchase.

---

## Advanced AI Feature Limits Table

| Advanced AI Feature | Business Starter | Business Standard / Plus Enterprise Standard / Plus | AI Expanded Access | AI Ultra Access |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| **Avatar Generation in Vids** | 10/month | 10/month | 100/month | 500/month |
| **Video Generation in Vids** | 20/month | 20/month | 200/month | 1,000/month |
| **Convert Slides to Vids with Avatars** | Promotional access until July 1, 2026 | Promotional access until July 1, 2026 | Promotional access until July 1, 2026 | Promotional access until July 1, 2026 |
| **Nano Banana Pro in Slides and Vids** | 5/month | 30/month | 300/month | 1,000/month |
| **Music Generation in Vids — 30s clips** | 20/month | 50/month | 250/month | — |
| **Music Generation in Vids — Full tracks up to 3 min** | 10/month | 20/month | 100/month | — |
| **Match writing style / match document format in Docs** | Promotional access; limits begin July 15, 2026 | Same | Same | Same |
| **AI Function in Sheets** | Promotional access; limits begin July 15, 2026 | Same | Same | Same |
| **Build and edit with Gemini in Sheets** | Promotional access; limits begin July 15, 2026 | Same | Same | Same |
| **Speech translation in Google Meet** | Promotional access; limits coming soon | Same | Same | Same |
| **Workspace Studio** | Promotional access; limits begin July 1, 2026 | Same | Same | Same |
| **Workspace Studio flow executions** | 100/month | 400/month | 2,000/month | 10,000/month |
| **Audio overviews for PDFs** | No access | 20/day | 40/day | 200/day |
| **Organize files in Drive** | Promotional access; limits begin July 15, 2026 | Same | Same | Same |
| **Gemini app 3.0 Pro** | Varies by feature | Varies by feature | Varies by feature | Varies by feature |
| **NotebookLM** | Standard access | Higher level access | Expanded level access | Highest level access |
| **Google Flow** | 50 credits/day | 50 credits/day | 2,000 credits/month† | 25,000 credits/month |

† **As of May 5, 2026**, new AI Expanded Access subscriptions are **not eligible** to receive credits for Google Flow.

# Document 102: Configure the Chat MCP server  |  Google Chat  |  Google for Developers

Source URL: https://developers.google.com/workspace/chat/api/guides/configure-mcp-server
Product area: chat-developers
Original file: docs/configure-the-chat-mcp-server-google-chat-google-for-developers-8746e948.md

# Configure the Google Chat MCP Server — Summary

**Source:** Google Developers — Google Chat API  
**URL:** https://developers.google.com/workspace/chat/api/guides/configure-mcp-server  
**Last updated:** 2026-06-05 UTC

---

## Overview

Google Chat provides a remote **Model Context Protocol (MCP)** server that lets AI agents securely interact with Google Chat data.

By configuring the Google Chat MCP server, AI applications such as **Google Antigravity** and **Claude** can perform actions in Google Chat.

The MCP server enables AI agents to:

- **Read data:** List and search conversations, and read messages.
- **Respect security:** Inherit the same permissions and data governance controls as the user.

---

## Prerequisites

You need:

- A **Google Cloud project**
  - See: [Create a project](https://developers.google.com/workspace/guides/create-project)
- An **MCP client**, such as [Google Antigravity](https://antigravity.google/)
- The **gcloud CLI** set up in a local development environment

### gcloud CLI setup

1. Install the Google Cloud CLI:  
   https://cloud.google.com/sdk/docs/install
2. If already installed, update it:

```bash
gcloud components update
```

3. If using an external identity provider, sign in with your federated identity.
4. Initialize the gcloud CLI:  
   https://docs.cloud.google.com/sdk/docs/initializing

---

## Configure the Google Chat MCP Server

To use the Google Chat MCP server:

1. Enable required APIs in your Google Cloud project.
2. Enable MCP services.
3. Configure a Google Chat app.
4. Set up OAuth consent.
5. Configure your MCP client.

---

## 1. Enable the Google Chat API

Enable the **Google Chat API** in your Google Cloud project.

### CLI

```bash
gcloud services enable chat.googleapis.com --project=PROJECT_ID
```

Replace:

- `PROJECT_ID` — your Google Cloud project ID

### Console

Use the Google Cloud Console flow:

- [Enable the APIs](https://console.cloud.google.com/flows/enableapi?apiid=chat.googleapis.com)

---

## 2. Enable the Google Chat MCP API

Enable the MCP components by enabling the **Google Chat MCP API**.

### CLI

```bash
gcloud services enable chatmcp.googleapis.com --project=PROJECT_ID
```

Replace:

- `PROJECT_ID` — your Google Cloud project ID

### Console

Use the Google Cloud Console flow:

- [Enable the MCP services](https://console.cloud.google.com/flows/enableapi?apiid=chatmcp.googleapis.com)

---

## 3. Configure the Chat App

You must configure a Chat app in your Google Cloud project.

### Steps

1. In Google Cloud Console, search for **Google Chat API**.
2. Go to:

   **Google Chat API > Manage > Configuration**

   Or use:  
   [Go to Google Chat API](https://console.cloud.google.com/apis/api/chat.googleapis.com/hangouts-chat)

3. Configure the Chat app:

| Field / Setting | Value |
|---|---|
| **App name** | `Chat MCP` |
| **Avatar URL** | `https://developers.google.com/chat/images/quickstart-app-avatar.png` |
| **Description** | `Chat MCP server` |
| **Functionality** | Turn off interactive features by toggling **Enable interactive features** off |
| **Logs** | Select **Log errors to Logging** |

4. Click **Save**.

Reference:

- [Choose a Google Chat app architecture](https://developers.google.com/workspace/chat/structure)

---

## 4. Set Up the OAuth Consent Screen

The Google Chat MCP server uses **OAuth 2.0** for authentication and authorization. You must configure OAuth consent before creating an OAuth client ID.

### Start OAuth Branding Setup

Go to:

- **Google Auth Platform > Branding**

Or use:  
[Go to Branding](https://console.cloud.google.com/auth/branding)

If **Google Auth Platform** is already configured, update settings in:

- [Branding](https://console.cloud.google.com/auth/branding)
- [Audience](https://console.cloud.google.com/auth/audience)
- [Data Access](https://console.cloud.google.com/auth/scopes)

If you see **Google Auth Platform not configured yet**, click **Get Started**.

### OAuth Consent Screen Setup

1. Under **App Information**:
   - **App name:** `Chat MCP Server`
   - **User support email:** Select your email address or an appropriate Google group
2. Click **Next**.
3. Under **Audience**:
   - Select **Internal**
   - If **Internal** is unavailable, select **External**
4. Click **Next**.
5. Under **Contact Information**:
   - Enter an email address for project notifications
6. Click **Next**.
7. Under **Finish**:
   - Review the [Google API Services User Data Policy](https://developers.google.com/terms/api-services-user-data-policy)
   - If you agree, select **I agree to the Google API Services: User Data Policy**
8. Click **Continue**.
9. Click **Create**.

### If You Selected External

Add test users:

1. Click **Audience**.
2. Under **Test users**, click **Add users**.
3. Enter your email address and any other authorized test users.
4. Click **Save**.

---

Go to:

**Data Access > Add or Remove Scopes**

Under **Manually add scopes**, paste these scopes:

```text
