# Document 103: Connect flows to other services with webhooks - Workspace Studio Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/workspace-studio/answer/16521900
Product area: workspace-studio
Original file: docs/connect-flows-to-other-services-with-webhooks-workspace-studio-help-8f651c2a.md

# Connect flows to other services with webhooks — Workspace Studio Help

**Source:** https://support.google.com/workspace-studio/answer/16521900  
**Status:** _This feature is in limited preview._

## Overview

Workspace Studio flows can send information to external services using a **webhook step**. A webhook sends an **HTTP request** to a specific URL you provide.

Example use case: posting a message to a Slack channel.

> **Important:** When you add variables to third-party integration steps or add-on steps, they might contain your Google Account data, such as the contents of a message in Gmail or Chat, or event information from Calendar. The flow can then share your Google Account data with a third-party service. Make sure you trust the third-party service.

---

## Add a webhook step

To add a webhook to a flow:

1. Go to [https://studio.workspace.google.com](https://studio.workspace.google.com/) and create a flow.
2. In the flow, click **Add step**.
3. Click **Send a webhook**.
4. Configure the webhook step.
5. Optionally add later steps that use the webhook response.
6. Optionally run a test.
7. When ready, click **Turn on**.

---

## Webhook configuration options

### 1. Webhook or API URL — required

Enter the full destination URL, including:

```text
http://
```

or

```text
https://
```

Key details:

- The URL must be **static**.
- Variables are **not allowed** in the URL for security reasons.
- The URL is stored securely.

---

### 2. Method — required

Choose the HTTP method for the request. The default is **POST**.

| Method | Purpose |
|---|---|
| **GET** | Retrieve data from a URL. |
| **POST** | Send data to create something, like a message or record. |
| **PUT** | Send data to replace existing data. |
| **PATCH** | Send data to do a partial update. |
| **DELETE** | Remove data. |

---

### 3. Payload — optional

The payload is the request body content.

Key details:

- Payloads are ignored for **GET** requests.
- Payloads are sent as **plain text**.
- Any format can be used, but Google recommends **JSON** for web services.
- If variables are inserted into the payload, ensure their content does **not break the payload format**.

> **Note:** Payload is sent as plain text. You can use any format, but we recommend using JSON format to easily send data to web services. If you use a variable to add content to the payload, make sure the variable’s content doesn't break the payload’s format.

---

## Using the webhook response

After the webhook runs, the webhook step outputs the response as **plain text** in a variable.

You can use this variable in later steps, including:

- Adding the raw response to another step
- Using an **AI-powered step** to clean up the response
- Extracting specific content from the response

---

## Test runs

You can test the flow before turning it on:

1. Click **Test run**.
2. Select the starting conditions.
3. Click **Start**.

Important behavior:

- A test run runs the flow **once**.
- It takes **real actions**, so you can verify the actual outcome.

---

## Example payloads

| **Method** | **Example payload** |
|---|---|
| POST | `{`<br>` "text": "Hello world!"`<br>` }` |
| GET | `payload is ignored for GET requests` |
| PUT | `{`<br>` "contactId": " _<variable>_",`<br>` "email": "dana@example.com"`<br>` }` |
| DELETE | `{`<br>` "recordId": " _<variable>_",`<br>` }` |
| PATCH | `{`<br>` "recordId": " _<variable>_",`<br>` }` |

---

## Monitor webhook activity

After a flow runs, check the **Activity** tab to review webhook activity.

If the webhook worked, you can see:

- The data that was sent
- The response that was returned

If the webhook had issues, you can see:

- An error message
- The full error code
- The response from the destination URL

This information can help troubleshoot problems with the webhook destination or request.

---

# Document 104: Connect the Google Workspace app to Gemini Apps - Gemini Apps Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/gemini/answer/15229592
Product area: gemini-apps
Original file: docs/connect-the-google-workspace-app-to-gemini-apps-gemini-apps-help-045ae31a.md

# Connect the Google Workspace App to Gemini Apps — Summary

**Source:** Google Gemini Apps Help — “Connect the Google Workspace app to Gemini Apps”  
**URL:** https://support.google.com/gemini/answer/15229592

---

## Overview

Gemini Apps can connect to your Google Workspace data to help you work across Google services. Once connected, Gemini can retrieve, summarize, organize, and act on information from supported Workspace apps.

With the **Google Workspace app**, Gemini Apps can help you:

- Summarize, answer questions, and find information from services like **Gmail, Docs, and Drive**
- Add, edit, and retrieve **Google Tasks** and reminders
- Create and retrieve **Google Keep** notes and lists
- Create and manage **Google Calendar** events

> **Tip:** You can also get personalized Gemini experiences based on your content in Gmail when you connect the Google Workspace app.

---

## Requirements

To connect Google Workspace to Gemini Apps, you must:

- **Be signed in to the Gemini app**
  - Use the **same account** you use with Google Workspace.
- **Have Keep Activity turned on**
  - Gemini can’t connect to Google Workspace if this setting is off.

### Important Notes

- Only **some Google Workspace services** are currently available in **Live chats**.
- Gemini may provide incorrect or outdated information.

> **Important:** Gemini Apps can hallucinate responses or provide outdated information, like from an older email when there’s a more recent one. To check the information, select and review the sources listed after the response.

---

## How to Use the Google Workspace App in Gemini Apps

1. Sign in to Gemini with the same account you use for Google Workspace.
2. Ask Gemini to get information from Google Workspace.
3. If Google Workspace is not yet connected, Gemini will offer the option to connect it.
4. Follow the on-screen instructions.

You can disconnect the Google Workspace app later from **Connected Apps settings** at:  
https://gemini.google.com/apps

### Prompting Tip

Do **not** paste the URL of a Google document or email into your prompt. Instead:

- Use keywords from the content
- Specify the relevant service or content type
- Use `@` to select a Google Workspace service

> **Tip:** Don’t paste the URL of a document or email in your prompt. To reference content in Google Workspace, use keywords from the content and specify the service or content type. To specify a Google Workspace service, enter `@` and select the service.

---

## Example Prompts

### Find Information

- “What dates did Erik propose in the email about the Grand Canyon hiking trip?”
- “Find the most recent lease agreement from my Drive and check how much the security deposit was”

### Summarize a Document

- “Find my resume titled June 2023 from my drive and summarize it to a short paragraph personal statement”

### Simplify and Organize Information

- “Find the doc from Ashley about the neighborhood clean-up project and summarize the proposal for me in 5 bullets”

### Capture Tasks in Google Tasks

- “Help me plan a camping trip and create a packing list for me.”
- Follow-up example: “Create a reminder to bring those things in Tasks.”

### Save Ideas and Notes to Google Keep

- “Recommend some places to visit in Kyoto and save them to Keep.”
- “Find me a recipe for apple pie and save the ingredients to Keep.”

### Check Schedule and Manage Calendar Events

- “What’s on my calendar today?”
- “Check the email that Alice sent about our trip to Tahoe this weekend, and add it to my calendar.”

---

## Why You Might Not Be Able to Connect Google Workspace

### Work or School Account Restrictions

For work and school accounts, a Google Workspace administrator must enable app connections first.

> **For work and school accounts, the Google Workspace administrator must first enable the ability to connect with apps.**

### Gmail Smart Features May Be Off

Gemini Apps require **Smart features in other Google products** to be enabled in Gmail settings.

If you turn this setting on:

1. Reload the Gemini app.
2. Try connecting Google Workspace again.

### You Must Mention Workspace Explicitly in New Chats

For every new conversation, explicitly ask Gemini to get or add something from your personal Google Workspace.

You can mention:

- **Gmail** or email
- **Docs** or documents
- **Drive** or PDFs
- **Calendar** or events
- **Tasks** or to-dos
- **Keep** or notes

You do **not** need to repeat this in follow-up questions within the same chat.

Example:

- **First prompt:** “Summarize my recent emails from Harper elementary school”
- **Next prompt:** “When is pajama day?”

---

## What Gemini Apps Can’t Do with Google Workspace

Gemini Apps currently can’t:

- Access **comments** or **images** in Docs or Gmail
- Access **pictures and videos** in Drive
  - This includes anything not in a document, spreadsheet, presentation, or PDF
- Create, draft, or delete:
  - Spreadsheets
  - PDFs
  - Documents
  - Presentations
- Manage Drive conten

[... summary truncated for context management ...]

---

# Document 105: Control Gemini App access to Workspace services  |  Google Workspace Help

Source URL: https://knowledge.workspace.google.com/admin/generative-ai/gemini-app/turn-google-apps-in-gemini-on-or-off
Product area: admin-console
Original file: docs/control-gemini-app-access-to-workspace-services-google-workspace-help-34c37b07.md

# Control Gemini App Access to Workspace Services – Summary

**Source:** [Google Workspace Help](https://knowledge.workspace.google.com/admin/generative-ai/gemini-app/turn-google-apps-in-gemini-on-or-off)  
**Last updated:** 2026-06-11 UTC

## Overview

As an admin, you can allow the Gemini app to interact with other Google services so users get more helpful, context-aware responses. This setting is available for most Google Workspace editions and qualifying Education plans with the Gemini app core service or add-on. You can also manage access to non-Google apps (e.g., Gemini for GitHub) via the Marketplace allowlist.

## Prerequisites

- **Smart features:** Google Workspace smart features must be turned on for your users.  
  → [Manage Google Workspace smart features for your users](https://knowledge.workspace.google.com/admin/security/manage-google-workspace-smart-features-for-your-users)
- **Conversation history:** The Gemini conversation history setting must be enabled for Workspace apps to work.  
- **Admin privilege:** You need the **Gemini Settings** administrator privilege.

## Supported Apps

### Workspace Apps (require Google Workspace license)
- Google Calendar
- Google Docs
- Google Drive
- Gmail
- Google Keep
- Google Tasks

> **Note:** Users with Gemini as an additional Google service **cannot** use Workspace apps.

### Other Google Apps (available to all Google users, including those without a Workspace license)
- Google Maps
- YouTube (including YouTube Music)
- Google Hotels
- Google Flights

These are treated as additional services and governed by the [Google Terms of Service](https://policies.google.com/terms).

## Using Workspace Apps – Key Behaviors

- **Service dependencies:** If your organization has disabled a particular Workspace service (e.g., Tasks), the corresponding Workspace app is unavailable in Gemini.  
  **Critical:** When a prompt contains multiple actions involving different services, and *any* of them is disabled, **none** of the actions are completed.  
  > Example: “Create a Calendar event and add a reminder” → If Tasks is off, the event won’t be created and no reminder is set.

- **Citations in Gmail:** To get links to additional Gmail information in Gemini’s responses (citations), Workspace apps must be turned on.

- **File uploads:** Users can upload Drive files to Gemini or to Gems only when Workspace apps are enabled.  
  → [Upload & analyze files in Gemini Apps](https://support.google.com/gemini/answer/14903178)  
  → [Use Gems in Gemini Apps](https://support.google.com/gemini/answer/15146780)

- **Turning off Workspace apps:** Existing chats that used Workspace apps remain available, but Gemini can no longer access additional Workspace data beyond the original prompt. Workspace access resumes only after re-enabling.

## Steps to Turn Google Apps in Gemini On or Off

1. Go to the **Google Admin console** → **Menu** → **Generative AI** → **Gemini app**.  
   *(Direct link: [Go to Gemini app](https://admin.google.com/ac/managedsettings/47208553126))*
2. Under **Apps**, select:
   - **Allow access to Workspace apps** (for Gmail, Calendar, Docs, etc.)
   - **Allow access to other Google apps** (for Maps, YouTube, Flights, Hotels)
3. (Optional) Choose an **organizational unit** or **configuration group** to scope the setting.  
   → Group settings override organizational units. [Learn more](https://knowledge.workspace.google.com/admin/users/advanced/customize-service-settings-using-configuration-groups#how-groups-work)
4. Click **Save**.

> Changes typically take effect within a few minutes but can take up to **24 hours**.

## Important Notes

- Users can individually turn off smart features for their accounts, which may affect Gemini’s ability to access Workspace data.  
  → [Turn Google Workspace smart features on or off (user instructions)](https://support.google.com/mail/answer/15604322)
- For non-Google apps (like third-party integrations), use the [Marketplace app allowlist](https://knowledge.workspace.google.com/admin/apps/manage-the-marketplace-app-allowlist-for-your-organization).

## Related Topics

- [Gemini Apps Help Center](https://support.google.com/gemini)
- [Use apps connected to Gemini with a work or school Google Account](https://support.google.com/gemini/answer/14959807)
- [Google Workspace with Gemini FAQ](https://knowledge.workspace.google.com/admin/generative-ai/workspace-with-gemini/gemini-for-google-workspace-faq-business)

---

# Document 106: Converse with Data Studio data  |  Google Cloud Documentation

Source URL: https://cloud.google.com/looker/docs/studio/conversational-analytics-looker-studio
Product area: looker-studio
Original file: docs/converse-with-data-studio-data-google-cloud-documentation-255c363a.md

# Converse with Data Studio Data — Comprehensive Summary

**Source:** Google Cloud Documentation — Data Studio  
**URL:** https://cloud.google.com/looker/docs/studio/conversational-analytics-looker-studio  
**Last updated:** 2026-06-11 UTC  
**Note:** Looker Studio is now called **Data Studio**.

---

## Overview

This documentation explains how to use **Conversational Analytics in Data Studio** to ask natural-language questions about connected data sources, manage conversations, understand generated results, and work within known limitations.

Conversational Analytics lets users interact with data through chat-style conversations. Responses may include text explanations, tables, visualizations, SQL, reasoning traces, and suggested follow-up insights.

For setup and connection instructions, see:

- **Set up Conversational Analytics in Data Studio**
- **Conversational Analytics overview**
- **Create and converse with data agents**
- **Enable advanced analytics with the Code Interpreter**

Google also links to guidance on **how and when Gemini for Google Cloud uses your data**.

---

## Key Excerpts & Important Original Text

### Query cancellation message

When a query is stopped, Conversational Analytics displays:

```text
The query was cancelled.
```

### Example question rephrasing

Conversational Analytics may rephrase user questions:

> Conversational Analytics might rephrase the question "What is the mean of user ages?" to "What is the average user age?"

### Example Fast mode mapping

A natural-language query such as:

> "What was our total revenue last month?"

Can be translated into a query that selects:

```text
total_revenue
```

and filters for the previous month.

### Sample conversation prompt

Example user question:

> "Can you plot monthly sales of hot drinks versus smoothies for 2023, and highlight the top selling month for each type of drink?"

Conversational Analytics responds with a line graph showing monthly sales for hot drinks and smoothies in 2023, highlighting **July** as the top-selling month for both categories.

### Example Insights output

For the prompt:

> "How many users are in each state?"

Conversational Analytics might return insights such as:

- "California, Texas, and Ohio are key states for business operations based on the data provided."
- "England and specific regions in China, namely Anhui and Guangdong, show significant business activity."
- "Some states, including Mie, Akita, and Iwate, have minimal presence based on the data."
- "The data indicates varying operational scales across different locations."

---

## Accessing Conversational Analytics

You can access Conversational Analytics in Data Studio in several ways:

- Go directly to **Conversational Analytics**:
  - https://datastudio.google.com/conversation
- Select **Conversational Analytics** from the Data Studio navigation panel.
- In a **Sandbox** workspace, choose **Conversations** from the **Create** menu.
- If **data agents** exist for your instance:
  - Select **Data source** from the **Create** menu.
  - Choose **Chat with my data** on the **Get started** page.

---

## Conversations: Core Concept

A **conversation** is a set of questions asked about a dataset or data agent.

Using multiple conversations is helpful for organizing different lines of inquiry.

The conversation experience differs between:

- **New Conversational Analytics**
- **Legacy Conversational Analytics**

The documentation contains separate guidance for each experience.

---

## Starting a Conversation

### New Conversational Analytics

All conversations happen with a **data agent** that was:

1. Built in **BigQuery**
2. Published to **Data Studio**

To start:

1. Go to the **Chat with your data** page.
2. Select the data agent you want to use.
   - Use the **Search agents** bar if needed.
3. Enter your question and press:
   - **Return** on Mac
   - **Enter** on PC

To chat with a different data agent, select:

```text
+ New conversation
```

### Permissions for Data Agents

If you lack required permissions:

- The agent appears on the **Chat with your data** page.
- The agent is inactive.
- Clicking the agent card shows a message explaining which IAM role or permission is needed for the agent’s Google Cloud project.

---

### Legacy Conversational Analytics

To create a new conversation:

1. Click **+ Create conversation** in Conversational Analytics.
2. Select either:
   - **Data source**
   - **Data agent**
3. Enter your question and press:
   - **Return** on Mac
   - **Enter** on PC

#### Data Source Option

To use an existing data source:

1. Select the **Data source** panel.
2. Choose a data source.

To create a new data source:

- Select **Connect to data**.

#### Data Agent Option

To use an existing data agent:

1. Select **Agents**.
2. Choose a data agent.

To create a new data agent:

- Select **+ Create agent**.

You can return to prior conversations from the **Recent** section.

---

## Conversing with Looker Data Sou

# Document 107: Create a flow from scratch - Workspace Studio Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/workspace-studio/answer/16447954?hl=en
Product area: workspace-studio
Original file: docs/create-a-flow-from-scratch-workspace-studio-help-27b1c3bd.md

# Create a Flow from Scratch – Workspace Studio Help

## Overview
Build a fully custom automation in Google Workspace Studio by creating a flow from scratch. Every flow consists of:

- **Starter** – the event or schedule that launches the flow (only one per flow).  
- **Step(s)** – tasks the flow performs after starting (actions, notifications, etc.). You can add multiple steps.

**Variables** are placeholders that automatically fill with data from previous steps, making flows dynamic (e.g., inserting the email subject into a Chat message).

---

## Quick Creation Steps

1. Go to [https://studio.workspace.google.com](https://studio.workspace.google.com/) and click **New flow**.
2. Click the **Starter** to choose the event or schedule that launches your flow.
3. Click **Choose a step** and select the task the flow will perform.
4. Name your flow by clicking the pencil icon at the top.
5. **(Optional)** Click **Test run** → **Start** to verify the flow immediately. A “Run Completed” message indicates success.
6. Once satisfied, click **Turn on**.

---

## Detailed Example: Auto-draft an Invoice Reply

This example creates a flow that automatically drafts a Gmail reply whenever an email containing the word “Invoice” arrives.

### Step 1 – Choose Your Starter
1. Click **New flow** → **Choose a starter** → **When I get an email**.
2. In the configuration panel, under *Which messages to check*, select **Specific emails**.
3. In the **Has the words** field, enter: `Invoice`.  
   *(This runs the flow only when an email’s subject or body contains “Invoice”.)*

### Step 2 – Add a Step
4. Below the starter, click **Choose a step** → under **Gmail**, select **Draft a reply**.

### Step 3 – Use Variables for a Dynamic Message
5. Inside the **Message** field of the “Draft a reply” step, type:

   ```
   Hello,

   I received the invoice regarding: [Email subject]

   I will review it and get back to you.
   ```
   - Insert `[Email subject]` by clicking **Variables +** → **Step 1: When I get an email** → **Email subject**.  
   - When the flow runs, the variable chip is replaced with the actual email subject.

### Step 4 – Name, Test & Turn On
6. Click the pencil icon to name your flow (e.g., “Invoice Draft Reply”).
7. Click **Test run**.
   - In the test window, type `Invoice` in the “Email received” field to find a matching recent email.
   - Select an email and click **Start**.
   - A “Run Completed” message indicates success; check your Gmail Drafts for the new draft.
8. Click **Turn on** to activate the flow.

---

## Key Concepts

- **Starter**: Event or schedule that triggers the flow. Example: “When I get an email” with filters.
- **Step**: An action (e.g., “Draft a reply”) or notification.
- **Variables**: Insert dynamic content from previous steps, like `[Email subject]`.  
  *Learn more: [How to use variables to pass data in your flow](https://support.google.com/workspace-studio/answer/16448468).*

> **Tip:** Variables let you create powerful, context-aware automations.

---

## Related Resources
- [Create a flow with Gemini AI](https://support.google.com/workspace-studio/answer/16448469)
- [Create a flow from a template](https://support.google.com/workspace-studio/answer/16448808)
- [Manage and share your flows](https://support.google.com/workspace-studio/answer/16766368)

---

*For a full list of starters and steps, see the [Guide to Starters and Steps in Workspace Studio](https://support.google.com/workspace-studio/answer/16765661).*

---

# Document 108: Create a flow with Gemini AI - Workspace Studio Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/workspace-studio/answer/16448469?hl=en
Product area: workspace-studio
Original file: docs/create-a-flow-with-gemini-ai-workspace-studio-help-928cd3ae.md

[Skip to main content](https://support.google.com/workspace-studio/answer/16448469?hl=en#search-form)

# Create a flow with Gemini AI

**Important:**

If you're signed in with a school account and are under 18, you can’t use any AI features in Workspace Studio. If a flow containing AI steps is shared with you, those steps will be removed when you open the flow.

To quickly create a flow, describe the tasks you want to automate and let Gemini build it for you.

For the best results, be specific in your description. Make sure to include:

- When the flow should start
- Which apps to use
- What steps the flow should take

To create a flow with Gemini AI:

1. On your computer, go to [https://studio.workspace.google.com](https://studio.workspace.google.com/).
2. In the "Describe a task for Gemini" box, enter a detailed description of what you want the flow to do.
   - **Good example:** "Notify me in Google Chat whenever I get an email from person@example.com"
   - **Bad example:** "Handle emails from person@example.com."
3. Click **Create**.

   - Gemini generates the steps for your flow.
     - Review each step to make sure it's correct.
     - You can edit, reorder, delete, or add new steps.
4. If a step needs info from a previous step, such as an email's subject line, add a variable. [Learn how to use variables to pass data in your flow](https://support.google.com/workspace-studio/answer/16448468).
5. Optional: Before you turn on your flow, to make sure it works properly, click **Test run**![and then](https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/3_l97rr0GvhSP2XV5OoCkV2ZDTIisAOczrSdzNCBxhIKWrjXjHucxNwocghoUa39gw=h36) **Start**.

   - This runs the flow immediately so you can verify the output.
   - If everything works fine, you get a “Run Completed” message.
6. After you complete your edits, click **Turn on**.

## Related resources

- [Create a flow from a template](https://support.google.com/workspace-studio/answer/16448808)
- [Create a flow from scratch](https://support.google.com/workspace-studio/answer/16447954)
- [Manage & share your flows in Google Workspace Studio](https://support.google.com/workspace-studio/answer/16766368)

# Document 109: Create a form with Gemini in Google Forms - Google Docs Editors Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/docs/answer/16346789?hl=en
Product area: forms
Original file: docs/create-a-form-with-gemini-in-google-forms-google-docs-editors-help-2d76a83e.md

# Create a Form with Gemini in Google Forms — Summary

**Source:** Google Docs Editors Help  
**URL:** https://support.google.com/docs/answer/16346789?hl=en

---

## Overview

Google Forms supports a Gemini-powered feature called **“Help me create a form”** that can generate new forms from a prompt. It can also create forms using files from Google Drive, including **Google Docs, Sheets, Slides, and PDFs**.

> **Important:** This feature requires an eligible Google Workspace or Google AI plan.  
> [Learn about Gemini features and plans](https://support.google.com/docs/answer/13952129).

### Availability

- The feature is **rolling out gradually**, so it may not be available to all users yet.
- It is currently available on **desktop**.
- Creating quizzes with Gemini has additional availability limits:
  - Available as part of **Gemini Alpha**
  - Admin-controlled access
  - Also available through **Google Workspace Experiments**
  - **Only available in English**

---

## Use Gemini to Create a Form

### Steps

1. On your computer, open a new form in [Google Forms](https://docs.google.com/forms/u/0/).
2. In the window that opens, enter a prompt describing the form you want.
   - Alternatively, in the toolbar, click **Help me create a form**.
   - To get prompt ideas, use suggested prompt tiles or click **Shuffle**.
3. Enter a prompt.

   Example:

   > “Create a simple RSVP form for a neighborhood potluck, collecting name, allergy info, and what dish they'll bring.”

   **Tip:** More detailed prompts usually produce better results.

4. Click **Create**.
5. Optional: After Gemini generates a draft, you can:
   - **Edit your prompt**
     1. At the top of the generated draft, click the prompt.
     2. Edit your prompt.
     3. Click **Submit**.
   - **Create a new version**
     - Click **Recreate** to generate another version for the same prompt or use case.

     > **Important:** After you create a new version, you can’t go back to the previous version.

6. When satisfied with the generated draft, click **Create form**.

### Current Limitations

- Gemini does **not yet support creating forms with multiple sections**.
- Gemini does **not yet support editing an existing form** with “Help me create a form.”
  - If used, the current form will be **replaced with the newly generated form**.

---

## Prompt Examples for Creating Forms

Google recommends including details to generate more complex and useful forms.

Example prompts:

> “Design a form to collect data on exercise habits and fitness goals of adults.”

> “Design a post-event survey to collect attendee feedback on a business networking event.”

> “Develop a 10-question quiz to assess basic knowledge of biology for high school students.”

> “Create an event planning form for a weekend yoga retreat. Include options for accommodation preferences (shared or private room), dietary restrictions, and interest in optional add-on activities. Make sure you have one question which asks which weekend in April 2025 works best for potential attendees.”

---

## Create a Quiz with Gemini in Forms

Gemini can also generate quizzes in Google Forms using **“Help me create.”**

### Feature Availability

- Available as part of **Gemini Alpha**
- Controlled by your organization’s administrator
  - If unavailable, your administrator may not have [Alpha features enabled](https://support.google.com/a/answer/14170809)
- Also available through [Google Workspace Experiments](https://support.google.com/docs/answer/13447104)
- Available **only in English**

### What Gemini Can Generate for Quizzes

- Based on your prompt, Gemini may generate either a **form** or a **quiz**.
- When a quiz is generated, Gemini also:
  - Marks correct answers
  - Assigns point values
- Supported question types include:
  - Multiple choice
  - Checkbox
  - Grid
  - Short answer
  - Long answer

> **Tip:** “Help me create” can also use your documents or curriculum to generate a quiz.

### Steps to Create a Quiz

1. On your computer, open a new form in [Google Forms](https://docs.google.com/forms/u/0/).
2. Enter a prompt describing your quiz.
   - Or use the toolbar and click **Help me create a form**.

   Sample prompt:

   > “Create a 10-question quiz on biology for high-school students based on my course curriculum.“

3. Click **Create**.
4. Review the generated quiz.
5. To review correct answers and point values, check the box next to **“Show answer key.”**
6. Optional: After generating a quiz, you can:
   - **Edit your prompt**
     1. At the top of the generated draft, click the prompt.
     2. Edit your prompt.
     3. Click **Submit**.
   - **Create a new version**
     - Click **Recreate**.

     > **Tip:** After you create a new version, you can’t go back to the previous version.

7. Click **Create quiz**.
8. Optional: After the quiz is created:
   - Edit questions or change the answer key.
   - Learn more: [Create and mark quizzes with Google Forms](https://support.google.com/docs/answer/7032287)


[... summary truncated for context management ...]

# Document 110: Create a form with Gemini in Google Forms (Workspace Experiments) - Google Docs Editors Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/docs/answer/15259015
Product area: forms
Original file: docs/create-a-form-with-gemini-in-google-forms-workspace-experiments-google-docs-editors-help-d8e549bd.md

# Create a form with Gemini in Google Forms (Workspace Experiments)

## Important Context & Availability

- This feature is part of **Google Workspace Experiments** — a trusted tester program for new AI features.
- Availability is limited: **may not be available in your country**, is rolling out gradually, **desktop only**, and **only in English**.
- To check access, open a form in Google Forms and look for the **“Help me create a form”** icon (![](https://storage.googleapis.com/support-kms-prod/YGDHYFzC6amFg7joerJbd1vXRAYKKWYepVig)).
- Sources for forms include prompts, or existing Drive files (Docs, Sheets, Slides, PDFs).

> **“Important:** This article is referring to Google Workspace Experiments, which is a trusted tester program for users to try new AI features. The Workspace Experiments program may not be available in your country.”

---

## How to Use Gemini to Create a Form

### Step-by-Step

1. Open a new form in [Google Forms](https://docs.google.com/forms/u/0/).
2. In the opening window, **enter a prompt** that describes your form (or click the toolbar icon **Help me create a form**).
3. **Example prompt**:
   > “Create a simple RSVP form for a neighborhood potluck, collecting name, allergy info, and what dish they'll bring.”
4. Click **Create**.
5. After generation, you can:
   - **Edit the prompt** (click the prompt at the top, modify, then click Recreate).
   - **Create a new version** (click Recreate).  
     > **“Important:** After you create a new version, you can’t go back to the previous version.”
6. When satisfied, click **Create form**.

> **Tip:** Explore suggested prompt tiles or click **Shuffle** for ideas. More detail in your prompt often yields better results.

> **Important:** The feature **does not yet support** editing an existing form — it will replace the existing form entirely. Also not yet supported: quiz settings or forms with multiple sections.

---

## Creating a Quiz with Gemini

- When a quiz is generated, **correct answers and point values** are automatically marked. Question types can include multiple‑choice, checkbox, grid, short‑answer, and long‑answer.
- **“Help me create”** can also generate quizzes from your own documents or curriculum.

### Steps

1. Open a new form in Google Forms.
2. Enter a quiz prompt, e.g.:
   > “Create a 10-question quiz on biology for high-school students based on my course curriculum.”
3. Click **Create**.
4. Review the generated quiz. Check **“Show answer key”** to see correct answers and points.
5. To refine:
   - **Edit the prompt** (click it, edit, click Submit).
   - **Recreate** for a fresh version (previous version lost).
6. Click **Create quiz**.
7. After generation, you can:
   - Edit questions or change the answer key ([learn more](https://support.google.com/docs/answer/7032287)).
   - In the “Questions” tab, click **Suggest questions** to get more questions based on the existing quiz.
8. Click **Publish** to distribute, then manage responders if needed.

---

## Prompt Writing Tips & Examples

More complex forms benefit from detailed prompts. Examples from the article:

> “Design a form to collect data on exercise habits and fitness goals of adults.”

> “Design a post-event survey to collect attendee feedback on a business networking event.”

> “Develop a 10-question quiz to assess basic knowledge of biology for high school students.”

> “Create an event planning form for a weekend yoga retreat. Include options for accommodation preferences (shared or private room), dietary restrictions, and interest in optional add-on activities. Make sure you have one question which asks which weekend in April 2025 works best for potential attendees.”

---

## Feedback & Safety

You can rate generated forms/quizzes and provide detailed feedback:

- Below the generated draft, click **Good suggestion** ![](https://storage.googleapis.com/support-kms-prod/mKqkrNZec6ZdDZqqln7wU7EGepuseErh6bLo) or **Bad suggestion** ![](https://storage.googleapis.com/support-kms-prod/hWToSpEzNmnGXO4uonbUrmlmgLRUUzGECUlT).
- If bad, select the issue and add comments. You can choose whether to share the generated outputs with your feedback.
- For general feedback, click **Help & feedback** ![](https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/tS95zJZJbw1Setw_BUqNTmPJ4p5Y7ViPOz3VCwPqifr9_fuBOGF01imFJZdmtl98evc=w36-h36) at the bottom of the page.

---

## Disabling the Feature

- To remove the “Help me create a form” button, you must **exit the entire Workspace Experiments program**.
- This **permanently loses access to all Workspace Experiments features**; you cannot rejoin.
- [Learn how to exit Workspace Experiments](https://support.google.com/docs/answer/13447104#labs-opt-out&exit-labs-docs&exit-labs-gmail).

---

## Data Collection & Privacy

When you use the “Help me create a form” prompt, Google stores:

- The prompts you enter
- The generated form
- Your feedback on the generated form

Review the [Google Workspace Experiments Privacy Notice and Terms](https://support.google.co

[... summary truncated for context management ...]

---

# Document 111: Create a list with AI in Google Keep - Google Keep Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/keep/answer/14626262?hl=en
Product area: keep
Original file: docs/create-a-list-with-ai-in-google-keep-google-keep-help-49e781ce.md

# Create a List with AI in Google Keep — Summary

**Source:** [Google Keep Help](https://support.google.com/keep/answer/14626262?hl=en)  
**Feature:** AI-generated lists in Google Keep

---

## Availability

> **Important:** This feature is available to users with Google AI Pro and Google AI Ultra, or a Pixel mobile device.

- The AI list creation feature is available in **Google Keep**.
- It lets users create a new list using the **“Help me create a list”** prompt.
- The feature is currently available on **Android**.

---

## How to Use AI to Create a List in Google Keep

1. Open the **Google Keep** app on your Android device.
2. Tap **Create**.
3. In the bottom-right corner, tap **Help me create a list**.
4. Enter a prompt.

   Example prompts:

   - `"Packing list for a camping trip with 2 kids in summer"`
   - `"Classic movies to watch on Halloween"`
   - `"Spring cleaning checklist for a 2-bedroom apartment"`

   > **Tip:** For better results, write specific instructions. For example: `"Groceries for a week for a vegetarian family of 3."`

5. Tap **Create**.
6. Optional actions after the list is generated:
   - **Send feedback about the suggestion:** Tap **Good suggestion** or **Bad suggestion**.
   - **Edit your prompt:** Tap the prompt at the top of the window, revise it, then tap **Refresh**.
7. Tap **Insert** to add the generated list to your note.

---

## Giving Feedback on a Generated List

Google notes that **Google Workspace with Gemini is constantly learning** and may not always support a request correctly.

If a generated list is inaccurate, unsafe, or otherwise problematic, users can submit feedback to help improve:

- AI-assisted Workspace features
- Broader Google AI efforts

### Important Privacy Warning

> Because feedback may be human readable, do not submit data that contains personal, confidential, or sensitive information.

### Feedback Steps

1. Below the generated list, select:
   - **Good suggestion**
   - **Bad suggestion**
2. If selecting **Bad suggestion**, choose the issue found and optionally enter additional feedback.
3. Optional: Select **What data will be attached?** to review data included with the feedback.
   - To exclude collected data, uncheck:
     > `"Attach collected data to your feedback to help us improve the product experience"`
4. Select **Submit**.

### General Feedback

To provide general feedback on the feature:

- Open the **Menu**
- Select **Help and feedback**

### Legal Issues

To report a legal issue, Google directs users to:

- [Create a legal request](https://support.google.com/legal/answer/3110420)

---

## Gemini Feature Suggestion Disclaimers

Google provides the following guidance about Gemini-powered suggestions:

- Gemini feature suggestions **do not represent Google’s views** and should not be attributed to Google.
- Users should **not rely on Gemini features** as:
  - Medical advice
  - Legal advice
  - Financial advice
  - Other professional advice
- Gemini features may suggest **inaccurate or inappropriate information**.
- Feedback helps make Gemini more **helpful and safe**.
- Enterprise end users may submit feedback, but are informed beforehand that feedback should not include:
  - Personal information
  - Sensitive information
  - Confidential information

---

## Related Resources

- [Make a list](https://support.google.com/keep/answer/6395451)
- [Create or edit a note](https://support.google.com/keep/answer/2888246)
- [Take image notes](https://support.google.com/keep/answer/6395566)

---

## Article Feedback Options

The Help article itself allows users to submit feedback on specific sections. Reportable issues include:

- **Inaccurate** — doesn’t match what appears in the product
- **Hard to understand** — unclear or translation is wrong
- **Missing info** — relevant but not comprehensive
- **Irrelevant** — doesn’t match the title and/or expectations
- **Minor errors** — formatting issues, typos, and/or broken links
- **Other suggestions** — ideas to improve the content

Users are reminded:

> Do not share any personal info

By continuing, users agree that Google uses their answers plus account and system info to improve services, subject to Google’s Privacy Policy and Terms.

---

# Create a List with Gemini in Google Keep — Summary

**Source:** Google Keep Help  
**URL:** https://support.google.com/keep/answer/15222789?hl=en

---

## Overview

Google Keep lets users create AI-generated lists using Gemini through the **“Help me create a list”** prompt.

### Availability

- Requires a **Google AI plan** to create lists with Gemini in Google Keep.
- The feature is available on **Pixel devices**.
- Usage may be subject to **Gemini usage limits**.
- To continue using Gemini in Google Keep, users may need to sign up for a Google AI plan.

---

## Key Excerpts

> You can use AI to create a new list in Google Keep when you select the "Help me create a list" prompt.

> You can create lists with Gemini in Google Keep with a Google AI plan.

> You can use “Help me create a list” on Pixel devices. Your usage may be subject to Gemini usage limits.

**Tip:**

> For better results, write specific instructions. For example: "Groceries for a week for a vegetarian family of 3."

---

## How to Use Gemini to Create a List

1. Open the **Google Keep app** on your device.
2. Tap **Create**.
3. At the bottom right, tap **Help me create a list**.
4. Enter a prompt.

   Example prompts include:

   - `"Packing list for a camping trip with 2 kids in summer"`
   - `"Classic movies to watch on Halloween"`
   - `"Spring cleaning checklist for a 2-bedroom apartment"`

5. Tap **Create**.
6. Optionally, after the list is generated:
   - Give feedback:
     - Tap **Good suggestion**
     - Or tap **Bad suggestion**
   - Edit the prompt:
     1. Tap the prompt at the top of the window.
     2. Edit your prompt.
     3. Tap **Refresh**.
7. Tap **Insert** to add the generated list to Google Keep.

---

## Prompting Guidance

For best results, Google recommends using **specific instructions** rather than vague prompts.

Example of a more specific prompt:

> "Groceries for a week for a vegetarian family of 3."

Specific prompts help Gemini generate more useful and relevant lists.

---

## Giving Feedback on a Generated List

Gemini for Google Workspace:

- Constantly learns
- May not support every request at this time

If a generated list is inaccurate, unsafe, or otherwise problematic, users can submit feedback. Google may use this feedback to improve AI-assisted Workspace features and broader AI efforts.

### Submit Feedback on a Suggested List

1. Below the generated list, select:
   - **Good suggestion**
   - Or **Bad suggestion**
2. If selecting **Bad suggestion**, choose the issue found and optionally enter additional feedback.
3. To include the generated output with feedback, check **“Output.”**
4. Select **Submit**.

### Provide General Feature Feedback

1. Open the **Menu**.
2. Select **Help and feedback**.

### Report Legal Issues

To report a legal issue, users should create a request through Google’s legal support page.

---

## Important Notes About Gemini Suggestions

Google provides several cautions about Gemini-generated content:

- Gemini feature suggestions **do not represent Google’s views** and should not be attributed to Google.
- Users should **not rely on Gemini** for:
  - Medical advice
  - Legal advice
  - Financial advice
  - Other professional advice
- Gemini may suggest **inaccurate or inappropriate information**.
- User feedback helps make Gemini more helpful and safe.
- Feedback should not contain **personal, sensitive, or confidential information**.

### Key Excerpt

> Gemini feature suggestions don’t represent Google’s views, and should not be attributed to Google.

> Do not rely on Gemini as medical, legal, financial, or other professional advice.

> Gemini may suggest inaccurate or inappropriate information. Your feedback makes Gemini more helpful and safe.

# Document 113: Create a notebook in NotebookLM - NotebookLM Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/notebooklm/answer/16206563?hl=en
Product area: notebooklm
Original file: docs/create-a-notebook-in-notebooklm-notebooklm-help-15689b9b.md

# Create a Notebook in NotebookLM — Summary

**Source:** [NotebookLM Help: Create a notebook in NotebookLM](https://support.google.com/notebooklm/answer/16206563?hl=en)

## Key Excerpts

> **Important:** At this time, the NotebookLM mobile app may have limitations to this feature.

> In NotebookLM, you can create individual notebooks. A notebook is a collection of sources for a specific project.

> **Tip:** Each notebook is independent. NotebookLM can’t access information across multiple notebooks at the same time.

> **Tip:** You can add or remove sources in your notebook by selecting **+ Add** or unselecting individual sources in the “Sources” panel.

> **Tip**: When you have 5+ sources, NotebookLM can auto-label & categorize sources, allowing you to quickly and easily organize your sources by topic.

> **Tip**: Any modifications made within exported Docs or Sheets files do not synchronize with your original NotebookLM content. Sharing permissions do not carry over from NotebookLM to exported files.

> Don't rely on NotebookLM for medical, legal, financial, or other professional advice. Any content regarding those topics is provided for informational purposes only and isn’t a substitute for advice from a qualified professional.

---

## What a Notebook Is

A **NotebookLM notebook** is an independent workspace containing sources for a specific project.

- Each notebook has its own sources, chat, and generated outputs.
- NotebookLM **cannot access information across multiple notebooks at the same time**.
- The mobile app may not fully support all notebook creation features.

---

## Create Your First Notebook

1. Open [NotebookLM](https://notebooklm.google.com/).
2. Select **Create new notebook**.
3. In the pop-up window, choose **Upload a source**.
4. Select or discover the sources you want to add.
5. Start collaborating with NotebookLM using the **Chat** and **Studio** panels.

---

## Main Notebook Panels

### Chat Panel

The **Chat** panel displays a generated summary of your sources.

You can use it to:

- Ask questions about your sources
- Give instructions
- Choose from suggested options
- Interact with NotebookLM based on the uploaded materials

---

### Studio Panel

The **Studio** panel is where NotebookLM generates outputs based on your sources.

Available output types include:

- **Notes**
- **Audio Overviews**
- **Video Overviews**
- **Mind maps**
- **Reports**
- **Data Tables**
- **Flashcards**
- **Quizzes**
- **Slide Decks**
- **Infographics**

NotebookLM may sometimes automatically create starter outputs such as reports, flashcards, infographics, slide decks, audio overviews, or video overviews when sources are first added.

- These auto-generated items are created once.
- They **do not count toward usage limits**.

---

## Reports

In the Studio panel, you can create reports by:

- Creating your own custom report
- Choosing from built-in options:
  - FAQ
  - Study guide
  - Briefing document
  - AI-suggested report type

### Exporting Reports

Open the **three dots menu** next to a generated report to select:

- **Export to Docs**
- **Export to Sheets**, if the report contains data tables

If exported to Sheets:

- Each data table populates its own tab in a Google Sheet.

---

## Data Tables

You can generate a data table directly or customize one.

Customization options include:

- Selecting the **pencil icon**
- Choosing a language
- Using the prompt box to define the table
- Describing rows and columns for a more focused, precise result

### Exporting Data Tables

Open the **three dots menu** next to a generated Data Table and select:

- **Export to Sheets**

Export behavior:

- The data table populates the first tab of a new Google Sheet.
- Citations populate the second tab.

---

## Viewing Prompts Used to Generate Artifacts

You can inspect the prompt used to generate certain Studio artifacts.

For audio overviews, video overviews, flashcards, quizzes, or reports:

- Select **View custom prompt** from the artifact’s three dots menu.

For flashcards, quizzes, and videos:

- A **Show prompt** option is also available next to the title in the viewer.

---

## Adding, Removing, and Organizing Sources

You can manage sources in the **Sources** panel.

Actions include:

- Add sources using **+ Add**
- Remove sources by unselecting individual sources

When a notebook has **5 or more sources**, NotebookLM can automatically label and categorize them by topic.

You can also manually organize sources by:

- Adding a new label
- Renaming labels
- Deleting labels
- Moving sources to different labels

---

## Create a Notebook in Gemini

You can also create a new notebook using **Gemini Apps**.

Related help article: [Notebooks in Gemini](https://support.google.com/gemini/answer/16972047)

---

## Update Notebook Emoji

After sources are uploaded, NotebookLM automatically assigns an emoji to represent the notebook.

To change it:

1. Open an existing notebook.
2. In the chat panel, select the curre

[... summary truncated for context management ...]

# Document 114: Create and use custom steps in flows - Workspace Studio Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/workspace-studio/answer/16433731?hl=en
Product area: workspace-studio
Original file: docs/create-and-use-custom-steps-in-flows-workspace-studio-help-6a29bb7c.md

[Skip to main content](https://support.google.com/workspace-studio/answer/16433731?hl=en#search-form)

# Create and use custom steps in flows

_This feature is in limited preview._

You can extend and customize Google Workspace Studio using Google Workspace add-ons. Just like add-ons for other services, you and other people in your organization can create custom steps so you can automate what you need most.

## On this page

- [Create custom steps](https://support.google.com/workspace-studio/answer/16433731?hl=en#create)
- [Install custom steps](https://support.google.com/workspace-studio/answer/16433731?hl=en#install)
- [Use custom steps in flows](https://support.google.com/workspace-studio/answer/16433731?hl=en#use)
- [Manage account access for add-on steps](https://support.google.com/workspace-studio/answer/16433731?hl=en#access)

## Create custom steps

![](https://storage.googleapis.com/support-kms-prod/4BMw4ze7sYv0aoC6pJUaNVGDdmDUWd1gje7A)

You and developers in your organization can create custom steps using Google Apps Script or your preferred coding language. You make them available to others by publishing them in the Google Workspace Marketplace.

[Learn how to create add-on steps](https://developers.google.com/workspace/add-ons/workflows).

## Install custom steps

![](https://storage.googleapis.com/support-kms-prod/4BMw4ze7sYv0aoC6pJUaNVGDdmDUWd1gje7A)

1. Go to [workspace.google.com/marketplace?host=workflow](https://workspace.google.com/marketplace?host=workflow).

This link takes you to a pre-filtered view of only add-ons and integrations that work with Workspace Studio.

2. When you find one you want to install, click it.
3. Click **Install**.
4. Follow the on-screen instructions to give the add-on permissions to access your Google Account data and complete the installation.

To uninstall an add-on step, go back to Workspace Marketplace, find the add-on, and click **Uninstall**. If your administrator installed the add-on for you, you might not be able to uninstall it.

## Use custom steps in flows

![](https://storage.googleapis.com/support-kms-prod/4BMw4ze7sYv0aoC6pJUaNVGDdmDUWd1gje7A)

After you install a Workspace Studio add-on, the steps show up in Studio.

The steps from the add-on are available wherever you create and edit flows, including:

- **When you create a flow with AI** on the Studio **Discover** page.
- **In the step-selection panel** in the flow editor. To find an add-on step, scroll to the section with the name of the add-on.

**Important:** When you add variables to third-party integration steps or add-on steps, they might contain your Google Account data, such as the contents of a message in Gmail or Chat, or event information from Calendar. The flow can then share your Google Account data with a third-party service. Make sure you trust the third-party service.

## Manage account access for add-on steps

![](https://storage.googleapis.com/support-kms-prod/4BMw4ze7sYv0aoC6pJUaNVGDdmDUWd1gje7A)

If you’re concerned about an add-on’s access to your Google Account data, you can delete its connections with your account. However, add-on steps might not work anymore and flows that use them could stop working.

1. Go to [https://myaccount.google.com/connections](https://myaccount.google.com/connections).
2. Search for the add-on in the list of apps and services and click it.
3. Click **Delete all connections you have with** **_add-on name_** and confirm you want to delete them.

To allow the add-on to connect again, in a flow, add an add-on step. You’re prompted to allow the required connections.

# Document 115: Create apps using Gemini for App Creation - AppSheet Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/appsheet/answer/14699210?hl=en
Product area: appsheet
Original file: docs/create-apps-using-gemini-for-app-creation-appsheet-help-5c13a00d.md

# Create Apps Using Gemini for App Creation — AppSheet Help Summary

**Source:** https://support.google.com/appsheet/answer/14699210?hl=en  
**Product:** Google AppSheet  
**Feature:** Gemini for App Creation

---

## Overview

**Gemini for App Creation** lets AppSheet users create apps by describing a business process or app idea in natural language. Gemini generates an initial app structure—including tables, columns, and relationships—that users can review, edit, and turn into an AppSheet app.

> Gemini for App Creation allows you to build apps by simply _describing_ a business process or idea in natural language.

---

## Availability

Gemini for App Creation is available to **all AppSheet paid accounts**, including:

- Google Workspace customers with:
  - **AppSheet Core**
  - **AppSheet Enterprise Plus**
- Individual accounts with:
  - **AppSheet Publisher Pro**
  - **AppSheet Starter**
  - **AppSheet Core**
  - **AppSheet Enterprise Plus**

---

## Admin Controls

Users with administrator privileges can disable AI-assisted app creation.

- The setting is managed through **Turn Gemini for App Creation on or off**
- The setting applies at the **team level**
- If an organization has multiple teams, Gemini must be disabled separately for each relevant team

---

## How to Access Gemini for App Creation

1. Go to **My Apps**:
   - Sign in to [AppSheet](https://appsheet.com/)
   - **My Apps** displays by default
   - Or click **My Apps** in the top navigation
   - Or select **My Apps** from the account profile drop-down

2. Click:

```text
+ Create > App > Start with Gemini
```

3. The Gemini-assisted chat interface opens.

---

## App Creation Workflow

Gemini for App Creation uses a **three-step process**:

1. **Describe your app idea or process**
2. **Review and edit the app structure**
3. **Create and customize your app**

---

# Step 1: Describe Your App Idea or Process

At the Gemini prompt, users can either:

- Enter a natural-language description of the desired app or process
- Click one of the provided sample prompts to auto-populate the prompt

The description is also called a **prompt**.

**Recommendation:** Provide as much detail as possible.

---

## Example Prompt

The article uses a facility inspection app as an example:

> **Manage inspections of my facility. Our inspectors conduct facility inspections, and every week we need to check that everything is operating correctly at each facility, pass or fail an inspection, and create and submit reports.**

Gemini responds by generating an app structure that summarizes the data the app will manage.

---

## Example Generated App Structure

For the facility inspection example, Gemini creates tables such as:

```text
Inspectors
Reports
Inspections
Facilities
```

### Example Table Columns

For the `Inspections` table, Gemini generates columns including:

```text
Id
Facility Id
Inspector Id
Date
Status
Report Id
```

---

## Tables, Columns, and Links

Gemini generates:

### Tables

Tables are used to track app data and are similar to tabs in a spreadsheet.

Example tables:

```text
Inspectors
Reports
Inspections
Facilities
```

### Columns

Columns define the attributes of each row in a table.

Example columns in the `Inspections` table:

```text
Id
Facility Id
Inspector Id
Date
Status
Report Id
```

### Links Between Tables

Gemini can create relationships between tables.

Example:

- In the `Inspections` table:
  - `Facility Id` links to the `Name` column in the `Facilities` table
  - `Inspector Id` links to the `Name` column in the `Inspectors` table

---

# Step 2: Review and Edit Your App Structure

After Gemini generates the app structure, users should review it and decide whether additional information needs to be tracked.

## If the Structure Is Satisfactory

Click:

```text
Create app
```

This proceeds to app creation and customization.

## If the Structure Is Not Satisfactory

Users can:

- Edit the app structure
- Click **Start over** to discard the current structure and begin again

---

## Editing the App Structure

Users can edit the generated app structure by:

- Adding a table
- Editing a table
- Adding, editing, or deleting columns
- Editing table names
- Deleting tables

---

## Add a Table

Click:

```text
+ Add table
```

A dialog opens where users can:

- Enter the table name
- Add columns

### Important Note About the `Id` Column

> The `Id` column is created automatically, providing a unique ID for the table that can't be edited or deleted.

---

## Edit a Table

Click a table in the app structure to open the **Edit table** dialog.

Within the dialog, users can:

- Edit the table name
- Add columns
- Edit column names
- Edit column types
- Edit single-select or multi-select option values
- Delete columns
- Delete the entire table

After making changes, click:

```text
Save
```

---

## Supported Column Editing Options

When adding or editing a column, users can:

- Enter a column name
- Select a column type from a drop-down
-

[... summary truncated for context management ...]

---

# Document 116: Create background images with Gemini in Google Meet - Computer - Google Meet Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/meet/answer/13954947?hl=en
Product area: meet
Original file: docs/create-background-images-with-gemini-in-google-meet-computer-google-meet-help-1fd66d19.md

# Create Background Images with Gemini in Google Meet — Summary

**Source:** Google Meet Help — “Create background images with Gemini in Google Meet”  
**URL:** https://support.google.com/meet/answer/13954947?hl=en

---

## Key Excerpts & Important Notes

> **Important:**
>
> - This feature requires an eligible Google Workspace or Google AI plan.
> - Sometimes, you may not be able to generate a background because Smart feature control is disabled.

> On Google Meet, you can use the "Generate a background" prompt to create background images using artificial intelligence. For example, you can ask Google Meet to create an illustration of a magical forest.

> This feature is now available on desktop and Android devices.

> Your self-view shows a mirrored or inverted background but other meeting participants can find it in a correct orientation.

> **Tip:** For better results, reference a style or mood, a setting, and objects in the environment. For example: “A cozy coffee shop with flowers.”

---

## Feature Overview

Google Meet allows eligible users to generate custom meeting backgrounds using Gemini AI through the **“Generate a background”** prompt.

You can describe the type of background you want, and Meet will generate suggested images you can apply as your meeting background.

### Example prompts

- `"Luxurious living room interior"`
- `"A magical, sunny forest glade"`
- `"A cozy coffee shop with flowers"`

### Availability

- Available on **desktop**
- Available on **Android**
- The page is specifically for **Computer**, but notes Android support as well
- Also available through **Google Workspace Experiments**, Google’s early access testing program

### Requirements

To use the feature, you need:

- An eligible **Google Workspace** or **Google AI** plan
- Smart features enabled, if required by your account settings

If background generation is unavailable, it may be because **Smart feature control is disabled**.

---

## How to Create an AI Background Before a Meeting

1. On a computer, go to [meet.google.com](https://meet.google.com/).
2. Select a meeting.
3. At the bottom-right of your self-view, click:
   - **Apply visual effects**
   - Then **Generate a background**
4. In the **“Generate a background”** setup, enter a prompt.

   Example prompts:

   - `"Luxurious living room interior"`
   - `"A magical, sunny forest glade"`

5. Click **Create** to generate a suggested background image.
6. Optional: Edit the prompt after generating:
   1. At the top of the right-side panel, click the prompt.
   2. Edit the prompt.
   3. Click **Create a new sample**.
   4. Click **Apply**.
7. To use a generated image, click the image.
8. To exit the setup, click **Close**.

---

## How to Create an AI Background During a Meeting

1. On a computer, go to [meet.google.com](https://meet.google.com/).
2. Join a meeting.
3. At the bottom of the meeting window, click:
   - **More options**
   - Then **Apply visual effects**
   - Then **Generate a background**
4. In the **“Generate a background”** setup, enter a prompt.

   Example prompts:

   - `"Luxurious living room interior"`
   - `"A magical, sunny forest glade"`

5. Click **Create** to generate suggested background images.
6. Optional: Edit the prompt after generating:
   1. At the top of the right-side panel, click the prompt.
   2. Edit the prompt.
   3. Click **Create a new sample**.
   4. Click **Apply**.
7. To use a generated image as your meeting background, click it.
8. To leave the background generation setup, click **Close**.

---

## Prompt-Writing Guidance

For better results, Google recommends including:

- A **style** or **mood**
- A **setting**
- Objects or details in the environment

### Good prompt example

> “A cozy coffee shop with flowers.”

This prompt works well because it includes:

- Mood: cozy
- Setting: coffee shop
- Objects: flowers

---

## Giving Feedback on Generated Backgrounds

Google notes that Gemini may not always support a request or may produce inaccurate or unsafe suggestions.

If a generated image is inaccurate, inappropriate, or unsafe, you can submit feedback.

### How to submit feedback

1. Hover over the generated image.
2. At the bottom of the image, click either:
   - **Good suggestion**
   - **Bad suggestion**
3. If you select **Bad suggestion**, you can:
   - Choose the issue you found
   - Enter additional feedback
4. Click **Submit**.

### Legal issues

To report a legal issue, Google directs users to create a legal request through Google’s legal support process.

---

## Gemini Feature Suggestions: Disclaimers & Limitations

Google provides several cautions about Gemini-generated suggestions:

- Gemini feature suggestions **do not represent Google’s views**.
- Gemini suggestions **should not be attributed to Google**.
- Do **not** rely on Gemini features for:
  - Medical advice
  - Legal advice
  - Financial advice
  - Other professional advice
- Gemini features may suggest **inaccurate or inappropriate information**

[... summary truncated for context management ...]

# Document 117: Create calculated fields with Gemini assistance  |  Data Studio  |  Google Cloud Documentation

Source URL: https://cloud.google.com/looker/docs/studio/create-calculated-fields-gemini
Product area: looker-studio
Original file: docs/create-calculated-fields-with-gemini-assistance-data-studio-google-cloud-documentation-9374845c.md

# Create Calculated Fields with Gemini Assistance — Data Studio

**Source:** Google Cloud Documentation — Data Studio  
**Page:** “Create calculated fields with Gemini assistance”  
**Last updated:** 2026-06-11 UTC  
**Audience:** Report editors in Data Studio

> Looker Studio is now called **Data Studio**.

---

## Overview

This documentation explains how to use **Gemini in Data Studio** to help create **calculated fields** by describing the field you want in natural language.

Data Studio uses Gemini to suggest a formula for a calculated field based on:

- Your prompt or description
- Fields from your data source
- Data Studio functions and operators

Gemini in Data Studio is part of the **Gemini for Google Cloud** portfolio and provides generative AI-powered assistance to help users analyze data and gain insights.

Relevant resources mentioned:

- [Gemini in Data Studio](https://docs.cloud.google.com/data-studio/gemini-overview)
- [Calculated fields in Data Studio](https://docs.cloud.google.com/data-studio/about-calculated-fields)
- [Gemini for Google Cloud overview](https://docs.cloud.google.com/gemini/docs/overview)
- [How and when Gemini for Google Cloud uses your data](https://docs.cloud.google.com/gemini/docs/discover/data-governance)

---

## Requirements Before You Begin

To use Gemini assistance for calculated fields, all of the following must be true:

- You must be a user under a **Data Studio Pro subscription**.
  - Data Studio Pro licenses are available at no cost to Looker users.
- To create **chart-specific calculated fields**, you must be an **editor of the report**.
- **Field Editing in Reports** must be enabled in the data source.
- **Gemini in Data Studio** must be enabled for your **Data Studio Pro project**.

---

## How to Create a Calculated Field with Gemini Assistance

Follow these steps:

1. While writing a calculated field, click the **Help me write a calculated field** icon in the bottom-right corner of the field editor.

2. If you have **not** started writing a calculated field:
   - Data Studio prompts you to either:
     - Select a suggested prompt, or
     - Describe the kind of calculated field you want to create.
   - To use a suggested prompt, click it.
   - To write your own prompt, enter a description and click **Create**.

3. If you have **already started** writing a calculated field:
   - Data Studio uses your existing formula as context.
   - Describe how you want to change the calculated field.
   - Click **Create**.

   Additional options while refining:
   - To edit your original prompt:
     - Hover over the prompt.
     - Click the **Edit** icon.
     - Update the prompt.
     - Click **Update**.
   - To remove the previous formula from Data Studio’s context:
     - Hover over the formula.
     - Click the **Reset** icon.
   - To refine the formula:
     - Click **Refine**.
     - Provide more details, such as:
       - Adding or removing a bucket
       - Adding another field to a concatenation
     - Click **Create**.

4. To add the generated formula to the calculated field definition, click **Apply**.

5. To save the field definition, click **Save**.

### Exiting Without Saving

To exit the formula generation dialog without saving changes:

- Click the **X** in the top-right corner, or
- Click outside the dialog.

---

## Limitations

Gemini assistance for calculated fields has these limitations:

- Gemini can only help create calculated fields that meet Data Studio’s standard criteria for what calculated fields can do.
- Data Studio will **not** suggest:
  - Parameters
  - `NATIVE_DIMENSION`
- The **Looker connector** supports only some calculated-field functions.

---

## Information Used by Gemini in Data Studio

When generating formulas, Data Studio uses:

- The prompt you write to create or edit a field
- The formula definition for any field you change or refine
- The schema of the underlying data source

---

## Sample Prompts and Generated Formulas

### Example 1: Merge City and State

Prompt:

```text
Merge city and state like City, State
```

Data Studio may return:

```sql
CONCAT(Users City, ", ", Users State)
```

---

### Example 2: Bucket Sale Price in Groups of 20

Prompt:

```text
bucket sale price in groups of 20
```

Data Studio may return:

```sql
CASE
      WHEN Order Items Sale Price < 20 THEN "0-20"
      WHEN Order Items Sale Price < 40 THEN "20-40"
      WHEN Order Items Sale Price < 60 THEN "40-60"
      WHEN Order Items Sale Price < 80 THEN "60-80"
      WHEN Order Items Sale Price < 100 THEN "80-100"
      WHEN Order Items Sale Price < 120 THEN "100-120"
      WHEN Order Items Sale Price < 140 THEN "120-140"
      WHEN Order Items Sale Price < 160 THEN "140-160"
      WHEN Order Items Sale Price < 180 THEN "160-180"
      WHEN Order Items Sale Price < 200 THEN "180-200"
      ELSE "200+"
END
```

---

## Troubleshooting

If an error occurs while creating a calculated field, verify that your request is asking for something achievabl

[... summary truncated for context management ...]

---

# Document 118: Create content for Shorts using AI-generated features - YouTube Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/youtube/answer/15260303
Product area: youtube-ai
Original file: docs/create-content-for-shorts-using-ai-generated-features-youtube-help-64727e48.md

# YouTube Help Summary: Create Content for Shorts Using AI-Generated Features

**Source:** [YouTube Help — Create content for Shorts using AI-generated features](https://support.google.com/youtube/answer/15260303)

## Overview

YouTube offers **experimental AI-generated creation tools for Shorts** that allow creators to generate **unique images or videos** from prompts. These can be used as:

- **Green screen backgrounds**
- **Standalone video clips**
- **Photo-to-video Shorts**
- **“Make me move” animated clips**
- Other AI Playground assets such as **backgrounds, tracks, and effects**

These tools are intended to help creators bring imaginative ideas to life directly inside the YouTube app.

> “These content creation tools are experimental AI-generated features from YouTube that let you bring your imagination to life.”

---

## Policy, Safety, and Disclosure Guidance

### Community Guidelines Still Apply

All content created with YouTube’s AI tools must follow YouTube’s **Community Guidelines**.

> “All content on YouTube, including AI-generated creations from these tools, must follow our Community Guidelines.”

Creators remain responsible for their published content, including content created with AI.

### Built-In Safeguards

YouTube has added safeguards to its AI tools to help reduce inappropriate or policy-violating outputs.

The tools may block:

- Prompts that violate YouTube policies
- Prompts involving sensitive topics
- Attempts to generate certain harmful or misleading content

Example:

> “Our AI tools are designed to help creators avoid generating photorealistic images of identifiable people, which could be misused for creating deep fakes or other harmful content.”

### Review Before Publishing

YouTube notes that generative AI tools can make mistakes, so creators should carefully review outputs before uploading.

> “As with any generative AI tool, mistakes are possible and we may not always get it right.”

### AI Disclosure

Creators using YouTube’s own generative AI tools **do not need to manually disclose** altered or synthetic content.

> **Note:** Creators who make a post or YouTube Short using YouTube's generative artificial intelligence (AI) tools don't need to take extra steps to disclose the use of altered or synthetic content. The tool will automatically disclose the use of AI for creators.

---

## Availability Notes

### General AI-Generated Shorts Features

> **Note:** These features are currently available in most countries for creators that have their YouTube device language set to English. These AI-generated features on Shorts may not be available in all locations.

### Regional Restrictions by Feature

| Feature | Availability Restrictions |
|---|---|
| **Green screen background** | Not available in the **European Economic Area (EEA)** |
| **Standalone video clip** | Not available in the **EEA** |
| **Reference images for standalone video prompts** | Not available for users in the **United Kingdom** |
| **Photo to video** | Not available in the **EEA, United Kingdom, and Switzerland** |
| **Make me move** | Not available in the **EEA, United Kingdom, and Switzerland** |

---

# AI-Generated Shorts Creation Tools

## 1. Create a Green Screen Background

This feature lets creators generate **images or videos from text prompts** and use them as a **green screen background** while recording a Short.

> **Note:** This feature is currently not available for countries in the European Economic Area (EEA).

### Steps

1. Open the **YouTube app**.
2. Tap **Create**.
   - Alternative: Tap the **sparkle icon** in the top-right corner, then select **Backgrounds**.
3. From the right menu, tap **Green screen**.
4. From the bottom, tap the **sparkle icon**.
5. Describe the content you want to generate and pick a style.
6. Tap **Create**.
7. Choose your image.
   - You can save or share the image by tapping **menu** → **Save to device** or **Share**.
8. Select **Use image** or **Create video**, then use it as a green screen background for recording.
9. Enter Short details, such as:
   - Description
   - Privacy settings
10. Tap **Upload Short** to publish.

---

## 2. Create a Standalone Video Clip

This feature allows creators to generate **standalone videos for Shorts** using:

- Text-based prompts
- Up to **3 reference images** from the creator’s gallery

> **Note:** This feature is currently not available for countries in the European Economic Area. Adding reference images to video generation prompts is not available for users in the United Kingdom.

### Steps

1. Open the **YouTube app**.
2. Tap **Create**.
   - Alternative: Tap the **sparkle icon** in the top-right corner, then select **Create video**.
3. On the bottom left, tap **Add** to open the media picker.
   - If prompted, grant access to photos and videos on your device.
4. At the top, select **Create video**.
5. Describe the content you want to generate.
   - To use specific visuals, tap the image/gallery icon and upload up to *

# Document 119: Create Content, Images & Audio Overviews with Gemini in Drive - Computer - Google Drive Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/drive/answer/16685527
Product area: drive
Original file: docs/create-content-images-audio-overviews-with-gemini-in-drive-computer-google-drive-b574a483.md

# Create Content, Images & Audio Overviews with Gemini in Google Drive — Summary

**Source:** Google Drive Help  
**Applies to:** Computer / Web version of Google Drive

> **Important**: To use Gemini in Drive, you must have an eligible Google Workspace or Google AI plan. [Learn more about Gemini features and plans](https://support.google.com/drive/answer/13952129?hl=en&co=DASHER._Family%3DBusiness-Enterprise).

---

## 1. Create a File with Gemini

You can use Gemini in Drive to generate structured content such as tables, proposals, or formatted lists, then export the result to Google Docs or Sheets.

### Steps

1. On your computer, go to [drive.google.com](https://drive.google.com/).
2. At the top right, click **Ask Gemini**.
3. Ask for a **table**, **proposal**, or **formatted list**.
4. Once generated, click either:
   - **Export to Docs**
   - **Export to Sheets**

### Important Note

> Any file created via Ask Gemini's immersive workspace automatically saves to your My Drive root folder.

---

## 2. Create Audio Overviews with Gemini

Gemini can generate an audio overview from supported files in Google Drive.

### Steps

1. On your computer, go to [drive.google.com](https://drive.google.com/).
2. Open a **PDF file**.
3. At the top right, click **Create audio overview**.
4. Wait for the audio overview to finish generating.
5. Click **Play** in the side panel to listen.
6. After generation, you’ll receive an email with the file link.

### Supported File Types

Audio overviews can be generated from:

- PDF files
- Google Docs
- Google Slides
- Microsoft Office Word Document
- Microsoft Office PowerPoint Presentation
- Plain Text files
- Videos

### Where Audio Overviews Are Saved

- All audio overviews are saved in the **“Audio overviews”** folder in **My Drive**.
- Audio files use this naming format:

```text
[File name] - audio overview - YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM UTC
```

### Important Limitation

> If you refresh the page or close the side panel or the tab before the generation is done, the file doesn’t generate.

### Audio Overview Tips & Limits

- Audio overviews can **only be generated on the web**.
- Generated audio overviews can be accessed on:
  - Web
  - iOS
  - Android
- You **can’t use a prompt in the side panel** to generate audio overviews.
- You can generate **up to 20 audio overviews per day**.

---

## 3. Create Images with Gemini

Gemini in Drive can also generate images from prompts.

### Steps

1. On your computer, go to [drive.google.com](https://drive.google.com/).
2. At the top right, click **Ask Gemini**.
3. Click **Tools** → **Create Images**.
4. Type a prompt.

---

## Related Google Drive Gemini Resources

- [Get started with Gemini in Google Drive](https://support.google.com/drive/answer/16686008)
- [Use Gemini in Drive for research & analysis](https://support.google.com/drive/answer/16963068)
- [Search & retrieve your files with Gemini](https://support.google.com/drive/answer/16685111)
- [Use Gems with Gemini in Google Drive](https://support.google.com/drive/answer/16684485)

---

## Help & Support Options

If more help is needed, Google suggests:

- [Post to the Google Drive Help Community](https://support.google.com/drive/community?hl=en&help_center_link=CNez-gcSTENyZWF0ZSBDb250ZW50LCBJbWFnZXMgJmFtcDsgQXVkaW8gT3ZlcnZpZXdzIHdpdGggR2VtaW5pIGluIERyaXZlIC0gQ29tcHV0ZXI)
- [Contact Google Support](https://support.google.com/drive/gethelp)
- [Watch Google Help video tutorials on YouTube](https://www.youtube.com/user/googlehelp?sub_confirmation=1)

Google also provides article feedback options, including reporting content as inaccurate, unclear, missing information, irrelevant, containing minor errors, or suggesting improvements.
