# Document 120: Create docs, apps & more with Canvas - Computer - Gemini Apps Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/gemini/answer/16047321
Product area: gemini-apps
Original file: docs/create-docs-apps-more-with-canvas-computer-gemini-apps-help-33bcb17d.md

# Create docs, apps & more with Canvas — Gemini Apps Help

**Source:** https://support.google.com/gemini/answer/16047321  
**Platform covered:** Computer  
**Product:** Gemini Apps / Canvas

---

## Overview

Canvas in Gemini lets you collaborate with Gemini to **create or edit docs, apps, slides, code, quizzes, Audio Overviews, infographics, web pages, and other formats**.

> **Important:** To use this feature, you must be signed in to Gemini Apps.

Canvas supports:
- Creating new docs, slide decks, quizzes, apps, and other content
- Editing generated docs and apps
- Direct code editing
- Adding Gemini-powered AI features to apps
- Exporting to Google Docs, Google Slides, PDFs, Colab, or copying content
- Sharing Canvas apps/content through public links
- Copying and editing shared Canvas content, with age and platform restrictions

---

## Start a New Chat with Canvas

1. Go to [gemini.google.com](https://gemini.google.com/).
2. Below the text box, click **Add Files** → **Canvas**.
3. Enter your prompt in the text box.
   - **Tip:** If you want a specific format or document type, such as a slide deck, include that in your prompt.
4. Optional: Click **Add files** to upload files or an image with your prompt.
5. Click **Submit**.

### Example prompts

> Create a slide presentation about…

> Create a quiz to test me on…

---

## Apps in Canvas

### Edit Apps & Code

You can edit an app either by:
- Asking Gemini to make changes using the prompt box
- Directly editing the app’s code

**Changes in Canvas are auto-saved.**

#### Open code, errors, logs, and changes

- To open and edit code: click **Code** in the top-right of the Canvas panel.
- To view preview errors and logs: click **Show console**.
- To view recent code changes: click **Code** → **Show recent changes**.

---

### Ask Gemini to Edit Selected Parts of an App

1. Open the chat with the app you want to edit.
2. In the bottom-right of the Canvas panel, click **Select & ask**.
3. Use the cursor to select the section you want to edit.
4. Enter a prompt describing the update you want.
5. Press **Enter**.

---

### Add AI to an App

> **Important:** To add AI to your app, you must be signed in to Gemini Apps and be **18 or over**.

You can add Gemini-powered features such as:
- Text generation
- Image generation

Steps:

1. Open the chat with the app you want to edit.
2. In the bottom-right of the Canvas panel, click **Add Gemini features**.
3. Gemini will provide an overview of the AI feature added to the app.
4. You can reply in the text box and ask Gemini to make additional changes.

---

## Docs in Canvas

### Edit Docs in Canvas

You can edit docs directly in Canvas or ask Gemini to help via the prompt box.

**Changes are auto-saved.**

#### Ask Gemini to edit selected text

1. Open the chat you want to edit.
2. In the Canvas panel, select the text you want to edit.
3. Enter a prompt explaining what you want updated.
4. Press **Enter**.

**Tip:** After selecting text, you can also click **Suggest edits** to have Gemini recommend changes.

---

### Ask Gemini to Make Quick Edits

1. Open the chat you want to edit.
2. In the Canvas panel, choose a quick-edit option:
   - **Change length** — adjust the length of text.
   - **Change tone** — make the text more casual or formal.

---

### Edit Text Style & Formatting in a Doc

1. Open the chat you want to edit.
2. Select the text in the Canvas panel.
3. Use the toolbar to format the text.

Available formatting options include:
- Stylize headings
- **Bold text**
- *Italicize text*
- Create bulleted lists
- Create numbered lists

**Tips:**
- If the full toolbar is not visible, click **More**.
- To view saved versions, click **Previous Version** or **Next Version** at the top.

---

## Add LaTeX to a Canvas Document

LaTeX is described as:

> LaTeX is a document preparation system widely used in academic, scientific, and technical fields to create high-quality documents with complex formatting, such as mathematical equations, tables, and multi-column layouts.

### Add LaTeX

1. Open the chat with the doc you want to edit.
2. At the top-right of the Canvas panel, click **Create**.
3. Select **LaTeX**.
4. Click on the formula within Canvas.

### Exporting or Sharing Docs with LaTeX

- You can export a doc containing LaTeX as a **PDF**, with preview before download.
- You can copy any Gemini response that includes LaTeX; the copied content will include the **unrendered LaTeX code**.

---

## Create Something Based on a Canvas Doc

> **Important:** For now, this feature isn’t available to users under 18.

You can transform an existing Canvas doc into other formats.

1. Open the chat with the doc you want to edit.
2. In the top-right of the Canvas panel, click **Create**.
3. Choose what you want to create.

Available options:
- **Slide show, custom visual, or app**  
  Enter a prompt describing what you want to turn the report or doc into.
- **Audio Overview**
- **Quiz**
- **Infographic**
- **Web page**

# Document 121: Create & manage AI mini-apps or custom workflows as Gems from Google Labs - Gemini Apps Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/gemini/answer/16802014?hl=en
Product area: gemini-apps
Original file: docs/create-manage-ai-mini-apps-or-custom-workflows-as-gems-from-google-labs-gemini-apps-help-5db7bd54.md

# Create & Manage AI Mini-Apps / Custom Workflows as **Gems from Google Labs**

**Source:** [Gemini Apps Help](https://support.google.com/gemini/answer/16802014?hl=en)  
**Product area:** Gemini web app, Gems, Google Labs / Opal

---

## Overview

**Gems from Google Labs** are custom AI mini-apps or workflows you can create, edit, remix, and share in the Gemini web app. They are powered by **Opal**, a Google Labs experiment, and let users build app-like AI workflows using natural language.

> You can create, edit, and share custom workflows bundled into AI mini-apps. These AI mini-apps are called Gems from Google Labs in the Gemini web app. They’re powered by Opal, a Google Labs experiment.

Users can:

- Create AI mini-apps from a text description.
- Edit app workflows by describing desired changes.
- Remix premade AI mini-apps from Google Labs.
- Share AI mini-apps with links, specific people, or public access.
- Use multiple AI models for workflows, including text, image, video, and music models.

> **Important:** This is an experimental feature and part of ongoing work to enhance Gemini experiences. This experiment will help us gather your feedback and refine the experience further.

---

## Requirements & Availability

To use Gems from Google Labs, you must:

- Be **18 or over**.
- Sign in to the Gemini web app with a **personal Google Account**.
- Use the same personal Google Account when signing in to **Opal**.

### Current limitations

> **Important:**
>
> - For now, this feature is available in the Gemini web app and in English only.
> - For now, you can create and edit Gems from Google Labs on a computer only.
> - For now, this feature isn’t available in the Gemini mobile app, Gemini in Google Messages, or Gemini in Chrome.

Additional limitation:

- Not available to users with **work or school Google Accounts**.
- Classic Gems **cannot currently be converted** into Gems from Google Labs.

---

## Create an AI Mini-App

You create AI mini-apps from the **Gem manager** in Gemini.

### Steps

1. On your computer, go to [gemini.google.com](https://gemini.google.com/).
2. On the left, click **Gems**.
3. Next to **“My Gems from Labs,”** click **New Gem**.
   - First-time users must review disclosures and follow on-screen instructions to sign in to **Opal**.
   - Use the same personal Google Account as Gemini.
4. In the text box at the bottom, describe the AI mini-app or workflow you want to create.
   - You can also click an example prompt for inspiration.
5. Click **Submit**.
   - Complex apps may take a few minutes to create and run.
   - Keep the chat open until the app is ready.
   - You can open another browser window or tab to start another chat.
6. When ready, Gemini shows:
   - The app workflow as steps.
   - A preview of the app.
7. Review the steps and click **Start app** to try it.
8. To add or change steps, describe your edits in the text box below the workflow.

### Tips

- You can remix premade AI mini-apps and customize them.
- Classic Gems cannot currently be converted into Gems from Google Labs.

---

## Find & Use an AI Mini-App

You can find both premade and personally created AI mini-apps in the **Gem manager**.

### Steps

1. Go to [gemini.google.com](https://gemini.google.com/) on a computer.
2. Click **Gems** on the left.
3. Browse the available AI mini-apps in two sections:
   - **Gems made by Labs:** Premade AI mini-apps provided by Google Labs.
   - **My Gems from Labs:** AI mini-apps you created or remixed.
4. Click the AI mini-app you want to use.
5. Click **Start**.

Related data information: [Learn about how your data from My Gems from Labs is handled](https://support.google.com/gemini/answer/13594961#gems_from_labs_data).

---

## Remix a Premade AI Mini-App

Premade apps from Google Labs can be edited and saved as your own.

### Steps

1. On your computer, go to [gemini.google.com](https://gemini.google.com/).
2. Click **Gems** on the left.
3. Under **“Gems made by Labs,”** select the premade AI mini-app you want to edit.
4. In the text box below the steps, describe your edits.

---

## Share an AI Mini-App

You can share AI mini-apps you create or remix.

### Steps

1. On your computer, go to [gemini.google.com](https://gemini.google.com/).
2. Click **Gems** on the left.
3. Click the AI mini-app you want to share.
4. Above the AI mini-app preview, click **Share**.
5. Choose how to share:
   - **Share a link:** Click **Copy link**, then paste it in a messaging app, email, forum thread, etc.
   - **Share with specific people by email:** Enter the email addresses of the people you want to share with.
   - **Manage general access:** Under **“General access,”** click the down arrow and choose who can access the app.
6. Click **Save**.

### General access options

- **Public:** Anyone can search on Google and access your AI mini-app without signing in to a Google Account.
- **Anyone with the link:** Anyone with the link can use the AI mini-app without signing in to a Google A

[... summary truncated for context management ...]

# Document 122: Create personalized documents with Gemini in Google Docs - Google Docs Editors Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/docs/answer/15541387?hl=en
Product area: docs-editors
Original file: docs/create-personalized-documents-with-gemini-in-google-docs-google-docs-editors-hel-d7209edd.md

# Create Personalized Documents with Gemini in Google Docs — Summary

**Source:** Google Docs Editors Help  
**URL:** https://support.google.com/docs/answer/15541387?hl=en

---

## Overview

Gemini in Google Docs can generate first drafts that use relevant context from:

- Google Drive files
- Google Chat
- Gmail
- The web

It can also reference existing documents to match:

- **Formatting** — layout, style, and structure
- **Writing style** — tone, sentence structure, and vocabulary complexity

Common use cases include creating:

- Proposals
- Project plans
- Blog posts
- Reports
- Memos
- Templates
- Personalized instructions

---

## Feature Availability

This feature has several access and platform requirements:

- Requires an eligible **Google Workspace** or **Google AI** plan.
- Available for:
  - Eligible Google Workspace with Gemini users
  - Google Workspace Experiments users
  - Google AI Ultra accounts
  - Google AI Pro accounts
- Workspace Experiments is a trusted tester program for personal accounts to try new AI features.
- To check Workspace Experiments access, open a new Google Doc and look for a bottom bar.
- The feature is currently available:
  - **On desktop**
  - **In English only**

> If you don’t have access to personalization when using Help me create, your experience will look different.

---

## Create Documents with Gemini

To generate a new document draft:

1. On your computer, open a document in [Google Docs](https://docs.google.com/document/).
2. At the bottom bar, enter a prompt describing the document you want to create.
3. Optional: Match an existing document’s formatting, including layout, style, and structure.
4. Optional: Reference an existing document’s writing style.
5. Optional: Add documents as references for the new content.
6. Click **Submit**.
7. Optional: To review sources used, select **Sources**.

---

## Match an Existing Document’s Format

You can use a Google Doc as a template so Gemini generates content matching its:

- Layout
- Style
- Structure

### Steps

1. On an empty Google Doc tab, select **Match doc format** above the bottom bar.
2. In the popup, choose the file to use.
3. Click **Add**.
4. Enter a prompt describing the document you want to create.
5. Press **Enter**.

The new content’s layout, style, and structure will be based on the selected document.

---

## Match a Writing Style

Gemini can reference a Google Drive document to match writing characteristics such as:

- Tone of voice
- Sentence structure
- Vocabulary complexity

### Steps

1. In Google Docs, go to the bottom bar and click **Tools**.
2. Select **Writing style** → **Add doc from Drive**.
3. In the popup, click **Add document**.
4. Select the file to use.
5. Click **Add**.
6. Gemini generates a summary of the writing style it will apply.
7. Click **Confirm**.
8. Optional: If the style summary is not right, select a different file.

### Example Writing Style Summary

> “Formal, objective, and direct. Concise phrasing but avoid acronyms.”

### Tip

If you paste new content into an existing document where Gemini previously used another document to match writing style, select **Match writing style**.

---

## Reference Other Files to Create Content

You can ask Gemini to use specific files to write, edit, or refine content.

### Example

> “Update the project timeline based on the latest updates from our meeting notes doc.”

### Steps

1. At the bottom bar, click **Sources** → **Add from Drive** or another location.
   - Alternatively, type `@` and search/select a file from the menu.
2. Select one or more files.
3. Click **Add**.

---

## Choose Where Gemini Can Search for Information

Gemini can proactively search selected locations for content related to your prompt.

Possible source locations include:

- Gmail
- Drive
- Web search
- Other available Workspace sources

You can choose which sources are enabled depending on the task.

---

## Example Use Cases and Prompts

| Goal | What to Use / Example Prompt |
|---|---|
| Generate new content using existing email information | “Draft a list of all upcoming deadlines for “website redesign project that are on my emails.” And also make sure “Gmail” is checked in under "Sources." |
| Match another file’s look | “Draft a new project proposal for 'Project Apollo.' Match the format of the '2024 Template' file.” |
| Write using your tone of voice | “Draft a project proposal for a new community garden. Match the writing style of my 'Previous Proposals' file.” |
| Use web information to generate new content | “Generate two paragraphs on the impact of high speed mobile internet on the transformation of the taxi cab industry. And also make sure “Web search” is turned on as a location." |

---

## Minimize or Hide the Gemini Bottom Bar

The Gemini bar automatically minimizes by default to reduce screen clutter.

### To minimize it manually

1. At the bottom bar, click the **Overflow menu**.
2. Select **Minimize Gemini bar**.

### To show it again

# Document 123: Create, use & manage your avatar on YouTube - YouTube Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/youtube/answer/16985237
Product area: youtube-ai
Original file: docs/create-use-manage-your-avatar-on-youtube-youtube-help-30bd1d5a.md

# Create, Use & Manage Your Avatar on YouTube — Summary

**Source:** YouTube Help — “Create, use & manage your avatar on YouTube”  
**URL:** https://support.google.com/youtube/answer/16985237

---

## Overview

YouTube avatars let creators generate videos featuring a digital version of themselves that can look and sound like them. The feature builds on YouTube’s AI “ingredients-to-video” creation tools and is available through:

- The **YouTube mobile app**
- The **YouTube Create app**

Avatars are created using a secure **“live selfie”** capture process that records your face and voice. Once created, the avatar can be used to generate AI videos from text prompts.

---

## Key Excerpts

> “An avatar builds on existing ingredients-to-video features in YouTube’s creation tools, making it easier and more accurate to add yourself into your videos.”

> “Avatars create a digital version of yourself so you can generate videos that look and sound like you, safely and securely.”

> “Only you can use your avatar to create original videos, you can delete it at any time, and all output is labeled as AI.”

> **Important:** “The YouTube AI Features Privacy Notice applies to avatars on YouTube.”

> **Note:** “This feature will be rolling out gradually.”

> **Important:** “Avatars and videos are managed separately. Deleting your avatar will not automatically delete the videos you published or their remixes. Deleting a video with your avatar will not delete your avatar from your account.”

> **Note:** “If your avatar is not used to create new content for 3 years, it will be automatically deleted.”

---

## Requirements

To create a YouTube avatar, you must be:

- **18 years or older**
- **The channel owner** of an existing YouTube channel

All content created with avatars must follow YouTube’s:

- **Community Guidelines**
- **AI Features Privacy Notice**
- Policies for AI-generated content

---

## Creating Your Avatar

You only need to create your avatar once. If you create, update, or delete it in one app, the change automatically appears in the other app.

### Important Sync Behavior

> “If you create, update, or delete your avatar in one app, those changes will automatically reflect in the other app.”

---

## Create an Avatar in the YouTube App

1. Sign in to the **YouTube app** on your mobile device.
2. Tap **Create**, then open the **AI playground** from the top-right corner.
3. Tap **Create video** → **Make a video with my avatar**.
4. Follow the on-screen prompts to record your selfie and voice.
5. Preview your avatar.
   - Tap **Next** to use it.
   - Tap **Retake** to record again.
6. Enter a prompt to generate a video, such as:

   > “I am flying high in the sky, up above the clouds saying I can fly!”

7. Tap the generate button.

---

## Create an Avatar from Eligible Shorts

On eligible Shorts in the Shorts feed, you can create your avatar and add it directly into a Short:

1. Tap **Remix** → **Reimagine**.
2. Tap **Add me to this scene**.

---

## Create an Avatar in the YouTube Create App

1. Sign in to the **YouTube Create app** on your mobile device.
2. On the **My Avatar** homepage banner, tap the generate/start button.
3. Follow the prompts to record your selfie and voice.
4. Preview your avatar.
   - Tap **Next** to use it.
   - Tap **Start over** to record again.
5. Enter a prompt, such as:

   > “I am flying high in the sky, up above the clouds with my cat giving me company.”

6. Tap the generate button.

---

## Tips for Recording Your Face & Voice

To make sure your avatar recording is accepted, follow these guidelines:

- **Phone position:** Hold your phone at eye level and stay centered on screen.
- **Lighting:** Use balanced lighting that is not too dim or too bright.
- **Visibility:** Make sure your eyes, nose, and mouth are visible.
  - Glasses are allowed.
  - Do **not** wear sunglasses, masks, or hats.
- **Audio environment:** Record in a quiet place with no other voices in the background.
- **Background:** Make sure there are no people or images of faces behind you.
- **Account owner:** The account owner must complete the process.
  - Do **not** allow minors to record themselves.
- **Selfie direction:** Follow the prompted action, then return your face to the center.

---

## Using Your Avatar to Create Videos

Once your avatar is created, you can add it during the video creation process.

---

## Use Your Avatar in the YouTube App

1. Open the **YouTube app** on your mobile device.
2. Tap **Create**, then open **AI playground** from the top-right corner.
3. Tap **Create video** → **Make a video with my avatar**.

---

## Use Your Avatar in Eligible Shorts

To add your avatar directly into an eligible Short:

1. Tap **Remix** → **Reimagine**.
2. Tap **Add me to this scene**.

---

## Use Your Avatar in the YouTube Create App

1. Open the **YouTube Create app**.
2. Tap **Generate video**.
3. On the prompt page, your avatar appears as an option to add.
4. Tap your avatar to use it as a referen

[... summary truncated for context management ...]

# Document 124: Create voiceovers with AI in Google Vids - Google Docs Editors Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/docs/answer/15070345
Product area: google-vids
Original file: docs/create-voiceovers-with-ai-in-google-vids-google-docs-editors-help-cda0f6e8.md

# Create Voiceovers with AI in Google Vids - Summary

## Feature Availability

- Requires an eligible Google Workspace subscription with Gemini features. [Learn about Gemini features and plans](https://support.google.com/docs/answer/13952129).
- [Supported languages for Google Workspace with Gemini](https://support.google.com/docs/answer/14925782).

## Key Tips

- When creating a video with "Help me create," a voiceover is automatically generated. See [Create a video with AI in Google Vids](https://support.google.com/docs/answer/15067819).
- Limit: **50 audio objects** per video (including music, sound effects, voiceovers). Subject to change.

---

## Recording Your Own Voiceover

1. Open [Google Vids](https://docs.google.com/videos/create?usp=hc).
2. Choose **Record** from the start menu, or click the Record icon in an existing video.
3. (Optional) Enter a script in the sidebar for your scene.
4. Under **Recording options**, select one of:
   - **Camera**
   - **Camera & screen**
   - **Screen**
   - **Voiceover**
5. Click **Start recording** at the bottom of the side panel.
   - For Camera or Screen & camera options, the script can be used in **teleprompter mode**. [Learn more about Vids recording studio](https://support.google.com/docs/answer/14956207).

---

## Generating an AI Voiceover

### Important Notes
- Generated voiceovers use AI models; voice options may change with model updates, but existing voiceovers won’t change.

### Steps (Scene-by-Scene or All Scenes)
1. Open [Google Vids](https://docs.google.com/videos?usp=hc).
2. Click **Voiceover** on the right.
3. Choose **Current scene** or **All scenes**.
4. Write your script in the text box (plain text only, max **2,500 characters**, no emojis/symbols).
5. (Optional) Use bracketed tags for emotional cues, pacing, non-verbal sounds:
   - `[Read this like you're excited]: Your script here.`
   - `This [pause] is amazing!`
   - `[laugh] That was a great point.`
6. Click **Insert voiceover**.
7. Select a voice from the **Voiceover** options, then click **Select** → **Insert voiceover**.
8. To edit an existing voiceover: select the scene, enter new script, click **Insert voiceover** → **Update voiceover** → **Replace**.

### Supported Languages for AI Voiceovers
Arabic, Bangla, Dutch, English, French, German, Hindi, Indonesian, Italian, Japanese, Korean, Marathi, Polish, Portuguese, Romanian, Russian, Spanish, Tamil, Telugu, Thai, Turkish, Ukrainian, Vietnamese.

---

## Syncing Script to Voiceover

- A **"Voiceover outdated" badge** appears when the script doesn’t match the generated voiceover (on the scene or in the list).
- **To sync:**
  1. Open Vids, click Voiceover.
  2. Select a scene, update the script.
  3. Click **Update voiceover** → **Replace** (for current scene) or **Update all voiceovers** → **Replace** (for all scenes).
- **Tip:** Hover over the badge to see "Click update to sync the script and voiceover."

---

## Important Notes About Gemini Features

- Gemini suggestions are not Google’s views; don’t attribute them to Google.
- Do not rely on Gemini for medical, legal, financial, or professional advice.
- Output may be inaccurate or inappropriate; feedback helps improve safety.
- Enterprise end users can submit feedback (must not include personal, sensitive, or confidential info).

---

## Provide Feedback

- **General feedback:** Go to **Help** → **Help Vids improve**.
- **Legal issues:** [Create a request](https://support.google.com/legal/answer/3110420).

---

# Document 125: Create with AI in the YouTube Create app - YouTube Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/youtube/answer/16631240
Product area: youtube-ai
Original file: docs/create-with-ai-in-the-youtube-create-app-youtube-help-4bebc3b2.md

# Create with AI in the YouTube Create App — Summary

**Source:** YouTube Help — “Create with AI in the YouTube Create app”  
**URL:** https://support.google.com/youtube/answer/16631240

---

## Core Overview

The YouTube Create app includes experimental AI tools that help creators generate and edit content, including:

- **Veo** — generate vertical video clips from text prompts and optional reference images.
- **Nano Banana** — generate images from text prompts and optional reference images.
- **Edit with AI** — turn raw clips/photos into a polished draft video with music, voiceover, captions, and effects.

---

## Key Excerpts

> You can create and edit content with AI in the YouTube Create app.

> These AI-generated features are experimental and are currently available to a limited number of creators in select countries.

> All content on YouTube, including AI-generated content made with YouTube Create, must follow our Community Guidelines.

> We may not accept prompts that violate our policies or touch on sensitive topics.

> Labels clearly indicate to viewers that the content they’re watching is synthetic.

---

# 1. Generate a Video with Veo

## What Veo Does

**Veo in YouTube Create** lets creators generate vertical AI video clips for shots they did not capture themselves. It supports control over:

- **Lighting**
- **Style**
- **Audio / sound effects via prompt**
- **Portrait video format**

## Availability & Requirements

> Veo on YouTube Create is currently available on mobile devices in Australia, Canada, India, New Zealand, and the United States.

Key requirements:

- Available only on **mobile devices**
- Available in:
  - Australia
  - Canada
  - India
  - New Zealand
  - United States
- **Prompts must be in English**
- Veo is currently available only for certain AI-generative tools
- Prompt limit: **900 characters**
- Reference images: up to **3 images**
- Aspect ratio: **9:16 Portrait**, default

## How to Access Veo

Open the **YouTube Create app**, then access the feature from one of several places:

1. **Homepage banner**
2. In the editor, tap **Add** to add a new video to the timeline, then choose the option to generate a new clip
3. In the editor, choose the option to generate a new clip when adding an overlay
4. From the bottom navigation tool in the editor, tap **Generate video**

## How to Generate a Video

1. Open the YouTube Create app.
2. Access **Generate video** through one of the available entry points.
3. Enter a text prompt describing the desired video.
   - You can also tap a suggested prompt.
   - Prompt limit: **900 characters**
   - Optional: upload up to **3 reference images** from your gallery.
4. Customize the prompt with:
   - **Style:** choose from **11 predefined styles**, such as:
     - Photorealistic
     - Vintage Film
     - Claymation  
     **Photorealistic** is the default.
   - **Lighting:** choose options like:
     - Soft morning light
     - Golden hour
     - Blue hour
     - Moonlight
   - **Audio:** describe desired audio and sound effects in the prompt.
   - **Aspect Ratio:** **9:16 Portrait**, default.
5. Tap **Generate**.
6. Tap **Use Video** to add the generated clip to your timeline.

---

# 2. Generate an Image with Nano Banana

## What Nano Banana Does

Nano Banana lets creators generate new images using:

- Text prompts
- Optional reference images from the device gallery

## Availability & Requirements

> This feature is currently available to users over 18 on mobile devices in Australia, Canada, India, New Zealand, and the United States. Prompts must be in English or Hindi.

Key requirements:

- Users must be **over 18**
- Available only on **mobile devices**
- Available in:
  - Australia
  - Canada
  - India
  - New Zealand
  - United States
- Prompts must be in:
  - **English**
  - **Hindi**
- Prompt limit: **900 characters**
- Reference images: up to **3 images**
- Generates **4 image options** per prompt

## How to Access Nano Banana

Open the **YouTube Create app**, then access image generation from:

1. **Homepage banner**
2. In the editor, tap **Add** to add a new video to the timeline, then choose the option to generate a new image
3. In the editor, choose the option to generate a new image when adding an overlay
4. From the bottom navigation tool in the editor, tap **Generate image**

## How to Generate an Image

1. Open the YouTube Create app.
2. Access **Generate image** through one of the available entry points.
3. Enter a description of the image you want to create.
   - Prompt limit: **900 characters**
   - Optional: add up to **3 reference images** from your device gallery.
4. Tap **Generate**.
   - The app creates **4 images** based on your prompt.
   - You can provide feedback with **thumbs up** or **thumbs down**.
5. Select the image you like.
6. Choose one of the following:
   - **Use Image** — adds the image to your timeline.
   - **Create Video** — turns the image into an **8-second video**.

# Document 126: Customise LLM backends for diagram generation in draw.io

Source URL: https://www.drawio.com/doc/faq/configure-ai-options
Product area: external_drawio
Original file: docs/customise-llm-backends-for-diagram-generation-in-draw-io-94f9d0dd.md

# Customise LLM Backends for Diagram Generation in draw.io

Source: https://www.drawio.com/doc/faq/configure-ai-options

## Overview

draw.io supports configuring custom LLM backends for AI-powered diagram generation. Users can bring their own API keys, custom endpoints, self-hosted LLMs, custom models, and customized system prompts.

## Important Limitation

Custom AI backends **do not work** in the Forge version of draw.io for Confluence Cloud or Jira Cloud — Atlassian's Content Security Policy blocks browser requests to third-party AI endpoints.

## Use Cases

1. **Private API Keys** — Use your own keys instead of draw.io's public backend (ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude)
2. **Custom Models** — Add support for new or custom AI models
3. **Self-Hosted LLMs** — Point to your own compatible LLM infrastructure
4. **Custom Prompts** — Tailor AI behavior to your workflow
5. **Enterprise Deployment** — Align with organizational AI policies

## Core Configuration Parameters

| Parameter | Description | Default |
|-----------|-------------|---------|
| `enableAi` | Enables AI diagram generation in templates, menus, toolbar, search | `true` (only on app.diagrams.net) |
| `gptApiKey` | ChatGPT API key | `null` |
| `gptUrl` | API endpoint for ChatGPT | `https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions` |
| `geminiApiKey` | Gemini API key | `null` |
| `claudeApiKey` | Claude API key | `null` |

## AI Actions

Selectable actions in the Generate dialog dropdown. Default: `['createPublic', 'create', 'update', 'assist']`

| Action Key | Dropdown Label | Description |
|------------|----------------|-------------|
| `createPublic` | Create Diagram (draw.io) | Uses public AI backend; diagram data cached on backend |
| `create` | Create Diagram | Generates new diagrams from prompts using your configured LLM |
| `update` | Prompt with Diagram / Prompt with Selection | Sends diagram/selection data with the prompt |
| `assist` | Prompt Only | Sends only the prompt, no diagram context |

## Clipboard Actions (No AI Config Required)

These actions allow use of an external AI tool to modify diagrams via XML copy/paste:

| Action | Behavior | Enable Condition |
|--------|----------|------------------|
| Copy diagram to clipboard | Copies full `mxGraphModel` XML with all cell IDs | Diagram not empty |
| Copy selection to clipboard | Copies XML of selected cells only | Cells selected |
| Update diagram from clipboard | Reads XML from clipboard, computes diff, applies changes | Clipboard has valid `mxGraphModel` XML |
| Insert diagram from clipboard | Inserts clipboard XML as new cells (no modification of existing) | Clipboard has valid `mxGraphModel` XML |

### Typical External AI Workflow

1. Select **Copy diagram to clipboard** (or **Copy selection to clipboard**)
2. Paste the XML into your preferred AI tool and describe the changes you want
3. Copy the AI's modified XML response
4. Select **Update diagram from clipboard** to apply changes, or **Insert diagram from clipboard** to add as new cells

## Global Placeholders & System Prompts (`aiGlobals`)

`aiGlobals` defines global placeholders for AI requests. The `{data}` placeholder contains the diagram/selection XML. Default includes API key references and system instructions for `create`, `update`, and `assist` actions.

## Custom AI Models (`aiConfigs` / `aiModels`)

- **`aiModels`** — Array of selectable AI models. Each references a key in `aiConfigs`.
- **`aiConfigs`** — Object mapping model names to their configuration (API key, endpoint, request/response formatting, system prompts).

Supports placeholders: `{data}`, `{model}`, `{apiKey}`, `{prompt}`, and custom keys from `aiGlobals`.

### Gemini Configuration Example

```
geminiApiKey: "YOUR_API_KEY"
aiModels: [{name: "Gemini 2.5", model: "gemini-2.5-flash", config: "gemini"}]
aiConfigs:
  gemini:
    apiKey: "geminiApiKey"
    endpoint: "https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta/models/{model}:generateContent"
    requestHeaders:
      X-Goog-Api-Key: "{apiKey}"
    request:
      contents: [{parts: [{text: "{prompt}"}]}]
    responsePath: "$.candidates[0].content.parts[0].text"
```

### Claude Configuration Example

```
claudeApiKey: "YOUR_API_KEY"
aiModels: [{name: "Claude", model: "claude-sonnet-4-20250514", config: "claude"}]
aiConfigs:
  claude:
    apiKey: "claudeApiKey"
    endpoint: "https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages"
    requestHeaders:
      X-API-Key: "{apiKey}"
      Anthropic-Version: "2023-06-01"
      Anthropic-Dangerous-Direct-Browser-Access: "true"
    request:
      max_tokens: 8192
      model: "{model}"
      messages:
        - {role: "assistant", content: "{action}"}
        - {role: "user", content: "{prompt}"}
    responsePath: "$.content[0].text"
```

---

# Document 127: Customize Chrome themes with AI - Google Chrome Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/chrome/answer/14516713
Product area: chrome-ai
Original file: docs/customize-chrome-themes-with-ai-google-chrome-help-ad858bcb.md

# Customize Chrome Themes with AI — Summary

**Source:** [Google Chrome Help](https://support.google.com/chrome/answer/14516713)  
**Feature:** Create custom Chrome themes using Generative AI via **Customize Chrome → Create with AI**.

---

## Overview

Chrome lets users create custom themes with Generative AI. Users can choose a **subject**, **style**, **mood**, and **color** to generate a theme for the Chrome New Tab page.

> “Create your own theme for Chrome through Generative AI. You can choose a subject, style, mood, and color based on your preferences with the Create with AI feature under ‘Customize Chrome.’”

---

## Requirements

To use **Create with AI**, you must be:

- Located in the **United States**
- At least **18 years old**
- Signed in to your **Google Account** in Chrome

---

## How to Create a Chrome Theme with AI

1. On your computer, open **Chrome**.
2. Open a **New Tab** page.
3. At the bottom right, select **Customize Chrome**.
4. Select **Change theme** → **Create with AI**.
5. Under **“Create theme with AI”**, configure the theme:
   - **Choose a subject:** Pick from a list of popular subjects.
   - Under **“Add optional details”**:
     - **Select a style:** Choose a preferred style. Styles include thumbnail previews.
     - **Select a mood:** Pick a mood from the list.
     - **Select a color:** Choose from available color options, or use **Custom color** for more choices.
6. Select **Create**.
7. Chrome generates a set of AI-created themes based on your selections.
8. Select a generated theme to apply it to the **New Tab** page.

**Tip:** Theme creation may take up to **10 seconds**.

---

## Error Messages and Fixes

### “Something went wrong. Please try again later.”

**Cause:** The server may be busy.  
**Fix:** Try again after a few minutes.

---

### “Looks like you’re offline.”

**Cause:** No network connectivity was detected.  
**Fix:**

1. Check your internet connection.
2. Click **Try again**.

---

### “Can't create a theme right now.”

**Cause:** The server may be busy.  
**Fix:** Try again after a few minutes.

---

## How Your Data Is Used

When using **Create themes with AI**, Google collects inputs and usage-related data, including:

- Selected theme options
- Feedback
- Theme usage

Google uses this information to improve the feature, including for:

- Generative model research
- Machine learning technologies
- Improving safety policies
- Addressing challenges in image generation models

> “To improve Create themes with AI, Chrome has a review process, which includes human review of data, to help us understand the types of problems that occur.”

Use of this feature is governed by:

- [Google Privacy Policy](https://policies.google.com/privacy)
- [Google Generative AI Prohibited Use Policy](https://policies.google.com/terms/generative-ai/use-policy)
- [Google Terms of Service](https://policies.google.com/terms)

> “You agree that your use of Create themes with AI is subject to the Google Terms of Service.”

---

## Related Resources

- [Customize your New Tab page in Chrome](https://support.google.com/chrome/answer/11032183)
- [Manage tabs in Chrome](https://support.google.com/chrome/answer/2391819)

---

## Additional Page Elements

The article also includes standard Google Help Center elements such as:

- Article feedback options
- Help community link
- Google Chrome Help Center navigation
- Privacy Policy and Terms of Service links
- AI support assistant prompt for Chrome help questions

# Document 128: Customize your Gemini in Chrome experience - Computer - Google Chrome Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/chrome/answer/16988996
Product area: chrome-ai

# Customize your Gemini in Chrome Experience — Summary

**Source:** Google Chrome Help — “Customize your Gemini in Chrome experience”  
**URL:** https://support.google.com/chrome/answer/16988996

---

## Key Excerpts & Important Facts

> **Important**: Gemini in Chrome is not available to all users at this time. We’re gradually releasing Gemini in Chrome, so it might not be available to you just yet.

Gemini in Chrome availability depends on:

- Supported regions
- Supported languages
- Gradual rollout status

---

## Overview

Gemini in Chrome can be customized to better match your style, preferences, and needs. Customization options include:

- Saving and reusing prompts as **Skills**
- Connecting Gemini to supported Google apps
- Using **Personal Intelligence** for more personalized responses
- Changing keyboard shortcuts
- Changing the voice used by **Gemini Live**

---

# Skills in Gemini in Chrome

## What Skills Do

Skills let you save useful AI prompts and reuse them with one click on any page.

> A skill works directly with the page you currently see. When you use a skill, Gemini in Chrome reads the content on your active tab and applies your saved instructions to it.

You can:

- Save custom prompts
- Run saved prompts on the active tab
- Browse built-in skills
- Remix ready-to-use skills from the built-in library

### Example Skills

- **Protein max:** Change a standard recipe by inserting high-protein substitutes wherever possible.
- **Check job fit:** Analyze a job description against your background and resume to determine fit and highlight relevant experience.
- **Explain simply:** Translate complicated text into simpler terms and analogies understandable to a kindergartener.

---

## Requirements for Using Skills

To use Skills in Gemini in Chrome, you must:

- Use a **Chromebook Plus**, **Mac**, or **Windows** computer
- Use the latest version of Chrome
- Have Gemini in Chrome turned on
- Sign in with a **personal Google Account**
- Use Gemini in Chrome with Chrome language set to **English (United States)**
- Be **18 years or older**

> **Note**: Skills are not currently available for managed or Enterprise accounts unless your organization's administrator has enabled the feature.

---

## Manually Enable Skills During Rollout

Skills are rolling out gradually. If you do not see them yet, you can try enabling them manually:

1. Check that you're on Chrome version 147 or higher by typing **chrome://version** into your address bar.
2. Go to chrome://flags and search for "Skills." Enable the Skills flag.
3. Relaunch Chrome.
4. Open the Gemini side panel and type / to confirm skills are available.

Important Chrome URLs:

```text
chrome://version
chrome://flags
```

---

## Browse Recommended Skills

To browse built-in skills:

1. Open Chrome on your computer.
2. Click **Ask Gemini** at the top of the browser.
3. In the text box, type a forward slash:

```text
/
```

4. Scroll through available skills.
   - Tip: Hover over a skill to learn what it does.
5. Click the skill you want.
6. Press **Enter** or click **Send**.

For a fuller list of available skills, enter:

```text
chrome://skills/browse
```

You can filter by skill type or search by name.

---

## Create Your Own Skills

You can create unlimited custom skills.

Steps:

1. Open Chrome.
2. Click **Ask Gemini**.
3. Type `/` in the text box to open the Skills menu.
4. Click **Add Skill +**.
5. Enter a name and prompt for the skill.
6. Click **Save**.

To reuse a custom skill later:

```text
/skill name
```

Type `/` followed by the custom skill’s name.

---

## Manage Your Skills

You can edit, create, delete, or copy skill instructions.

Steps:

1. Open Chrome.
2. Click **Ask Gemini**.
3. Type `/` to open the Skills menu.
4. At the bottom, click **Browse Skills**.
5. Choose an action:
   - **Change an existing skill:** click **Edit**
   - **Create a new skill:** click **+ Add**
   - **Delete a skill:** click **More > Delete**

Direct link to your skills page:

```text
chrome://skills/yourSkills
```

Additional tips:

- To reuse a skill’s text, click **More > Copy Instructions**
- In the Edit skill popup, click the pencil icon to make instructions more robust

---

Connected Apps allow Gemini in Chrome to work with supported Google services.

> **Important:** Only some Connected Apps are available in Gemini in Chrome.

If Google Workspace is connected, Gemini in Chrome can help you:

- Create events in Google Calendar

## Google Apps Gemini in Chrome Can Connect To

> **Note:** In some countries, Connected Apps do not use Personal Intelligence to tailor your answer.

Gemini in Chrome currently supports connections to:

- **Google Workspace**, including:
  - Gmail
  - Google Calendar
  - Google Docs
  - Google Drive
  - Google Keep
  - Google Tasks
- Google Photos
- YouTube
- Search services

# Document 129: Data agents in Data Studio  |  Google Cloud Documentation

Source URL: https://cloud.google.com/looker/docs/studio/conversational-data-agents?hl=en
Product area: looker-studio
Original file: docs/data-agents-in-data-studio-google-cloud-documentation-ec1946bf.md

# Data Agents in Data Studio — Summary

**Source:** Google Cloud Documentation — Data Studio  
**URL:** https://cloud.google.com/looker/docs/studio/conversational-data-agents?hl=en  
**Last updated:** 2026-06-11 UTC

> Looker Studio is now called Data Studio.

## Overview

**Data agents** let users curate the **Conversational Analytics** experience for specific datasets and use cases. They help analysts provide the system with context and instructions so it can answer data questions more effectively.

Data agents can be used to:

- Map business terms to specific fields
- Specify preferred fields for filtering and grouping
- Define custom calculations or concepts
- Provide contextual instructions for more accurate responses

The experience differs between:

- **New Conversational Analytics**
- **Legacy Conversational Analytics**

---

# New Conversational Analytics Experience

In the new experience, **data agents are created and managed in BigQuery**, then made available in Data Studio after being shared with users.

## Key Behavior

> Conversational Analytics data agents become available in Data Studio after they are first created in BigQuery and shared with Data Studio users.

Once a data agent is **published in BigQuery**, it automatically appears in the list of agents on the **Chat with your data** page in Data Studio.

---

## Create and Share a Data Agent

To create and share a data agent:

1. Create the agent in **BigQuery**.
2. Grant users access to chat with it in BigQuery and Data Studio.
3. Publish the agent.
4. The agent appears automatically in Data Studio under **Chat with your data**.

You can also copy a Data Studio link from BigQuery:

- From the agent card:
  1. Select **Open actions**
  2. Select **Copy link**
  3. Select **Data Studio**

- From the **Details** panel:
  1. Select **Copy agent link**
  2. Select **Data Studio**

Pasting the link in a browser opens a new conversation with the agent.

---

## Edit a Data Agent

Data agents in the new experience are edited in **BigQuery**.

Options:

- Edit directly in BigQuery
- From Data Studio, open the agent **Details** panel and select **Edit in BigQuery**

From BigQuery, you can also:

- Revoke access
- Delete the agent

---

## Start a Conversation with an Agent

To start a conversation:

1. Navigate to the **Chat with your data** page.
2. Select the data agent you want to use.
   - Use the **Search agents** bar if needed.
3. Enter your question.
4. Press **Return** on Mac or **Enter** on PC.

To chat with another agent:

- Select **+ New conversation** from the main navigation.

You can return to previous conversations from the **Recent** section.

---

## Agent Metadata

Data Studio shows data agent information in two places:

1. **Agent card**
2. **Details panel**

---

### Agent Card

The agent card appears on the **Chat with your data** page.

It includes:

- **Agent**: Name of the agent, as written by the creator
- **Description**: Description written by the creator
- **Updated**: Timestamp of when the agent was last published from BigQuery
- **Open actions** menu with:
  - **Details**: Opens the Details panel
  - **Edit in BigQuery**: Opens the agent configuration in BigQuery
  - **Copy link**: Copies the link to the agent
  - **Send Feedback**: Opens a feedback panel

---

### Agent Details Panel

The **Details** panel appears while chatting with a data agent.

It includes:

- **Agent**: Agent name
- **Description**: Agent description
- Link to edit the agent in BigQuery
- Link to copy the agent URL
- **Knowledge sources**:
  - BigQuery tables
  - BigQuery views
  - BigQuery user-defined functions, or UDFs
- **Project**: Google Cloud project containing the data agent
- **Labels**: Labels assigned to the BigQuery resources, if any
- **Last published**: Timestamp of last publication from BigQuery
- **Created**: Timestamp of creation in BigQuery

---

# Legacy Conversational Analytics Experience

In the legacy experience, data agents are created, edited, shared, deleted, and restored directly in **Conversational Analytics** within Data Studio.

---

## Create and Edit Data Agents

### Create a New Data Agent

To create a new data agent:

1. In Conversational Analytics, go to the **Chat with your data** page.
2. Select the **Agents** tab.
3. Select **+ Create agent**.

Alternatively:

1. In the left navigation panel, select **Manage agents**.
2. Select **+ Create agent**.

On the **New agent** page, provide:

### Required Agent Information

#### Agent Name

Enter a unique and descriptive name.

#### Description

Briefly describe:

- What the agent can do
- What data it uses
- How users should use it

Users see this description when selecting or receiving the shared agent, so it should clearly explain the agent’s purpose.

#### Data

Connect to a new or existing data source:

1. In the **Data** field, click **Select data**.
2. In the **Select data for Agent** window, click the data source name.
3. Click **Select**.

Alternatively, se

# Document 130: Develop with Google Chat  |  Google for Developers

Source URL: https://developers.google.com/workspace/chat/overview
Product area: chat-developers
Original file: docs/develop-with-google-chat-google-for-developers-1f260c06.md

# Develop with Google Chat — Summary

**Source:** Google for Developers — Google Chat  
**URL:** https://developers.google.com/workspace/chat/overview  
**Last updated:** 2026-04-20 UTC

---

## Page Purpose

This page introduces the **Google Chat API** and explains how developers can build **Google Chat apps** that integrate services, workflows, and resources directly into Google Chat conversations.

---

## Key Excerpts

> Google Chat apps integrate services directly into chats, enabling users to access information and take action without leaving the conversation.

> The Chat API consists of gRPC services or REST resources and methods that grant access to Chat, including spaces, space members, messages, message reactions, message attachments, space events, and user read states.

> Calling the Chat API requires authentication.

> The recommended way for most developers to call the Google Chat API is with our officially supported Cloud Client Libraries for your preferred language, like Python, Java, or Node.js.

> The Chat API lets you build Google Chat apps that bring your services and resources right into Google Chat.

---

## Google Chat API Overview

The **Google Chat API** provides access to Google Chat through:

- **gRPC services**
- **REST resources and methods**

It lets developers work with core Google Chat entities such as:

- Spaces
- Space members
- Messages
- Message reactions
- Message attachments
- Space events
- User read states
- User notification settings
- Custom emoji
- Sections

A video introduction is available:

- **Introduction to Google Chat API** — Google Workspace Developers  
  https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=cPHUsVvonjE

---

## Core Google Chat API Concepts

### Spaces

**Spaces** are places where people and apps converse and share files.

Types of spaces include:

- **Direct messages (DMs):** 1:1 conversations between two users or between a user and a Chat app.
- **Group chats:** Conversations between three or more users and Chat apps.
- **Named spaces:** Persistent collaboration areas where people send messages, share files, and work together.

**Resource references:**

- RPC: `google.chat.v1.Space`
- REST: `v1/spaces`

**Example operations:**

- Create a space
- Set up a space
- Get a space
- List spaces
- Update a space
- Delete a space
- Find a direct message

---

### Members

**Members** are users and Chat apps that have joined or been invited to a space.

**Resource references:**

- RPC: `google.chat.v1.Membership`
- REST: `v1/spaces.members`

**Example operations:**

- Create a membership
- Get a membership
- List memberships
- Update a membership
- Delete a membership

---

### Messages

**Messages** include:

- Text communications
- Card-based communications
- File attachments

Users can also react to messages with emoji.

**Resource references:**

- RPC: `google.chat.v1.Message`
- REST: `v1/spaces.messages`
- Cards: `v1/cards`

**Example operations:**

- Create a message
- Get a message
- List messages
- Update a message
- Delete a message

---

### Reactions

**Reactions** represent emoji responses to messages.

Examples include:

> 👍, 🚲, and 🌞

**Resource references:**

- RPC: `google.chat.v1.Reaction`
- REST: `v1/spaces.messages.reactions`

**Example operations:**

- Create a reaction
- List reactions
- Delete a reaction

---

### Custom Emoji

**Custom emoji** are emoji created and shared within an organization in Google Chat.

They can be:

- Included in message content
- Used as reactions to messages

**Resource references:**

- RPC: `google.chat.v1.CustomEmoji`
- REST: `v1/customEmojis`

**Example operations:**

- Create a custom emoji
- Delete a custom emoji
- Get details about a custom emoji
- List custom emojis in an organization

---

### Sections

**Sections** let users group conversations and customize the list of spaces shown in the Google Chat navigation panel.

Types include:

- Predefined system sections
- User-defined custom sections

**Resource references:**

- RPC: `google.chat.v1.Section`
- REST: `v1/users.sections`

**Example operations:**

- Create a section
- Update a section
- Delete a section
- Change the position of a section
- List sections
- List spaces in a section
- Move a space to a different section

---

### Media and Attachments

**Media** represents files uploaded to Google Chat, including:

- Images
- Videos
- Documents

**Media resource reference:**

- REST: `v1/media`
- RPC: Unavailable

**Attachments** are instances of media attached to messages.

**Attachment resource references:**

- RPC: `google.chat.v1.Attachment`
- REST: `v1/spaces.messages.attachments`

**Example operations:**

- Upload media as an attachment
- Download media as an attachment
- Get an attachment

---

### Space Events

**Space events** represent changes to a space or its child resources, including:

- Members
- Messages
- Reactions

**Resource references:**

- RPC: `google.chat.v1.SpaceEvent`
- REST: `v1/spaces.spaceEvents`

**Example related operations 

[... summary truncated for context management ...]

---

# Document 131: Disclosing use of GenAI content - Computer - YouTube Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/youtube/answer/14328491
Product area: youtube-ai
Original file: docs/disclosing-use-of-genai-content-computer-youtube-help-b6ac4565.md

# YouTube Help: Disclosing Use of GenAI Content

**Source:** YouTube Help — “Disclosing use of GenAI content”  
**URL:** https://support.google.com/youtube/answer/14328491

---

## Core Requirement

YouTube requires creators to disclose when they use AI to **meaningfully alter or generate realistic content**, especially when viewers could reasonably believe what they are seeing or hearing is real.

> “To help keep viewers informed about the content they're viewing, we require creators to disclose when they use AI to meaningfully alter or generate photorealistic content.”

Creators must disclose GenAI content that:

- **Makes a real person appear to say or do something they didn’t do.**
- **Alters footage of a real event or place.**
- **Generates a realistic scene that didn’t actually occur.**

This applies to content that is **fully or partially altered or created using AI tools**, including AI audio, video, image creation, or editing tools.

---

## What Counts as GenAI Content Requiring Disclosure?

Disclosure is required when content is:

- **AI-generated or meaningfully AI-altered**
- **Realistic or photorealistic**
- Capable of misleading viewers about what actually happened, what someone said, or what someone did

YouTube emphasizes that the examples provided are **not exhaustive**.

---

## Examples Creators Do **Not** Have to Disclose

Creators generally do **not** need to disclose:

- **Non-realistic AI content**
- **Minor edits** to realistic content
- Edits that are primarily aesthetic and do not mislead viewers about reality

> “Minor edits are ones that are primarily aesthetic, and don't alter the content in a way that could mislead the viewer about what actually happened.”

### Non-Realistic Content

Disclosure is not required for examples such as:

- Someone riding a unicorn through a fantastical world
- Green screen used to depict someone floating in space
- Using an AI-generated or altered animation of a missile in a fully animated video

### Minor Edits or AI Assistance

Disclosure is not required for:

- Applying beauty filters
- Color adjustment or lighting filters
- Special effects filters, such as background blur or vintage effects
- Production assistance, such as using generative AI tools to create or improve:
  - Video outlines
  - Scripts
  - Thumbnails
  - Titles
  - Infographics
- Caption creation
- Video sharpening, upscaling, or repair
- Voice or audio repair
- Idea generation
- Cloning one’s own voice to create voiceovers or dubs
- Gameplay footage from video games
- AI generating or extending a backdrop to simulate a moving car
- Using effects to enhance previously recorded audio

---

## Examples Creators **Need** to Disclose

Creators must disclose AI-generated or meaningfully AI-altered content when it appears realistic.

Examples requiring disclosure include:

- AI-generated music
- AI-generated extra footage of a real place, such as a video of a surfer in Maui for a promotional travel video
- AI-generated realistic videos of a match between two real professional tennis players
- Making it appear as if someone gave advice they did not actually give
- Showing a realistic depiction of a tornado or other weather event moving toward a real city when it did not actually happen
- Making it appear as if hospital workers turned away sick or wounded patients
- Depicting a public figure stealing something they did not steal
- Depicting a public figure admitting to stealing something when they did not make that admission
- Making it look like a real person has been arrested or imprisoned

---

## How to Disclose GenAI Content

Creators disclose AI-generated or meaningfully AI-altered realistic content during the upload process in YouTube Studio.

### Steps on Computer

1. Go to [YouTube Studio](https://studio.youtube.com/).
2. Follow the steps to upload content.
3. In the **Attributes** section, under **“AI use,”** select:
   - **Yes** if the content meets the disclosure requirements
   - **No** if it does not
4. Continue selecting other video details.

The AI disclosure setting is available in YouTube Studio on both **computer and mobile devices**.

---

## Labels for AI Content

If a creator selects **“Yes”** to indicate that content is realistic and generated or meaningfully altered with AI, YouTube will add a label to the video.

### Possible Label Placements

Labels may appear in different places depending on the type of content:

- **Video player label**  
  For photorealistic AI content, a label may appear directly in the video player.

- **Expanded description label**  
  For non-photorealistic or animated AI content, labels may appear in the expanded description field.

> **Note:** Disclosing AI content won’t limit a video’s audience or impact its eligibility to earn money.

---

## Automatic Detection of AI Content

YouTube may automatically apply an AI label on the video player or in the expanded description for:

- Content made using YouTube’s GenAI tools
- Content that c

[... summary truncated for context management ...]

---

# Document 132: Docusign rapidly scales its AI strategy by enabling its global workforce with Gemini

# Docusign rapidly scales its AI strategy by enabling its global workforce with Gemini

Source: https://workspace.google.com/blog/customer-stories/docusign-rapidly-scales-its-ai-strategy-by-enabling-its-global-workforce-with-gemini?hl=en

Customer Stories

# Docusign rapidly scales its AI strategy by enabling its global workforce with Gemini

March 25, 2026

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Hero_1.max-2500x2500.jpg](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Hero_1.max-2500x2500.jpg)

##### Shivi Singh Verma

Senior Manager, Engineering (Cloud Apps), Docusign

##### Try Google Workspace at No Cost

Get a business email, all the storage you need, video conferencing, and more.

[SIGN UP](https://workspace.google.com/business/signup/welcome?source=gafb-blog_article-body-en)

**NotebookLM is a key differentiator for us. Because its knowledge base is built on our own unique data, everyone at Docusign can easily find any information they need.**

**Snapshot:** As a pioneer in electronic signatures, Docusign has always operated with a technology-first mindset. In its search for innovation, it became an early adopter of Gemini and today relies on Google Workspace with Gemini to power its global team’s productivity and collaboration.

- **90% reduction in time** spent on performance review preparation with NotebookLM
- Saves **1-4 hours** per employee per week
- 80% of employees feel **Gemini has a positive impact** on their day-to-day work
- Automated support chat for new policies with **NotebookLM and Gemini-based chatbots**
- Switched from **Office365 to Google Workspace** to give Docusign a more modern, intuitive, and collaborative working experience

## **Origin story**

From its inception, [Docusign](https://www.docusign.com/) has helped lead efforts to standardize and redefine the perception of electronic signatures. Our teams do this by committing themselves to using new technologies to shape internal operations and develop our products for more than 1.7 million customers. Most recently, that means dismantling barriers to AI and weaving it into our daily workflows. We see our company’s collaboration solutions as the starting place for our teams’ innovation, but that wasn’t always the case.

Our previous collaboration solutions were disconnected and software sprawl made it challenging for everyone to work together. Our systems of record and everyday work tools were disconnected, forcing employees to spend valuable time searching for information before they could act. We knew there had to be more efficient ways to connect our global teams and deliver faster by offering better-connected and more user-friendly tools.

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Google_Workspace_Inline_Image_1_gvyrFzv.max-2000x2000.jpg](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Google_Workspace_Inline_Image_1_gvyrFzv.max-2000x2000.jpg)

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Google_Workspace_Inline_Image_1_gvyrFzv.max-2000x2000.jpg](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Google_Workspace_Inline_Image_1_gvyrFzv.max-2000x2000.jpg)

By building a list of “gold standard” Gemini responses, Docusign works to continually refine and hone prompting to make every team as efficient as possible.

## **The switch to Google Workspace and NotebookLM**

We chose to move from Office365 to Google Workspace to give Docusign a more modern, intuitive, and collaborative working experience. It was especially important for us to work in a cloud-first environment that reflected how our teams already operated, including native integration with our other apps. Now, we trust Google Drive as our central repository for all of our data while communicating through Gmail, collaborating in real time through Docs and Slides, and even collecting internal data with Forms. The real evolutionary leap came when we adopted [Gemini](https://cloud.google.com/ai/gemini-for-work?hl=en), giving our teams access to generative AI directly within the apps they already use every day.

After consulting with other enterprise users, we saw that Gemini’s feature set was growing, and it supported our ability to scale AI quickly. It also meets the same enterprise security standards as the rest of Google Workspace, allowing us to confidently integrate AI while maintaining our zero-trust architecture and access controls. Our implementation team then helped introduce our entire org to the ways that AI could fit uniquely into our workflows. They educated us, for example, on the best practices for prompting.

Investing early in training helped accelerate responsible AI adoption across the organization. We used Forms to collect feedback from our teams. That survey found 80% of participants reported a positive impact on their work lives and 67% saved 1-4 hours of time each week, boosting their productivity. With that time back, everyone at Docusign can focus their attention on higher impact, strategic work to grow the company. As a result, Gemini is now a critical tool for us on new projects, data analysis, and employee learning.

[NotebookLM](https://notebooklm.google/) has also become a powerful knowledge and research assistant for our teams. Because its knowledge base is built on our own unique data, everyone at Docusign can easily find any information they need, and customize it for their format preferences. I can use it to generate infographics for updates to leadership just as easily as I can craft more tailored, comprehensive performance reviews for my team. Conducting quarterly performance reviews used to take me four to five hours per person. With unique notebooks, I can query existing notes, build a report, and be ready to meet with them in less than 30 minutes. As thousands of employees at Docusign embrace this paradigm shift, we’ll only continue to innovate and drive insights faster.

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/GoogleWorkspaceInlineImage2.max-1500x1500.jpg](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/GoogleWorkspaceInlineImage2.max-1500x1500.jpg)

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/GoogleWorkspaceInlineImage2.max-1500x1500.jpg](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/GoogleWorkspaceInlineImage2.max-1500x1500.jpg)

With Gemini and NotebookLM embedded into day-to-day work at Docusign, every team member is helping build an AI-first culture.

## **Looking ahead**

NotebookLM has already had such an impact at both the individual and the organizational level by introducing more automation and broadening access to our institutional knowledge. We continue to refine these processes by evaluating prompt accuracy against “gold standard” reference answers to ensure we’re getting the most out of each request.

We want to continue to support our teams through solutions like [Gemini Enterprise](https://cloud.google.com/gemini-enterprise). Our vision is to deploy agents that serve as a single point of entry for groups of employees at Docusign. This may look like an HR agent that supports new hires with questions about onboarding or agents that support our product engineers by surfacing product documentation. This ongoing work to improve access will help all of us drive innovation, collaboration, and productivity across the globe.

# Document 133: Draft emails with Gemini in Gmail - Computer - Gmail Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/mail/answer/13955415?hl=en
Product area: gmail
Original file: docs/draft-emails-with-gemini-in-gmail-computer-gmail-help-30929ecf.md

# Draft Emails with Gemini in Gmail — Summary

**Source:** [Gmail Help: Draft emails with Gemini in Gmail](https://support.google.com/mail/answer/13955415?hl=en)  
**Platform covered:** Computer

---

## Overview

In Gmail, Gemini’s **“Help me write”** feature can help you draft or revise emails directly in the compose window.

You can use it to:

- **Generate a new email draft**, such as:
  - A birthday invitation
  - An introduction to a potential business contact
- **Refine existing text** for tone and clarity, such as turning a rough outline into a formal email.

---

## Requirements

You can use **“Help me write”** with:

- **An eligible Google Workspace or Google AI plan**
  - Available globally in multiple supported languages.
- **A personal Google Account**
  - Available in supported languages.
  - Restricted to the **US only**.

> Learn more: [Supported languages for Google Workspace with Gemini](https://support.google.com/mail/answer/14925782#zippy=%2Cgemini-summary-cards)

---

## How to Draft an Email with “Help me write”

1. On your computer, open [Gmail](https://mail.google.com/).
2. At the top left, click **Compose**.
   - Alternatively, you can **reply to an email** or **open an existing draft**.
3. In the compose window:
   - Enter your message, **or**
   - Leave the field blank.
4. At the bottom, in the prompt bar, enter your prompt.
   - If the prompt bar is not visible, click **Help me write**.
5. Click **Create**.

---

## How to Refine Your Draft

To polish or revise a draft, you can either enter a new instruction in the prompt bar or use preset refinement options.

### Example refinement prompts

> "Make it more professional."

> "Mention the Friday deadline."

### Built-in refinement options

Use these options in the prompt bar:

- **Formalize**
- **Friendly**
- **Shorten**

### Switching between drafts

**Tip:** To switch between different generated drafts, use:

- **Undo**
- **Redo**

These buttons appear at the right side of the prompt bar.

---

## How to Improve Gemini Draft Results

For the most relevant and helpful drafts, make prompts **specific and detailed**.

| What to include in your prompt | Example |
|---|---|
| Mention the person or group you are writing to. | “Write an apology to **my child's teacher** for their absence.” |
| Clarify the subject and required action. | “ **Decline the meeting** on Thursday.” |
| Use tone words like “formal,” “casual,” or other adjectives. | “Write a **formal** invitation to a business partner.” |
| To improve factual accuracy, mention a specific email or file. | “Write a summary of **the Q4 earnings email** I received last week.” |

---

## Personalization: How “Help me write” Uses Your Data

**Important:** To have “Help me write” match your tone and writing style, your drafts must be in **English**.

When you use **“Help me write,”** Gemini may proactively use relevant information from:

- Other emails
- Google Drive files

Examples of details it may reference include:

- Flight times
- Soccer schedules
- Hotel booking codes

This allows Gemini to draft a personalized response that includes relevant details and better matches your tone and style **without requiring explicit instructions**.

Google states that this information is only used to improve drafts when you use **“Help me write.”**

### View sources used by Gemini

**Tip:** To see which emails and documents Gemini used to personalize a response, click **Sources**.

---

## Giving Feedback About a Draft

Google Workspace with Gemini is still learning and may not always support your request correctly.

If a generated draft is inaccurate or appears unsafe, you can submit feedback.

### Steps to submit feedback

1. Below the generated text, click:
   - **Good suggestion**, or
   - **Bad suggestion**
2. Enter your feedback.
3. Click **Submit**.

### Feedback with screenshots

To send feedback with screenshots:

1. Click **Help** in the top right of Gmail.
2. Select **Send feedback to Google**.

Learn more: [Send feedback in Gmail](https://support.google.com/mail/answer/9212652)

### Reporting legal issues

To report a legal issue, [create a request](https://support.google.com/legal/answer/3110420).

---

## Important Notes About Gemini Suggestions

Google provides several cautions about Gemini-generated suggestions:

- Gemini feature suggestions **don’t represent Google’s views** and should not be attributed to Google.
- Do **not** rely on Gemini features as:
  - Medical advice
  - Legal advice
  - Financial advice
  - Other professional advice
- Gemini features may suggest **inaccurate or inappropriate information**.
- User feedback helps make Gemini more helpful and safe.
- Enterprise end users can submit feedback about their experience.
  - They are informed before submitting that feedback data should not contain **personal, sensitive, or confidential information**.

---

## Article Feedback Options

The page also allows users to give feedback about the help article itself.

Use

[... summary truncated for context management ...]

---

# Document 134: Draft emails with Gemini in Gmail (Workspace Experiments) - Computer - Gmail Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/mail/answer/13384326
Product area: gmail
Original file: docs/draft-emails-with-gemini-in-gmail-workspace-experiments-computer-gmail-help-c479deda.md

# Draft emails with Gemini in Gmail (Workspace Experiments) – Help Summary

> **Important:** This feature is part of **Google Workspace Experiments**, a trusted tester program for trying new AI capabilities. To check access, open a new message in Gmail and look for the “Help me write” icon at the bottom of the compose window.  
> [Learn where Workspace Experiments features are available](https://support.google.com/mail/answer/13607340).

With **“Help me write”** you can:

- **Generate a new email** from scratch (e.g., birthday invitation, business introduction)
- **Refine existing draft text** – adjust tone, clarity, or turn a rough outline into a formal email

---

## How to Draft an Email

1. Open [Gmail](https://mail.google.com/) on your computer.
2. Click **Compose** (top left), or reply to an email / open a draft.
3. In the compose window, enter your message or leave it blank.
4. In the prompt bar at the bottom, type your instructions.
   - If the prompt bar isn’t visible, click the **Help me write** icon ![](https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/ydRjKg11Kk4r2VOow0WHbjFZn-3feYiMEhWRz86QLOTBt-dcJbW-pL6ARo3C2d6yqXc=h36).
5. Click **Create** ![](https://storage.googleapis.com/support-kms-prod/ueLgY4D6D1QIVr34WsLU2RvYQXnb4GOLGiQy).

---

## Refining Your Draft

Inside the prompt bar, you can improve the draft by:

- **Entering a new instruction**  
  Examples:  
  - *“Make it more professional.”*  
  - *“Mention the Friday deadline.”*

- **Using one‑click options:**  
  - **Formalize** ![](https://storage.googleapis.com/support-kms-prod/hOzlU2nuGFIEICtzJNPlqfS4BS3oZgLsU4pp)  
  - **Friendly** ![](https://storage.googleapis.com/support-kms-prod/EYhnfD3loaHL2d7TrAHgj9fNTsZojIXsXpL1)  
  - **Shorten** ![](https://storage.googleapis.com/support-kms-prod/lwYUMWRkDG7EDCuq1yElOxcbqXLI0E4Ew36E)

> **Tip:** Switch between different versions of the draft using the **Undo** ![](https://storage.googleapis.com/support-kms-prod/rzcifgL57J7GAvdegxOzByopChjbJ72SXZqs) and **Redo** ![](https://storage.googleapis.com/support-kms-prod/50xDcPiV3SWeZtBe0Lll96mRu6DczOYeM4jA) buttons.

---

## How to Write Better Prompts (Table)

To get the most relevant, personalised, and helpful drafts, be as specific and detailed as possible.

| What to include in your prompt | Example |
|-------------------------------|---------|
| Mention the recipient or group | *“Write an apology to **my child's teacher** for their absence.”* |
| Clarify the subject and required action | *“**Decline the meeting** on Thursday.”* |
| Use tone words like “formal,” “casual,” etc. | *“Write a **formal** invitation to a business partner.”* |
| Reference a specific email or file for factual accuracy | *“Write a summary of **the Q4 earnings email** I received last week.”* |

---

## How “Help me write” Personalises Drafts

> **Important:** Personalised tone and style matching works only for drafts in **English**.

When you use “Help me write”, it **automatically pulls details from your recent emails and Drive files** (e.g., flight times, soccer schedules, hotel booking codes) to draft a response that includes relevant facts and matches your tone – without you having to explicitly request it.  
That information is used **only to improve your drafts** when you activate the feature.

- Click **Sources** ![](https://storage.googleapis.com/support-kms-prod/64kv2TsNFyuB9nKep1VDyPew3gIK495exCmJ) to see which emails and documents Gemini used for personalization.

---

## Giving Feedback on a Draft

Google Workspace with Gemini learns from your feedback.

1. Below the generated text, click **Good suggestion** ![](https://storage.googleapis.com/support-kms-prod/7j7OkRZfOcT69Vz6rrOiSRTPFjmKhseJIom5) or **Bad suggestion** ![](https://storage.googleapis.com/support-kms-prod/jyZQMHVBqIlMeIxVgJQZSo1iOp4iheXGMHa5).
2. Enter your feedback.
3. Click **Submit**.

> **Important:**  
> - To include screenshots, click the **Help** icon ![](https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/_Fo0qdrwewWdt_w7aDisG8jfn_2SHouHlv3ikSfWT2JJzRC_k7aK4YoCNQIdKov9HM0=h36) at the top right of Gmail, then **Send feedback to Google**.  
> - For legal issues, [create a request](https://support.google.com/legal/answer/3110420).

---

## Turning Off Workspace Experiments

To disable “Help me write” and all other Workspace Experiments features, you must **exit the Workspace Experiments programme**.  
**This action is permanent** – you will lose access to all related features and cannot rejoin.  
[Learn how to exit Workspace Experiments](https://support.google.com/mail/answer/13447104#labs-opt-out&exit-labs-docs&exit-labs-gmail).

---

## Important Disclaimers & Data Usage

- Workspace Experiments suggestions **do not** represent Google’s views; do not rely on them as professional advice (medical, legal, financial, etc.).
- Suggestions may be **inaccurate or inappropriate** – your feedback helps improve them.
- **Do not include personal, confidential, or sensitive information** in your prompts.
- Google uses Experiments data

[... summary truncated for context management ...]

# Document 135: Duet AI for Google Workspace Enterprise — now available for preorder

# Duet AI for Google Workspace Enterprise — now available for preorder
