# Equifax embraces secure generative AI with Gemini

Source: https://workspace.google.com/blog/customer-stories/equifax-embraces-gemini-for-secure-innovation-across-business-units?hl=en

Customer Stories

# Equifax embraces secure generative AI with Gemini

June 24, 2025

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Google_Workspace_Blog_Hero_images_3.max-2500x2500.png](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Google_Workspace_Blog_Hero_images_3.max-2500x2500.png)

##### JK Krug

Vice President of Digital Employee Experience, Equifax

##### Google Workspace Newsletter

Keep up with the evolving future of work and collaboration with insights, trends, and product news.

[SIGN UP](https://workspace.google.com/?source=gafb-blog_article-body-en#newsletter)

“Gemini is not a quick fix — it’s a collaborative partner that can support our objectives, and it’s up to us to learn how to communicate with it.”

**Snapshot:** Global credit bureau Equifax adopted Gemini to enhance productivity while strengthening its security posture. As a result of its Gemini trial, Equifax found that:

- 97% of participants wanted to keep their Gemini licenses
- 90% of participants experienced an increase in work quality and quantity
- Employees from nearly all business units **saved more** **than one hour per day**

---

### The mission and the challenge

[Equifax](https://www.equifax.com/about-equifax/who-we-are/) delivers insights to its customers that power decisions to move people forward. Over the past seven years, the organization has invested $3 billion in [The Equifax Cloud™](https://www.equifax.com/about-equifax/equifax-cloud/), a top-tier global technology and security infrastructure that has changed nearly every facet of its infrastructure and continues to set Equifax apart in the industry.

During this transformation, we knew we needed a partner who could help enhance enterprise [communication and collaboration](https://workspace.google.com/blog/customer-stories/how-equifax-achieved-ruthless-collaboration-google-workspace?e=48754805) across its teams, from anywhere around the world, in a secure, cloud-native environment, which we were able to do with the adoption of [Google Workspace](https://workspace.google.com/intl/en_ca/)

Security was at the forefront of our enterprise’s most critical needs as we looked to implement a generative AI solution. During our search, we ran a platform “bake-off” and found that [Gemini](https://gemini.google.com/) met all our security needs. The solution inherits all our existing Workspace security settings to keep our data, prompts, and work within the tenant, giving us peace of mind to deploy generative AI across our enterprise safely

Here’s how we use Gemini today to foster innovation across teams while improving our security posture.

![Video Thumbnail](https://img.youtube.com/vi/6MWXXcGiLec/hqdefault.jpg)

Learn how Equifax securely deployed generative AI across its global organization with Gemini.

### The switch to Gemini

Our goal for using Gemini was to increase efficiency and productivity, freeing up time for end users to work on more impactful initiatives and projects. To better understand the tool’s ROI, Equifax worked with Google Workspace and [SADA](https://sada.com/), an Insight company and Google Premier Partner, to run a six-month trial with more than 1,500 participants and a waitlist of over 300 people. SADA created custom Gemini training agendas to dive into specific use cases for Equifax. The SADA team also ran demos to show how Gemini integrates across the Workspace suite. This made it easy to understand how Gemini can enhance every step of our workflows.

The journey of integrating Gemini wasn't just about adoption — it was about discovery. With the support of our Google Workspace Customer Success team, we built a value-driven framework to help with audience selection, survey design, and measurement so that Equifax could effectively evaluate the value of Gemini. By providing a structured way to assess the tool’s business impact, we unlocked a wealth of insights into the specific tasks where it was truly making a difference. This allowed us to understand how our team was experiencing the power of Gemini on a daily basis.

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/unnamed_7.max-600x600.jpg](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/unnamed_7.max-600x600.jpg)

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/unnamed_7.max-600x600.jpg](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/unnamed_7.max-600x600.jpg)

Global credit bureau Equifax uses Gemini within Workspace applications to create and collaborate.

After the trial, 97% of users asked to keep their Gemini licenses, 90% saw an increase in work quality and quantity, and employees from almost every business unit saved more than one hour per day by using the tool. That’s valuable time back in their schedules to focus on more important work. For example, our help desk representatives use Gemini to look deeper at the data to determine how to improve the service they provide our end users. Other teams use the “take notes for me” feature to create transcripts, summaries, and action items from [Google Meet](https://meet.google.com/) calls, putting all the details in one place so we can share with anyone who misses a meeting.

### Looking ahead

As we prepare for phase two of our Gemini deployment with Google Workspace and SADA, we’re taking what we learned from the trial to deliver the best possible resources to our teams. We are rolling out Gemini to our nearly 22,000 employees and contractors, focusing on the most requested training topics, like prompting and use of the side panel, and team-specific use cases, such as those in marketing and finance. SADA will facilitate open office hours, creating an open forum where employees can drop in and ask questions if they feel stuck.

We’re also excited to evaluate new Gemini-enabled tools as we look to expand our generative AI usage in creative ways. [NotebookLM](https://notebooklm.google/) has the ability to upscale our collaboration with Gemini and allow us to do more with our data, while [Google Vids](https://workspace.google.com/intl/en_ca/products/vids/) can empower us to generate engaging video content at scale. In our experience, once people start using Gemini, they simply don’t want to stop using it — and we’re confident that Gemini will continue to support our efficiency, productivity, and security goals.

# Document 147: Expanding Google AI in Workspace with new zero trust and digital sovereignty controls

Source URL: https://workspace.google.com/blog/identity-and-security/accelerating-zero-trust-and-digital-sovereignty-ai?hl=en
Product area: workspace-site
Original file: docs/expanding-google-ai-in-workspace-with-new-zero-trust-and-digital-sovereignty-con-6e4eb508.md

# Expanding Google AI in Workspace with new zero trust and digital sovereignty controls

Source: https://workspace.google.com/blog/identity-and-security/accelerating-zero-trust-and-digital-sovereignty-ai?hl=en

Product Announcements

# Expanding Google AI in Workspace with new zero trust and digital sovereignty controls

August 24, 2023

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Nextsecurity.max-2500x2500.png](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Nextsecurity.max-2500x2500.png)

##### Yulie Kwon Kim

VP, Product - Workspace

##### Andy Wen

Senior Director, Product Management, Workspace

##### Try Google Workspace at No Cost

Get a business email, all the storage you need, video conferencing, and more.

[SIGN UP](https://workspace.google.com/business/signup/welcome?source=gafb-blog_article-body-en)

Security, confidentiality, and compliance continue to be top of mind for commercial and public sector organizations. And for good reason: last year cybersecurity attacks grew [38%](https://www.checkpoint.com/press-releases/check-point-software-releases-its-2023-security-report-highlighting-rise-in-cyberattacks-and-disruptive-malware/), with each data breach costing organizations an average of [$4.35M](https://www.ibm.com/reports/data-breach). The sheer scale of modern attacks and the sophistication of motivated adversaries are something that legacy productivity solutions can’t keep pace with. There is a better way — a cloud-native architecture rooted in zero-trust principles and augmented with AI-powered threat defenses. This is how we architected Google Workspace, resulting in real-world benefits for our customers:

- 0 known exploited vulnerabilities vs. 40+ in a legacy productivity suite ([The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency](https://www.cisa.gov/known-exploited-vulnerabilities-catalog), 11/2021 - 8/2023)
- 41% fewer security incidents on average than other email solutions (At-Bay, [Ranking Email Security Solutions](https://www.at-bay.com/ranking-email-security-solutions/), 2/2023)
- Up to 50% savings on cyber security insurance premiums (At-Bay, [Ranking Email Security Solutions](https://www.at-bay.com/ranking-email-security-solutions/), 2/2023)

In security, the job is never done, which is why **we’re unveiling new zero trust, digital sovereignty, and threat defense controls powered by Google AI to help organizations keep their data safe.**

### Enable safer work from anywhere with AI-powered zero trust controls

The zero trust security model has become an essential part of reducing security risks for a distributed workforce. Workspace provides built-in controls, such as data loss prevention (DLP) and context-aware access (CAA), to help organizations accelerate their zero trust adoption and meet industry frameworks, such as [CISA’s Zero Trust Maturity Model](https://www.cisa.gov/zero-trust-maturity-model).

Roche is one of many customers that has been using zero trust capabilities in Workspace to enable safer work: “Our organization has been striving to break away from VPN and office network connections for years,” said **Tim Ehrhart**, **Domain Lead**, **Information Security**, **Roche**. “Context-Aware Access (CAA) has helped us manage our risks by not making access a binary choice, but allowing for more flexibility in access policies and allowing them to be applied to the right people, applications, and data. Since using CAA, we've been able to allow our users to use more of Google Workspace for a broader set of scenarios with more confidence in the safety of that work.”

Today, we are introducing new capabilities to provide IT and security teams with more granular controls over data use and access, including:

- **Leveraging Google AI to automatically and continuously classify and label data in Google Drive to help ensure data is appropriately shared and protected from exfiltration:** Administrators can now use confidentiality-preserving AI models, customized uniquely for their organization, to automatically classify and label new and existing files in Drive. Data protection controls, such as DLP or CAA, can then be applied based on the security policy. This is now available in preview.

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/original_images/4_MB_AI_Classification_Labels_GIF_.gif](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/original_images/4_MB_AI_Classification_Labels_GIF_.gif)

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/original_images/4_MB_AI_Classification_Labels_GIF_.gif](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/original_images/4_MB_AI_Classification_Labels_GIF_.gif)

Automatic data classification and labeling in Drive powered by Google AI

- **Enforcing context-aware DLP controls in Drive:** Workspace admins can set criteria, such as device location or security status, that must be met in order for a user to be able to share sensitive content in Drive. This new capability provides more granular controls to help prevent unintended data loss and will be available later this year in preview.
- **Extending enhanced DLP controls to Gmail:** Already available in Google Chat, Drive, and Chrome to help security teams control sharing sensitive information inside and outside the organization, enhanced DLP controls are coming to Gmail and will be available in preview later this year.

### Go beyond data residency with digital sovereignty controls

Enterprise and public sector organizations recognize the importance of digital sovereignty, but often settle for data residency without fully understanding its limitations. Workspace can provide a step change in attestable digital sovereignty with secure-by-default infrastructure, technical data access controls, and industry certifications all in a single cloud instance.

PwC UK piloted [Sovereign Controls](https://workspace.google.com/blog/product-announcements/announcing-sovereign-controls-for-google-workspace) in Workspace to help meet demanding regulatory requirements: "PwC UK participated in the CSE Gmail pilot and I observed first hand that Google understands the importance of technical data boundaries, not only for PwC and our requirements, but for that of our clients,” said **Shaun Bookham**, **UK Operations and Technology Director**, **PwC UK**. “Through influencing the development of these CSE and Access Management capabilities, I have confidence that Google will continue to adapt to rapidly evolving regulatory requirements, enabling us to transform the way we work whilst remaining at the forefront of sovereignty and compliance."

Today, we are introducing new capabilities to provide organizations with enhanced controls over their data that help with:

- **Preventing third-party access to sensitive data:** Through the ownership of the encryption keys, client-side encryption (CSE) helps organizations add an additional layer of data protection and prevent third-party access from external entities such as Google or foreign governments. Today, we're introducing a number of CSE enhancements, including the support of mobile apps in Google Calendar, Gmail, and Meet (generally available), the ability to set CSE as default for select organizational units (available in preview later this year), guest access support in Meet (available in preview later this year), comments support in Docs (available in preview later this year), and the ability for users to view, edit, or convert Microsoft Excel files (available in preview).
- **Selecting the location of your encryption keys:** Through strategic partnerships with a global security provider Thales, Stormshield, and Flowcrypt, we're enabling CSE customers to store their encryption keys with their trusted partner in the country of their choice to simplify set up and support local regulatory compliance.

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/original_images/GmailMobileCSE_R01_01-5MB.gif](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/original_images/GmailMobileCSE_R01_01-5MB.gif)

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/original_images/GmailMobileCSE_R01_01-5MB.gif](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/original_images/GmailMobileCSE_R01_01-5MB.gif)

Sending a new email with CSE protection enabled by default

- **Choosing where data is stored and processed:** Today, organizations can choose the storage location of their data at rest. Available in preview later this year, organizations will be able to choose where their covered data is processed (EU or US). Organizations also have the option to store a copy of their Workspace data in a country of their choice.
- **Enforcing regional support personnel access:** Today, customers worldwide can use Access Approvals to control Google support access for troubleshooting purposes, and can monitor Google actions with Access Transparency. Additionally, customers can use Access Management to limit approved access to US-based support. Available in preview later this year, customers will also be able to limit access to EU-based support.

### Prevent cyber threats before they emerge

Social engineering attacks, such as phishing, are one of the most common entry points for data breaches. Threat defense controls in Workspace help customers prevent, detect, and respond to social engineering and other identity-based attacks before they emerge. Today, we’re introducing additional capabilities to help security teams defend against account takeovers, including:

- **Making 2-Step verification (2SV) mandatory for select enterprise administrators:** Compromised administrator accounts can have an outsized impact, and 2SV can result in a [50% decrease](https://blog.google/technology/safety-security/reducing-account-hijacking/) in accounts being compromised. Starting later this year, in a phased approach, select administrator accounts of our resellers and largest enterprise customers will be required to add 2SV to their accounts to strengthen their security.
- **Requiring multi-party approval for sensitive administrator actions:** Workspace administrators can require additional approval by another administrator to complete a sensitive action, such as changing 2SV settings for a user, to provide an extra layer of defense against malicious actions. This will be available in preview later this year.

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/MultiPartyMPA_copy.max-2000x2000.jpg](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/MultiPartyMPA_copy.max-2000x2000.jpg)

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/MultiPartyMPA_copy.max-2000x2000.jpg](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/MultiPartyMPA_copy.max-2000x2000.jpg)

Reviewing a request from another administrator to complete a sensitive action

- **Protecting additional sensitive actions in Gmail:** We are extending Google’s AI-powered defenses to provide automated protection for additional sensitive actions in Gmail, such as email filtering or forwarding. This protection is available in preview.
- **Exporting logs to Chronicle in just a few clicks:** Workspace administrators can now export Workspace logs into [Chronicle](https://chronicle.security/) to identify anomalies and help improve their response time to threats. This integration is available in preview.

### Getting started

We are constantly at work to help our customers — from small businesses and large enterprises to schools and governments — strengthen their security and compliance posture and keep their data safe. To learn more, get a no-cost [trial](https://workspace.google.com/business/signup/welcome), check out our [technical documentation](https://support.google.com/a/topic/7556782), attend our upcoming sessions at [Next ‘23](https://cloud.withgoogle.com/next/session-library#productivity-and-collaboration-apps), and [sign up](https://docs.google.com/forms/d/1tEk2cfC7msKYUEl76MPu5aVPUdsO46Rkru0zT9vOHXU/edit) for early access to try new capabilities. 

For information on securing AI systems, check out [Google SAIF](https://blog.google/technology/safety-security/introducing-googles-secure-ai-framework/), a conceptual framework to help practitioners collaboratively secure AI technology as it continues to grow.

# Document 148: Explore Inspiration tab on YouTube - YouTube Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/youtube/answer/15575509
Product area: youtube-ai
Original file: docs/explore-inspiration-tab-on-youtube-youtube-help-546161a0.md

# Explore Inspiration Tab on YouTube — Summary

**Source:** YouTube Help — “Explore Inspiration tab on YouTube”  
**URL:** https://support.google.com/youtube/answer/15575509

## Overview

The **Inspiration tab** in YouTube Studio helps creators brainstorm and generate content suggestions using AI. It can assist with:

- **Video ideas**
- **Titles**
- **Thumbnails**
- **Hooks**
- **Outlines**
- **Related video/topic research**

It uses channel data and audience-related signals to suggest ideas and help creators evaluate whether a topic may be worth pursuing.

---

## Key Excerpts

> “The Inspiration tab helps you generate and brainstorm suggestions for ideas, titles, thumbnails, and outlines for your content with the assistance of AI tools.”

> “AI-generated content ideas using the Inspiration tab in YouTube Studio may be inaccurate or inappropriate and vary in quality.”

> “Generated content ideas do not represent YouTube's views.”

> “Don't include personal information while using this feature, or rely on ideas, outlines, or other outputs from this feature for professional advice.”

> “The Inspiration tab is available in all global markets only on desktop.”

> “Currently, this feature only provides English-only suggestions.”

> “Prompts and input connected with your channel as part of your interaction with Inspiration will be deleted automatically after 45 days.”

---

## Availability & Important Notes

- Available **globally**, but **desktop only**.
- Suggestions are currently **English-only**.
- If the **Inspiration** tab does not appear on the **Content** page, the creator does not have access to the feature.
- Creators who saved ideas in previous versions of Inspiration can export them using **Google Takeout**.
- AI outputs may be:
  - Inaccurate
  - Inappropriate
  - Variable in quality
- AI-generated ideas **do not represent YouTube’s views**.
- Users should avoid entering **personal information**.
- Outputs should not be relied on for **professional advice**.

---

## How to Get Started

1. Sign in to [YouTube Studio](https://studio.youtube.com/).
2. In the left menu, select **Content**.
3. From the top menu, click the **Inspiration** tab.
4. Begin brainstorming topics for your next video.

---

## Brainstorming Features

When opening the Inspiration tab:

- You will see **nine suggested ideas** based on your channel’s data.
- You can refine ideas or brainstorm new ones by:
  - Entering prompts in the input box
  - Clicking suggested topic chips
  - Selecting previously published videos from the **+ menu**
- To explore variations:
  - Click **More like this** on an idea card.
  - Click **Show more** at the bottom of the page to load **9 similar but new ideas**.
- You can navigate between iterations using **back and forward arrows**.
  - Ideas explored during the session are retained until the page is reloaded.
- To save an idea for later, click the **bookmark** icon on the idea card.

---

## Image Preferences

Creators can adjust how AI-generated images appear:

- Open settings via the **setting icon**.
- Choose an image style:
  - **Digital painting**
  - **Sketch**
- Or disable images entirely if preferred.

---

## Understanding Idea Potential

The Inspiration tab provides context and signals to help creators evaluate ideas.

### Idea Card

Each idea card includes:

- Idea name
- Brief summary
- Relevant image
- Data-driven insights into possible YouTube performance

Signals may include:

- **Audience interest indicators**
- **Aggregated comment insights**
- **Prediction signals**

### Channel Alignment

The tool helps creators understand:

- How an idea fits their existing channel content
- What unique angle they can bring
- How the idea connects to previous videos

### Related Interest

Creators can evaluate whether an idea is worth pursuing based on recent topic interest, including:

- Related videos
- Creator names
- Views
- Thumbnails
- Upload dates

---

## Inspiration Card Types

### Hooks

Hooks help creators capture attention at the beginning of a video.

They may suggest:

- Captivating visuals
- Thought-provoking statements
- Compelling actions

Goal: encourage viewers to keep watching.

### Outlines

Outlines help structure videos from start to finish.

Creators can use suggested outlines for:

- Planning videos
- Drafting scripts
- Simplifying production

### Titles

The Titles card suggests possible video titles based on:

- Similar videos
- Trends

Suggestions can be customized to fit the creator’s content.

### Thumbnails

The Thumbnails card uses AI to suggest eye-catching, customizable thumbnails.

Creators can:

- Use AI-generated thumbnail concepts
- Change image style to **digital painting** or **sketch**
- Use thumbnails as inspiration for the final design

### Related Videos

This card shows videos relevant to:

- Topics the creator’s audience watched over the past **28 days**
- The creator’s saved searches

It helps validate an idea’s potential by showing:

- Recent topic

[... summary truncated for context management ...]

---

# Document 149: Extend Google Workspace Studio  |  Google Workspace add-ons  |  Google for Developers

Source URL: https://developers.google.com/workspace/add-ons/studio
Product area: workspace-studio
Original file: docs/extend-google-workspace-studio-google-workspace-add-ons-google-for-developers-d77a70c9.md

# Extend Google Workspace Studio — Summary

**Source:** Google for Developers — Google Workspace add-ons  
**URL:** https://developers.google.com/workspace/add-ons/studio  
**Last updated:** 2026-04-20 UTC  
**License:** Content under Creative Commons Attribution 4.0; code samples under Apache 2.0.

---

## Purpose of the Guide

These guides explain how developers can **extend Google Workspace Studio** by building **custom steps** that can be run inside Workspace Studio flows.

> “These guides explain how to extend the functionality of Google Workspace Studio by building custom steps that flows can run.”

### Recommended starting point

To begin, Google recommends the quickstart:

- [Build a calculator step with Apps Script](https://developers.google.com/workspace/add-ons/studio/quickstart-calculator)
- “Try the quickstart” link points to the same guide.

---

## What Workspace Studio Flows Do

**Flows** let Google Workspace users automate tasks across services by combining steps **without writing code**.

> “Flows let Google Workspace users automate tasks across services by combining a series of steps without writing any code.”

Developers can extend flows by exposing their app’s functionality as flow steps:

> “By extending flows, you let users add your app's functions as steps.”

---

## Example Flow: Customer Question Triage

A sample flow for handling customer emails:

1. Starts when an email from a customer is received.
2. Prompts Gemini to triage the email.
3. Creates a task for follow-up with the sales or support team.

The page includes a figure showing a user configuring a flow that triages customer questions with Gemini.

> **Figure 1:** A user configures a flow that triages customer questions with Gemini.

---

## Workspace Studio Concepts

### Flows

Users build **flows** in Workspace Studio to automate tasks in Google Workspace and beyond.

Key traits:

- Deep system integration
- Contextual awareness
- Can optionally use AI

> “Flows have deep system integration, contextual awareness, and can optionally use AI.”

---

### Step

A **step** is a single task in a flow’s automated process.

Important details:

- Represents one task in a sequence.
- Follows a starting event.
- Runs **synchronously**:
  - Each step completes before the next step begins.
- Users determine the order of steps.
- Steps can have inputs and outputs, but they are not required.

Examples of steps:

- “send an email”
- “post in a Chat space”
- “ask Gemini”
- Tasks outside Google Workspace, such as creating a CRM lead

> “Each step runs synchronously, meaning it completes its operation before the next step in the sequence begins.”

---

### Input Variable

An **input variable** is received by a step.

Key points:

- Set by the user on a step’s configuration card.
- Used while configuring the step.
- Examples:
  - Email address
  - Datetime
  - Gemini prompt

> “Input variables are received by steps.”

---

### Output Variable

An **output variable** is returned by a step and can be passed to another step.

Example use case:

- A step outputs an email address.
- Another step uses that email address as the recipient of an email.

> “Output variables are returned by steps, and can be sent to another step.”

---

### Dynamic Variable

A **dynamic variable** is a variable whose data can only be determined when the user configures the flow.

Example:

- Google Forms can have varying questions and answers.
- The number and content of those questions and answers are unknown until a specific Form starts a flow.

> “Dynamic variables account for this case.”

---

### Custom Resource

A **custom resource** is a custom data structure used to group multiple variables together.

Example:

- To create a CRM lead, pass a custom resource containing:
  - Email address
  - Street address
  - Name

> “A custom data structure that you can define to group multiple variables together.”

---

### Card

A **card** is a building block for user interfaces in add-ons.

Cards support:

- Defined layouts
- Interactive UI elements, such as buttons
- Rich media, such as images

Cards include special features for building flows:

- `IncludeVariables`: A property that enables dynamic variable inclusion.
- `Type`: Defines the type of data that input variables expect.

---

### Activity Log

An **activity log** describes what happens when a flow runs.

By default:

- Activity logs include the name of the starter or step.
- These names are statically defined in the manifest.

Developers can also provide customized activity logs.

> “Describes what happens when an flow runs.”

---

## What You Can Build

Workspace Studio flows are built on the **Google Workspace add-ons platform**.

If you already have an existing add-on:

- You can extend it to include flows.
- This is done by updating the add-on manifest to include a flow-specific section.

> “If you already have an existing add-on, you can extend its functionality to include Flows by updating its manifest to 

[... summary truncated for context management ...]

---

# Extend the Editors with Google Workspace add-ons Stay organized with collections Save and categorize content based on your preferences.

![Spark icon](/_static/images/icons/spark.svg)

## Page Summary

outlined\_flag

- Google Workspace add-ons extend Google Docs, Sheets, and Slides with custom interfaces to enhance productivity and automate tasks, saving users time and effort.
- These add-ons allow integration with third-party systems and the creation of Homepage, REST API, and Link preview interfaces directly within the Editors.
- Add-ons are built using Card service and configured using a manifest file, with specific sections to enable Editor functionality.
- They are not supported on mobile clients and offer functionalities such as building Editor interfaces and defining Editor actions.

Google Docs, Google Sheets, and Google Slides provide a cloud-based
productivity solution with real-time collaboration. However, modifying and
surfacing relevant information in the editors is often a time-consuming task.

You can save your users time and effort by extending Docs,
Sheets, and Slides with add-ons.
add-ons for Google Forms isn't available yet.

When you build a Google Workspace add-on, you can
define custom interfaces that are
inserted directly into the Editors. These interfaces help automate tasks,
present additional information to the user, or let the user interact with a
third-party system without having to switch to a new browser tab.

With Google Workspace add-ons, you can create the following Editor interfaces:

- Homepage interfaces
- REST API interfaces
- Link preview interfaces

**Note:** You can't use Google Workspace add-ons for
Editors on mobile clients.

## See what you can make

Google Workspace add-ons are built using the
[Card service](/apps-script/reference/card-service/card-service).
See
[Build add-ons](/workspace/add-ons/how-tos/building-workspace-addons)
for an overview.

Google Workspace add-on behavior is configured using a
[manifest](/workspace/add-ons/concepts/workspace-manifests).
To enable Google Workspace add-on for Editors, include
Editor-specific sections.

When configuring your add-on for
editors, you must decide what
interfaces to create for your add-on and what
actions it can take. See the following guides for more information:

- [Build Google editor
  interfaces](/workspace/add-ons/editors/gsao/building-editor-interfaces)
- [Editor actions](/workspace/add-ons/editors/gsao/editor-actions)
- [Manifest structure for
  add-ons](/workspace/add-ons/concepts/workspace-manifests#manifest_structure_for_g_suite_add-ons)
- Try a sample:
  - [Plan travels with an AI agent accessible across Google Workspace](/workspace/add-ons/samples/travel-concierge)
  - [Build Gemini Enterprise agents that are tightly integrated with Google Workspace data stores, APIs, and add-ons](//codelabs.developers.google.com/ge-gws-agents)
  - [Build Vertex AI agents that are tightly integrated with Google Workspace data stores, APIs, and add-ons](//codelabs.developers.google.com/vertexai-gws-agents)

# Document 151: Fact-check statements with an ADK AI agent and Gemini model  |  Apps Script  |  Google for Developers

Source URL: https://developers.google.com/apps-script/samples/custom-functions/fact-check
Product area: apps-script-ai
Original file: docs/fact-check-statements-with-an-adk-ai-agent-and-gemini-model-apps-script-google-for-d-37d81ba6.md

# Fact-check statements with an ADK AI agent and Gemini model — Markdown Summary

**Source:** Google for Developers — Apps Script sample  
**URL:** https://developers.google.com/apps-script/samples/custom-functions/fact-check  
**Last updated:** 2026-04-20 UTC  
**Coding level:** Advanced  
**Duration:** 30 minutes  
**Project type:** [Custom function](https://developers.google.com/apps-script/guides/sheets/functions)

---

## Overview

This guide shows how to create a **Google Sheets custom function** named `FACT_CHECK` using a **container-bound Google Apps Script project**, powered by:

- A **Vertex AI Agent Engine ADK agent** for reasoning.
- A **Gemini model from Vertex AI** for output formatting and text generation.

The function lets users fact-check statements directly in Google Sheets.

> “A fact-check custom function for Google Sheets to be used as a bound Google Apps Script project powered by a Vertex AI agent and Gemini model.”

The sample demonstrates two AI capabilities inside Sheets:

1. **AI agents** for sophisticated, multi-tool, multi-step reasoning using ADK agents deployed in Vertex AI Agent Engine.
2. **AI models** for advanced understanding, generation, and summarization using Gemini models from Vertex AI.

---

## What the Solution Does

The custom function analyzes a statement, grounds the response using recent web information, and returns the result in the requested format.

### Function Name

```text
FACT_CHECK
```

### Usage

```text
=FACT_CHECK("Your statement here")
```

Returns a concise, summarized fact-check result.

```text
=FACT_CHECK("Your statement here", "Your output formatting instructions here")
```

Returns a fact-check result formatted according to the second argument.

### AI Components

- **Reasoning:** [LLM Auditor ADK AI Agent — Python sample](https://github.com/google/adk-samples/tree/main/python/agents/llm-auditor)
- **Output formatting:** [Gemini model](https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/generative-ai/docs/models)

### API Access

The Apps Script custom function calls Vertex AI REST APIs using:

```text
UrlFetchApp
```

Reference: [UrlFetchApp](https://developers.google.com/apps-script/reference/url-fetch/url-fetch-app)

---

## Objectives

By following the guide, you learn how to:

- Understand what the solution does.
- Understand how the solution is implemented.
- Deploy the Vertex AI agent.
- Set up the Apps Script project.
- Run and test the custom function in Google Sheets.

---

## Architecture

The solution uses both **Google Workspace** and **Google Cloud** resources.

At a high level:

- Google Sheets hosts the custom function.
- Apps Script executes the custom function logic.
- Apps Script calls Vertex AI REST APIs.
- Vertex AI Agent Engine hosts the deployed **LLM Auditor ADK agent**.
- Gemini formats and summarizes the final response.
- A Google Cloud service account authenticates API access.

Architecture image referenced in the guide:

```text
https://developers.google.com/static/apps-script/samples/images/fact-check-flow.png
```

---

## Prerequisites

You need:

- A Google Account.
  - Google Workspace accounts may require administrator approval.
- A web browser with internet access.
- Prerequisites for the LLM Auditor ADK agent:
  - **Python 3.11+**
  - **Python Poetry**
  - **Google Cloud CLI**

Relevant setup links:

- Python: https://docs.python.org/3/using/index.html
- Poetry: https://python-poetry.org/docs/
- Google Cloud CLI: https://cloud.google.com/sdk/docs/install

---

# Environment Preparation

The environment setup includes:

1. Creating a Google Cloud project.
2. Enabling billing.
3. Enabling Vertex AI API.
4. Creating a service account.
5. Creating a private key.

---

## 1. Create a Google Cloud Project

### Using Google Cloud Console

1. Go to **IAM & Admin > Create a Project**.
2. Enter a descriptive project name.
3. Optionally edit the **Project ID**.
   - The project ID cannot be changed after creation.
4. Choose a project location.
5. Click **Create**.

Direct link:

```text
https://console.cloud.google.com/projectcreate
```

### Using gcloud CLI

Use Cloud Shell or a local shell with Google Cloud CLI installed and initialized.

```bash
gcloud projects create PROJECT_ID
```

Replace:

- `PROJECT_ID` with the desired Cloud project ID.

---

## 2. Enable Billing for the Cloud Project

### Using Google Cloud Console

1. Go to **Billing > My Projects**.
2. Select the organization associated with the project.
3. In the project row, open **Actions > Change billing**.
4. Choose the Cloud Billing account.
5. Click **Set account**.

Direct link:

```text
https://console.cloud.google.com/billing/projects
```

### Using gcloud CLI

List available billing accounts:

```bash
gcloud billing accounts list
```

Link a billing account to the project:

```bash
gcloud billing projects link PROJECT_ID --billing-account=BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID
```

Replace:

- `PROJECT_ID` with the Cloud project ID.
- `BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID` with the billing account ID.

# Document 152: Find information in faster & easier ways with AI Overviews in Google Search - Computer - Google Search Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/websearch/answer/14901683
Product area: search-ai
Original file: docs/find-information-in-faster-easier-ways-with-ai-overviews-in-google-search-computer-g-927ef31b.md

# Summary: AI Overviews in Google Search (Computer)

**Source:** Google Search Help — “Find information in faster & easier ways with AI Overviews in Google Search”  
**URL:** https://support.google.com/websearch/answer/14901683

---

## Key Excerpts & Important Notices

> **Important:** We’re currently updating settings for Google Search services, which include Search, Maps, Shopping, Flights, Hotels, Translate and News. The settings described in this article are being updated so may not match your current experience.

> Find what you're looking for faster and easier with AI Overviews in search results. AI Overviews can take the work out of searching by providing an AI-generated snapshot with key information and links to dig deeper.

> **Important:** AI responses may include mistakes.

> AI Overviews are a core Google Search feature, like knowledge panels. Features cannot be turned off.

> AI Overviews use generative AI, which is a type of artificial intelligence that learns patterns and structures from the data it is trained on and uses that to create something new.

> While exciting, this technology is rapidly evolving and improving, and may provide inaccurate or offensive information. AI Overviews can and will make mistakes.

---

## What AI Overviews Are

**AI Overviews** are AI-generated summaries that may appear in Google Search results when Google determines generative AI can be especially helpful.

They are designed to:

- Provide a quick snapshot of key information.
- Pull together information from a range of sources.
- Include links so users can explore further.
- Help users understand topics faster and with less manual searching.

Google is gradually expanding AI Overviews to more users, languages, and regions.

---

## Availability

AI Overviews are currently available to **all users** in many listed countries and territories across:

- **Americas**
- **Asia-Pacific**
- **Europe, the Middle East & Africa**

They are also available in many languages, including:

- Afrikaans
- Akan
- Albanian
- Amharic
- Arabic
- Armenian
- Azerbaijani
- Basque
- Belarusian
- Bengali
- Bosnian
- Bulgarian
- Burmese
- Catalan
- Cebuano
- Chinese Simplified
- Chinese Traditional
- Croatian
- Czech
- Danish
- Dutch
- English
- Estonian
- Faroese
- Filipino
- Finnish
- French
- Galician
- German
- Georgian
- Greek
- Gujarati
- Hausa
- Hebrew
- Hindi
- Hungarian
- Icelandic
- Indonesian
- Irish
- Italian
- Japanese
- Kannada
- Kazakh
- Khmer
- Kinyarwanda
- Korean
- Kurdish
- Kyrgyz
- Lao
- Latvian
- Lithuanian
- Macedonian
- Malay
- Malayalam
- Maltese
- Maori
- Marathi
- Mongolian
- Nepali
- Norwegian
- Odia

- Urdu
- Uzbek
- Vietnamese
- Welsh
- Western Frisian
- Wolof
- Yoruba
- Zulu

AI Overviews may also appear when users:

- **Search with an image**
- Use **Circle to Search**

This applies in countries, territories, and languages where AI Overviews are supported.

---

## Can AI Overviews Be Turned Off?

No. Google states that AI Overviews are a **core Google Search feature**, similar to knowledge panels.

However, users can choose the **Web filter** after performing a search.

The **Web filter** shows only text-based links and removes features such as:

- AI Overviews
- Knowledge panels
- Other rich Search features

---

## How AI Overviews Work

AI Overviews use **generative AI**, which learns patterns and structures from training data and produces new responses.

Google warns that AI Overviews may:

- Be inaccurate
- Be offensive
- Contain mistakes
- Need independent verification

### How to Double-Check AI Overview Responses

Google recommends:

1. **Check important information in more than one place**
   - Click supporting web links.
   - Review other Google Search results.
   - Evaluate sources carefully.

2. **Ask multiple versions of your question**
   - Rephrase the query.
   - Ask for different opinions.
   - Ask for more detail.

3. **Send feedback to Google**
   - Feedback helps improve AI Overviews for everyone.

---

## Continue from AI Overview into AI Mode

Users can transition from an AI Overview into a deeper conversation in **AI Mode**.

This feature is available on **mobile devices** in all regions and languages where both AI Overviews and AI Mode are supported.

### How It Works

1. Select **Show more** at the bottom of the AI Overview.
2. Type a follow-up question in the **Ask anything** bar.
3. Continue the conversation in **AI Mode**.

The context from the original search carries over, so users do not need to start again.

### Returning to Search Results

To return to the Google Search results page with the AI Overview:

- Select **Close**.
- Or long press and swipe inward from either side of

[... summary truncated for context management ...]

# Summary: AI Overviews in Google Search (Google Search Help)

**Source:** Google Search Help — “Find information in faster & easier ways with AI Overviews in Google Search”  
**URL:** https://support.google.com/websearch/answer/13572151

---

## Key Excerpts / Important Notices

> **Important:** We’re currently updating settings for Google Search services, which include Search, Maps, Shopping, Flights, Hotels, Translate and News. The settings described in this article are being updated so may not match your current experience.

> **Important:** AI responses may include mistakes.

> AI Overviews are a core Google Search feature, like knowledge panels. Features cannot be turned off.

> The feedback you submit includes your most recent search query and its search results.

---

## What AI Overviews Are

**AI Overviews** are AI-generated summaries shown in Google Search results when Google’s systems determine that generative AI may be especially helpful.

They are intended to help users:

- Find information **faster and more easily**
- Get a **snapshot of key information**
- Access **links to supporting sources** for deeper research
- Quickly understand information gathered from a range of sources

Google is gradually expanding AI Overviews to more users, languages, and regions.

---

## Availability

AI Overviews are currently available to **all users** in many supported **countries, territories, and languages**.

### Supported Regions

AI Overviews are available across broad regions including:

- **Americas**
  - Includes the United States, Canada, Mexico, Brazil, Argentina, Chile, Colombia, Peru, many Caribbean nations and territories, and others.
- **Asia-Pacific**
  - Includes India, Japan, Korea, Australia, New Zealand, Indonesia, Philippines, Singapore, Vietnam, Taiwan, Hong Kong, Pakistan, Bangladesh, and many Pacific island territories.
- **Europe, the Middle East & Africa**
  - Includes the United Kingdom, Germany, France, Italy, Spain, Netherlands, Sweden, Switzerland, Türkiye, Saudi Arabia, UAE, South Africa, Nigeria, Kenya, Egypt, Israel, and many others.

### Supported Languages

AI Overviews are available in a wide range of languages, including:

- Afrikaans, Akan, Albanian, Amharic, Arabic, Armenian, Azerbaijani
- Basque, Belarusian, Bengali, Bosnian, Bulgarian, Burmese
- Catalan, Cebuano, Chinese Simplified, Chinese Traditional, Croatian, Czech
- Danish, Dutch, English, Estonian, Faroese, Filipino, Finnish, French
- Galician, German, Georgian, Greek, Gujarati, Hausa, Hebrew, Hindi
- Hungarian, Icelandic, Indonesian, Irish, Italian, Japanese, Kannada
- Kazakh, Khmer, Kinyarwanda, Korean, Kurdish, Kyrgyz, Lao
- Latvian, Lithuanian, Macedonian, Malay, Malayalam, Maltese, Maori
- Marathi, Mongolian, Nepali, Norwegian, Odia, Oromo, Pashto
- Persian / Farsi, Polish, Portuguese, Punjabi, Quechua, Romanian
- Romansh, Russian, Serbian, Serbian Latin, Sindhi, Sinhala, Slovak
- Slovenian, Somali, Southern Sotho, Spanish, Swahili, Swedish
- Tajik, Tamil, Telugu, Thai, Tswana, Turkish, Turkmen, Ukrainian
- Urdu, Uzbek, Vietnamese, Welsh, Western Frisian, Wolof, Yoruba, Zulu

---

## AI Overviews with Image Search and Circle to Search

AI Overviews may also appear when users:

- **Search with an image**
- Use **Circle to Search**

This applies in countries, territories, and languages where AI Overviews are supported.

---

## Can You Turn Off AI Overviews?

No. Google states that AI Overviews are a **core Search feature**, similar to knowledge panels, and **cannot be turned off**.

However, users can choose the **Web filter** after performing a search.

### Web Filter

The **Web filter** displays only text-based links and removes Search features such as:

- AI Overviews
- Other enhanced Search result features

---

## How AI Overviews Work

AI Overviews use **generative AI**, a type of artificial intelligence that learns patterns and structures from training data and uses them to generate new content.

Google emphasizes that this technology is still evolving and may produce:

- Inaccurate information
- Offensive information
- Mistakes or misleading responses

---

## How to Double-Check AI Overview Responses

Google recommends users verify important information rather than relying solely on AI Overviews.

### Recommended Checks

- **Check important information in more than one place**
  - Open the supporting links shown in the AI Overview
  - Compare with other Google Search results
- **Ask multiple versions of your question**
  - Try rephrasing
  - Ask for different opinions
  - Ask for more detail
- **Send feedback**
  - Report anything that appears incorrect, biased, unhelpful, or problematic

---

## Continue from AI Overview into AI Mode

Users can transition from an AI Overview into a deeper conversational experience in **AI Mode**.

### Availability

This feature is available on **mobile devices** in regions and languages where both:

- AI Overviews
- AI Mode

are supported.

1. Sel

# Document 154: Find times to meet in Google Calendar - Computer - Google Calendar Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/calendar/answer/6294878
Product area: calendar
Original file: docs/find-times-to-meet-in-google-calendar-computer-google-calendar-help-2d725700.md

# Find times to meet in Google Calendar

> Original page: [Google Calendar Help – Computer](https://support.google.com/calendar/answer/6294878)  
> Platforms covered: **Computer** | Android | iPhone & iPad

---

## Gemini integration (tip)

- If you have an eligible Google Workspace plan, you can use **Gemini in Google Calendar** to help find a meeting time.  
  [Learn how to let Gemini find times to meet](https://support.google.com/calendar/answer/16690875).

---

## Find someone else’s calendar

1. Open [Google Calendar](https://www.google.com/calendar) on your computer.
2. On the left, click **Search for people**.
3. Enter the person’s name.

---

## Find a time when someone is available

Use this method to compare your calendar with guests who have **shared their calendars with you**.

1. Open [Google Calendar](https://www.google.com/calendar).
2. Top left, click **Create** → **Event**.
3. Add guests to your event.
4. Click **Find a time**.
5. *(Optional)* To adjust the view, click **Filter and view** ⋮:
   - **Show or hide rooms:** Click **Show rooms**.
   - **Filter guests:** Select **No guests**, **Required guests**, or **All guests**.
6. Locate an open time slot across your calendar and your guests’ calendars.

**Tips for room scheduling:**

- To check a room’s availability:
  1. Click **Add rooms** or **Add room booking**.
  2. Choose a room.
- If a room is selected but you can’t see its schedule, click *Filter and view* ⋮ → **Show rooms**.

---

## Compare calendars in “Day” view

Use this persistent setting to see calendars side by side (separate columns) instead of overlapped.

1. Open [Google Calendar](https://www.google.com/calendar).
2. At the top right, click **Settings menu** ⚙️ → **Settings**.
3. On the left, click **View options**.
4. Enable the checkbox **“View calendars side by side in Day View”**.
5. Switch your main calendar to **Day** view.

---

## Requirements for finding someone’s calendar

- The person’s calendar must be **shared publicly** or **shared within your organisation**.
- If they have not shared with you, you can send a request; after they share, you’ll get an email confirmation.
- People sharing their calendar can control:
  - **What information** others can see about their events.
  - **Permissions** others have.  
  → [Learn how to change event visibility settings](https://support.google.com/calendar/answer/34580).

---

## Related resources

- [Let Gemini in Google Calendar find times to meet](https://support.google.com/calendar/answer/16690875)
- [Change the visibility of events or tasks on your calendar](https://support.google.com/calendar/answer/34580)

---

## Give feedback / Need more help?

- **Post to the help community** – [Get answers from community members](https://support.google.com/calendar/community?hl=en&help_center_link=CN6agAMSMEZpbmQgdGltZXMgdG8gbWVldCBpbiBHb29nbGUgQ2FsZW5kYXIgLSBDb21wdXRlcg)
- Link to privacy policy, terms of service, etc. (as on original page)

---

# Document 156: Frequently asked questions - NotebookLM Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/notebooklm/answer/16269187?hl=en&ref_topic=16179690
Product area: notebooklm
Original file: docs/frequently-asked-questions-notebooklm-help-f3fa8122.md

# Frequently asked questions - NotebookLM Help

## Mobile App
- The early version of the app includes many core features of NotebookLM and is designed to help you understand anything, anywhere. [Learn more about the NotebookLM mobile app](https://support.google.com/notebooklm/answer/16296687).

## Access Issues

### General Access
- Verify your age in your Google Account before accessing NotebookLM. [Learn how to access age-restricted content and features](https://support.google.com/accounts/answer/10071085).
- Check if your country is supported in the [180+ regions where Gemini app is available](https://support.google.com/gemini/answer/13575153).

### Work or School Email
> Businesses, schools and universities, organizations, and enterprise users can access higher limits and expanded features with enterprise-grade data protection through:
> - [Google Workspace](https://workspace.google.com/solutions/ai/)
> - [Google Workspace for Education](https://edu.google.com/intl/ALL_us/workspace-for-education/editions/compare-editions/)
> - [Google Cloud](https://cloud.google.com/resources/notebooklm-enterprise)

- If you can't access with a work or school email, learn how to [turn additional Google services on or off](https://support.google.com/a/answer/181865?hl=en#zippy=%2Cturn-services-on-or-off-for-users). Work with your workspace admin to turn on NotebookLM.
- [Learn more about using NotebookLM with a work or school Google account](https://support.google.com/notebooklm/answer/16337734).

## Questions Not Answered
NotebookLM may not answer because of:

- **Safety flags**: The source content contains language that triggers a safety flag. This occurs with sensitive topics like violence, sexuality, or obscenity, even in historical contexts.
- **Unclear phrases**: When your notebook has many sources, refine your question to help NotebookLM locate the most relevant pieces of information.
  - **Tip:** Make your question more precise and understandable.
- **Information not in sources**: NotebookLM only answers based on the information provided in your uploaded sources. If the answer isn't in the source material, it won't respond.

## Reporting Offensive Responses
1. Select thumbs down (⊖).
2. Choose **Offensive/unsafe**.
3. Click **Submit**.

We’ll review the report and take appropriate actions to improve the safety of our model.

## Notebook Sharing Icons
- **Lock icon**: Notebook is private (shared with no one).
- **Shared icon**: Notebook shared individually with users.
- **Globe icon**: Notebook is publicly shared.

## Reporting Infringed Rights in a Shared Notebook
If you find content that violates the law:
- Open the settings menu in the notebook and select **Report Notebook**, or visit this [direct link](https://support.google.com/legal/troubleshooter/1114905).
- We’ll review and consider blocking, limiting, or removing access.
- **Tip:** You can also report through [report content on Google](https://support.google.com/legal/troubleshooter/1114905).

## Data Privacy
> Your data will never be shared by NotebookLM.

[Learn how NotebookLM protects your data](https://support.google.com/notebooklm/answer/16179536).

## Usage Limits
- **Notebooks**: up to 100
- **Sources per notebook**: up to 50, each up to 500,000 words
- **Daily limits**:
  - 50 chat queries
  - 3 audio generations

[Learn more about upgrading NotebookLM to increase your limits](https://support.google.com/notebooklm/answer/16213268).

## Source File Limits
- Per source: 500,000 words or up to 200 MB for local uploads. There is no page limit.

## Source Import Failures
Sources can’t be imported if:
- It exceeds the word limit (500,000) or file size limit (200 MB).
- The original PDF file is copy-protected.

## How Data Is Used in Prompts
- **Notes**: Used only when you specifically select a note.
- **Sources**: Always used, either the entire set or the subset you select.
- **Conversation history**: Used to generate responses.

## Citations
If your source content is too short, NotebookLM may reference the entire document without citing individual text from your source.

## Recovering Deleted Notes
Currently, there is no way to recover deleted notes.

## Duplicating a Notebook
This feature isn’t supported yet.

## Still Have Questions?
- General users: send your questions to `notebooklm-user-support@google.com`.
- Workspace users: [contact Google Workspace support](https://support.google.com/a/answer/1047213).

---

# Document 157: From brainstorming to building teams: FinQuery innovates with Gemini for Google Workspace

Source URL: https://workspace.google.com/blog/customer-stories/finquery-innovates-gemini-google-workspace?hl=en
Product area: workspace-site
Original file: docs/from-brainstorming-to-building-teams-finquery-innovates-with-gemini-for-google-w-5eac5036.md

# From brainstorming to building teams: FinQuery innovates with Gemini for Google Workspace

Source: https://workspace.google.com/blog/customer-stories/finquery-innovates-gemini-google-workspace?hl=en

Customer Stories

# From brainstorming to building teams: FinQuery innovates with Gemini for Google Workspace

June 21, 2024

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/FinQuery_hero.max-2500x2500.jpg](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/FinQuery_hero.max-2500x2500.jpg)

##### Amanda Taylor

Vice President of Infrastructure, FinQuery

##### Andrew Larson

CTO, FinQuery

##### Google Workspace Newsletter

Keep up with the evolving future of work and collaboration with insights, trends, and product news.

[SIGN UP](https://workspace.google.com/?source=gafb-blog_article-body-en#newsletter)

When your company’s mission is focused on simplifying people’s lives with technology, you have to be sure your employees are reaping the same benefits you promise your customers. At [FinQuery](https://finquery.com/), we've created a platform that gives organizations total visibility into financial accounting, contracts, and software subscriptions to see where they’re spending their money. As an organization, cost management and efficiency are in our DNA, and we want to help our people do their best work. Our company is also remote-first, so effective digital collaboration is critical for our success and an invaluable tool for attracting top tech talent.

We initially adopted [Google Workspace](https://workspace.google.com/) because it's a best-in-class, all-in-one solution for email management, content development, collaboration, and video conferencing. Having an all-inclusive and easy-to-use collaboration solution makes us more attractive to quality talent looking to us as a potential employer. Plus, most of our employees are already proficient with it on day one.

At the end of 2023, we enabled [Gemini for Google Workspace](https://workspace.google.com/solutions/ai/) and started to casually experiment with its features. Gemini did everything from providing the framework for playful internal emails that read like Shakespearean poems to [generating fun backdrops for Google Meet](https://support.google.com/meet/answer/10058482?hl=en&co=GENIE.Platform%3DAndroid). It also helped us create internal team logos, based on inside jokes, to help liven up weekly sprint demos for our engineering teams. All this lightweight experimentation was great, but we were ready to apply Gemini’s power to our day-to-day work.

### Looking for “the sparkle” in everyday workflows

As an early adopter of Gemini, we understood that AI was quickly becoming a cornerstone  for successful companies, so we were excited to fully implement the tool in our processes for next-level productivity. Today, everyone at FinQuery knows to look for the Gemini sparkle when we’re drafting a document or composing an email.

We frequently have group brainstorming sessions, and it’s 20 times harder to start with a blank page than to start with something on it. Gemini helps us generate ideas to kick off the process, and then it builds on our ideas by highlighting additional information relevant to the topic. When we’re not in meetings, many of us spend a lot of our day in our Gmail inboxes, where Gemini is giving us time back by helping to draft responses faster. As the VP of Infrastructure, Amanda Taylor estimates that she’s **getting back 20% of the time spent writing emails**.

Amanda also uses Gemini as a coach or personal assistant. She can tell it about a business problem she’s facing, and it helps to brainstorm solutions. These can be as simple as helping to reframe the tone or refine the language in an email, or as complex as helping the business enablement team develop massive cross-organizational project plans. These project plans typically take months of work and hours of individual effort to consolidate, but with Gemini, the team now manages to complete a draft in just a matter of minutes, using previous plans as inputs. After 15 minutes of refining the content and formatting, an idea could be ready to share across the company.

### Solving complex problems for technical teams with Gemini

While all of our daily tasks in Workspace have become more efficient with Gemini, we’ve also found applications for generative AI to be of assistance in the more complex areas of our business. Our engineering teams are able to utilize Gemini to debug and troubleshoot their code. Teams can ask onboarding questions as they’re learning new systems, integrations, or tools; it’s like having an engineering mentor built into their computer.

Recently, we were tasked with evaluating new monitoring and observability tools that help us keep closer tabs on the health and security of our engineering infrastructure. A task like this should have taken us days of research and careful comparison, but we faced urgency. The team called in Gemini, and with a quick prompt, it sifted through the most popular tools on the market along with pros, cons, and key features to consider. With this blueprint, we were able to craft a clear, succinct one-pager that was practically ready to share with our CTO. Once we selected our tool, we were able to launch directly into system designs with Gemini’s assistance.

Gemini is now front and center for all of our work, and it helps everyone at FinQuery to be more productive, collaborative, and responsive. It’s only going to increase our efficiency  as we use it, so we’re excited to continue as a  leader of the generative AI charge in fintech.

- [Productivity and Collaboration](https://workspace.google.com/blog/productivity-collaboration)

# Document 158: Fullstory writes collaboration into its culture with Google Workspace and Gemini

Source URL: https://workspace.google.com/blog/customer-stories/fullstory-collaboration-culture-with-google-workspace-and-gemini?hl=en
Product area: workspace-site
Original file: docs/fullstory-writes-collaboration-into-its-culture-with-google-workspace-and-gemini-e5302d96.md

# Fullstory writes collaboration into its culture with Google Workspace and Gemini

Source: https://workspace.google.com/blog/customer-stories/fullstory-collaboration-culture-with-google-workspace-and-gemini?hl=en

Customer Stories

# Fullstory writes collaboration into its culture with Google Workspace and Gemini

May 8, 2025

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Fullstory_Banner_resize.max-2500x2500.png](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Fullstory_Banner_resize.max-2500x2500.png)

##### Forest Donovan

Senior Director of Strategic Alliances, Fullstory

### The mission and the challenge

*Are you getting the full story from your customer data?*

Digital marketing is all about seeing the bigger picture, and that picture starts with data. At [Fullstory](https://www.fullstory.com/), we help brands harness insights from their customer interactions to understand their behaviors and build more personalized experiences. In other words, we help them see the full story in their data and use it to its full potential.

We also recognize the trust that our customers put in us to keep their user data safe. From the customer solutions we build to our internal team collaboration tools, security is essential to our work. That’s one of many reasons we’ve relied on [Google Workspace](https://workspace.google.com/intl/en_ca/) since day one. With its integrated communication and collaboration tools, Workspace gives us a secure, central place to do our work and the tools to better serve our customers.

As generative AI has expanded across industries, we also faced increasing pressure from leadership to explore how AI could augment our work. But rather than letting employees experiment on their own with unapproved tools that could jeopardize our data security, we wanted to implement the new technology in a safe and productive way. As part of Workspace, [Gemini](https://workspace.google.com/solutions/ai/) gave us the trust and control we needed to feel comfortable deploying generative AI to all of our users.

![Video Thumbnail](https://img.youtube.com/vi/GvPl_8jWqcE/hqdefault.jpg)

Learn how Fullstory uses Google Workspace and Gemini to accelerate creativity and innovation without compromising on data security.

### Using Google Workspace and Gemini

We use the full suite of tools in Workspace and continue to embrace new solutions as they’re available. The majority of our collaborative design and writing happens within [Google Docs](https://workspace.google.com/products/docs/), giving us a single source of truth for files and removing the need for many versions. We share new ideas and present customers’ stories using [Google Slides](https://workspace.google.com/intl/en_ca/products/slides/), which allows us to visually present information in engaging ways. We then analyze and organize data within [Google Sheets](https://workspace.google.com/intl/en_ca/products/sheets/) so everyone has visibility into important insights. As a distributed workforce, we also rely heavily on [Google Meet](https://workspace.google.com/products/meet/) to empower our teams and customers to get things done, no matter where they are.

We also go to great lengths to ensure that our data remains in the Workspace platform at all times. We can define granular policies around how information and files are shared and viewed to guarantee that every piece of data in [Google Drive](https://workspace.google.com/products/drive/) is protected and version-controlled at all times. We can also adjust those policies as our business needs evolve. Only Google can bring this level of deep integration between products, and we’d have a hard time trusting any other vendor with our data.

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/unnamed_KZCwcMh.max-1400x1400.png](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/unnamed_KZCwcMh.max-1400x1400.png)

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/unnamed_KZCwcMh.max-1400x1400.png](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/unnamed_KZCwcMh.max-1400x1400.png)

Fullstory uses Gemini together with Google Vids to quickly create compelling video content to share with clients and prospects.

Our security-minded culture played a big role in our adoption of Gemini. As we explored our options for generative AI tools, we had three core criteria. First, we needed to be absolutely sure that our data was protected and contained within our environment. Second, the solution needed to be easy to use and provide tangible improvements to productivity. Lastly, the chosen AI tool needed to gain alignment across our legal, compliance, security, and IT teams. As an integrated part of Workspace, Gemini effectively met all of these requirements.

We now use Gemini across Workspace applications to enhance our productivity and improve our employee experience. All of our data is already there in Workspace, making it safe and easy for Gemini to ingest. For example, Gemini can take meeting notes and summarize them into a structured format that we can turn into a 30-second video script. We can then plug that script into [Google Vids](https://workspace.google.com/intl/en_ca/products/vids/) to create a high-gloss video with full voiceover and imagery that we can share with customers, send to prospects for cold outreach, or use for event promotion and follow-up. Considering that I have zero background in creating or producing a video, it’s amazing that I can pull this off.

Gemini also plugs directly into Fullstory’s behavioral data platform itself, which integrates with an ecosystem of partners via APIs. We already capture all of the data associated with a web or mobile session for our customers and create structure for that data, but with Gemini, we can now use session data to help power more informed and contextually aware AI agents to deliver better customer experiences.

### Looking ahead

Beyond analyzing how our customers’ end users engage with their websites and mobile apps, Fullstory can also provide insights into our own internal use of tools like Workspace. With Gemini, we can see how our employees are using specific Workspace solutions to better understand where we can drive efficiencies and encourage adoption.

We know that Workspace has a significant impact on our ability to communicate and collaborate at Fullstory, internally and externally. We believe Gemini has the power to take that even further in the short and long term. Right now, there’s a lot of buzz around generative AI and people still feel like there’s a bit of magic to it. In the long term, that will help us foster a culture of experimentation and curiosity where people are willing to learn how new tools can support their daily jobs.

Posted in

- [Customer Stories](https://workspace.google.com/blog/customer-stories)
- [AI and Machine Learning](https://workspace.google.com/blog/ai-and-machine-learning)

AI and Machine Learning

### How AI is giving small businesses a major advantage

By Lisa Gevelber • 2-minute read](https://workspace.google.com/blog/ai-and-machine-learning/how-ai-is-giving-small-businesses-a-major-advantage)

[![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Hero_1.max-700x700.jpg](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Hero_1.max-700x700.jpg)

# Document 159: Gemini AI features now included in Google Workspace subscriptions  |  Generative AI  |  Google Workspace Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/a/answer/15756885?hl=en
Product area: admin-console
Original file: docs/gemini-ai-features-now-included-in-google-workspace-subscriptions-generative-ai--76398c81.md

# Gemini AI Features Now Included in Google Workspace Subscriptions

**Source:** Google Workspace Help — “Gemini AI features now included in Google Workspace subscriptions”  
**URL:** https://support.google.com/a/answer/15756885?hl=en  
**Last updated:** **2026-06-11 UTC**

---

## Overview

Google began including new Gemini AI features directly in **Google Workspace Business and Enterprise editions** starting in **January 2025**. These features were previously available through separate **Google Workspace with Gemini add-ons**.

The goal is to improve productivity and innovation across organizations by bringing Gemini-powered AI into Workspace apps and services.

---

## Key Excerpts

> In January 2025 we started adding new AI features designed to improve productivity and innovation in organizations with Google Workspace Business and Enterprise editions. Previously these features were included in Google Workspace with Gemini add-ons.

> Your existing Google Workspace data protections are automatically applied to AI features.

> Generative AI doesn't change these commitments — it reaffirms their importance.

> **Your interactions with Gemini stay within your organization**. Gemini does not share your content outside your organization without your permission.

> **Your existing Google Workspace protections are automatically applied.** Gemini brings the same enterprise-grade security as the rest of Google Workspace.

> **Your content is not used for any other customers.** Your content is not human reviewed or used for Generative AI model training outside your domain without permission.

---

## Add-ons No Longer Available for Purchase

As part of the transition, the following add-ons are no longer available for purchase:

- **Gemini Business–Legacy**
- **Gemini Enterprise–Legacy**
- **AI Meetings and Messaging**
- **AI Security**

### Education Exception

The **Google AI Pro for Education add-on** remains available to educational organizations.

Previously, this was known as:

- **Gemini Education**
- **Gemini Education Premium**

Google recommends reviewing the related [Google Workspace blog announcement](https://workspace.google.com/blog/product-announcements/empowering-businesses-with-AI) for more details.

---

## AI Features Coming to Organizations

Available AI features depend on the organization’s **Google Workspace edition**.

### Highlighted Gemini and AI Features

- **Gemini in the side panel**
  - Available in:
    - Gmail
    - Docs
    - Sheets
    - Slides
    - Drive
    - Chat
  - Provides AI assistance directly in the flow of work.
  - Related help: [Gemini in the side panel](https://support.google.com/a/users/answer/15146419)

- **Help me write in Gmail and Docs**
  - Helps users quickly compose professional emails and documents.
  - Related help: [Help me write](https://support.google.com/a/users/answer/14747862)

- **Take notes for me in Meet**
  - Automatically captures meeting notes so users can focus on the conversation.
  - Related help: [Take notes for me in Meet](https://support.google.com/meet/answer/14754931)

- **Gemini app**
  - Lets users chat with Google’s next-generation AI for:
    - Learning
    - Brainstorming
    - Planning
    - Coding
    - Deep research
    - Data analysis
  - Available on laptop or mobile device.
  - Includes **Gems**, which can be used to build a team of AI experts for repeatable tasks or complex projects.
  - Related admin help: [Gemini app settings](https://knowledge.workspace.google.com/admin/generative-ai/gemini-app/turn-the-gemini-app-on-or-off#advanced)

- **NotebookLM**
  - Helps accelerate team knowledge sharing.
  - Users can upload sources to generate:
    - Instant insights
    - Podcast-like **Audio Overviews**
  - Enhanced version includes:
    - Notebook customization
    - Advanced sharing
    - Usage analytics
    - Increased Audio Overviews
    - Increased queries
    - Increased notebooks
    - Increased sources
  - Related product page: [NotebookLM Plus](https://notebooklm.google/plus)

- **Gemini in AppSheet**
  - Includes:
    - **Gemini for App Creation**
      - Build apps by describing a business process or idea in natural language.
      - Related help: [Gemini for App Creation](https://support.google.com/appsheet/answer/14699210)
    - **Gemini in AppSheet Solutions**
      - Add AI tasks in automations to extract and categorize information from images and documents.
      - Related help: [Gemini in AppSheet Solutions](https://support.google.com/appsheet/answer/16045284)

---

## Check Which Gemini Features Are Included

Google provides edition-specific comparison pages:

- [Business editions — Gemini features](https://knowledge.workspace.google.com/admin/getting-started/editions/compare-business-editions#gemini)
- [Enterprise editions — Gemini features](https://knowledge.workspace.google.com/admin/getting-started/editions/compare-enterprise-editions#gemini)

---

Admins and end use
