# Document 181: Generate a slide with Gemini in Google Slides - Google Docs Editors Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/docs/answer/16961475?hl=en
Product area: slides
Original file: docs/generate-a-slide-with-gemini-in-google-slides-google-docs-editors-help-280bfe25.md

# Generate a Slide with Gemini in Google Slides — Summary

**Source:** Google Docs Editors Help  
**URL:** https://support.google.com/docs/answer/16961475?hl=en

## Overview

Gemini in Google Slides lets eligible users **generate and edit slides using prompts**. It can help build presentations, reuse the visual style of existing presentations, adjust slide images/content, and reference Drive files to create or refine slide content.

---

## Feature Availability

Key requirements and limitations:

- Requires an **eligible Google Workspace or Google AI plan**.
- Available in **Google Workspace Experiments**, a trusted tester program for users on personal accounts.
- Currently available:
  - **Desktop only**
  - **English only**

> “With Gemini in Google Slides, you can generate and edit slides with a prompt. It helps you build your presentation and lets you adjust the images and content on the slide at any time.”

---

## Generate a Slide with Gemini

You can create a new slide from scratch or reuse the visual style of an existing presentation.

### Steps

1. On your computer, open a presentation in [Google Slides](https://docs.google.com/presentation).
2. At the top right, click **Ask Gemini**.
3. In the prompt bar, enter a prompt describing the slide you want to create.
4. Optional:
   - To match another presentation’s visual style:
     1. Click **Match a presentation style** → **Add from Drive**.
     2. Select a presentation.
     3. Click **Add**.
   - To start from scratch:
     - Click **More options** → **Clear history**.
5. When the preview appears:
   - To insert the generated slide as a new slide:
     - Beside **Replace**, click the **Down arrow** → **Insert**.
   - To replace the selected slide:
     - Click **Replace**.

### Important Tips

- **You can only generate one slide at a time.**
- Generated slides can be edited after creation.

---

## Edit Slides with Gemini

Gemini can directly revise the **design or content** of the current slide based on a prompt.

### Example Prompts

> “Simplify this slide with less text and add an image.”

> “Convert to two columns.”

### Steps

1. Open a presentation in [Google Slides](https://docs.google.com/presentation).
2. Click **Ask Gemini** in the top-right corner.
3. In the prompt bar, describe the changes you want.
4. When the updated slide preview appears:
   - To insert it as a new slide:
     - Beside **Replace**, click the **Down arrow** → **Insert**.
   - To replace the selected slide:
     - Click **Replace**.
   - If you don’t want to apply the changes:
     - Click **Close** to close the preview.
     - Or click **Ask Gemini** again to refine your prompt or clarify the requested changes.

---

## Reference Other Files to Create Content

Gemini can use specific files from Drive to write, edit, summarize, or refine slide content.

### Example Prompt

> “Create a summary slide based on @ProjectNotes.”

### Steps

1. In the side panel, click **Add sources** → **Add from Drive**.
   - Alternatively, type `@` and search/select a file from the menu.
2. Select one or more files.
3. Click **Add**.
4. To review files Gemini used:
   - In the right side panel, click **Sources**.

### Source Search Settings

You can choose where Gemini is allowed to proactively search for information related to your prompt. Google links to more information about **source settings** for Gemini in Workspace.

---

## Give Feedback About Gemini in Slides

Google notes that Gemini may not always support a request correctly.

> “Google Workspace with Gemini is constantly learning and may not be able to support your request.”

If a suggestion is inaccurate or unsafe, users can submit feedback to help improve AI-assisted Workspace features.

### Privacy Warning

> “Because feedback may be human readable, do not submit data that contains personal, confidential, or sensitive information.”

### Feedback Steps

1. Below generated content, click:
   - **Good suggestion**, or
   - **Bad suggestion**
2. If selecting **Bad suggestion**, choose the issue and optionally enter more feedback.
3. Click **Submit**.

### General Feedback

To provide general feedback on Gemini in Slides:

- Go to **Help** → **Help Slides improve**

### Legal Issues

To report a legal issue, Google directs users to create a legal request through Google’s legal support page.

---

## Article Feedback Options

The article itself includes feedback categories such as:

- **Inaccurate** — doesn’t match what appears in the product
- **Hard to understand** — unclear or translation is wrong
- **Missing info** — relevant but not comprehensive
- **Irrelevant** — doesn’t match the title or expectations
- **Minor errors** — formatting issues, typos, or broken links
- **Other suggestions** — ideas to improve the content

Users are reminded:

> “Do not share any personal info”

---

## Additional Help Resources

Google provides links to:

- Google Docs Editors Help Center
- Google Docs Editors Community
- Google Workspace Lea

[... summary truncated for context management ...]

---

# Document 182: Generate and validate draw.io diagrams with AI

Source URL: https://www.drawio.com/docs/reference/diagram-generation/
Product area: external_drawio
Original file: docs/generate-and-validate-draw-io-diagrams-with-ai-fcb0e59b.md

# Generate and validate draw.io diagrams with AI

Source: https://www.drawio.com/docs/reference/diagram-generation/

## Overview

draw.io diagram files (`.drawio`) use a well-defined XML format that AI systems can generate and validate programmatically. Two reference documents are provided to help AI models produce correct diagram files.

- **draw.io Style Reference** — Comprehensive guide covering `.drawio` file structure, style properties, shape types, edge routing, colors, HTML labels, layers, groups, and examples.
- **mxfile.xsd** — XML Schema Definition for validating `.drawio` file structure.

## draw.io MCP Server Integration Options

Four approaches provided by the draw.io MCP server, all sharing a common XML generation reference:

| Approach | What it does | Production endpoint |
|----------|--------------|---------------------|
| MCP App Server | Renders diagrams inline in AI chat as an interactive viewer | mcp.draw.io/mcp |
| MCP Tool Server | Opens diagrams in the draw.io editor in browser; supports XML, CSV, and Mermaid | @drawio/mcp on npm |
| Skill + CLI | Generates native .drawio files with optional PNG/SVG/PDF export via draw.io Desktop CLI | Copy skill file to Claude Code |
| Project Instructions | Claude generates draw.io URLs via Python; no installation required | Paste into Claude Project |

All approaches share the same XML generation rules via shared/xml-reference.md covering edge routing, containers, layers, tags, metadata, dark mode, and style properties.

## File Format Overview

A full `.drawio` file is XML:

```
<mxfile>
  <diagram id="page-1" name="Page-1">
    <mxGraphModel dx="0" dy="0" grid="1" gridSize="10" guides="1"
                  tooltips="1" connect="1" arrows="1" fold="1"
                  page="1" pageScale="1" pageWidth="850" pageHeight="1100"
                  math="0" shadow="0">
      <root>
        <mxCell id="0" />
        <mxCell id="1" parent="0" />
        <!-- Diagram elements go here with parent="1" -->
      </root>
    </mxGraphModel>
  </diagram>
</mxfile>
```

### Core Concepts

- Diagram elements are `mxCell` elements
- Shapes use `vertex="1"`
- Connectors use `edge="1"`
- Visual appearance via `style` attribute (semicolon-separated `key=value` pairs)
- Example: `rounded=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;fillColor=#DAE8FC;`

## Simplified Format: mxGraphModel Only

AI may generate only the `<mxGraphModel>` element without `<mxfile>` and `<diagram>` wrappers:

```
<mxGraphModel adaptiveColors="auto">
  <root>
    <mxCell id="0" />
    <mxCell id="1" parent="0" />
    <mxCell id="2" value="Hello" style="rounded=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;" vertex="1" parent="1">
      <mxGeometry x="100" y="100" width="120" height="60" as="geometry" />
    </mxCell>
  </root>
</mxGraphModel>
```

- draw.io accepts both formats; it auto-wraps bare `<mxGraphModel>`
- Recommended for AI when multi-page diagrams are not needed

## File-Level Variables

`<mxfile>` supports a `vars` attribute with JSON key-value pairs. Variables are referenced in labels/tooltips using `%variableName%`. Set `placeholders="1"` on `UserObject` or as a style property for substitution.

## Key Rules for AI Generation

1. Always include the two structural cells — `id="0"` (root container) and `id="1" parent="0"` (default layer)
2. Use uncompressed XML — AI should generate plain XML, not compressed/Base64
3. All IDs must be unique within a diagram
4. Vertices need `vertex="1"`, edges need `edge="1"` — mutually exclusive

## URL Encoding for Draw.io Editor

To open an AI-generated diagram directly in draw.io, compress the XML and pass it via URL hash using `#create` parameter.

## Inline Viewer

To display a diagram inline in a web page without the full editor, use the draw.io viewer library: load `viewer-static.min.js` from CDN, then use `<div class="mxgraph">` with diagram config in `data-mxgraph` attribute.

---

# Document 183: Generate Audio Overview in NotebookLM - NotebookLM Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/notebooklm/answer/16212820?hl=en
Product area: notebooklm
Original file: docs/generate-audio-overview-in-notebooklm-notebooklm-help-532308e2.md

# Generate Audio Overview in NotebookLM — Summary

Source: [NotebookLM Help](https://support.google.com/notebooklm/answer/16212820?hl=en)

## Key Excerpts & Important Notes

> **Important:** At this time, the NotebookLM mobile app may have limitations to this feature.

> Audio Overviews are deep-dive discussions between AI hosts that provide in-depth summaries of the key topics in your uploaded sources. These overviews are designed to be an objective reflection of your source content, rather than subjective opinions from the AI hosts.

> **Important:**
>
> - Audio Overviews, including voices, are AI-generated and may contain inaccuracies or audio glitches.
> - NotebookLM may take a couple of minutes to generate the Audio Overview.

> **Tip:** You can also listen to Audio Overviews while continuing to work within NotebookLM. You can explore relevant quotes, get citations, and query sources while listening to an Audio Overview in the background.

> **Important:** The interactive mode experience is currently available only in English.

> You can generate Audio Overviews in 80+ languages.

---

## What Audio Overviews Are

**Audio Overviews** are AI-generated audio discussions in NotebookLM based on uploaded notebook sources. They summarize and explain key topics from the sources, typically through AI-hosted conversations.

They are intended to be:

- **Source-grounded**
- **Objective reflections** of the uploaded material
- Useful for listening while continuing to work in NotebookLM

However, they may include:

- Inaccuracies
- Audio glitches
- AI-generated voice artifacts

---

## Generate an Audio Overview

### Requirements

To generate or delete an Audio Overview, you need **edit access** to the notebook.

### Steps

1. Open an existing notebook or create a new notebook.
2. Upload sources.
3. In the **Studio** panel, select **Audio Overview**.
4. NotebookLM generates the audio in the background.
   - You can continue generating other artifacts.
   - You can navigate to other screens while it processes.
5. Wait a couple of minutes for generation to complete.

---

## Customize an Audio Overview

Within the generation panel, you can set preferences before generating the audio.

### Format Options

- **Deep Dive — default**
  - An in-depth conversation.
  - Two hosts unpack and connect topics from your sources in a lively discussion.

- **The Brief**
  - A quick overview.
  - A single speaker delivers key takeaways in under two minutes.

- **The Critique**
  - Critical feedback.
  - Two hosts provide a constructive evaluation of material such as an essay or design document.

- **The Debate**
  - Multiple perspectives.
  - Two hosts engage in a formal back-and-forth debate on the topic.

### Other Customization Options

- Choose a **language**.
- Tailor the **length**:
  - Shorter
  - Default
  - Longer  
  - **English only**
- Add a **custom prompt** to:
  - Focus on specific topics
  - Adjust the expertise level
  - Provide other instructions

---

## Manage an Audio Overview

### Playback & Feedback

- Change playback speed:
  - Select **More**
  - Choose **Change playback speed**

- Send feedback:
  - Tap **Thumbs up**
  - Tap **Thumbs down**

### Work While Listening

You can continue using NotebookLM while the Audio Overview plays in the background, including:

- Exploring relevant quotes
- Getting citations
- Querying sources

---

## Load a Previous Audio Overview

> **Important:** This is only applicable to notebooks that have previously generated Audio Overviews.

### Steps

1. Open an existing notebook.
2. In the **Studio** panel, find the previously generated audio.
3. Select **Load**.
4. Wait for the overview to load.

### View the Original Prompt

To see the prompt used to generate an Audio Overview:

1. Open the three-dot menu next to the artifact in the **Studio** panel.
2. Select **“View custom prompt”**.

---

## Share an Audio Overview

There are three ways to share an Audio Overview.

---

### Option 1: Share a Link

#### Steps

1. Generate an Audio Overview.
2. In the audio player, select **Share**.
3. Confirm that:
   - The notebook is shared with the recipient, **or**
   - The notebook is public/open to **“Anyone with a link”**
   - Viewers have access to the **“full notebook”**
4. Copy the Audio Overview link.
5. Share the link with the recipient.
6. Save to apply changes.

#### Important Sharing Notes

- Public notebook sharing is only enabled for **consumer accounts**.
- Public sharing is currently disabled for:
  - Workspace Enterprise accounts
  - Education accounts
- Only notebook **owners and editors** can:
  - Make generated audio publicly available
  - Manage access
- Owners/editors can turn off public access by changing the notebook access level in the sharing panel.
- If an audio is deleted, previously generated share links stop working.

---

### Option 2: Share the Entire Notebook

#### Steps

1. Generate an Audio Overview.
2. Share the notebook.
3. Other users can access the

[... summary truncated for context management ...]

# Document 184: Generate & edit images with Gemini in Google Slides & Vids - Google Docs Editors Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/docs/answer/13951829?hl=en
Product area: slides
Original file: docs/generate-edit-images-with-gemini-in-google-slides-vids-google-docs-editors-help-3b81c1cf.md

# Generate & Edit Images with Gemini in Google Slides & Vids — Summary

**Source:** Google Docs Editors Help  
**Topic:** Using Gemini 3 Pro image features in **Google Slides** and **Google Vids**

---

## Feature Availability

- Requires an eligible **Google Workspace** or **Google AI** plan.
- Supported language availability depends on Google Workspace with Gemini language support.
- Gemini image generation and editing works in:
  - **Google Slides**
  - **Google Vids**

---

## Key Excerpts

> **Feature availability**
>
> - This feature requires an eligible Google Workspace or Google AI plan.
> - Learn about supported languages for Google Workspace with Gemini.

> You can generate and edit an image with Gemini 3 Pro image in:
>
> - Google Slides
> - Google Vids

> **Tip:** For better results, avoid figurative language in your prompt. When you create a prompt, you can include the subject, setting, distance to subject, materials, or background.

> **Important:** This functionality generates an image of a slide based on your active slide and presentation content. It’s currently only available in Google Slides. Beta indicates that this feature is new and may make mistakes.

> **Generated images are for use only within Google Slides and Google Vids.**

---

# Generate & Insert an Image

## Where It Works

- Google Slides
- Google Vids

## Steps

1. On your computer, open:
   - a presentation in **Google Slides**, or
   - a video in **Google Vids**.
2. Click the slide or scene where you want to add the image.
3. Open the image generation tool:
   - In **Slides**:  
     **Insert → Help me visualize → Image**
   - In **Vids**:  
     **Insert → Generate an image**
4. In the right panel, enter a prompt.
5. Optionally customize:
   - **Style** using **Add a style**
   - **Aspect ratio**
6. Click **Create**.
7. Preview the generated image by clicking it.
8. To insert it:
   - Click the image in the right panel.
   - In the preview window, click **Insert**.

## Example Prompts

Original examples from the article:

- “A beautiful photograph of a Hawaiian beach at sunset with palm trees in the distance.”
- “A plate of freshly baked blueberry muffins on a wooden kitchen table next to a large window.”
- “Watercolor painting of hydrangeas in a water pitcher.”
- “A futuristic cityscape with skyscrapers and flying cars set at night with HD details and shadows.”

## Aspect Ratio Options

| Option | Ratio |
|---|---:|
| **Square** | 1:1 |
| **Wide** | 16:9 |
| **Tall** | 9:16 |

## Prompting Tip

For better results, avoid figurative or vague language. Include concrete details such as:

- Subject
- Setting
- Distance to subject
- Materials
- Background

Example:

> “A close up of a boat made out of wood on a lake surrounded by trees at sunrise.“

---

# Edit an Image

## Steps

1. Open a presentation in **Google Slides** or a video in **Google Vids**.
2. Click an image.
3. Select **Edit image**.
4. Enter a prompt describing the desired change.
5. Click **Create**.
6. Preview the result by clicking the generated image.
7. To replace the original, click **Replace with image**.

## Optional Insert Choices

Next to **Replace with image**, click the down arrow to choose:

- **Insert as image**
- **Insert as background**

You can also close the preview with **Close**.

## Example Edit Prompts

- “Place this image within a frame hanging in an art gallery.”
- “Add a fluffy white cat.”

---

# Create a Slide in Google Slides — Beta

## Important Notes

- Available **only in Google Slides**.
- This feature generates an **image of a slide**, based on:
  - the active slide, and
  - the presentation content.
- It is a **beta** feature and may make mistakes.
- To generate an editable slide instead, use the separate **Generate a slide** feature.

## Steps

1. Open **Google Slides**.
2. Click a slide.
3. Go to:  
   **Insert → Help me visualize → Slide**
4. In the side panel, enter a prompt for the slide.
5. Click **Create**.
6. Optionally edit the generated slide image using another prompt.
7. Select the generated slide image when satisfied.
8. Choose:
   - **Insert as new slide**, or
   - **Insert on this slide**

## Recommended Prompt Details

More detailed prompts usually produce better results. Include:

- Title and body text
- Goal
- Overall tone
- Layout and visual style
- Technical specifics, such as fonts

## Example Edit Prompt

- “Update the title header to be a lighter shade of blue and underline it.”

---

- Available **only in Google Slides**.
- Generates an **image of an infographic**, based on:
  - the active slide, and

1. Open **Google Slides**.
2. Click a slide.
3. Go to:  
   **Insert → Help me visualize → Infographic**
4. In the side panel, enter a prompt.
5. Click **Create**.
6. Optionally edit the generated infographic image with another prompt.
7. Select the gene

# Document 185: Generate presentations with Gemini in Google Slides - Google Docs Editors Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/docs/answer/17111393?hl=en
Product area: slides
Original file: docs/generate-presentations-with-gemini-in-google-slides-google-docs-editors-help-4704a070.md

# Generate Presentations with Gemini in Google Slides — Summary

**Source:** Google Docs Editors Help  
**URL:** https://support.google.com/docs/answer/17111393?hl=en  
**Feature availability:** Desktop and English only.

---

## Key Excerpts & Important Facts

> **“With Gemini in Google Slides, you can generate fully editable presentations. This helps you save time and focus on how to refine your message.”**

> **“Currently on desktop and in English only.”**

Example prompt:

> “Create a 5-slide presentation on quantum computing for a general audience with a modern design.”

Important caution:

> **“Keep the tab open until Gemini generates your presentation. You can switch to other tabs or windows but not close Google Slides.”**

Feedback privacy warning:

> **“Because feedback may be human readable, do not submit data that contains personal, confidential, or sensitive information.”**

---

## Availability & Eligible Plans

Gemini presentation generation in Google Slides is available for:

- **Google AI Ultra and Google AI Pro accounts**
- **Gemini Alpha**
  - Access is controlled by your administrator.
  - If you do not see the feature, your admin may not have enabled Alpha features.
- **Google Workspace Experiments**
  - A trusted tester program for personal accounts to try new AI features.

---

## What Gemini Can Do in Google Slides

Gemini in Google Slides can generate a **fully editable presentation** with:

- Text
- Images
- A presentation structure
- A slide-by-slide plan
- Optional use of reference files
- Optional style matching from an existing deck

Gemini guides users through a short, tailored conversation to refine the presentation before generating slides.

---

## How to Generate a New Presentation

### 1. Open Google Slides

Go to [Google Slides](https://docs.google.com/presentation) on your computer.

### 2. Start a Presentation

From the start screen:

- Click **“Presentation”**

Or, in a blank presentation with no existing slides:

- Click **Ask Gemini** in the top-right corner.

### 3. Describe the Presentation

In the Gemini side panel, enter what you want to create.

Include details such as:

- Topic
- Audience
- Number of slides
- Tone or style
- Design preferences
- Goals or desired outcome

Example:

> “Create a 5-slide presentation on quantum computing for a general audience with a modern design.”

---

## Optional: Add Reference Sources

You can upload files from Google Drive for Gemini to use as references.

### Steps

1. Click **Add sources**
2. Select **Add from Drive**
3. Choose the file you want to use
4. Click **Add**

Gemini may also be allowed to search and reference relevant content from:

- The web
- Google Drive
- Chat
- Gmail

Source access depends on settings. Users can manage source settings through Google Workspace controls.

---

## Optional: Match an Existing Presentation Style

You can upload an existing deck so Gemini can match its presentation style.

### Steps

1. Click **Match presentation style**
2. Select **Add from Drive**
3. Choose the deck you want Gemini to use
4. Click **Add**

This helps Gemini align the generated presentation with an existing visual style or format.

---

## Refining the Presentation Plan

After the initial prompt and optional sources/style deck, Gemini may ask follow-up questions or clarification questions.

- These questions vary based on the topic and references provided.
- You can answer them to improve the output.
- You may also skip them.

After this step, click **Next**.

---

## Gemini’s Generated Presentation Plan

Before creating the deck, Gemini generates a plan that includes:

- **Overview**
- **Sources**
  - A list of documents used as references
- **Steps**
  - The content outline for each slide

---

## Editing the Presentation Outline

Before approving the final deck, you can modify Gemini’s outline.

### Edit a Slide Title or Content

- Click the slide outline.
- Edit the brief description or title.

### Add a New Slide

- Hover over any title.
- Click **Add step**.
- Enter the new slide title.

### Remove a Slide

- Hover over the slide.
- Click **Delete**.

### Apply Changes

- Click **Update plan** to have Gemini regenerate the outline based on your edits.

---

## Generate the Final Presentation

Once satisfied with the plan:

1. Click **Approve**
2. Keep Google Slides open while Gemini generates the presentation

Important:

- You may switch to other tabs or windows.
- Do **not** close the Google Slides tab until generation is complete.

---

## After the Presentation Is Generated

Once Gemini creates the deck, you can:

- Ask Gemini to refine the content **one slide at a time**
- Ask Gemini to generate additional slides
- Manually edit any deck element
- Ask Gemini to add or edit images

Related capabilities include:

- Generating individual slides with Gemini
- Generating and editing images in Google Slides and Vids

---

## Giving Feedback About Gemini in Slides

Google notes that Gemini may not always supp

[... summary truncated for context management ...]

# Document 186: Generate questions with Gemini in Google Forms (Workspace Experiments) - Google Docs Editors Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/docs/answer/16362810?hl=en
Product area: forms
Original file: docs/generate-questions-with-gemini-in-google-forms-workspace-experiments-google-docs-ffa7c876.md

# Generate Questions with Gemini in Google Forms (Workspace Experiments) — Summary

**Source:** Google Docs Editors Help  
**URL:** https://support.google.com/docs/answer/16362810?hl=en

## Key Excerpts

> **Important:** This article is referring to Google Workspace Experiments, which is an invite only program for users to try new AI features. The Workspace Experiments program may not be available in your country.

> To check if you have access to Workspace Experiments program features, open a new form on Google Forms and look for “Help me create a form” at the top.

> This feature is currently available on desktop and in English.

> You can use Gemini to quickly add relevant new questions to your form based on your current content.

> **Important:** You can use forms and quizzes in English only with a single section.

> Because feedback may be human readable, do not submit data that contains personal, confidential, or sensitive information.

---

## Overview

Google Forms users in the **Google Workspace Experiments** invite-only program can use **Gemini** to generate suggested questions for a form based on the form’s existing content.

### Availability

- Available only through **Google Workspace Experiments**
- Invite-only program
- May not be available in all countries
- Currently available:
  - On **desktop**
  - In **English**
- Forms/quizzes must be:
  - In **English**
  - Limited to a **single section**

### How to Check Access

Open a new form in **Google Forms** and look for:

> “Help me create a form”

at the top of the form.

---

## Add Questions to Your Form with Gemini

To generate suggested questions:

1. On your computer, open a form in [Google Forms](https://google.com/forms).
2. Click **Suggest questions**.
   - You must already have **at least 2 questions** in the form for Gemini to generate suggestions.
3. Select the generated questions you want to include.
   - Optional: Click **Retry** to generate a new set of questions.
4. When the suggestions look good, click **Insert suggestions**.

---

## Give Feedback on Suggested Questions

Google notes that Gemini-powered Workspace features are still learning and may not always support a request correctly.

You can provide feedback if a suggestion is:

- Inaccurate
- Unsafe
- Inappropriate
- Otherwise problematic

### Feedback Steps

1. Below the generated text, click:
   - **Good suggestion**
   - or **Bad suggestion**
2. If you choose **Bad suggestion**, select the issue and optionally provide more details.
3. Click **Submit**.

### General Feature Feedback

To provide general feedback on the feature:

- At the bottom of the page, click **Help & feedback**
- Then click **Help forms improve**

### Legal Issues

To report a legal issue, [create a request](https://support.google.com/legal/answer/3110420).

---

## Important Safety, Accuracy, and Privacy Notes

Google provides several warnings about Workspace Experiments suggestions:

- Suggestions **do not represent Google’s views** and should not be attributed to Google.
- Do **not** rely on Workspace Experiments features for:
  - Medical advice
  - Legal advice
  - Financial advice
  - Other professional advice
- Suggestions may be:
  - Inaccurate
  - Inappropriate
- User feedback helps improve safety and usefulness.
- Do **not** include personal, confidential, or sensitive information in prompts or feedback.

---

## Workspace Experiments Data Use

Google uses Workspace Experiments data and metrics to:

- Provide products and services
- Improve products and services
- Develop products, services, and machine learning technologies across Google

### Human Review

Workspace Experiments data may be:

- Read
- Rated
- Annotated
- Reviewed by human reviewers

Google states that when it uses Google-selected input to generate output, it will **aggregate and/or pseudonymize** that content and the resulting output before human review, unless the content is specifically submitted as feedback.

---

## Data Collected in Google Forms

When you generate a question in Forms, Google uses and stores:

- The generated question
- Form content referenced to generate questions
- Your feedback on the generated question

For details, review the [Google Workspace Experiments Privacy Notice and Terms for Personal Accounts](https://support.google.com/docs/answer/13447401).

---

## Related Resources

- [Create a form with Gemini in Google Forms (Workspace Experiments)](https://support.google.com/docs/answer/15259015)
- [Summarize responses with Gemini in Google Forms (Workspace Experiments)](https://support.google.com/docs/answer/16232990)
- [Google Workspace Experiments Privacy Notice and Terms for Personal Accounts](https://support.google.com/docs/answer/13447401)

- [Google Workspace Learning Cente

# Document 187: Generate Video Overviews in NotebookLM - NotebookLM Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/notebooklm/answer/16454555?hl=en
Product area: notebooklm
Original file: docs/generate-video-overviews-in-notebooklm-notebooklm-help-38229282.md

# Generate Video Overviews in NotebookLM — Summary

**Source:** [NotebookLM Help](https://support.google.com/notebooklm/answer/16454555?hl=en)  
**Topic:** How to generate, customize, play, manage, share, download, and set languages for Video Overviews in NotebookLM.

---

## Key Excerpts & Important Notes

> **Important:** At this time, the NotebookLM mobile app may have limitations to this feature.

> Video Overviews transform the sources in your notebook into an engaging video. They distill complex information into clear, digestible content, providing a comprehensive and engaging visual deep dive of your material.

> To generate or delete a Video Overview, you need edit access to a notebook.

> Video Overviews, including voices and visuals, are AI-generated and may contain inaccuracies or audio glitches.

> NotebookLM may take a while to generate the Video Overview — sometimes more than 30 minutes.

> NotebookLM uses the preferred language from your Google Account settings as your default output language.

---

# What Are Video Overviews?

**Video Overviews** are AI-generated videos created from the sources inside a NotebookLM notebook. They are designed to turn complex material into a more digestible, visual explanation.

They can include:

- Narration
- Visuals
- Storytelling or structured explanation
- Source-based summaries
- Custom focus areas based on user instructions

---

# Generate a Video Overview

## Requirements

- You must open an existing notebook or create a new one.
- You must upload sources.
- You need **edit access** to generate or delete a Video Overview.

## Steps

1. Open an existing notebook or create a new notebook and upload sources.
2. In the **Studio** panel, select **Video Overview**.
3. Optionally customize the video before generation.
4. Select **Generate**.

## Background Generation

Video generation runs in the background, so you can:

- Generate other artifacts at the same time
- Navigate to other screens
- Return to the notebook later

Generation may take a while, sometimes **more than 30 minutes**.

---

# Optional Customization Settings

Before generating a Video Overview, you can customize several options.

## Format

Choose the type of video:

### Cinematic

- Available only to users **18+**
- Currently supports **English only**
- Designed as a rich, immersive experience
- Uses visuals and storytelling to unpack complex ideas

### Explainer

- A structured, comprehensive overview
- Connects ideas across your sources

### Brief

- A shorter, bite-sized video
- Helps quickly communicate the core ideas of a document

---

## Language

You can choose the narration language from NotebookLM’s supported languages.

NotebookLM supports Video Overviews in **80+ languages**.

---

## Visual Style

Available for **18+ users**, excluding Cinematic Video Overviews.

Example visual styles include:

- **Classic**
- **Whiteboard**
- **Watercolor**
- **Retro Print**
- **Heritage**
- **Paper-craft**
- **Kawaii**
- **Anime**

You can also:

- Let NotebookLM **auto-select** a visual style
- Choose **Custom** and enter a description to generate a custom visual style

---

## Steering Prompt

Use a steering prompt to guide NotebookLM’s focus.

Examples of what you can specify:

- Important sources
- Specific topics
- Desired emphasis
- Areas to prioritize or avoid

---

# Manage a Video Overview

After a Video Overview is generated, you can manage playback and feedback.

Available controls:

- Change playback speed from **“1x”**
- Use the playback slider to move forward or backward
- Tap **rewind** or **skip forward**
- Enter full-screen mode
- Send feedback by selecting:
  - **Good video**
  - **Bad video**

---

# Play a Previously Generated Video Overview

> **Important:** This only applies to notebooks that already have generated Video Overviews.

## Steps

1. Open an existing notebook.
2. In the **Studio** panel, select the title of the generated Video Overview.
3. When the video player opens, press **Play**.

## Tip

To view the prompt used to generate the overview:

- Open the three-dot menu next to the artifact in the Studio panel
- Select **View custom prompt**

---

# Share a Video Overview

There are three sharing options.

---

## Option 1: Share a Link

### Steps

1. Generate a Video Overview.
2. In the video player, select **Share**.
3. Confirm that:
   - The notebook is shared with the recipient, or
   - The notebook is public and open to **Anyone with a link**
   - Viewers have access to the **full notebook**
4. Copy the Video Overview link.
5. Save to apply changes.

### Important Sharing Notes

- Public notebook sharing is only enabled for **consumer accounts**.
- Public sharing is currently disabled for:
  - Workspace Enterprise accounts
  - Education accounts
- Only notebook **owners and editors** can make a generated video publicly available and manage access.
- Owners or editors can turn off public access by changing the notebook access level in the sharing panel.
-

#### What is an AI-generated presentation?

An AI-generated presentation is a slide deck created with help from artificial intelligence, used to tell a story and information in a concise, highly visual format. Presentations are made to engage with your audience and spark conversation. GenerativeÂ AI can be a helpful thought partner in building a compelling slide deck. With Gemini in Slides you can use generative AI to create original images and generate a slide using content from your Drive.

#### How to use Gemini in Slides

With Gemini in Slides, you can create images, generate new slides, summarise a presentation and reference your Drive files to create a new slide.

You can access Gemini in Slides by clicking the Ask Gemini icon. Once the icon is clicked, you can either select a suggested prompt or compose your own unique prompt to help you quickly generate slides and fine-tune your presentation.

Analyst and public relations

Prepare for analyst or press briefings

[Learn more](/intl/en_uk/resources/ai/prompts-for-communications/#analyst-and-pr-gemini-in-slides)

![Icon of a trend line moving upwards](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-workspace-assets/uploads/7uffzv9dk4sn-7lhlUqx0fVAeW6iMVPeVZf-04117c0852e12530756a63174560acdb-Gemini_at_Work_TemplateWorkspaceSelector_Marketing.svg)

Chief marketing officer

Brainstorm content and thought leadership

[Learn more](/intl/en_uk/resources/ai/prompts-for-executives/#cmo-gemini-in-slides)

![Icon of a commercial building](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-workspace-assets/uploads/7uffzv9dk4sn-7M7NJT2DD61ilDlLtL5PhR-85b528bf215454e89cb90156f0983721-Gemini_at_Work_TemplateGeminiPricing_Gemini_for_work.svg)

Chief information officer

Communicate technical topics to non-technical audiences

[Learn more](/intl/en_uk/resources/ai/prompts-for-executives/#chief-information-officer)

![Gemini in Slides creating a slide based on April product launch, shown with a man and a woman looking at a laptop. ](https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/_ekLAOk4kXDLeDOXRq8CfwcwH5Zdz7ka-FFfTCPvkYO0UBXtrY185ZbO-BuJqnVfBNMzuh1ArdT4eeTY4upmu12OMxFGPyvk-08=e365-pa-nu-rw-w608)

#### How Gemini in Slides can help you craft presentations

**Generate slide ideas and outlines**

Are you struggling to build a slide deck even though you have a concept in mind? Use Gemini in Slides to transform your ideas into engaging presentation decks. With Gemini in Slides, you can easily create slides from scratch or use existing information from Drive files or Gmail emails. For instance, you can ask Gemini in Slides to create a slide summarising a product launch plan or visualising a production process based on an existing document. Gemini in Slides makes it easy to generate high-quality slides with minimal effort.

**Fine-tune your presentation content**

Get help tailoring your presentation. Use Gemini in Slides to transform your initial content into a more compelling narrative. Gemini in Slides can also help you generate speaker notes for individual slides or provide an executive summary for your presentation. Ask Gemini in Slides to create agenda slides or make messaging more concise throughout your presentation. Need help crafting an on-brand elevator pitch to sum up your deck? Gemini in Slides can help with that too.

**Design with AI-generated visuals**

The right visuals can impact your presentation. Click Ask Gemini and enter a prompt to *Create an image* or *Suggest images for this slide*. Gemini in Slides uses the context of a slide to generate charts and graphs to illustrate information in a visually appealing way. You can also add your own prompt, and Ask Gemini in Slides to create custom images to make your slides more engaging.

**Collaborate efficiently**

As a thought partner, you can use Gemini in Slides to quickly extract relevant information that can be used in your presentation. Gemini in Slides can help quickly reference files in Drive to create a slide. You can also tap Gemini in Slides to generate summaries, add speaker notes and ensure that everyone is working in sync before it's time to present. Your team can help to improve Gemini in Slides' output by giving feedback on suggestions to polish your presentation.

#### Considerations and best practices

Gemini in Slides can help you create decks faster, simplifying the process and giving you more time to devote to other pressing tasks. Generative AI is constantly evolving, getting better and more efficient over time. However, it's important to keep AI best practices in mind to make sure that its output is factually correct.

**Fact checking and editing**

Read and review your deck before presenting to catch any errors. If your deck includes any medical, legal or financial advice, make sure that you review carefully and share with your organisation's legal team to ensure that no false claims have been made.

**Maintaining control**

Gemini in Slides lets you give feedback on generated output. If a suggestion is inaccurate, click Good suggestion or Bad suggestion to the bottom right of the text or image suggestion. You can also add additional feedback to help Gemini in Slides improve output and future suggestions. As part of Google's policy, enterprise end users are informed before submitting feedback that feedback data shouldn't contain personal, sensitive or confidential information.

**Audience awareness**

Gemini in Slides can help you capture your audience's attention while minimising the tedious work involved in creating a compelling deck. When crafting prompts, tell Gemini in Slides who your audience is to help tailor output.

## Frequently asked questions

Expand all

- Benefits include saving time on presentation outlines and design and creating professional-looking images without design expertise.
- Gemini in Slides can create images, generate new slides, summarise a presentation, write or rewrite content and reference your Drive files or Gmail emails as you write or create a new slide.

![A row of icons displaying Google Workspace products.](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-workspace-assets/uploads/7uffzv9dk4sn-3o5OAIAU0V4vxIn3lNLoiB-2c2568f4c76442271ff3a08e323ffbe3-6-Logo_Lockup_Recreated.optimized.svg)

# Generative AI for presentations

Source: https://workspace.google.com/intl/en_ca/resources/presentation-ai?hl=en

# Generative AI for presentations

Transform ideas into presentations with help from AI. Use Gemini in Slides to create custom visuals, generate a slide, and more.

[Get started](https://workspace.google.com/business/signup/welcome?source=gafb-presentation_ai-hero-en-CA&hl=en-CA&ga_region=amer&ga_country=ca&ga_lang=en)[Contact sales](https://workspace.google.com/intl/en_ca/contact/?source=gafb-presentation_ai-hero-en-CA)

![Gemini creates an image from a prompt with âhelp me visualizeâ, shown with man at computer](https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/NCwXrJ7htjtQgtH93xugmSqGoFcTaiS4GQqFFn03BFH4HpItdxspd3h41BLhgYVH59_WC3pzvoAxVpwIaJ8lDzH3BxbOt7l4NXTj=e365-pa-nu-rw-w528)

#### What is an AI generated presentation?

An AI-generated presentation is a slide deck created with help from artificial intelligence, used to tell a story and information in a concise, highly visual format. Presentations are made to engage with your audience and spark conversation. Generative AI can be a helpful thought partner in building a compelling slide deck. With Gemini in Slides you can use generative AI to create original images and generate a slide using content from your Drive.

#### How to use Gemini in Slides

With Gemini in Slides, you can create images, generate new slides, summarize a presentation, and reference your Drive files to create a new slide.

You can access Gemini in Slides by clicking the Ask Gemini icon. Once the icon is clicked, you can either select a suggested prompt or compose your own unique prompt to help you quickly generate slides and fine-tune your presentation.

# Document 191: Generative AI training & help - Google Workspace Learning Center

Source URL: https://support.google.com/a/users/answer/14506784?hl=en
Product area: workspace-learning-center
Original file: docs/generative-ai-training-help-google-workspace-learning-center-49b30e73.md

# Generative AI Training & Help — Google Workspace Learning Center

**Source:** Google Workspace Learning Center  
**URL:** https://support.google.com/a/users/answer/14506784?hl=en  
**Purpose:** Help users learn how to use Google’s generative AI offerings—especially **Gemini**—to improve productivity at work.

---

## Overview

Google’s page provides training, help resources, and guidance for using **Google Workspace with Gemini**. It covers:

- How to use Gemini across Google Workspace apps
- Prompting tips and role-specific use cases
- Introductory explanations of generative AI and LLMs
- Training resources for users, administrators, and developers
- Product help centers and related support links

---

## Key Excerpts & Important Notices

### Purpose Statement

> Use Google's generative AI offerings to boost your productivity at work

### Safety, Accuracy & Feedback Warnings

Before using Google Workspace with Gemini, Google highlights several important caveats:

> Gemini feature suggestions don’t represent Google’s views, and should not be attributed to Google.

> Don’t rely on Gemini features as medical, legal, financial, or other professional advice.

> Gemini features may suggest inaccurate or inappropriate information. Your feedback makes Gemini more helpful and safe.

> Enterprise end users can submit feedback to Google Workspace regarding their experience using generative AI features. End users are informed before submitting the feedback that feedback data should not contain personal, sensitive, or confidential information and may be read by humans.

### What Gemini Is

> Gemini is an AI-powered assistant from Google with enterprise-grade security and privacy.

Google describes Gemini as a tool to help users:

- Write
- Visualize
- Organize
- Connect with colleagues
- Chat via **gemini.google.com**

---

## Main Sections on the Page

The article is organized around four core areas:

1. **Use Google Workspace with Gemini**
2. **Get tips for using Gemini in your role at work**
3. **Learn about generative AI**
4. **Get more training & help**

---

# 1. Use Google Workspace with Gemini

Gemini can be used both **inside Google Workspace apps** and as a standalone chat experience.

## Quick Start Resources

- [Quick start guide](https://support.google.com/a/users/answer/14505994)
- [Cheat sheet](https://support.google.com/a/users/answer/14143478)

---

## Gemini in Workspace Apps

Gemini is available across:

- Gmail
- Docs
- Sheets
- Slides
- Meet
- Vids
- Google Workspace Studio

Google describes Gemini in Workspace as AI assistance embedded directly into tools users already use.

### Key Description

> Gemini provides AI assistance directly within the Google Workspace apps you already use, helping you brainstorm, research, analyze, synthesize, and unlock key insights from your documents, spreadsheets, and other sources quickly.

### Workspace Gemini Learning Resource

- [Learn how Workspace with Gemini can help you and your team](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1daSMVKm3B5MM_8AlqJSu3JnfOdZg2Ujb/view?usp=drive_link)

---

## Common Gemini Tasks & Resource Links

### Get Started

Use Gemini to begin generating or drafting content:

- [Start with a great prompt](https://support.google.com/a/users/answer/14590328)
- [Write a quick first draft](https://support.google.com/a/users/answer/14747862)
- [Generate a first draft of a video](https://support.google.com/a/users/answer/14819770#AI)

### Refine & Organize Content

Use Gemini to improve, structure, summarize, or visualize work:

- [Polish your writing](https://support.google.com/a/users/answer/14512929)
- [Illustrate a presentation](https://support.google.com/a/users/answer/14514640)
- [Summarize content & organize data](https://support.google.com/a/users/answer/14506398)

### Automate Everyday Work

Use AI flows in Google Workspace Studio:

- [Create custom AI flows to draft emails, track tasks, and more](https://support.google.com/a/users/answer/16430486)

### Engage with Your Audience

Use Gemini to improve meetings and presentation experiences:

- [Create a custom background & enhance your meeting experience](https://support.google.com/a/users/answer/14638683)

---

## Chat with the Gemini App

Gemini can also be used outside Workspace apps as a standalone chat experience:

> Chat with Gemini as a standalone experience at gemini.google.com.

Related resource:

- [Brainstorm with Gemini](https://support.google.com/a/users/answer/14628736#3)

---

## Gemini Usage Limits

Depending on account setup, users may have monthly Gemini usage limits.

> Depending on how your account is set up, you might have monthly usage limits for Gemini.

Resource:

- [Learn about limits](https://support.google.com/a/users/answer/14796585)

---

# 2. Role-Specific Gemini Tips at Work

Google provides role-based resources showing how Gemini can help with everyday workplace tasks, including:

- Recruiting and hiring employees
- Creating marketing materials
- Managing a sales

# Document 192: Get AI-powered responses with AI Mode in Google Search - Computer - Google Search Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/websearch/answer/16420827
Product area: search-ai
Original file: docs/get-ai-powered-responses-with-ai-mode-in-google-search-computer-google-search-help-048f89de.md

# Google Search Help: AI Mode in Google Search — Summary

**Source:** https://support.google.com/websearch/answer/16420827  
**Topic:** How to access, use, personalize, manage, and give feedback on **AI Mode** in Google Search.

---

## Key Excerpts

> **Important:** We’re currently updating settings for Google Search services, which include Search, Maps, Shopping, Flights, Hotels, Translate and News. The settings described in this article are being updated so may not match your current experience.

> AI Mode is Google’s most powerful AI search experience. You can ask anything and get an AI-powered response, with the ability to go deeper through follow-up questions and helpful links to the web.

> AI Mode expands what AI Overviews can do with more advanced reasoning and ways of interacting. It divides your question into subtopics and searches for each one simultaneously.

> **Tip:** AI responses may include mistakes.

> As with any early-stage AI product, AI Mode doesn’t always get it right.

> **Important:** AI Mode in Search Labs is available to users 13 and above.

> **Important:** Gemini 3 Pro in AI Mode has daily usage limits.

---

# 1. What AI Mode Is

**AI Mode** is an AI-powered Google Search experience designed for deeper, more interactive search.

It can:

- Answer complex questions using AI-generated responses.
- Let users ask **follow-up questions**.
- Provide **helpful web links** for deeper exploration.
- Search across multiple subtopics at once using a technique Google calls **“query fan-out.”**
- Build on the capabilities of **AI Overviews**, with more advanced reasoning and interaction.

Google says AI Mode relies on Search’s understanding of web information and is supported by high-quality web content to improve factuality. If Google does not have enough confidence in an AI-generated response, AI Mode may instead show a set of web links.

---

# 2. Important Limitations

AI Mode is an early-stage AI product and may make mistakes.

Possible issues include:

- Misinterpreting web content.
- Missing important context.
- Producing inaccurate or unhelpful answers.
- Getting personalization wrong.

Google recommends users verify important information using multiple sources.

---

# 3. Availability

## AI Mode Availability

AI Mode is available in many countries and territories across:

- **Americas**
- **Asia-Pacific**
- **Europe, the Middle East & Africa**

The article includes long country/territory tables covering many regions, including the United States, Canada, Brazil, Mexico, India, Japan, Australia, New Zealand, United Kingdom, Germany, France, Italy, Spain, South Africa, United Arab Emirates, and many others.

## Supported Languages

AI Mode supports a broad set of languages, including:

- English
- Spanish
- French
- German
- Italian
- Portuguese Brazil
- Hindi
- Arabic
- Japanese
- Korean
- Chinese Simplified and Traditional
- Indonesian
- Vietnamese
- Turkish
- Russian
- Ukrainian
- Polish
- Dutch
- Swedish
- Thai
- Tamil
- Telugu
- Urdu
- Bengali
- Swahili
- Yoruba
- Zulu  
- And many more

The full article lists dozens of supported languages.

---

# 4. Recommended Settings for Best Experience

For the best experience, Google recommends enabling:

- **Search history**
- **Personalized recommendations**

Without these settings:

- You can still access AI Mode.
- You may not be able to pick up where you left off with previous searches.

Special cases:

- **Workspace users** with Search and Assistant turned off cannot use Search history unless enabled by an administrator in the Admin console.
- **Supervised children’s accounts** can have settings managed through the **Family Link app**.

---

# 5. How to Access AI Mode

You can access AI Mode in three ways:

1. Go to:  
   **https://google.com/ai**

2. Go to:  
   **https://www.google.com**  
   Enter a question in the Search bar, then select **AI Mode**.

3. In the **Google app**, tap **AI Mode** on the home screen.

---

# 6. How to Use AI Mode

AI Mode supports multiple input types:

- Text
- Voice
- Images
- PDFs

It also supports follow-up questions and continued exploration.

## Use Text

1. Enter a question in the **Ask anything** bar near the bottom of the screen.
2. To ask a follow-up or new question, type again into the **Ask anything** bar.

## Use Voice

1. Tap the **Microphone** icon in the **Ask anything** bar.
2. Say your question.
3. Tap the microphone again to ask a follow-up or new question.

## Use an Image or PDF

1. Click the image upload icon to upload an image, or the PDF upload icon to upload a PDF from your computer.
2. You can also upload by:
   - Drag and drop
   - Copy and paste
3. Ask a follow-up or new question in the **Ask anything** bar.

---

# 7. How AI Mode Works

AI Mode uses a **query fan-out** technique:

- It divides a user’s question into subtopics.
- It searches for each subtopic simultaneously.
- It gathers information from multiple data sources.
- It synthesizes the results into an easier-to

[... summary truncated for context management ...]

# Document 193: Get AI-powered responses with AI Mode in Google Search - Computer - Google Search Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/websearch/answer/16011537
Product area: search-ai
Original file: docs/get-ai-powered-responses-with-ai-mode-in-google-search-computer-google-search-help-4df1d0f0.md

# Google Search Help: AI Mode in Google Search — Markdown Summary

**Source:** https://support.google.com/websearch/answer/16011537  
**Title:** *Get AI-powered responses with AI Mode in Google Search - Computer - Google Search Help*

---

## Key Excerpts

> **Important:** We’re currently updating settings for Google Search services, which include Search, Maps, Shopping, Flights, Hotels, Translate and News. The settings described in this article are being updated so may not match your current experience.

> AI Mode is Google’s most powerful AI search experience. You can ask anything and get an AI-powered response, with the ability to go deeper through follow-up questions and helpful links to the web.

> AI Mode expands what AI Overviews can do with more advanced reasoning and ways of interacting. It divides your question into subtopics and searches for each one simultaneously.

> **Tip:** AI responses may include mistakes.

> As with any early-stage AI product, AI Mode doesn’t always get it right.

> AI mode uses a “query fan-out” technique, dividing your question into subtopics and searching for each one simultaneously across multiple data sources.

> **Important:** Gemini 3 Pro in AI Mode has daily usage limits.

> **Note:** Consenting to this experience means that all Search services will be permitted to access Workspace and Google Photos. Search services include Google Search (incl. AI Mode, Discover), Maps, Shopping, News, Flights, Hotels, and Translate.

---

# 1. What AI Mode Is

**AI Mode** is Google Search’s advanced AI-powered search experience. It lets users:

- Ask broad or complex questions.
- Receive AI-generated answers.
- Ask follow-up questions.
- Explore cited web links and supporting sources.
- Go deeper than standard **AI Overviews**.

AI Mode is designed for questions that may otherwise require multiple searches, such as:

- Exploring a new concept.
- Comparing options.
- Researching multiple aspects of a topic.
- Asking follow-up questions in context.

Google says AI Mode relies on Search’s understanding of web information and high-quality web content to improve factuality. If Google does not have enough confidence in an AI-generated response, AI Mode may instead show a set of web links.

---

# 2. How AI Mode Works

AI Mode uses a technique Google calls **“query fan-out.”**

## Query Fan-Out

AI Mode:

1. Breaks a question into subtopics.
2. Searches for each subtopic simultaneously.
3. Searches across multiple data sources.
4. Combines the results into a single AI-powered response.
5. Provides links so users can explore further.

This allows AI Mode to gather more relevant web content than a single traditional search query.

---

# 3. Accuracy, Limitations, and Fact-Checking

Google emphasizes that AI Mode is an early-stage AI product and may make mistakes.

Possible issues include:

- Misinterpreting web content.
- Missing context.
- Producing inaccurate or incomplete responses.
- Personalization errors.

## How to Double-Check AI Mode Responses

Google recommends:

- **Check important information in more than one place.**
  - Use the AI source links.
  - Review other Google Search results.
- **Ask multiple versions of your question.**
  - Ask for different perspectives.
  - Ask for more detail.
- **Send feedback** if something appears incorrect.

---

# 4. AI Mode Availability

AI Mode is available in many countries, territories, and languages.

## Supported Regions

The article lists supported countries and territories across:

- **Americas**
- **Asia-Pacific**
- **Europe, the Middle East & Africa**

Examples include:

- United States
- Canada
- Mexico
- Brazil
- United Kingdom
- Germany
- France
- India
- Japan
- Australia
- South Korea
- South Africa
- United Arab Emirates
- Many others across the Americas, Europe, Africa, Asia, and Oceania

## Supported Languages

AI Mode supports a broad list of languages, including:

- English
- Spanish
- French
- German
- Portuguese Brazil
- Hindi
- Japanese
- Korean
- Chinese Simplified
- Chinese Traditional
- Arabic
- Bengali
- Indonesian
- Italian
- Dutch
- Polish
- Russian
- Turkish
- Vietnamese
- Urdu
- Swahili
- Yoruba
- Zulu
- Many additional languages

---

# 5. Recommended Settings for Best Experience

For the best AI Mode experience, Google recommends enabling:

- **Search history**
- **Personalized recommendations**

Without these settings, users can still access AI Mode, but they cannot resume previous searches where they left off.

## Workspace Accounts

For Google Workspace users:

- If **Search and Assistant** are turned off, Search history is unavailable.
- Only a Workspace administrator can enable it through the Admin console.

## Supervised Child Accounts

For supervised child accounts:

- Settings can be managed using the **Family Link app**.

---

# 6. How to Access AI Mode

Users can access AI Mode in three ways:

1. Go directly to:  
   **https://google.com/ai**

2. Go to:  
   **https://www.google.com**  
   Enter a qu

[... summary truncated for context management ...]

# Document 194: Get an AI Overview in Gmail search - Computer - Gmail Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/mail/answer/16789526
Product area: gmail
Original file: docs/get-an-ai-overview-in-gmail-search-computer-gmail-help-7d55a97a.md

# Get an AI Overview in Gmail Search — Summary

**Source:** https://support.google.com/mail/answer/16789526  
**Platform covered:** Computer  
**Product:** Gmail Help

---

## Key Excerpts & Important Notes

> **Important:** This feature is gradually rolling out to Gmail and may not be available to you yet.

> **Important:** This feature is only available in the US and in English.

> **Important:** If you use search operators, like `is:unread` or `from:`, the AI Overview isn't available.

> **Important:** If you turn off smart features, it also turns off other AI-powered Workspace features, like the Gemini in Gmail side panel.

---

## What AI Overview in Gmail Search Does

Gmail can generate an **AI Overview** when you search your inbox using **natural language questions** or **key phrases**, such as:

- `"When is my flight to Hawaii?"`
- `"Find the latest Cymbal Team Meeting notes."`

Based on relevant emails, Gmail can:

- Provide a **key fact or summary** directly above search results.
- **Synthesize information across multiple emails** into one answer.
- Save time by reducing the need to open and read individual messages.

AI Overviews work best with **descriptive natural language queries**. If no overview appears, try asking a more specific question or using a more descriptive phrase.

---

## Eligibility & Requirements

### Availability

- Available only in the **United States**
- Available only in **English**
- Gradually rolling out, so it may not appear for all eligible users yet

### Personal Google Accounts

You need one of these eligible Google AI plans:

- [Google AI Plus](https://support.google.com/googleone/answer/16882689)
- [Google AI Pro](https://support.google.com/googleone/answer/14534406)
- [Google AI Ultra](https://support.google.com/googleone/answer/16286513)

### Work or School Accounts

You need an eligible **Google Workspace plan**.

- [Learn more about Gemini features and plans](https://support.google.com/docs/answer/13952129#zippy=%2Cgoogle-workspace-business-plans)

### Smart Features Requirement

You must turn on smart features:

- Smart features for **Workspace**
- Separate smart features setting for **Gmail, Chat, and Meet**

Related settings page:

- [Smart features in your account](https://support.google.com/mail/answer/15604322#change_smart_feature_settings)

### Gmail App Limitation

- AI Overviews in the **Gmail app** are available only with a **Google AI Plus, Pro, or Ultra** plan.
- For **work or school accounts**, the feature is limited to the **web**.

---

## How to Get an AI Overview in Gmail Search

1. On your computer, open [Gmail](https://mail.google.com/).
2. In the search box at the top, enter a **natural language question** or **key phrase**.
3. Press **Enter**.

### Search Operators Are Not Supported

AI Overview is not available if your search uses operators such as:

```text
is:unread
from:
```

To use AI Overview, ask a natural language question instead of using Gmail search operators.

---

## Example Queries to Try

| Search objective | Example queries |
|---|---|
| **Get answers** | - `"When is the next meeting with my manager?"`<br>- `"Did the customer reply about the commercial sale?"`<br>- `"When is my next appointment?"`<br>- `"What's the check-in code for my rental?"` |
| **Summarize information** | - `"Summarize my upcoming trip."`<br>- `"Summarize updates for the upcoming launch."`<br>- `"Get outstanding invoices from emails with vendors."` |
| **Extract specific details** | - `"Show me my concert tickets."`<br>- `"Track my latest return."`<br>- `"Confirmation number for my flight tomorrow."`<br>- `"Tracking number for the laptop shipment."` |

---

## How Gmail Generates AI Overview Answers

When you search:

1. Gmail finds the **most relevant emails**.
2. Gmail generates an AI Overview using information from those emails.
3. The result is shown as a **direct summary or answer** above search results.

The feature is designed to help you avoid manually reviewing individual messages.

---

## Feedback on AI Overviews

You can rate an AI Overview directly next to the result by selecting:

- **Good suggestion**
- **Bad suggestion**

This sends feedback about the quality of the AI-generated overview.

---

## Privacy & Data Controls

Google states that emails used for AI Overviews are protected by the same **security and privacy commitments** as the rest of Gmail.

To control use of your data for AI-powered features, update your **Google Workspace smart feature settings**.

---

## How to Turn Off AI Overview in Gmail Search

AI Overview can be disabled by turning off Workspace smart features.

> **Important:** Turning off smart features also disables other AI-powered Workspace features, including the **Gemini in Gmail side panel**.

### Steps

1. On your computer, go to [Gmail](https://mail.google.com/).
2. At the top right, click **Settings** → **See all settings**.
3. Under the **General** tab, scroll to **Google Workspace smart features**.
4. Cl

[... summary truncated for context management ...]

## Frequently asked questions

Take a look at our FAQs to learn more.

Expand all

- Gemini is automatically included in Google Docs for [Workspace plan subscribers](https://workspace.google.com/pricing.html?source=gafb-ai_docs-body-en&hl=en&ga_region=amer&ga_country=us&ga_lang=en). Access Gemini in Docs from the upper-right corner of your window and prompt Gemini to help you with writing, refining, and ideating content.
- As a large language model (LLM), Gemini recognizes grammar and spelling errors and can help you refine your writing. Ask Gemini to proofread your work for errors, and to correct grammar, tense, punctuation, and more.
- Gemini can help you adapt your writing style to suit specific audiences or needs. Ask Gemini for assistance in creating everything from formal business proposals aimed at potential investors, a persuasive blog post detailing product benefits, or playful ad copy to catch the attention of new customers.
- Gemini can help you improve the clarity and structure of your work. Ask Gemini to remove redundancies, shift from passive to active voice, or serve up an executive summary to capture the attention of your audience from the start. Gemini can also recommend changes to flow, helping you tell a crisper, more compelling narrative.
- Gemini in Docs currently supports 25+ languages including Chinese, English, Italian, Spanish, and more. Learn more [here](https://support.google.com/docs/answer/13607340?sjid=13910694473419608705-NA#zippy=%2Chelp-me-write%2Cgemini-in-docs-side-panel).

## Refine and improve your writing

Transform your drafts into polished, high-quality content. Gemini helps you perfect your message, adapt your tone, and ensure your writing is clear, concise, and compelling for any audience.

![Modifying a selected section of a Google Doc using Gemini ](https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/SZgDE8qDNpnbAljpgNzXp1Wk4p9F6riYc0Rj8gRaw9PfWKf1B_0mnTflwdVkKoTALErJf4qyTGOj0farFXIpVqnHPErT_cBuez0=e365-pa-nu-rw-w608)

## Refine your documentâs tone

Perfect your message with AI-powered tone adjustments. Gemini can help you achieve the right voice for any audience or purpose. Ensure your content reads the way you want it to, whether thatâs in a professional, friendly, or persuasive tone.

## Optimize the level of detail in your document

Get your message across concisely or expand on key ideas with the help of Gemini. Gemini shortens lengthy passages for quick reads or elaborates on complex topics to provide more detail. Optimize your document's length to perfectly suit the needs of your audience.

![Gemini in Google Docs elaborates on a short piece of selected text](https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/wr1mo10g17MzLFXydOekRaf9YbeOWSs1wn7z_eUyrc32KzIPP6SfVRdYITxiEctt3PObCBy8OAmxzs7nq8eoavyDWDR6XgT98AfjWQ=e365-pa-nu-rw-w608)

## Guide Gemini with custom prompts

Unlock new potential by giving Gemini specific instructions tailored to your document. From drafting unique sections to generating creative alternatives, your custom prompts empower Gemini to assist exactly how you need it. Transform your ideas into polished content with precision and ease.

## Consume and understand content faster

Quickly grasp the essentials of any document. Gemini helps you get up to speed in minutes, whether you're reviewing a long report or preparing for a meeting.

## Smart summarization

Don't get lost in lengthy documents. Gemini can instantly summarize key points or draft executive summaries, helping your audience better understand complex information.

## Frequently asked questions

Take a look at our FAQs to learn more.

Expand all

- Gemini is automatically included in Google Docs for [Workspace plan subscribers](https://workspace.google.com/pricing.html?source=gafb-ai_docs-body-en-CA&hl=en-CA&ga_region=amer&ga_country=ca&ga_lang=en). Access Gemini in Docs from the upper-right corner of your window and prompt Gemini to help you with writing, refining, and ideating content.
- As a large language model (LLM), Gemini recognizes grammar and spelling errors and can help you refine your writing. Ask Gemini to proofread your work for errors, and to correct grammar, tense, punctuation, and more.
- Gemini can help you adapt your writing style to suit specific audiences or needs. Ask Gemini for assistance in creating everything from formal business proposals aimed at potential investors, a persuasive blog post detailing product benefits, or playful ad copy to catch the attention of new customers.
- Gemini can help you improve the clarity and structure of your work. Ask Gemini to remove redundancies, shift from passive to active voice, or serve up an executive summary to capture the attention of your audience from the start. Gemini can also recommend changes to flow, helping you tell a crisper, more compelling narrative.
- Gemini in Docs currently supports 25+ languages including Chinese, English, Italian, Spanish, and more. Learn more [here](https://support.google.com/docs/answer/13607340?sjid=13910694473419608705-NA&hl=en-CA#zippy=%2Chelp-me-write%2Cgemini-in-docs-side-panel).

# Document 198: Get started with Ask Gemini in Google Chat - Computer - Google Chat Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/chat/answer/17036303?hl=en
Product area: chat
Original file: docs/get-started-with-ask-gemini-in-google-chat-computer-google-chat-help-c3b294c2.md

# Get started with Ask Gemini in Google Chat — Summary

**Source:** https://support.google.com/chat/answer/17036303?hl=en  
**Platform covered:** Computer / Google Chat / Gmail Chat

---

## Overview

**Ask Gemini in Google Chat** is an AI-powered assistant designed to help users manage work, find information, summarize updates, draft communications, generate images, and manage calendar events from within Google Chat.

It can help you:

- Find quick answers from scattered information across **Google Workspace data**
- Summarize updates on key topics across **Chat spaces**
- Identify **daily priorities**
- Search the web or Workspace content such as **Gmail, Google Drive, and Google Calendar**
- Draft emails or chat messages
- Generate images
- Catch up on unread messages and updates
- Schedule, reschedule, delete, or find calendar events

---

## Feature Availability

> **Feature availability**
>
> - Ask Gemini features are rolling out gradually as part of the Gemini Alpha; only some Gemini Alpha users will have access to Ask Gemini.
> - You may not have access to some or all of the Ask Gemini features.
>
> - Your administrator controls access to the Gemini Alpha. If you can’t find these features, your administrator may not have Alpha features enabled for your organization.

**Important requirement:**

> **Important:** To use these features, you must enroll in Workspace Smart Features.

---

## How to Find Ask Gemini in Google Chat

1. On your computer, open:
   - [Google Chat](https://chat.google.com/), or
   - [Gmail](https://mail.google.com/)
     - In Gmail, click **Chat** on the left.

2. Find **Ask Gemini**.

### Locations and Shortcuts

| Where to find Ask Gemini in Chat | Steps |
|---|---|
| **Shortcuts or Conversation list** | On the left, under **“Shortcuts,”** click **Ask Gemini**. |
| **Keyboard shortcut** | **ChromeOS and Windows:** `Ctrl + g`<br>**macOS:** `Command + g` |

---

## Use Ask Gemini to Answer Specific Questions

Ask Gemini can search across your Workspace or the web and provide a unified response. It keeps context across conversations, so you may not need to repeat details every time.

### Steps

1. Open **Ask Gemini** in Google Chat.
2. Type a prompt in the message field.
3. Click **Send**.

### Example Prompts

> “Find me the deck about [Project Name] that [Person’s Name] shared with me last week.”

> “Give me the latest update on [Example Topic].”

> “What did [Person's Name] ask me last week about [Example Topic] and when am I meeting them next?”

**Tip:** Ask Gemini includes **citations** to show the source of its information.

---

## Use Ask Gemini to Prepare for Your Day

You can ask Gemini for current action items based on the most recent available information.

### Steps

1. Open **Ask Gemini** in Google Chat.
2. Enter a prompt.
3. Click **Send**.

### Example Prompt

> “What are my action items?”

---

## Use Ask Gemini to Draft Communications

Ask Gemini can draft or refine emails and chat messages.

### Steps

1. Open **Ask Gemini** in Google Chat.
2. Enter a prompt.
3. Click **Send**.

### Example Prompts

> “Draft an email to [Person’s Name] summarizing our progress on [Topic Name].”

> “Refine the following chat message I am sending to [Person’s Name]: [Paste your draft here]. Make sure it’s professional and concise.”

---

## Use Ask Gemini to Generate Images

You can create images directly from Chat using Ask Gemini. Prompts can specify style, setting, subject, or purpose.

### Steps

1. Open **Ask Gemini** in Google Chat.
2. Enter an image-generation prompt.
3. Click **Send**.

### Example Prompts

> “Generate an image of a futuristic workspace in an ultra-realistic style.”

> “Create an image showing the team welcoming our new sales lead.”

> “Create a colorful icon for [Project Name].”

---

## Use Ask Gemini for Catch-Up Summaries

Ask Gemini can summarize missed updates, unread messages, emails, and activity in spaces.

### Steps

1. Open **Ask Gemini** in Google Chat.
2. Enter a catch-up prompt.
3. Click **Send**.

### Example Prompts

> “What did I miss on [date or topic]?”

> “Summarize some of the latest updates in [Space Name].”

> “Catch me up on unread messages.”

---

## Use Ask Gemini to Schedule, Manage, or Find Events

Ask Gemini can help with calendar-related tasks, including:

- Scheduling events
- Rescheduling existing events
- Deleting events
- Finding open time slots
- Checking calendar availability

### Steps

1. Open **Ask Gemini** in Google Chat.
2. Enter a calendar-related prompt.
3. Click **Send**.

### Example Prompts

> “Create a 30 minute meeting with [Person’s Name] this week.”

> “Schedule 1 hour of focus time for me this week.”

> “Reschedule meeting with [Person’s Name] to next week.”

---

## Give Feedback About Ask Gemini

Google notes that Gemini may not always support a request or provide accurate/safe suggestions.

> Google Workspace with Gemini is constantly learning and may not be able to support your request.

If a suggest

[... summary truncated for context management ...]
