# How AI is giving small businesses a major advantage

Source: https://workspace.google.com/blog/ai-and-machine-learning/how-ai-is-giving-small-businesses-a-major-advantage?hl=en

AI and Machine Learning

# How AI is giving small businesses a major advantage

May 13, 2026

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/5522i_GWS_AI_small_business_advantage_Blog.max-2500x2500.jpg](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/5522i_GWS_AI_small_business_advantage_Blog.max-2500x2500.jpg)

##### Lisa Gevelber

Founder of Grow with Google

##### Try Google Workspace at No Cost

Get a business email, all the storage you need, video conferencing, and more.

[SIGN UP](https://workspace.google.com/business/signup/welcome?source=gafb-blog_article-body-en)

Every year, Google’s Economic Impact Report tracks how our tools — including AI — help Americans build careers and businesses. In 2025, over 19.5 million U.S. businesses used our tools to reach new customers, and we’ve trained over 13 million Americans in digital skills, including 400,000 graduates from Google Career Certificates.

According to the U.S. Chamber of Commerce, more than 80% of small businesses using AI reported higher sales, increased profits, and larger teams in 2025. AI is helping small businesses save time, spot important trends, improve service, and enter new markets.

Reading through our latest impact report, I was struck by the stories of small business owners using AI to thrive. Here are three that really stood out:

### **Jamie’s Farm Granola in New York**

Jamie Kim’s granola journey started in her college dorm kitchen, and today, it’s in over 300 stores. She’s growing 40% year-over-year by slow-baking her granola with ghee.

Putting AI to work:

- **Gemini** helps her organize her stock-tracking, saving her 20 hours a week on paperwork.
- **Nano Banana** helps her design website backgrounds and test out new packaging looks, making it easier to share ideas with stores.
- **NotebookLM** helps her quickly find answers in long, complicated store manuals, preventing costly shipping or labeling mistakes.

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Copy_of_NY_Schaefer_126450_V1.max-2200x2200.jpg](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Copy_of_NY_Schaefer_126450_V1.max-2200x2200.jpg)

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Copy_of_NY_Schaefer_126450_V1.max-2200x2200.jpg](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Copy_of_NY_Schaefer_126450_V1.max-2200x2200.jpg)

### **PlaygroundEquipment.com in Greenfield, Indiana**

Nic Breedlove and his father, Victor, launched a playscapes business in 2006 and today they supply schools, parks, and cities all over the country with play structures and design services. They serve around 6,000 customers a year through an expansive network of distributors and their revenue grew 18% last year alone.

Putting AI to work:

- **Google Workspace with Gemini** helps them manage files, track inventory, and analyze data for financial reporting or from meetings.
- **Gemini** helps them produce four new case studies a month and then repurpose those into 80 videos and 160 social posts.
- **Gemini** helps the 3D design team brainstorm materials, spot missing design elements, and build a custom rendering tool.

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Copy_of_IN-Thompson-0322_V1.max-2200x2200.jpg](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Copy_of_IN-Thompson-0322_V1.max-2200x2200.jpg)

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Copy_of_IN-Thompson-0322_V1.max-2200x2200.jpg](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Copy_of_IN-Thompson-0322_V1.max-2200x2200.jpg)

### **321 Foto in Seattle, Washington**

Marilee Kimball founded 321 Foto to turn photo booths into creative spaces for connection. It has grown from a single booth to a boutique agency handling over 500 events a year.

Putting AI to work:

- **Gemini and Nano Banana** help turn event photos into creative art — from digital bugs for a pest control company to '80s video game characters.
- **Gemini** summarizes meetings and tracks next steps.
- A custom **Gem** stores the company’s brand voice and knowledge, acting as a handy team reference.

"What excites me most is how these tools open up new possibilities," Marilee says. "They let us mix photography, art, and tech to create something truly unique."

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Copy_of_WA-Wilder-89_V1.max-2200x2200.jpg](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Copy_of_WA-Wilder-89_V1.max-2200x2200.jpg)

[Read the full report](https://economicimpact.google/) to discover more stories about how American small businesses are using AI to grow.

# Document 238: How AI is helping me have better meetings

Source URL: https://workspace.google.com/blog/ai-and-machine-learning/how-ai-is-helping-me-have-better-meetings?hl=en
Product area: workspace-site
Original file: docs/how-ai-is-helping-me-have-better-meetings-227b9dcf.md

# How AI is helping me have better meetings

Source: https://workspace.google.com/blog/ai-and-machine-learning/how-ai-is-helping-me-have-better-meetings?hl=en

AI and Machine Learning

# How AI is helping me have better meetings

March 20, 2026

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/5492s_How_I_m_using_AI_to_transform_my_mee.max-2500x2500.jpg](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/5492s_How_I_m_using_AI_to_transform_my_mee.max-2500x2500.jpg)

##### Laura Mae Martin

Executive Productivity Advisor, Google

##### Try Google Workspace at No Cost

Get a business email, all the storage you need, video conferencing, and more.

[SIGN UP](https://workspace.google.com/business/signup/welcome?source=gafb-blog_article-body-en)

Some of us remember what it was like to join a meeting by dialing into a teleconference line, muting yourself, and awkwardly trying to jump in when you had a question. Thankfully, as remote and hybrid work took off and teams became more distributed across time zones, meetings became more collaborative with videoconferencing, allowing everyone to fully participate, no matter their location.

But as evolved as meetings have become, we should remember to ask ourselves: “Why are we meeting in the first place?”

At its heart, a good meeting should bring people together to achieve a specific goal. Now, with AI tools like Gemini in Google Meet, getting to that goal is easier than ever. Here’s how AI is helping me have better meetings.

### **Set meetings up for success**

Getting a meeting on the calendar can take more time than the meeting itself. But AI helps me simplify that process. Built with Gemini, *Help me schedule* in Gmail suggests optimal meeting times based on attendees’ Calendars and the context of our email thread. This gives me time back before the meeting even starts.

I use that time to prepare so I can show up ready to collaborate. Gemini helps revisit past meeting notes, surface outstanding action items, and identify what needs further discussion. The [Brainstormer Gem](https://blog.google/products-and-platforms/products/gemini/google-gems-tips/) can suggest agenda topics based on my goals, and it can even help shape the flow of the conversation. This sets the stage for a clear and intentional meeting.

### **Let AI handle the busywork so you can stay engaged**

Brainstorming can be hard when I’m trying to take notes and keep up with a rapidfire conversation. Gemini takes that work off my plate. It captures notes, generates captions, translates in real time, and keeps track of the discussion. If I join a meeting late or miss a beat as ideas fly, *Ask Gemini* in the Meet side panel can bring me up to speed without disrupting the flow.

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/5492s_How_I_m_using_AI_to_transform_my_mee.max-2000x2000_sMNKlHV.jpg](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/5492s_How_I_m_using_AI_to_transform_my_mee.max-2000x2000_sMNKlHV.jpg)

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/5492s_How_I_m_using_AI_to_transform_my_mee.max-2000x2000_sMNKlHV.jpg](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/5492s_How_I_m_using_AI_to_transform_my_mee.max-2000x2000_sMNKlHV.jpg)

Focus on the conversation. Let Gemini take the notes.

### **Keep everyone on the same page after the meeting ends**

The end of a meeting shouldn’t mean the end of momentum. AI-generated notes help us turn meetings into game plans. With *Take notes for me* in Meet, Gemini can share notes and action items through Gmail and attach them to the Calendar invite, so my team has access afterward.

Instead of eight people walking away with eight different interpretations, there’s a single, shared source of truth. This reliable foundation can reduce follow-up emails and cut unnecessary meetings.

My team spends less time asking “What did we decide, again?” and more time acting on those decisions. And when we’re all clear on our responsibilities and timelines, work can move faster without constant check-ins.

I often use those notes as a springboard for Gemini. I’ll ask it to turn them into suggested updates for a Slides presentation, or refine a plan while the discussion is still fresh. It’s a simple way to keep the momentum of the meeting without starting from scratch.

### **Smarter meetings, better work**

Meetings have come a long way. And now AI supports them as the productive workspaces they were always meant to be — bringing people together around ideas, decisions, and clear paths forward. When AI keeps us all in the loop and provides shared context, people can spend more time contributing, deciding, and getting things done.

Next time you schedule a meeting, think of AI as your capable meeting assistant that helps everyone walk in prepared and leave aligned and motivated. If a meeting is worth scheduling or accepting, it should set us up to do our best work.

[Download our ebook](https://workspace.google.com/learning/content/better-meetings-greater-impact-ebook?e=48754805) on having better meetings, or [watch for our webinar](https://workspace.google.com/learning/resources/better-meeings-greater-impact-gemini-webinar?e=48754805).

Posted in

- [AI and Machine Learning](https://workspace.google.com/blog/ai-and-machine-learning)
- [Meet](https://workspace.google.com/blog/meet)

# Document 239: How AI-powered features use your data in Google Maps - Google Maps Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/maps/answer/16833938
Product area: maps-ai
Original file: docs/how-ai-powered-features-use-your-data-in-google-maps-google-maps-help-e531099a.md

# Summary: How AI-powered features use your data in Google Maps

**Source:** Google Maps Help — “How AI-powered features use your data in Google Maps”  
**URL:** https://support.google.com/maps/answer/16833938

---

## Key Excerpts

> **Important**: We’re currently updating settings for Google Search services, which include Search, Maps, Shopping, Flights, Hotels, Translate, and News. These are rolling out gradually over the next few months. If the settings in this article do not match what you see, history and personalized recommendations for Search services are still controlled by [Web & App Activity](https://myactivity.google.com/personalization/settings/waa).

> Google Maps AI-powered features may use your location to provide more relevant and helpful answers. Additionally, these features may use your account information to provide personalized responses based on your activity and interests.

> We do not use the questions you type into Google Maps to train our AI models, though we do analyze them to improve our products and services.

> AI-powered answers can sometimes be incorrect. Be sure to double-check responses.

---

## Overview

Google Maps’ AI-powered features may use **location data** and **account activity** to provide more relevant, helpful, and personalized responses. Some related settings are currently being updated across Google Search services, including **Search, Maps, Shopping, Flights, Hotels, Translate, and News**.

If the new settings are not visible yet, Google says history and personalization for Search services are still controlled through **Web & App Activity**.

---

## AI-powered features and location data

Google Maps AI-powered features may use or collect location data in specific cases:

- **AI-powered answers**
  - Store the answers you receive.
  - Those stored answers may include your location.

- **Lens in Maps**
  - May store your location.

- **Gemini in Navigation**
  - May store your location.

### Exception

When you click on a place and ask a question about it:

- **Maps does not use or collect your location** for that interaction.

---

## How Maps personalizes AI-powered responses

If **Personalized Recommendations** are enabled, Google Maps may personalize AI-powered responses using account information, including:

- Places you searched for.
- Places you got directions to, **if Maps history is being saved**.
- Saved lists or labels added to places.
- Reviews, ratings, and photos you posted for places.
- Activity from your **Search history**, used to tailor responses to your tastes and preferences.

Relevant controls/resources:

- [Personalized Recommendations](https://www.google.com/search-personalization/)
- [Maps history](https://support.google.com/maps/answer/3137804)
- [Search history](https://myactivity.google.com/search-services/history/search)
- [Web & App Activity](https://myactivity.google.com/personalization/settings/waa)

---

## Use of data to improve AI features

Google states that:

- Questions typed into Google Maps are **not used to train AI models**.
- However, Google **does analyze those questions** to improve products and services.

---

## Verifying AI-powered answers

Google warns that AI-powered answers may sometimes be wrong and recommends users double-check important information.

### Recommended verification steps

- Check important information in more than one place.
- Click links to AI sources.
- Try other Google Search results.
- Ask multiple versions of the same question.
- Request different opinions or more details.
- Send feedback if something seems incorrect.

Related guide:

- [Evaluate info you find with Google](https://support.google.com/websearch/answer/12003459)

---

## Giving feedback on AI-powered answers

Users can submit feedback if an AI-powered answer is:

- Unsafe
- Not helpful
- Inaccurate
- Otherwise problematic

### Feedback steps

1. Below the answer:
   - If helpful, tap **Thumbs-up**.
   - If it needs improvement, tap **Thumbs-down**.
2. If you tap **Thumbs-down**, select a reason.
3. Enter any additional feedback.
4. Tap **Submit**.

Feedback is used to improve the AI-powered experience.

---

## Related resources

- [Use Gemini while you navigate](https://support.google.com/maps/answer/6041199)
- [Evaluate info you find with Google](https://support.google.com/websearch/answer/12003459)
- [Google Maps Help Center](https://support.google.com/maps/?hl=en)
- [Google Maps Community](https://support.google.com/maps/community?hl=en)

---

## Article feedback options

Google allows users to submit feedback about the help article itself. Possible issue categories include:

- **Inaccurate** — doesn’t match what the user sees in the product.
- **Hard to understand** — unclear or translation is wrong.
- **Missing info** — relevant but not comprehensive.
- **Irrelevant** — doesn’t match the title or expectations.
- **Minor errors** — formatting issues, typos, or broken links.
- **Other suggestions** — ideas to improve the 

[... summary truncated for context management ...]

# How Augustana University is building its future with Google Workspace and NotebookLM

Source: https://workspace.google.com/blog/customer-stories/augustana-university-building-future-with-google-workspace-and-notebooklm?hl=en

Customer Stories

# How Augustana University is building its future with Google Workspace and NotebookLM

October 23, 2025

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Augustana-University_Blog_Hero.max-2500x2500.jpg](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Augustana-University_Blog_Hero.max-2500x2500.jpg)

##### Dr. Peter Folliard

Dean of the School of Music and Vice President for Innovation

##### Try Google Workspace at No Cost

Get a business email, all the storage you need, video conferencing, and more.

[SIGN UP](https://workspace.google.com/business/signup/welcome?source=gafb-blog_article-body-en)

“Students can upload notes, documents, webpages, and any other helpful resources to NotebookLM, and then build quizzes, generate flashcards, and adapt the resources to fit their learning styles.”

**Snapshot:** Augustana University uses Google Workspace for Education Plus to save on operational costs, improve administrative efficiency, and introduce new AI-forward teaching strategies. Using tools like Gemini and NotebookLM, the university is preparing students for the future while streamlining operations with:

- New customizable learning modules for students with NotebookLM
- **$15,000 per year savings by switching from Zoom to Google Meet**
- A cost effective and secure eSignature solution within Drive and Docs

\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_

### **The mission and challenge**

At [Augustana University](https://www.augie.edu/), our mission is to provide an education of enduring worth that challenges the intellect, fosters integrity, and integrates faith with learning and service in a diverse world. Part of my role is ensuring we’re delivering on that promise by empowering our community with technology that enhances both teaching and learning. My guiding question has always been: Are we preparing students for their future, or are we clinging to our past?

This mindset has reshaped everything, from how we teach in the classroom to how we operate as an institution. Innovation isn’t just about shiny new tools; it’s about finding smarter, better ways to work. When I moved into this role, I saw opportunities to consolidate, innovate, and invest in tools that helped us support our faculty and students.

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Google_Workspace_Inline_Image_2.max-2000x2000.jpg](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Google_Workspace_Inline_Image_2.max-2000x2000.jpg)

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Google_Workspace_Inline_Image_2.max-2000x2000.jpg](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Google_Workspace_Inline_Image_2.max-2000x2000.jpg)

With Google Workspace, Augustana University’s staff and faculty can quickly and easily connect as they build the next generation of AI-driven education.

### **The switch to Google Workspace Education Plus and generative AI**

Before a recent upgrade, we’d been using [Google Workspace for Education Fundamentals](https://edu.google.com/intl/ALL_us/workspace-for-education/editions/education-fundamentals/) for more than a decade. In the Augustana School of Music, I’ve encouraged my team to rely on Workspace as their core work tool, because it’s easy to use and helps us collaborate in real time. They share their Google Calendar availability across the department and files using Drive, while Chat and Meet are their daily real-time communication tools. We work well together and communicate clearly because we have all of these intuitive, secure-by-design tools in one place. Streamlining our communication tools helped us deprecate third-party solutions. We save the university $15,000 per year by switching away from Zoom and entirely over to Google Meet. Similarly, having access to a secure [eSignature](https://workspace.google.com/resources/esignature/) solution directly in Drive and Docs has helped give everyone on campus access to a tool that we wouldn’t be able to afford otherwise.

To continue transforming how Augustana University operates, we recently upgraded to [Google Workspace for Education Plus](https://edu.google.com/intl/ALL_us/workspace-for-education/editions/education-plus/), with the help of Google Cloud Premier partner, [Burwood Group Inc](https://cloud.google.com/find-a-partner/partner/burwood-group-inc). Their hands-on approach to educating my team throughout the implementation helped all of us upskill; they’re also willing to jump in and do the work themselves — that availability and flexibility is exactly what you want in a partner. Together, we’ve been able to further align our tools with our mission of preparing students for their future while adding new layers of security to our existing collaboration tools. We trust Google to be a secure solution while supporting our vision for continuous improvement and change.

Introducing new AI solutions was a major catalyst for this next step in our journey. We’re teaching students how to use tools like [NotebookLM](https://workspace.google.com/products/notebooklm/) to personalize their learning experiences. They can upload notes, documents, webpages, and any other helpful resources, and then build quizzes, generate flashcards, and adapt the resources to fit their learning styles. Similarly, faculty and staff are using [Gemini](https://gemini.google.com/) to automate workflows, brainstorm new ideas, as well as introduce new teaching modalities and topics.

In our administrative offices, Google Workspace allows people who aren’t technical experts to innovate quickly and easily with generative AI. I recently created a complex, 18-page Google Form with conditional routing in under five minutes using a combination of Gemini and [Apps Script](https://workspace.google.com/products/apps-script/). Previously, a task like that would have taken me hours, if not a full afternoon to do on my own. With that time back, the team and I can focus on curriculum design, research, and taking meaningful steps to improving our students’ experience.

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Google_Workspace_Inline_Image_3.max-2000x2000.jpg](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Google_Workspace_Inline_Image_3.max-2000x2000.jpg)

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Google_Workspace_Inline_Image_3.max-2000x2000.jpg](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Google_Workspace_Inline_Image_3.max-2000x2000.jpg)

Educators are now integrating NotebookLM into their courses, so students can personalize their class experience based on their own learning styles and preferences.

### **Looking ahead**

Employers expect [39% of key skills](https://www.weforum.org/stories/2025/01/future-of-jobs-report-2025-jobs-of-the-future-and-the-skills-you-need-to-get-them/) required in the job market will change by 2030. We know technology will play a massive role in that shift, and as AI continues to evolve, we’ve launched initiatives like AI microgrants — incentivizing faculty to embed AI into their curriculum to meet that need. We can support educators who want to pursue Google certifications to learn AI best practices and then integrate them into their classroom. As educators, this is our opportunity to build a more agile education model that evolves alongside technology and sets our students up for success in a very different job market.

We have to rethink how we work, teach, and learn — part of that is seeing the things we’ve always done through an AI lens. For example, we just launched our “Welcome to the Future” class that combines a college 101 welcome course with writing, presenting, information literacy, and critical thinking — all with a technology-minded focus. Working with the Google team to upskill our faculty, simplify our administrative work, and empower our students for the future reassures me that we have more than a platform provider; we have a real collaborator supporting the university.

# Document 241: How Flashpoint is building a productive, happy workforce with Gemini for Google Workspace

# How Flashpoint is building a productive, happy workforce with Gemini for Google Workspace

Source: https://workspace.google.com/blog/customer-stories/how-flashpoint-builds-productive-happy-workforce-gemini-google-workspace?hl=en

Customer Stories

# How Flashpoint is building a productive, happy workforce with Gemini for Google Workspace

October 17, 2024

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Flashpoint_hero.max-2500x2500.png](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Flashpoint_hero.max-2500x2500.png)

##### The Google Workspace Team

##### Google Workspace Newsletter

Keep up with the evolving future of work and collaboration with insights, trends, and product news.

[SIGN UP](https://workspace.google.com/?source=gafb-blog_article-body-en#newsletter)

“The biggest impact we're seeing from Gemini today is a huge boost in productivity. With Gemini helping with the more mundane tasks, our teams can focus on the ones that matter the most.”   
                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             — **Tyler Predale**, **director of IT**, **Flashpoint**

**Snapshot:** With [Gemini for Google Workspace](https://workspace.google.com/intl/en_uk/solutions/ai/), [Flashpoint](https://flashpoint.io/) is improving efficiency and productivity across its workforce, allowing employees to focus on the work that counts.

Find out how Flashpoint is using Gemini to:

- Communicate and collaborate more effectively
- Maximize ROI
- Increase employee satisfactionDedicate more time to keeping customers secure

---

### The key to securing businesses against emerging threats

In a world of increasingly complex security challenges, Flashpoint uses threat data and intelligence to help organizations quickly identify cyber threats and protect their people, assets, and places.

Key to Flashpoint’s success is enabling its global teams to work efficiently together. As a remote-first company in a highly sensitive industry, Flashpoint needed to find a way to make this cross-border collaboration as smooth as possible, while maintaining robust security.

### Removing the barriers to collaborating across borders

Flashpoint chose [Google Workspace](https://workspace.google.com/) to enable its employees to stay connected and collaborate in real time to get work done, wherever they are.

“Flashpoint was born on Google Workspace and it’s helped us grow to where we are today,” says Tyler Predale, Director of IT at Flashpoint. “As a remote company, being able to collaborate across the globe in a secure manner is paramount.”

With Google Docs, Slides, and Sheets, Flashpoint’s remote teams can work on the same documents, such as sales presentations and marketing materials, in real time. “They can see each other’s notes, add comments, and truly collaborate as if they were sitting next to each other,” Predale explains.

[Google Meet](https://workspace.google.com/intl/en_uk/resources/video-conferencing/), meanwhile, is Flashpoint’s go-to meeting tool, bringing teams in different regions closer together and enabling them to stay connected through secure video calls. By reducing friction between teams and enabling smooth, efficient collaboration, Flashpoint employees can focus on what matters, delivering the best threat data and intelligence.

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Meet-Reaction--Activites-Panel_Blogpost_1.max-1800x1800.jpg](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Meet-Reaction--Activites-Panel_Blogpost_1.max-1800x1800.jpg)

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Meet-Reaction--Activites-Panel_Blogpost_1.max-1800x1800.jpg](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Meet-Reaction--Activites-Panel_Blogpost_1.max-1800x1800.jpg)

Flashpoint uses Google Meet to connect teams across different regions

### Improving productivity and job satisfaction with Gemini for Google Workspace

Now, with Gemini for Google Workspace, Flashpoint can work even more efficiently by incorporating generative AI into a wide range of workflows to increase productivity.

As cyber security specialists, Flashpoint made security a key consideration when choosing its generative AI solution. “As a company that takes security very seriously, data security and privacy were paramount,” explains Predale. “That's where Gemini really checked the box for us. We know that our data is protected and we can focus on collaborating and getting the full productivity advantages of generative AI.”

![Video Thumbnail](https://img.youtube.com/vi/C6n2JyPntUI/hqdefault.jpg)

How Flashpoint boosts productivity and security with Gemini for Google Workspace

How Flashpoint puts Gemini to work:

- **Communication:** When faced with a blank page at the start of an email or document, people can take time to get started. Now, with Gemini’s Help me write feature in Docs and Gmail, Flashpoint employees can create content faster, allowing them to spend more time focused on other tasks. For those with English as a second language who found written communications challenging, Gemini is enabling them to write with confidence and communicate more effectively.
- **Ideation and research:** Flashpoint’s marketing team needs to generate a large amount of content. With Gemini, they can research topics more quickly, brainstorm ideas for campaigns, and experiment with different copy variants, helping them to conceive and create marketing materials more quickly.
- **Coding:** Flashpoint’s IT team works around the clock to protect customers against emerging cyber threats. Developers now use Gemini to help them write Python scripts and create documentation, allowing them to dedicate more time to product innovation.
- **Hardened security****:** Securing businesses is what Flashpoint does best. With Gemini’s robust security and privacy commitments protecting customer data, Flashpoint can focus on developing even stronger cyber threat intelligence services for its customers.

With Gemini improving efficiency across the organization, Flashpoint employees can produce more work in a shorter time. “That time equates to money,” says Predale. “Gemini for Google Workspace has had a really positive impact on our ROI.”

Gemini has had a positive impact on morale, too. With Gemini handling the more mundane tasks, Flashpoint employees can focus on the more creative, valuable parts of their job. “Through our surveys, we have seen that Gemini has helped improve employee satisfaction ” Predale explains.

### Looking ahead

As Flashpoint expands its use of Gemini across the organization, Predale is confident there are even more efficiency gains to come: “We believe Gemini for Google Workspace is going to have profound impacts on the way we work, allowing our employees to focus on mission-critical tasks across the organization. The tool is constantly improving, it’s bringing in new features all the time, and we're excited about the roadmap ahead.”

# Document 245: How Google Workspace with Gemini billing works  |  Generative AI  |  Google Workspace Help

Source URL: https://knowledge.workspace.google.com/admin/generative-ai/workspace-with-gemini/how-google-workspace-with-gemini-billing-works
Product area: admin-console
Original file: docs/how-google-workspace-with-gemini-billing-works-generative-ai-google-workspace-help-edb534ea.md

# How Google Workspace with Gemini Billing Works – Summary

**Source:** [Google Workspace Help](https://knowledge.workspace.google.com/admin/generative-ai/workspace-with-gemini/how-google-workspace-with-gemini-billing-works)

---

## Billing Cycle

- After signing up, the **primary payment method is charged at the start of the next month**.
- Example: If service starts in May, charge occurs at the beginning of June.
- Billing continues automatically each month thereafter.

> *Note:* For customers with no prior Google payments, a **$100 early payment** may be required before purchasing AI Ultra Access.

---

## Billing Plans

### Flexible Plan
- Pay **per user per month**.
- Licenses can be added or removed anytime.
- **Prorated billing** for partial months.
  - *Example:* License added April 1, removed April 15 → charged for 15 days (half a month).
- No advance commitment required.

### Annual/Fixed-Term Plan
- Pay each month for a **monthly portion of the committed licenses**.
- Licenses can be **added anytime**, increasing the monthly charge.
- **Reducing licenses is only possible at renewal** (end of contract term).
- **Lowest per-user monthly price** compared to Flexible Plan.
- Cancelling before end of term → **full remaining contract value** is charged.

---

## Cancellation Charges

| **Plan**              | **Charge after Cancellation**                                                                                                 |
|-----------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| **Flexible Plan**     | Charged for days used in the month (e.g., cancel on 15th → half a month of service).                                         |
| **Annual/Fixed-Term** | **Full remaining contract balance** is charged. You remain responsible for the total commitment even if cancelled early.     |

---

## Threshold Billing

- If you **exceed your monthly billing threshold**, the primary payment method is **charged immediately** to reduce the unpaid balance.  
  _(Refer to [Understand threshold billing](https://knowledge.workspace.google.com/admin/billing/understand-google-workspace-bills-and-charges#threshold_billing) for details.)_

---

## Important Note for New Workspace Customers

- Purchases of **AI Ultra Access directly online** are billed on the **Flexible Plan** automatically.
- If the account has **never paid for Google services**, a **$100 early payment** may be required before purchase.  
  Learn how to [remove restrictions on new Google Workspace accounts](https://knowledge.workspace.google.com/admin/billing/remove-restrictions-on-new-google-workspace-accounts).

---

## Education‑Specific Pricing

The page includes a table for **Google AI Pro for Education** (only plan shown). Standard prices are listed; reseller discounts may apply.

| **Plan**                | **Flexible Plan**              | **Annual/Fixed-Term Plan**      |
|-------------------------|--------------------------------|---------------------------------|
| **Google AI Pro for Education** | **$24 USD per user/month**     | **$20 USD per user/month**      |

> *Business/Enterprise pricing sections were not expanded on this page, but billing plan rules apply identically.*

---

## Key Takeaways

- All charges are **monthly**; no upfront annual payment even for Annual/Fixed-Term – it’s a commitment, not a lump sum.
- Proration applies **only** to the Flexible Plan when licenses change mid‑month.
- Annual/Fixed-Term saves money **per license**, but **locks you in** – early exit incurs full remaining liability.
- Immediate threshold billing can trigger if unpaid balance exceeds the account’s threshold.
- Educational institution pricing is slightly lower through the Annual/Fixed-Term plan.

For questions not covered, the page suggests contacting a reseller or using the provided feedback options.

---

# Document 246: How Google Workspace with Gemini helps protect users from malicious content and prompt injection - Workspace Studio Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/workspace-studio/answer/16204578
Product area: workspace-studio
Original file: docs/how-google-workspace-with-gemini-helps-protect-users-from-malicious-content-and-0f4ac70e.md

[Skip to main content](https://support.google.com/workspace-studio/answer/16204578#search-form)

# How Google Workspace with Gemini helps protect users from malicious content and prompt injection

Learn how Google Workspace with Gemini helps protect users from malicious content and prompt injection attacks.

## Malicious content and prompt injection in generative AI

Similar to email and content threats like malware and [phishing](https://support.google.com/mail/answer/8253) attacks, security threats can target generative AI tools. Prompt injection is an attempt to elicit an unintended or harmful response from generative AI tools. Attackers may commit prompt injection not only by directly submitting queries, but also by sharing malicious content with a user, who then unintentionally references this malicious content in prompts in generative AI tools like Gemini Apps or Google Workspace with Gemini.

To help protect Gemini users, Google uses advanced security measures to identify risky and suspicious content.

## How Google detects malicious content and prompt injection

To help protect you from malicious content and prompt injection, Gemini in Workspace may filter or block some responses if malicious activity is detected.

### When Gemini identifies activity related to a prompt that may be malicious

- Gemini in Workspace warns you that the content has security risks.
- Sometimes, none of your content can be used to generate a response.
- Sometimes, only some of your content is used to generate a response, with the malicious content excluded by Gemini.

For example, if you ask Gemini in Gmail to summarize email messages, and one of those messages has malicious content, Gemini may not respond to your prompt for safety reasons.

## Help avoid unsafe content

### Pay attention when interacting with content from someone you don’t know

- Take notice of warnings from Google on content you receive in other tools like Gmail.
- Avoid clicking links from untrustworthy sources.
- Use caution when interacting with shared content from unknown senders, like files in Drive.

### Report malicious messages in Gmail

If you get a deceptive message or URL in Gmail, you can report it as phishing. Phishing is an attempt to steal personal information using deceptive emails, messages, ads, or websites that appear legitimate. For example, a phishing email might look like it's from your bank and request private information about your bank account. [Learn how to report phishing in Gmail](https://support.google.com/mail/answer/8253).

### Report malicious documents in Docs and Drive

If you get malicious documents, files, images, and other content in Docs and Drive, you can report them. [Learn how to report abusive content](https://support.google.com/docs/answer/2463296).

### Report malicious behavior in Gemini in Workspace apps

If you get a response that’s inaccurate or that you feel is unsafe, you can give feedback on that response to let us know. [Learn how to report abuse in Gemini in Workspace apps](https://support.google.com/mail/answer/16115590).

## Related resources

- [Avoid and report phishing emails](https://support.google.com/mail/answer/8253)
- [Report a violation](https://support.google.com/docs/answer/2463296)
- [Report abuse in Gemini in Workspace apps](https://support.google.com/mail/answer/16115590)

# Document 247: How MEDITECH is integrating Gemini into highly regulated healthcare workflows

Source URL: https://workspace.google.com/blog/customer-stories/how-meditech-integrating-gemini-highly-regulated-healthcare-workflows?hl=en
Product area: workspace-site
Original file: docs/how-meditech-is-integrating-gemini-into-highly-regulated-healthcare-workflows-eb7c1da4.md

# How MEDITECH is integrating Gemini into highly regulated healthcare workflows

Source: https://workspace.google.com/blog/customer-stories/how-meditech-integrating-gemini-highly-regulated-healthcare-workflows?hl=en

Customer Stories

# How MEDITECH is integrating Gemini into highly regulated healthcare workflows

March 22, 2025

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Meditech.max-2500x2500.png](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Meditech.max-2500x2500.png)

##### Kimberly Hansen

Manager of Corporate Technologies, MEDITECH

##### Google Workspace Newsletter

Keep up with the evolving future of work and collaboration with insights, trends, and product news.

[SIGN UP](https://workspace.google.com/?source=gafb-blog_article-body-en#newsletter)

**Snapshot:**

- **7 hours saved** per employee per week with Gemini
- **Improved job satisfaction** as team members can focus on more strategic projects
- **Faster, simpler access** to complex industry information with Gemini summarization

---

### Origin story

In their earliest days, electronic health record (EHR) platforms were so complicated and cumbersome that they were only used by organizations with the resources to maintain enormous servers. These platforms are now fast-moving decision-making tools and are must-haves for healthcare teams. [MEDITECH](https://ehr.meditech.com/) provides an enterprise EHR solution that supports healthcare customers of all sizes — from four-bed care facilities to integrated delivery networks with thousands of care sites — across more than 23 countries.

Serving all of our customers, regardless of their size, requires us to be efficient and able to easily communicate across our company. In 2012, we strategically transitioned from a proprietary collaboration suite to Google Workspace, a cloud-based solution. Recognizing its potential to help transform the way we work and the familiarity our team already had with it in their personal lives, we embraced this technological shift. Since then, MEDITECH has experienced significant growth and evolution in tandem with Workspace.

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Screenshot_2025-03-21_at_1.23.21PM.max-2200x2200.png](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Screenshot_2025-03-21_at_1.23.21PM.max-2200x2200.png)

See how MEDITECH is enhancing productivity and efficiency with Gemini and Google Workspace.

### The switch to Google Workspace and Gemini

I’ve been with MEDITECH for 30 years now, and it’s my job to ensure our staff members have the tools they need to succeed. As interest grew around integrating generative AI into our internal workflows, it fell to me to explore our options. Introducing new technology into MEDITECH's ecosystem is especially complicated due to the highly regulated nature of our industry, and we’re committed to complying with data privacy and security regulations. [Gemini](https://workspace.google.com/solutions/ai/) is a covered service under our BAA with Google, meaning internal data will be private and won’t be used to train the AI model. Having this assurance made choosing Gemini a no brainer.

Working closely with Google Cloud Premier Partner, [Onix](https://www.onixnet.com/), we started our Gemini rollout with a trial group of users spread across different teams and divisions. We wanted to ensure that a wide range of responsibilities were represented in the sample group, and those users all had several use cases that they wanted to test. Onix was instrumental in supporting these users throughout the trial period; they had a dedicated [Google Site](https://sites.google.com/) that offered learning tips and tricks, as well as training and transformation lab recordings to empower our teams to push the limits of what Gemini could do for us.

Our team created a spreadsheet to track each division’s use cases, specific feedback on Gemini’s features, and any results from the tests. We found that we were able to save an average of seven hours per employee per week by reducing some manual tasks such as notetaking during meetings or drafting and revising emails and documents. One common use case that saves time across our teams is the ability to automatically summarize our [Google Meet](https://workspace.google.com/products/meet/) calls.

We’re also using Gemini to keep our teams up to date on things like industry trends. We’re constantly reviewing new healthcare regulations and industry research in order to stay ahead of compliance requirements or potential opportunities. And now employees are using Gemini to rapidly consolidate, summarize, and distribute that news through [Google Chat](https://workspace.google.com/products/chat/), so we can ensure all of our teams are well-informed and equipped to build the best possible solutions for our customers.

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/MEDITECH_productivity.max-1400x1400.jpg](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/MEDITECH_productivity.max-1400x1400.jpg)

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/MEDITECH_productivity.max-1400x1400.jpg](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/MEDITECH_productivity.max-1400x1400.jpg)

With a wide range of use cases across departments and functions, MEDITECH is using Gemini to save its employees hours of manual work.

### Looking ahead

When I showed our leadership team the results of our Gemini trial, our president and CEO just said, “Adopting Gemini is the right thing to do.” Since then, we’ve been able to roll out Gemini to our entire organization, and we’re seeing new use cases every day. One of our developers recently shared that he’s scheduling Meet calls with himself to share his new ideas out loud, asking Gemini to take notes for him, and by the end of the call, he’ll have a summarized, organized version of his thoughts.

Our teams overall are feeling more satisfied with their jobs because they’re able to automate these more mundane tasks and focus on innovation. I’m excited to see how this energy trickles down into our products and the many new ways we’ll be able to support our customers.

Posted in

- [Customer Stories](https://workspace.google.com/blog/customer-stories)
- [AI and Machine Learning](https://workspace.google.com/blog/ai-and-machine-learning)

##### Related articles

[![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/5522i_GWS_AI_small_business_advantage_Blog_h.max-700x700.jpg](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/5522i_GWS_AI_small_business_advantage_Blog_h.max-700x700.jpg)

# Document 248: How Pepperdine University, Pennymac, and Adore Me use Gemini for Google Workspace to focus on what they do best

Source URL: https://workspace.google.com/blog/customer-stories/how-customers-use-gemini-google-workspace-focus-what-they-do-best?hl=en
Product area: workspace-site
Original file: docs/how-pepperdine-university-pennymac-and-adore-me-use-gemini-for-google-workspace-22bf5ae6.md

# How Pepperdine University, Pennymac, and Adore Me use Gemini for Google Workspace to focus on what they do best

Source: https://workspace.google.com/blog/customer-stories/how-customers-use-gemini-google-workspace-focus-what-they-do-best?hl=en

Customer Stories

# How Pepperdine University, Pennymac, and Adore Me use Gemini for Google Workspace to focus on what they do best

October 24, 2024

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Screenshot_2024-10-22_at_3.30.33PM.max-2200x2200.png](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Screenshot_2024-10-22_at_3.30.33PM.max-2200x2200.png)

##### The Google Workspace Team

##### Google Workspace Newsletter

Keep up with the evolving future of work and collaboration with insights, trends, and product news.

[SIGN UP](https://workspace.google.com/?source=gafb-blog_article-body-en#newsletter)

#### Gemini for Google Workspace is doing more than saving us time. It’s creating new value and more ways to serve our customers than we couldn’t have imagined before.

Ranjan Roy, VP of Strategy at Adore Me

**Snapshot:**

Organizations of all kinds — from universities to ecommerce companies to large financial services firms —  are uncovering new ways to use [Gemini for Google Workspace](https://workspace.google.com/blog/product-announcements/gemini-for-google-workspace) to accelerate their missions and growth. Discover:

- How [Pepperdine University](https://www.pepperdine.edu/) enhances learning and productivity
- How [Pennymac](https://www.pennymac.com/) is finding innovative ways to use generative AI in the highly regulated financial services industry
- How [Adore Me](https://www.adoreme.com/) reduced 35 hours of work to 30 minutes

---

### New ways of working with Gemini

Since introducing [Gemini for Google Workspace to enterprise organizations](https://workspace.google.com/blog/product-announcements/gemini-for-google-workspace), we’ve seen our customers embrace it to forge new ways of working around the globe. Here’s how leaders from [Pepperdine University](https://www.pepperdine.edu/), [Pennymac](https://www.pennymac.com/), and [Adore Me](https://www.adoreme.com/) are exploring it within their organizations.

### Improving accessibility and lesson plans at Pepperdine University

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/pepperdine_logo.max-2200x2200.png](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/pepperdine_logo.max-2200x2200.png)

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/pepperdine_logo.max-2200x2200.png](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/pepperdine_logo.max-2200x2200.png)

Pepperdine University specializes in strengthening the lives of students through purpose, service, and leadership. Jonathan See, its chief information officer, leads the university’s approach to using technology across its campuses to unify the student experience.

He first introduced [Google Workspace](https://workspace.google.com/intl/en_uk/) to Pepperdine to improve collaboration across groups, communities, and teams. “We used to drive an hour to another campus for a meeting. With [Google Meet](https://workspace.google.com/intl/en_uk/products/meet/), we shifted to a virtual-meeting culture,” says See. “My IT team now has more than 100 people spread across the US.”

After a successful shift to a remote-first work environment, See and his team saw a chance to take another technological leap forward with Gemini. “I see gen AI as a great tool for our faculty and students,” says See. “Faculty can create more customized lesson plans; students can use it for idea generation to support their research.”  He’s exploring how to use Gemini to educate students on generative AI literacy before sending them into the workforce.

The teams behind the university’s administrative work are also rapidly adopting Gemini to write meeting minutes and create custom images for Google Slides presentations. “Our Office for Student Accessibility has seen a tremendous benefit in using generative AI,” says See. “Live captioning and translation in Google Meet are closing huge gaps in equity and accessibility by bringing down the language barrier for some of our students.”

### Securely working faster in a highly regulated industry with Pennymac

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/pennymac_larger_logo.max-1200x1200.png](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/pennymac_larger_logo.max-1200x1200.png)

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/pennymac_larger_logo.max-1200x1200.png](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/pennymac_larger_logo.max-1200x1200.png)

Due to strict regulations and laws, financial services is often seen as an industry associated with slow change, but Pennymac uses Workspace to protect its customers and maintain internal confidentiality while still moving quickly. As one the largest lenders and mortgage servicers in the US, it’s constantly on the lookout for ways to improve its customer experience. Pennymac ​​ believes that they can achieve that by investing more into their technology platforms.

The team trusts Workspace to keep its data safe and secure while keeping open lines of communication throughout the organization. Pennymac brought in Gemini to make its day-to-day operations even more efficient. They believe in democratizing Gemini and putting it into the hands of employees across many different departments. The more they can test, the more efficiencies they can find, the faster they can reduce costs, and ultimately pass savings on to their customers.

Pennymac’s HR team has found success using Gemini to create job descriptions as well as getting started on policy and procedure documents. On a more technical level, Pennymac is also testing Gemini in concert with [Vertex AI](https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai?hl=en). By drawing on its proprietary data, the company can ask Gemini questions about regulations and best practices to improve the underwriting process.

### Cutting out “soul-sucking” work to focus on creative problem-solving at Adore Me

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Adore_Me_logo.max-1000x1000.png](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Adore_Me_logo.max-1000x1000.png)

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Adore_Me_logo.max-1000x1000.png](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Adore_Me_logo.max-1000x1000.png)

Adore Me launched in 2012 as the first lingerie brand to offer extended sizing across a wide range of styles and categories. As a direct-to-consumer company, it relies heavily on technology to reach its customers. It was already using AI and machine learning extensively to personalize product recommendations and improve the overall customer experience, which put it in a prime position to integrate generative AI into its workflows.  
  
“We saw so much opportunity with generative AI, but we wanted to bring in a solution that would help onboard and educate people. We also needed something that everyone, especially our security team, felt comfortable with,” Ranjan Roy, VP of strategy at Adore Me, explains. “That strategy drove us to Gemini for Google Workspace.”

“When we started rolling out Gemini, we would ask our teammates, ‘What is the most soul-sucking part of your job? What is the part of your job that you hate the most?’ We wanted to focus on reducing that workload,” he says.

For Adore Me’s copywriting team, the most monotonous task was keeping up with hundreds of new product descriptions. The team mapped out a prompt library to help draft descriptions monthly, reducing more than 35 hours of work down to 30 minutes.

“We’ve really embraced Gemini as part of our work culture,” Roy shares. “We've held image generation ‘prompt-offs’ where we came up with a challenge: Two people wrote prompts for Gemini, shared the results, and others voted for their favorites. It’s helped teach more of our users how to effectively use or build on prompts in our existing library to customize their output.”

The Adore Me copywriting team used to regularly produce speaker bios for conferences and webinars. With the company’s ever-expanding prompt library, speakers can now grab a prompt, fill in key points such as work experiences or career highlights, and Gemini does the rest.

“Gemini is doing more than saving us time. It’s creating new value and more ways to serve our customers that we couldn’t have imagined before,” Roy shares.

From bringing down language barriers to ensuring regulatory compliance or transforming monotonous tasks into creative competitions, these three organizations show us that the possibilities with generative AI are endless.

---

See how you can use [Gemini for Workspace](https://workspace.google.com/solutions/ai/) to improve life for employees and customers today.

# Document 249: How to use Gems - Gemini Apps Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/gemini/answer/15236405
Product area: gemini-apps
Original file: docs/how-to-use-gems-gemini-apps-help-9dd6dcf2.md

[Skip to main content](https://support.google.com/gemini/answer/15236405#search-form)

## Get started with Gems in Gemini Apps

# How to use Gems

[Previous: What you'll learn](https://support.google.com/gemini/answer/15235906?hl=en&ref_topic=15236104)[Next: Get a quick start with premade Gems](https://support.google.com/gemini/answer/15235907?hl=en&ref_topic=15236104)

![](https://storage.googleapis.com/support-kms-prod/zY0QaDiBw6WBTWdpCduEnUxFkrkLwFvpc405)

With Gems, you can create specific and repeatable instructions for Gemini to follow.

## How Gems can be helpful

By creating a Gem with all of the info about your goals and preferences, or using a premade Gem designed for a certain action, the Gem is a shortcut every time you want to explore a topic with similar instructions. You can create different Gems for your unique needs and reuse them to simplify your prompts and questions in Gemini Apps.

![](https://storage.googleapis.com/support-kms-prod/sL33rV35aqSspo7IKobLBg2bTqo0lmbn0yVJ)

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| #### ![](https://storage.googleapis.com/support-kms-prod/1TEShTsEgVgMJA8eIUBB8OitAImNslHCCT3z) | ### Examples |

With Gems, you can get responses that follow custom instructions like:

- **Workout requirements.** Create a Gem that includes any physical limitations and time limits to make sure Gemini maximizes your time and provides suitable movements for each new workout.
- **Diet preferences.** Create a Gem that has specific ingredients to include, or a diet to follow like vegan or high protein, every time you ask for a new recipe.
- **Gardening specifications.** Create a Gem based on your garden's climate and light exposure to get relevant ideas whenever you ask Gemini gardening questions.

# Document 250: How we're protecting your Google Workspace data in the era of generative AI

Source URL: https://workspace.google.com/blog/identity-and-security/protecting-your-data-era-generative-ai?hl=en
Product area: workspace-site
Original file: docs/how-we-re-protecting-your-google-workspace-data-in-the-era-of-generative-ai-00c87e2c.md

# How we're protecting your Google Workspace data in the era of generative AI

Source: https://workspace.google.com/blog/identity-and-security/protecting-your-data-era-generative-ai?hl=en

Identity and Security

# How we're protecting your Google Workspace data in the era of generative AI

August 11, 2023

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/20526_GWS_GenAI_Privacy_Blog_Header_V3.4.max-2500x2500.png](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/20526_GWS_GenAI_Privacy_Blog_Header_V3.4.max-2500x2500.png)

##### Yulie Kwon Kim

VP, Product - Workspace

##### Google Workspace Newsletter

Keep up with the evolving future of work and collaboration with insights, trends, and product news.

[SIGN UP](https://workspace.google.com/?source=gafb-blog_article-body-en#newsletter)

**Editorial note:** Duet AI for Google Workspace is now Google Workspace with Gemini. Additional information on security and compliance controls for business, education, and public-sector customers is available in the [Privacy Hub](https://support.google.com/a/answer/15706919). You can also [download our privacy, security, and compliance guide](https://workspace.google.com/learning/content/gemini-privacy-security-compliance-whitepaper?e=48754805).

---

For nearly 25 years, Google has built helpful, secure products that give users choice and control over their data. It’s a bedrock principle for us. This was the case back when we first launched Gmail in 2004, and it remains true as we enter the era of generative AI with [Duet AI in Google Workspace](https://workspace.google.com/blog/product-announcements/duet-ai). Across Google Cloud and Workspace, we’ve long shared robust [privacy commitments](https://cloud.google.com/privacy) that outline how we protect user data and prioritize privacy. Generative AI doesn’t change these commitments — it actually reaffirms their importance.

We’ve designed our technology so that your [Google Workspace](https://workspace.google.com/) content (emails, documents, etc.) will not be shared with other users without permission. This commitment covers all of our Google Workspace products for personal and business use, including Gmail, Google Docs, Sheets, Slides, Drive, Calendar, Meet, and Chat.

### Our privacy commitments to all Workspace users

We want to be completely clear that generative AI does not change our foundational privacy protections for giving users choice and control over their data. To that end, here are key facts about how Workspace data is handled:

- **Your data is your data.** The content that you put into Google Workspace services (emails, documents, etc.) is yours. We never sell your data, and you can delete your content or export it.
- **Your data stays in Workspace.** We do not use your Workspace data to train or improve the underlying generative AI and large language models that power Bard, Search, and other systems outside of Workspace without permission.
- **Your privacy is protected.** Interactions with intelligent Workspace features, such as accepting or rejecting spelling suggestions, or reporting spam, are anonymized and/or aggregated and may be used to improve or develop helpful Workspace features like spam protection, spell check, and [autocomplete](https://blog.google/products/gmail/gmail-ai-features/). This extends to new features we are currently developing like improved prompt suggestions that help Workspace users get the best results from Duet AI features. These features are developed with strict [privacy protections](https://support.google.com/mail/answer/10400210) that keep users in control. (See below for more detail on additional privacy, security, and compliance commitments we make for business customers).
- **Your content is not used for ads targeting.** As a reminder, Google does not collect, scan, or use your content in Google Workspace services for advertising purposes.

These are the privacy commitments that Workspace extends to all of its users. But they aren’t just words. To ensure we continually meet these high standards, [independent auditors validate](https://workspace.google.com/learn-more/security/security-whitepaper/page-5.html) our practices against international standards and best practices.

### Security and compliance commitments for business, education, and public-sector customers

When Google Workspace commercial customers adopt [Duet AI for Google Workspace Enterprise](https://workspace.google.com/blog/product-announcements/duet-ai-now-available-preorder) they get the same robust data protection and security standards that come with all Google Workspace services, with specific protections for businesses, education, and public-sector customers:

- **Your interactions with Duet AI stay within your organization.** Duet AI stores any prompts or generated content alongside your Workspace content and does not share them outside your organization.
- **Your existing Google Workspace protections are automatically applied.** Duet AI brings the same enterprise-grade security as the rest of Google Workspace, automatically applying your organization’s existing controls and data handling practices, such as data-regions policies and Data Loss Prevention.
- **Your content is not used for any other customers**. None of your content is used for model training outside of your domain without permission.

Workspace’s robust security and privacy commitments for customers can be found [here](https://support.google.com/a/answer/60762?hl=en), and business or public-sector organizations that want to learn more about Duet AI for Google Workspace Enterprise can find more [here](https://workspace.google.com/blog/product-announcements/duet-ai-now-available-preorder).

- [Identity and Security](https://workspace.google.com/blog/identity-and-security)
- [AI and Machine Learning](https://workspace.google.com/blog/ai-and-machine-learning)

# Document 251: Hydro Ottawa lights up the future of work with Gemini for Google Workspace

Source URL: https://workspace.google.com/blog/customer-stories/hydro-ottawa-lights-future-work-gemini-google-workspace?hl=en
Product area: workspace-site
Original file: docs/hydro-ottawa-lights-up-the-future-of-work-with-gemini-for-google-workspace-f4c247f1.md

# Hydro Ottawa lights up the future of work with Gemini for Google Workspace

Source: https://workspace.google.com/blog/customer-stories/hydro-ottawa-lights-future-work-gemini-google-workspace?hl=en

Customer Stories

# Hydro Ottawa lights up the future of work with Gemini for Google Workspace

October 30, 2024

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Reduced_Hydro_Ottawa_hero.max-2500x2500.png](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Reduced_Hydro_Ottawa_hero.max-2500x2500.png)

##### The Google Workspace Team

##### Google Workspace Newsletter

Keep up with the evolving future of work and collaboration with insights, trends, and product news.

[SIGN UP](https://workspace.google.com/?source=gafb-blog_article-body-en#newsletter)

“The collaborative nature of the Workspace tools has not only accelerated our existing processes but also shed light on any shortcomings within them.”— **Santhosh Jayasankar**, **Manager of Enterprise Architecture**, **Hydro Ottawa Limited**

**Snapshot:** Powering Canada’s capital city requires powerful tools. That’s why [Hydro Ottawa](https://www.hydroottawa.com/en) relies on [Google Workspace](https://workspace.google.com/intl/en_ca/). Learn how Workspace helps the electricity provider:

- Harness the power of secure collaboration
- Augment everyday tasks daily with [Gemini for Google Workspace](https://workspace.google.com/intl/en_ca/solutions/ai/)
- Remove friction from day-to-day processes to deliver better, more cost-effective services

---

### The mission and the challenge

What if generative AI could help power a nation’s capital? Hydro Ottawa is on a journey to find out.

Since 1916, Hydro Ottawa has managed critical infrastructure for Canada’s capital, distributing electricity to 364,000 homes and businesses. At Hydro Ottawa, security is the cornerstone of their operations. They take a zero-risk approach when adopting new technologies, going beyond their core offering to provide customers with safe and reliable power.

This is why the company chose Google Workspace as its collaboration platform in 2020 and why it opted into the Gemini for Google Workspace pilot in 2024. Here’s how Hydro Ottawa uses Workspace and Gemini to power productivity and generate more reliable, cost-friendly services.

### The switch to Google Workspace and Gemini

Hydro Ottawa’s journey with Workspace began with [Google Meet](https://meet.google.com/) at the onset of the COVID-19 pandemic. When teams couldn’t meet in person, the company needed a secure solution that could keep them connected with as little friction as possible — and Meet seamlessly filled that need. By the time **Theresa Sippert** joined as **Manager of Information Management** less than a year later, Workspace fueled nearly all of Hydro Ottawa’s communication and collaboration needs.

“Since Hydro Ottawa had already adopted Google Workspace, it was easy for me to integrate into the team and get up to speed while working remotely during COVID-19,” Sippert says. “Everything I do now, from asking quick questions in [Google Chat](https://chat.google.com/), to writing in [Docs](https://www.google.com/docs/about/), to building presentations in [Slides](https://workspace.google.com/intl/en_ca/products/slides/), happens in Workspace.”

During the pandemic, the Director on Sippert’s team also started a bi-weekly Meet to give people a place to connect and socialize with their team. “It was more of a networking moment than a business meeting and was a fantastic way to feel like part of a team when we couldn’t be on site,” she says.

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/HydroOttawa2.max-1600x1600.jpg](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/HydroOttawa2.max-1600x1600.jpg)

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/HydroOttawa2.max-1600x1600.jpg](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/HydroOttawa2.max-1600x1600.jpg)

Google Workspace fulfills Hydro Ottawa’s remote and in-office collaboration needs

As generative AI expanded across industries, the team wondered how to safely bring its power to Hydro Ottawa. “If you want generative AI to work for your organization, you have to take it to where the data is,” says **Santhosh Jayasankar**, **Manager of Enterprise Architecture** at Hydro Ottawa. “Since much of our unstructured data, like emails and documents, is stored in Google Workspace, we already had robust security measures in place through the platform. This made joining the Gemini for Google Workspace pilot a no-brainer.”

The pilot began with 50 people and quickly grew to over 100 as team members discovered the value Gemini could bring to their everyday tasks. Deployed across all divisions to give every team an opportunity to explore its capabilities, Hydro Ottawa uses Gemini to:

- create job posts for recruiters
- conduct legal and technical research
- write policies
- generate content for internal and public-facing communications across channels
- summarize large documents and Google Slides presentations
- provide structured meeting agendas

“Collaboration is an important aspect of any work environment, and if you can’t do that with the tools you have, you’re blocked before you’ve started,” Jayasankar says. “The collaborative nature of the Workspace tools has not only accelerated our existing processes but also shed light on any shortcomings within them.”

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/HydroOttawa3.max-1600x1600.png](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/HydroOttawa3.max-1600x1600.png)

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/HydroOttawa3.max-1600x1600.png](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/HydroOttawa3.max-1600x1600.png)

Hydro Ottawa uses Google Workspace to keep teams connected

### Looking ahead

Hydro Ottawa’s usage of Workspace and Gemini continues to grow, with additional users onboarded every week. The team has also started using Gemini in the side panel of Workspace applications to make it even easier to perform quick tasks like summarizing emails or documents, or searching shared drives to access relevant information.

"The Google Gemini pilot gave us a lens into the productivity gains and automation opportunities that AI could bring to our organization as we work towards the energy transition in our sector,” says **Mark Fernandes**, **Chief Information & Technology Officer** at Hydro Ottawa. “It enhances our employee experience and puts the necessary tools into their hands to better serve our customers."

Sippert agrees: “AI is everywhere and the Gemini for Google Workspace pilot was an opportunity to shape the technology before it became available to the masses. We got to be on the ground floor of something exciting and be part of something bigger than ourselves.”

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/HydroOttawa4.max-1600x1600.png](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/HydroOttawa4.max-1600x1600.png)

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/HydroOttawa4.max-1600x1600.png](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/HydroOttawa4.max-1600x1600.png)

Google Meet gives teams a place to connect from anywhere

# Document 252: Improve & polish your writing - Google Workspace Learning Center

Source URL: https://support.google.com/a/users/answer/14512929
Product area: workspace-learning-center
Original file: docs/improve-polish-your-writing-google-workspace-learning-center-3d50e397.md

# Improve & Polish Your Writing with Gemini in Google Workspace

**Source:** Google Workspace Learning Center  
**URL:** https://support.google.com/a/users/answer/14512929  
**Guide section:** *Get started with Gemini for Google Workspace*  
**Topic:** Using Gemini in Google Docs to rewrite and refine writing on a computer.

---

## Key Excerpts

> _You can use this feature only if your organization supports it. For help, contact your administrator._

> This guide explains how to improve and polish your writing using Google Workspace with Gemini on your computer.

> Use Google Workspace with Gemini to quickly rewrite content. Shorten, elaborate, or adjust the tone using Gemini.

> You can even use Gemini to turn existing text into a new form, such as a poem or a script.

### Example Prompts

Original examples from the article:

- `Rewrite this content like you're telling a story.`
- `Turn this content into a short blog post with bullet points highlighting the top 3 benefits for customers.`

---

## Before Using Gemini in Google Workspace

Google provides several important cautions and usage notes:

- **Availability depends on your organization.** If the feature isn’t available, contact your Google Workspace administrator.
- **Gemini suggestions do not represent Google’s views** and should not be attributed to Google.
- **Do not rely on Gemini for professional advice**, including:
  - Medical advice
  - Legal advice
  - Financial advice
  - Other professional guidance
- **Gemini may generate inaccurate or inappropriate information.**
- User feedback helps improve Gemini’s usefulness and safety.
- Enterprise end users may submit feedback about generative AI features in Google Workspace.
  - Before submitting feedback, users are informed that feedback:
    - Should not contain personal, sensitive, or confidential information
    - May be reviewed by humans

---

## Main Feature: Rewrite Content in Google Docs

Gemini can help rewrite selected text in a Google Doc. It can be used to:

- Shorten content
- Elaborate on content
- Adjust tone
- Transform text into another format, such as:
  - A poem
  - A script
  - A story
  - A blog post
  - Bullet points

---

## How to Rewrite Content with Gemini in Google Docs

1. On your computer, open a **Google Doc** file.
2. Click where you want to write in the document.
3. Select the content you want to rewrite.
4. On the left, click **Help me write**.
5. Choose a rewriting option, or enter a custom prompt.
6. Review Gemini’s response and refine it as needed.
7. When finished, choose one of the following:
   - **Insert** — Adds the new content below the original.
   - **Replace** — Swaps the original content with the new Gemini-generated version.

---

## Related Learning Center Navigation

This guide is part of a Gemini for Google Workspace learning sequence:

- **Previous:** *Summarize content & organize data*
- **Next:** *Illustrate a presentation*

Additional related resource:

- **Docs Editors Help Center:** Learn more about Gemini writing assistance in Google Docs.

---

## Additional Notes

- Google promotes advanced Google Workspace features for businesses with a link to try Google Workspace.
- The page includes article feedback tools where users can report issues such as:
  - Inaccurate information
  - Hard-to-understand content
  - Missing information
  - Irrelevant content
  - Minor errors
  - Other suggestions
- Users are reminded not to share personal information in feedback.

---

# Document 253: Improve your Chat messages with Refine - Google Chat Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/chat/answer/16512046
Product area: chat
Original file: docs/improve-your-chat-messages-with-refine-google-chat-help-418bdcd3.md

[Skip to main content](https://support.google.com/chat/answer/16512046#search-form)

# Improve your Chat messages with Refine

To improve the clarity and tone of your message before you send it, use Refine to edit your message in Google Chat.

**Important:** This feature requires an eligible Google Workspace or Google AI plan. [Learn about Gemini features and plans](https://support.google.com/docs/answer/13952129).

You can use Refine in the following languages:

- English
- French
- German
- Italian
- Japanese
- Korean
- Portuguese
- Spanish

If your draft text isn’t in these languages, you receive an error message.

## Refine your message

1. On your computer, open [Google Chat](https://chat.google.com/) or [Gmail](https://mail.google.com/).


   - **In Gmail:** On the left, click **Chat**.
2. Enter a message in Chat.
3. To refine your message:
   - Click Formatting options ![](https://storage.googleapis.com/support-kms-prod/fukkWXYGVSLm6EY3Ddsh6vsxbdmNNKAA0i5X).
   - Activate the Refine hint text:
     - **Mac:** Press **Option + H**.
     - **Windows:** Press **Alt + H**.
   - Highlight the text you want to refine.
4. Click Refine ![](https://storage.googleapis.com/support-kms-prod/hmj7qlpK26lHFvYgUMfB8qf9ge6ost3RQ54Y).

   - Above the Compose area, a refined version of your message displays.
5. To replace your original text with the refined version, click **Replace**.

**Tips:**

- To dismiss the Refine hint text and prevent it from appearing again throughout the current compose session, press **Esc**. The Refine hint text appears again after a page refresh.
- To manage the Refine hint text suggestion, in the Chat settings, under "Message & Media," turn **Smart Compose** on or off.

## Turn Refine on or off

1. On your computer, open [Gmail](https://mail.google.com/).
2. At the top right, click Settings ![](https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/GMF3aAfNCij0aDjnx4S9qlZSB8vSb4UDdxlzPPqHAFukiP3k4Q8RGBq6Nr2jGFMPpD4=h36)![and then](https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/3_l97rr0GvhSP2XV5OoCkV2ZDTIisAOczrSdzNCBxhIKWrjXjHucxNwocghoUa39gw=h36)**See all settings**.
3. Under the "General" tab, in the "Google Workspace smart features" section, click **Manage Workspace smart features settings**.
4. Turn on **Smart features in Google Workspace**.
5. At the bottom, click **Save**.

## Learn about Gemini feature suggestions

- Gemini feature suggestions don’t represent Google’s views, and should not be attributed to Google.
- Don’t rely on Gemini features as medical, legal, financial, or other professional advice.
- Gemini features may suggest inaccurate or inappropriate information. Your feedback makes Gemini more helpful and safe.
- Enterprise end users can submit feedback about their experience using this feature. End users are informed before they submit feedback that the feedback data shouldn’t contain personal, sensitive, or confidential information.

[Learn how to use Google Workspace with Gemini & double-checking responses](https://support.google.com/a/users/answer/16304582).

## Related resources

- [Send a direct message](https://support.google.com/chat/answer/7652736)
- [Reply to a message in Google Chat](https://support.google.com/chat/answer/7654374)
- [Use Smart Compose in Google Chat](https://support.google.com/chat/answer/13568047)
