# Document 254: Incubeta drives AI performance with adoption of Gemini for Google Workspace

Source URL: https://workspace.google.com/blog/customer-stories/incubeta-drives-ai-performance-with-gemini-google-workspace?hl=en
Product area: workspace-site
Original file: docs/incubeta-drives-ai-performance-with-adoption-of-gemini-for-google-workspace-0977643f.md

# Incubeta drives AI performance with adoption of Gemini for Google Workspace

Source: https://workspace.google.com/blog/customer-stories/incubeta-drives-ai-performance-with-gemini-google-workspace?hl=en

Customer Stories

# Incubeta drives AI performance with adoption of Gemini for Google Workspace

October 16, 2024

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Copy_of_Google_Workspace_Blog_Hero_images.max-2500x2500.png](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Copy_of_Google_Workspace_Blog_Hero_images.max-2500x2500.png)

##### The Google Workspace Team

##### Google Workspace Newsletter

Keep up with the evolving future of work and collaboration with insights, trends, and product news.

[SIGN UP](https://workspace.google.com/?source=gafb-blog_article-body-en#newsletter)

**"Gemini has revolutionized the way we work.” — Rob Ratcliffe, group CIO, Incubeta**

---

Incubeta, a global digital marketing leader, recently announced its successful implementation of [Gemini for Google Workspace](https://c212.net/c/link/?t=0&l=en&o=4272368-5&h=2765665176&u=https%3A%2F%2Fworkspace.google.com%2Fsolutions%2Fai%2F&a=Gemini+for+Google+Workspace), showcasing the transformative power of generative AI (gen AI)  for businesses. With Gemini for Workspace, Gemini — Google's AI-powered assistant — is built right into the collaboration apps relied on by more than 3 billion users and over 10 million paying customers, including Gmail, Docs, Sheets, and more.

Incubeta has leveraged Gemini for Google Workspace to enhance productivity, streamline workflows, and unlock new levels of creativity across various teams, including client delivery. Incubeta's experience with Gemini highlights the platform's versatility and ease of use. A company-wide trial and internal knowledge-sharing initiatives fostered rapid adoption across the organization.

**Key benefits have included:**

- **Increased efficiency:** Gemini's AI-powered tools, such as Help Me Write and Gemini Search, have significantly reduced time spent on tasks like client prep time, email and document creation, and market research, allowing employees to focus on higher-value activities.
- **Enhanced creativity:**Gemini has empowered Incubeta's teams to explore new creative possibilities, generating innovative marketing campaigns and delivering more impactful results for clients by driving production time down from days to minutes.
- **Improved collaboration:** Gemini has helped to foster a more collaborative environment, enabling teams to share knowledge and insights more effectively.
- **Streamlined reporting and insights:** Incubeta is using Gemini to curate proactive, conversational, and context-driven insights for its clients, resulting in up to 50% ROI improvement for short-term campaigns.

"Gemini has revolutionized the way we work," says **Rob Ratcliffe**, **group CIO**, at **Incubeta**. "From our development teams streamlining code refinements to our client teams automating mundane tasks, Gemini has freed up valuable time and resources, allowing us to focus on delivering exceptional results for our clients. Gemini has not only saved us time but has also unlocked creativity, offering new opportunities for teams to experiment with image curation, ad copy generation, and the ability to think about client campaigns with a fresh perspective," adds Rob.

"Incubeta's success with Gemini for Google Workspace is a testament to the platform's ability to empower businesses with AI," says **Justin Anderson**, **director**, **Google Workspace UK/I**, **Google Cloud**. "By embracing Gemini's capabilities, Incubeta is enhancing productivity, streamlining workflows, and fostering a culture of innovation. We're excited to see how Gemini continues to help Incubeta deliver exceptional value to their clients and achieve their business goals."

Today, more than 100,000 organizations empower their employees with Gemini for Google Workspace as a powerful collaboration partner that can act as a coach, source of inspiration, and productivity booster — all while ensuring that each user and organization has [control over their data](https://c212.net/c/link/?t=0&l=en&o=4272368-5&h=253420540&u=https%3A%2F%2Fworkspace.google.com%2Fblog%2Fidentity-and-security%2Fprotecting-your-data-era-generative-ai%3Fe%3D48754805&a=control+over+their+data). Incubeta's successful implementation of Gemini for Workspace underscores the growing importance of deploying gen AI in the modern workplace. Incubeta is paving the way in demonstrating the tangible benefits for businesses seeking to boost productivity, spark innovation, and accelerate growth.

About Incubeta:  
Incubeta is a digital marketing company built specifically to help businesses upgrade their growth. An international team of experts in marketing technology, data, media and creative - Incubeta is a team of over 800 creators, thinkers, makers, and doers.

# Document 255: Introducing Duet AI for Google Workspace

Source URL: https://workspace.google.com/blog/product-announcements/duet-ai?hl=en
Product area: workspace-site
Original file: docs/introducing-duet-ai-for-google-workspace-06556546.md

# Introducing Duet AI for Google Workspace

Source: https://workspace.google.com/blog/product-announcements/duet-ai?hl=en

Product Announcements

# Introducing Duet AI for Google Workspace

May 11, 2023

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Blog_post_2436_x_1200.max-2500x2500.jpg](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Blog_post_2436_x_1200.max-2500x2500.jpg)

##### Aparna Pappu

GM and Vice President, Google Workspace

##### Try Google Workspace at No Cost

Get a business email, all the storage you need, video conferencing, and more.

[SIGN UP](https://workspace.google.com/business/signup/welcome?source=gafb-blog_article-body-en)

**Editorial note: Duet AI for Google Workspace is now Gemini for Google Workspace. [Learn more](https://workspace.google.com/blog/product-announcements/gemini-for-google-workspace).**

---

From the very beginning, Google Workspace was built to allow you to collaborate in real time with other people. Now, you can collaborate with AI. We’ve been embedding the power of generative AI across all the Workspace apps. These features help you write, help you organize, help you visualize, help you accelerate workflows, have richer meetings and much more. This powerful new way of working is what we’re calling **Duet AI for Google Workspace**.

This builds on [the vision](https://youtu.be/6DaJVZBXETE) for Workspace we shared in March and the launch of generative AI features in Gmail and Google Docs to trusted testers. We’ve been thrilled to see how people have started using these tools to help them get things done at work and in their personal lives — including client outreach emails, job applications, project plans, and so much more. Helping people write better is something we’ve been working on for years — our AI-powered grammar, spell check, smart compose and reply have helped people write over 180 billion times in the last year alone.

### Helping you write, even on the go

Duet AI already works behind the scenes in Workspace to help you write — whether it’s refining existing work or helping you get started in both Gmail and Docs. Now we’re bringing this experience to Gmail mobile — imagine being on your phone and having the ability to draft complete responses with just a few words as a prompt. Our initial launch of mobile will be fast-followed by contextual assistance — allowing you to create professional replies that automatically fill in names and other relevant information.

### Creating original images from text, right within Google Slides

A picture is worth a thousand words, but until now, creating unique and compelling visuals for presentations has been a manual and time-consuming process. We’re embedding Duet AI into Slides so you can easily generate images with a few words — the real power of these image models is that they can visualize something that has never existed. Perhaps you’re a marketer doing early concept brainstorming with your creative agency on a light-hearted campaign to get Parisians to go on safari and want to provide clear visual input early in the process to prevent wasted work later on. Now, you can generate an original visual that conveys your unique artistic vision, all from a simple prompt.

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/original_images/Slides_blog.gif](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/original_images/Slides_blog.gif)

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/original_images/Slides_blog.gif](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/original_images/Slides_blog.gif)

Now, you can generate a bespoke image for a Slides presentation with the help of Duet AI.

### Turning ideas into action and data into insights with Google Sheets

Duet AI is here to help you analyze and act on your data in Sheets faster than ever before with automated data classification and the creation of custom plans.

Classification tools understand the context of data in a cell, and can assign a label to it, helping eliminate the burden of manual data entry. Whether you're a product development team analyzing the sentiment of user feedback, or an HR recruiter summarizing input from interviews, this saves time and toil in developing a clear and visually engaging analysis.

Our new *help me organize* capability in Sheets automatically creates custom plans for tasks, projects, or any activity that you want to track or manage — simply describe what you’re trying to accomplish, and Sheets generates a plan that helps you get organized. Whether you’re an event team planning an annual sales conference or a manager coordinating a team offsite, Duet AI helps you create organized plans with tools that give you a running start.

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/original_images/Sheets_blog.gif](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/original_images/Sheets_blog.gif)

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/original_images/Sheets_blog.gif](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/original_images/Sheets_blog.gif)

You can create custom plans for tasks, projects, or any activity you want to track or manage.

### Fostering more meaningful connections in Google Meet

We’re weaving Duet AI into Meet and introducing the ability to generate unique backgrounds for your video calls. This helps users express themselves and deepen connections during video calls while protecting the privacy of their surroundings. Imagine a manager celebrating the employee of the month with a visual mashup of their favorite things during a company town hall meeting, or a sales exec jumping on a call with a prospective customer and using a custom background to reflect the customer’s industry or market. It's a subtle, personal touch to show you care about the people you’re connecting with and what’s important to them. And you can change that visual with an equally stunning and original one — all in just a few clicks.

### Keeping you in the flow of projects with AI building blocks in Docs

Users tell us they love smart canvas because a simple @ mention can save them hours of work by keeping the team focused and collaborating in the document where they’re already working. Now, we’re integrating these capabilities into the new assisted writing experience in Docs. If you're writing a job description, Duet AI will not only help you write the content, it will also include smart chips for information like location and status, and variables for details you’d want to customize like your company name — helping you go from concept to completion faster without ever leaving your document.

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/original_images/Docs_blog.gif](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/original_images/Docs_blog.gif)

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/original_images/Docs_blog.gif](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/original_images/Docs_blog.gif)

Simply enter a topic you’d like to write about, and a draft will instantly be generated for you in Docs, including smart chips for information like location and status.

Our new upgraded neural models for grammar have helped people generate professional-grade writing — not just in English, but also in Spanish, French, Japanese, and more. Now we’re bringing even more powerful capabilities to help people with proofreading, tone, and style in their writing. You’ll see a new proofread suggestion pane that offers suggestions for writing concisely, avoiding repetition, and leveraging a more formal or active voice. Plus, it puts you in total control over when you see proofread suggestions and how you act on them.

### Helping users unleash creativity while increasing productivity

Since March, we’ve welcomed hundreds of thousands of trusted testers to Workspace Labs. These testers come from enterprise organizations and educational institutions as well as people who use Workspace in their personal lives. We've been encouraged — and inspired — by their feedback.

“Adore Me has built our organization in a way that encourages cross-functional, multidisciplinary projects and the ability to write often presents a roadblock, especially with a highly international team. The ability to quickly create production-worthy copy with generative AI features in Docs and Gmail has been accelerating projects and processes in ways that have even surprised us!”   
— **Romain Liot, Chief Operating Officer, Adore Me**

"Instacart is always looking for opportunities to adopt the latest technological innovations, and by joining this program, we have access to the new features and can discover how generative AI will make an impact for our teams using Google Workspace.” — **JJ Zhuang, Chief Architect, Instacart**

"We’re excited to test out the new generative AI Workspace experiences at Lyft. Whether it be kicking off a plan for a new campaign or drafting an email update to our community of drivers, we’re enthusiastic about how these new tools can help our teams move faster and be more productive." — **Lyft**

### Keeping customers in control

We know that AI is no replacement for the ingenuity, creativity, and smarts of real people. We’re designing our products in accordance with [Google’s AI Principles](https://ai.google/principles/) that keep the user in control, letting AI make suggestions that you’re able to accept, edit, and change. We’ll also deliver the corresponding administrative controls so that IT can set the right policies for their organization.

### Sign up for Workspace Labs

The best way to learn more about AI in Workspace is to use it, which is why we’re excited to open up Workspace Labs to the public and manage the [waitlist](https://workspace.google.com/labs-sign-up/) as we scale to even more users and countries in the weeks ahead.

# Document 257: Learn about Ask Studio in YouTube Studio - YouTube Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/youtube/answer/16291691
Product area: youtube-ai
Original file: docs/learn-about-ask-studio-in-youtube-studio-youtube-help-22ed5861.md

# Learn about Ask Studio in YouTube Studio — Summary

**Source:** YouTube Help — https://support.google.com/youtube/answer/16291691  
**Topic:** Ask Studio, an AI assistant inside YouTube Studio for creators.

---

## Overview

**Ask Studio** is an AI creative partner integrated into **YouTube Studio**. It helps creators:

- Summarize comments and audience feedback
- Understand channel and video performance stats
- Brainstorm video ideas, concepts, and outlines
- Get creative feedback on scripts and unpublished videos

> Ask Studio is an AI creative partner integrated into YouTube Studio that helps you summarize comments and feedback from your videos, understand your video channel stats, and brainstorm ideas and outlines for your next videos.

---

## Eligibility & Availability

- Available to **most YouTube creators globally**.
- Available **only on web browsers on a computer**.
- If the **Ask Studio** icon does not appear in the top corner of YouTube Studio, the creator does **not** currently have access.

### Important Disclaimer

> **Note:** Responses provided by Ask Studio do not reflect YouTube’s views. Quality and accuracy of responses may vary. Use discretion when using Ask Studio and do not rely on it for professional advice, including medical, legal, or financial advice.

---

## How to Get Started

1. Sign in to [YouTube Studio](https://studio.youtube.com/) or access [Ask Studio](https://studio.youtube.com/?d=as) directly.
2. In the **top-right corner**, select **Ask Studio**.
3. Choose a suggested prompt or type your own question.

### Ask Studio in Analytics

Ask Studio can also be used from the **Analytics** page.

- Select suggested prompts to explore channel metrics in more detail.

---

## Best Practices for Using Ask Studio

### 1. Begin with a Clear Objective

Decide what you want help with, such as:

- Community insights
- Comment summaries
- Video analytics
- Performance comparisons
- New video ideas

### 2. Ask Specific Questions

More precise prompts produce better responses.

Instead of:

> "tell me about my channel"

Try:

> "What are the common themes in comments on my recent video about \[topic\]?"

Or:

> "How did my \[video title\] perform recently?"

### 3. Add Videos to the Chat

You can add a video by:

- Typing it into the chat
- Clicking the **Add** button in the chat box

### 4. Use Suggested Prompts

Suggested prompts can be used as starting points, such as:

> "Summarize comments”

You can also use the built-in guide:

> “How can Ask Studio help me?”

### 5. Experiment and Refine

If the first response is not what you need:

- Rephrase the prompt
- Ask follow-up questions
- Narrow the scope of your request

---

## Create and Develop Video Concepts

Ask Studio can help generate, refine, and save video ideas.

---

## Save an Idea Card

When Ask Studio generates a suggestion worth keeping, you can save it as an **Idea card**.

### Steps to Save an Idea Card

1. In your Ask Studio conversation, find the suggestion you want to save.
2. On the Idea card, click the save icon.
3. To find it later, go to the **Inspiration** tab and select **Saved Ideas**.

### Refining Saved Ideas

- Open any saved card from the **Inspiration** tab.
- This launches a new Ask Studio interaction so you can continue developing the idea.

---

## Get Feedback on Your Videos

Ask Studio can provide creative best practices and suggestions for your content, including unpublished videos.

### Script Feedback

- Copy and paste your script directly into Ask Studio.
- Ask for feedback based on creative best practices.

### Feedback Before Publishing

You can select videos from your channel that are:

- Draft
- Private
- Unlisted

Ask Studio can then provide creative feedback before publication.

### Note on Suggestions

Ask Studio’s recommendations are optional.

> These ideas are suggestions. You can always choose if you want to incorporate feedback into your final video.

---

## Data Collection & Use

When you interact with Ask Studio, YouTube collects data related to use of the feature, including:

- Conversations
- Feedback submitted
- Usage data

This information is used to:

- Develop Ask Studio
- Improve the product experience
- Improve YouTube products, services, and machine-learning technologies

### Conversation Retention

There are two retention-related details in the article:

- Conversations connected with your channel as part of Ask Studio interactions are **deleted automatically after 45 days**.
- Ask Studio conversation history is saved in recent conversations for **30 days** before automatic deletion.

### Human Review

To improve quality, human reviewers may read and process conversations.

YouTube says it protects privacy by:

- Disconnecting conversations from your Google Account before reviewers see them
- Using automated tools to recognize and remove personal information, such as:
  - Email addresses
  - Phone numbers

Relevant policies:

- [Privacy Policy](https://policies.google.c

[... summary truncated for context management ...]

---

# Document 258: Learn about availability of Gemini features in Google Vids - Google Docs Editors Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/docs/answer/15609411
Product area: google-vids
Original file: docs/learn-about-availability-of-gemini-features-in-google-vids-google-docs-editors-h-46e8799c.md

# Learn about availability of Gemini features in Google Vids — Summary

**Source:** Google Docs Editors Help — <https://support.google.com/docs/answer/15609411>  
**Topic:** Which Google account types can use Gemini features in **Google Vids**, and what usage limits apply.

---

## Key Excerpts / Important Notes

> **Google Vids is an AI-powered video creation app. Use Vids to create, write, produce, edit, collaborate, and share your videos.**

> **For work or school accounts, your administrator controls access to Google Vids.**

> **Important:**
>
> - For work or school accounts, your admin controls access to Google Vids and can turn off access to generative AI features.
> - Make sure you sign in with the correct Google Account that can access the feature.
> - If your account doesn’t have access to a Google Vids feature, the associated option won’t display in the “Getting started” screen.

---

## Core Availability Rules

You can use **basic Google Vids features** with a Google Account.  
To use **Gemini features in Google Vids**, availability depends on account type and plan.

Account categories covered:

- **Work / Business accounts**
- **Education accounts**
- **Personal Google Accounts**
- **Google AI accounts**
- **Workspace add-ons**
- **Workspace Individual**
- **Workspace for Nonprofits**

---

## Default Monthly Usage Limits

Unless a table specifies otherwise, Gemini feature limits in Google Vids are:

| Feature | Default limit |
|---|---:|
| **AI avatars** | Up to **25 AI avatar generations/month** |
| **AI video clips** | Up to **50 video clip generations/month** |
| **Help me create** | **Unlimited** |
| **AI voice-over** | **Unlimited** |
| **Image generation & editing** | **30/month*** |
| **Background removal** | **Unlimited** |
| **Slides to Vids with AI voiceovers** | **Unlimited** |
| **Music generation** | Account-type specific |
| **Slides to Vids with AI Avatars** | **10/month** |

\* Image generation/editing limits apply **across Workspace products**, including **Google Slides** and **Google Docs**.

---

# Work or Business Accounts

## Google Workspace Business Plans

| Feature | Business Starter | Business Standard | Business Plus |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| **AI avatars** | 10/month | Available | Available |
| **AI video clips** | 20/month | Available | Available |
| **Help me create** | Available | Available | Available |
| **AI voice-over** | Available | Available | Available |
| **Image generation & editing** | 5/month* | Available | Available |
| **Background removal** | Available | Available | Available |
| **Slides to Vids with AI voiceovers** | Available | Available | Available |
| **Slides to Vids with AI avatars** | 5/month | Available | Available |
| **Music generation** | 30s clips: 20/month; full songs: 10/month | 30s clips: 50/month; full songs: 20/month | 30s clips: 50/month; full songs: 20/month |

\* Applies across Workspace products including Google Slides and Google Docs.

---

## Google Workspace Enterprise Plans

| Feature | Enterprise Starter | Enterprise Standard | Enterprise Plus |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| **AI avatars** | 10/month | Available | Available |
| **AI video clips** | 20/month | Available | Available |
| **Help me create** | Available | Available | Available |
| **AI voice-over** | Available | Available | Available |
| **Image generation & editing** | 5/month* | Available | Available |
| **Background removal** | Available | Available | Available |
| **Slides to Vids with AI voiceovers** | Unlimited | Available | Available |
| **Slides to Vids with AI avatars** | 5/month | Available | Available |
| **Music generation** | 30s clips: 20/month; full songs: 10/month | 30s clips: 50/month; full songs: 20/month | 30s clips: 50/month; full songs: 20/month |

\* Applies across Workspace products including Google Slides and Google Docs.

---

## Google Workspace Essentials Plans

| Feature | Essentials Starter | Essentials | Enterprise Essentials | Enterprise Essentials Plus |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| **AI avatars** | Not available | Available | Available | Available |
| **AI video clips** | Not available | Available | Available | Available |
| **Help me create** | Not available | Available | Available | Available |
| **AI voice-over** | Not available | Available | Available | Available |
| **Image generation & editing** | Not available | Available | Available | Available |
| **Background removal** | Not available | Available | Available | Available |
| **Slides to Vids with AI voiceovers** | Not available | Available | Available | Available |
| **Music generation** | Not available | Not available | Not available | Not available |

---

## Google Workspace Individual

Workspace Individual uses a **credit system**.

| Feature | Google Workspace Individual |
|---|---:|
| **AI avatars** | Not available |
| **AI video clips** | 10/month |
| **Help me create** | 5 credits/use |
| **AI voice-over** | 1 credit/voiceover |
| **Image generation & editing** | 1 credit

# Document 259: Learn about Gemini features in Gmail - Gmail Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/mail/answer/16831098?hl=en
Product area: gmail
Original file: docs/learn-about-gemini-features-in-gmail-gmail-help-a7fe2a47.md

# Learn about Gemini Features in Gmail — Summary

**Source:** [Gmail Help: Learn about Gemini features in Gmail](https://support.google.com/mail/answer/16831098?hl=en)

## Core Message

Gemini features in Gmail are designed to make Gmail a **“personal, proactive inbox assistant.”** Availability depends on:

- **Account type**
- **Paid plan eligibility**
- **Country/region**
- **Language**
- **Platform**
- **Local regulations and Google’s AI principles**

Google says availability will expand gradually to more countries and territories.

---

## Key Excerpts

> “Gemini features turn Gmail into your personal, proactive inbox assistant.”

> “We’ll gradually expand to more countries and territories in a way that's consistent with local regulations and our AI principles.”

### Important Availability Notes

> “Access to Gemini features in Gmail varies based on account type. Users with eligible Google AI or Google Workspace plans can access some features globally. However, availability is currently limited to the US for users with personal Gmail accounts without a paid plan.”

> “If a feature is available in multiple languages for Google Workspace or Google AI plans, a personal account in the US can use the feature in those supported languages.”

---

## Gemini Features in Gmail

| Feature | What It Does | Availability |
|---|---|---|
| **AI Inbox** | Reviews top priorities and topics so you can catch up on your inbox. | **Google AI Plus, Pro, or Ultra:** English, **US only**<br>**Platforms:** Web, iOS, Android |
| **AI Overview in Gmail search** | Generates a concise summary or answer from your emails directly above search results when you ask a natural-language question. | **Google AI Plus, Pro, or Ultra:** English, **US only**<br>**Eligible Google Workspace plan:** English, **US only**<br>**Platforms:** Web, iOS*, Android* |
| **AI Overview conversation summaries** | Summarizes long email conversations with key points and replies when you open a thread. | **Google AI Plus, Pro, or Ultra, or eligible Google Workspace plan:** English, French, German, Italian, Japanese, Korean, Portuguese, Spanish; **Global**<br>**Personal Gmail accounts:** English, French, German, Italian, Japanese, Korean, Portuguese, Spanish; **Global**<br>**Platforms:** Web, iOS, Android |
| **Help me schedule** | Proposes times to meet in an email to someone else. | **Eligible Google Workspace plan:** English, **US only**<br>**Platforms:** Web |
| **Help me write** | Drafts new emails or refines existing text based on a prompt. | **Google AI Plus, Pro, or Ultra, or eligible Google Workspace plan:** English, French, German, Italian, Japanese, Korean, Portuguese, Spanish; **Global**<br>**Personal Gmail accounts:** English, French, German, Italian, Japanese, Korean, Portuguese, Spanish; **US only**<br>**Platforms:** Web, iOS, Android |
| **Proofread** | Provides writing suggestions beyond spelling and grammar, including conciseness, tone, and style. | **Google AI Plus, Pro, or Ultra:** English<br>**Eligible Google Workspace plan:** English, **Global**<br>**Platforms:** Web |
| **Suggested Replies** | Offers detailed, context-aware reply suggestions at the bottom of an email thread. | **Google AI Plus, Pro, or Ultra, or eligible Google Workspace plan:** English; **Global**<br>**Personal Gmail accounts:** English; **US only**<br>**Platforms:** Web, iOS, Android |
| **Summarize and draft emails** | Uses the Gmail side panel to help summarize, analyze, and generate content using insights from email messages. | **Eligible Google Workspace plan:** Arabic, Catalan, Chinese, Czech, Danish, Dutch, English, Finnish, French, German, Greek, Hebrew, Hungarian, Indonesian, Italian, Japanese, Korean, Malay, Norwegian, Polish, Portuguese, Romanian, Russian, Spanish, Swedish, Thai, Turkish, Ukrainian, Vietnamese; **Global**<br>**Platforms:** Web, iOS, Android |

---

## Footnotes and Special Conditions

### Mobile Availability for AI Overview in Gmail Search

\* **Google AI Plus, Pro, and Ultra only.**

This means iOS and Android support for **AI Overview in Gmail search** applies only to Google AI Plus, Pro, and Ultra users.

### Gemini Side Panel Availability Change

\*\* Google notes that it is changing how AI appears in Gmail:

> “We're working to make it easier to use AI directly in Gmail. As such, Google AI Pro and Google AI Ultra subscribers in the US may no longer see Ask Gemini in Gmail or be able to open the Gemini in Gmail side panel. New in-line AI experiences in Gmail, such as AI Overviews and ‘Help me write,’ are available directly in the product.”

**Implication:** Some Google AI Pro and Ultra subscribers in the US may lose access to the **Ask Gemini** side panel in Gmail, while newer inline AI features remain available directly inside Gmail.

---

## Key Takeaways

- **Paid plan users** generally get broader access than unpaid personal Gmail users.
- **Personal Gmail accounts without a paid plan** have more limited availability, often **US only**,

[... summary truncated for context management ...]

# Document 260: Learn about generative AI - Google Search Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/websearch/answer/13954172
Product area: search-ai
Original file: docs/learn-about-generative-ai-google-search-help-f015f265.md

# Learn about Generative AI — Google Search Help Summary

**Source:** Google Search Help — “Learn about generative AI”  
**URL:** https://support.google.com/websearch/answer/13954172

---

## Overview

Generative AI is a type of artificial intelligence that can help users **create content** and support creativity, productivity, and learning. Google explains what generative AI is, how it works, how to use it responsibly, why its outputs must be evaluated, and how Google develops AI according to its AI principles.

---

## Key Excerpts

> **“Generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) is a type of AI that can help you create content. It can help you be more creative, productive, and knowledgeable.”**

> **“Generative AI is a type of machine learning model. Generative AI is not a human being. It can’t think for itself or feel emotions. It’s just great at finding patterns.”**

> **Important:** Google’s experiences powered by generative AI can help you start the creative process. They’re not meant to do all the work for you or be the creator.

> **“Because generative AI is experimental and a work in progress, it can and will make mistakes.”**

> **“Think critically about the responses you get from generative AI tools. Use Google and other resources to check information that’s presented as fact.”**

> **“Our generative code features are still experimental and you’re responsible for your use of suggested code or coding explanations.”**

---

## What Generative AI Is

Generative AI is a **machine learning model** designed to create new content by recognizing and applying patterns learned during training.

### Important Clarifications

- Generative AI is **not human**.
- It **does not think for itself**.
- It **does not feel emotions**.
- It is effective at **finding and reproducing patterns**.

### How AI Use Has Evolved

Previously, AI was mainly used to:

- Understand information
- Recommend information

Now, generative AI can also help create new content, including:

- **Images**
- **Music**
- **Code**
- Other creative or informational outputs

---

## How Machine Learning Models Are Trained

Machine learning models learn through **observation and pattern matching**, a process called **training**.

### Example: Learning What a Sneaker Is

To understand what a sneaker is, a model may be trained on **millions of photos of sneakers**. Over time, it recognizes common features such as:

- Footwear worn by humans
- Laces
- Soles
- Logos

### How Training Enables Generation

A trained model can:

1. Take an input like “Generate an image of sneakers with a goat charm.”
2. Connect what it has learned about **sneakers**, **goats**, and **charms**.
3. Generate a new image, even if it has never seen that exact image before.

---

## How Large Language Models Power Generative AI

Generative AI and **Large Language Models**, or **LLMs**, are part of the same broader technology area.

### Difference Between Generative AI and LLMs

- **Generative AI** can be trained on many types of data.
- **LLMs** use **words** as their main source of training data.

### How LLM-Based Experiences Work

Experiences powered by LLMs, such as **Gemini** and **AI Overviews**, predict likely next words based on:

- The user’s prompt
- The text generated so far
- Patterns learned during training

This flexibility allows LLMs to produce creative responses.

### Example

If prompted to complete:

> “Harry [blank],”

an LLM might predict:

- “Styles”
- “Potter”

depending on context and learned patterns.

---

## How to Use Generative AI

Google emphasizes that generative AI should be used as a **starting point or assistant**, not as a full replacement for human creativity, judgment, or responsibility.

### 3 Suggested Uses

#### 1. Brainstorm Creative Ideas

Example:

- Get help writing a prequel to your favorite movie.

#### 2. Ask Unusual or Complex Questions

Example:

- “Which came first, the chicken or the egg?”

#### 3. Get an Extra Boost of Help

Examples:

- Suggest a title for a story you wrote.
- Help identify the species of an animal or insect in an image.

### Responsible Use

Users should use generative AI responsibly and review Google’s:

- [Generative AI Prohibited Use Policy](https://policies.google.com/terms/generative-ai/use-policy)

---

## AI Can and Will Make Mistakes

Generative AI is **experimental** and still developing. Google warns that it can produce incorrect, misleading, or misunderstood responses.

---

## Common Types of Generative AI Errors

### 1. Hallucinations: Making Things Up

Generative AI may invent answers. This is called a **hallucination**.

> **“When generative AI invents an answer, it's called a hallucination.”**

### Why Hallucinations Happen

Unlike Google Search, which retrieves information from the web, LLMs do **not** gather information directly. Instead, they predict likely next words based on:

- User input
- Training patterns
- Previously generated text

### Example

A user might ask:

> “Who’s going to 

[... summary truncated for context management ...]

---

# Document 261: Learn about Google Workspace Studio limits - Workspace Studio Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/workspace-studio/answer/16765942
Product area: workspace-studio
Original file: docs/learn-about-google-workspace-studio-limits-workspace-studio-help-1596aa2b.md

[Skip to main content](https://support.google.com/workspace-studio/answer/16765942#search-form)

# Learn about Google Workspace Studio limits

**Important:** Limits are subject to change.

The Google Workspace Studio limits:

- The number of flows you can create
- How often flows can run per day

## Understand Workspace Studio limits

- **Daily run limit:** This is the maximum number of times all of your flows combined can run in a 24-hour period.
- **Created flows:** You can create a maximum of 25 flows. This includes all flows you create, whether they’re turned on or not.
- **Flows with a Gmail starter:** No more than 25 of your active flows can be started by events in Gmail.
- **Steps per flow:** An flow can have a maximum of 20 steps.

## Learn what happens when you reach the daily run limit

**Important:** The daily run limit resets every 24 hours. Once the limit resets, your flows run again as scheduled.

If you reach your daily run limit, your active flows stop running.

- Error notifications appear in the Workspace Studio side panel of apps, like Gmail or Google Chat.
- After you reach the limit, flows’ attempts to run fail.
- To find which flows are affected, visit the “Activity” tab.

## Learn what to do if you reach a limit

If you frequently hit your daily run limit, you can:

- **Turn off flows:**Disable any flows you don’t currently need.


1. On your computer, go to [studio.workspace.google.com](https://studio.workspace.google.com/).
2. Click **My flows**.
3. Next to a flow, click More ![More](https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/zrEnCyUpV1ojNclw8WBleBtD_fvmPsi_uzzvr-fSGf9fhZ3mm7N9boscCx1P9q1tX8Q=h36)![and then](https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/3_l97rr0GvhSP2XV5OoCkV2ZDTIisAOczrSdzNCBxhIKWrjXjHucxNwocghoUa39gw=h36)**Turn off**.
- **Optimize your flows:**
  - **Frequency:** Check scheduled flows to determine if they can run less often. For example, if real-time updates aren’t critical, update a flow that runs every minute to run every hour.
  - **Filtering:** To prevent flows from unnecessarily running, make your starters more specific.For example, you can add conditions to the starter to only run for emails from a specific sender or a specific word in the subject line instead of from all emails.

## Related resources

- [Get started with Google Workspace Studio](https://support.google.com/workspace-studio/answer/16444479)
- [Navigate the Workspace Studio interface](https://support.google.com/workspace-studio/answer/16447677)

# Document 262: Learn about information stored on your photos - Computer - Google Photos Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/photos/answer/15532903
Product area: photos-ai
Original file: docs/learn-about-information-stored-on-your-photos-computer-google-photos-help-0813047a.md

[Skip to main content](https://support.google.com/photos/answer/15532903#search-form)

# Learn about information stored on your photos

With Google Photos, you can find certain information about a photo's metadata, which can be based on the International Press Telecommunications Council ( [IPTC](https://iptc.org/about-iptc/)) standards. IPTC includes copyright information, the creator's name, captions or image descriptions. Sometimes AI edits are included, but can be edited or removed.

This article focuses on photos that contain only IPTC metadata. IPTC is a long-standing format used by photographers and media organizations to describe and manage images, but the information can be edited or removed.

You may also find digital watermarks like SynthID embedded into images edited with generative AI which will [surface](https://blog.google/products/search/about-this-image-google-search/) on tools like Google Search or Lens. [Learn more about SynthID](https://deepmind.google/discover/blog/identifying-ai-generated-images-with-synthid/).

**Important:**

- Not all images have AI info. The AI info section is removed for photos that haven’t been edited with AI or that don’t follow IPTC standards.
- Note that IPTC metadata can be modified.

# Document 263: Learn about NotebookLM - Computer - NotebookLM Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/notebooklm/answer/16164461?hl=en
Product area: notebooklm
Original file: docs/learn-about-notebooklm-computer-notebooklm-help-04ca7bdb.md

# Learn about NotebookLM — Markdown Summary

**Source:** Google NotebookLM Help — “Learn about NotebookLM”  
**URL:** https://support.google.com/notebooklm/answer/16164461?hl=en

---

## Overview

**NotebookLM** is an **AI-powered research assistant** designed to help users refine, organize, and transform ideas using uploaded or discovered source materials.

### Key excerpt

> NotebookLM is an AI-powered research assistant designed to help you refine and organize your ideas.

With NotebookLM, users can:

- Upload or discover sources, including:
  - PDFs
  - Websites
  - YouTube videos
  - Audio files
  - Google Docs
  - Google Slides
- Use **Gemini’s advanced reasoning and interaction capabilities** across:
  - Text
  - Graphs
  - Images
  - Audio
  - Multiple languages
- Chat with a notebook to receive **grounded answers based on uploaded sources**
- Get **clear inline citations** for accuracy, transparency, and trust
- Transform sources into formats such as:
  - Study guides
  - Briefings
  - Audio overviews
  - Mind maps
  - Other approachable summaries and outputs

---

## Availability

NotebookLM is available through a browser for eligible users in supported countries and territories.

### Languages

- NotebookLM currently supports **80+ languages**.

### User eligibility

NotebookLM is available to users who:

- Are above the **age of consent**, or the applicable minimum age in their country
- Are in a **supported country or territory**
- Have verified their age in their Google Account when required

### Important age-related note

> NotebookLM has stricter content policies that help prevent potentially inappropriate or harmful responses for users under 18. Certain features may only be available to users over 18.

### Google Workspace and Education availability

- Most users with a **business or school Google Account** have access to NotebookLM.
- For **Workspace for Education** users, NotebookLM is available to users of **all ages**.
- If using a school or work-managed account, the organization’s admin may need to enable NotebookLM access.

---

## Supported Countries & Territories

NotebookLM is supported across a large list of countries and territories.

### Important mobile-app distinction

> **Important:** The NotebookLM mobile app may have different availability.

The browser version’s supported locations include many countries and territories across:

- North America
- South America
- Europe
- Africa
- Asia
- Oceania
- The Caribbean
- Overseas territories and dependencies

### Notable availability detail

- **Mainland China** is listed as supported for **Workspace only**.

Examples of supported countries and territories include:

- United States
- United Kingdom
- Canada
- Australia
- India
- Japan
- South Korea
- Brazil
- Mexico
- France
- Germany
- Spain
- Italy
- Netherlands
- Sweden
- Switzerland
- Singapore
- South Africa
- United Arab Emirates
- Türkiye
- Taiwan
- Vietnam
- Philippines
- Indonesia
- New Zealand
- Mainland China — **Workspace only**

The full article contains the complete country and territory list.

---

## Requirements

To use NotebookLM, users need:

- A **personal Google Account** they manage, or
- A **work or school Google Account**
- To be **signed in** to their Google Account

### For managed work or school accounts

If using a school or work-managed account:

- The organization’s administrator must turn on access to NotebookLM.

---

## Why NotebookLM May Be Unable to Answer Questions

NotebookLM may fail to answer or may produce limited answers for several reasons.

### 1. Safety flags

Source content may trigger a safety filter.

This can happen with sensitive topics such as:

- Violence
- Sexuality
- Obscenity

This may occur even when the material is presented in a historical or academic context.

### 2. Unclear phrasing

When a notebook contains many sources, NotebookLM first retrieves the most relevant information based on the user’s question, then generates an answer from that information.

If the question is vague, the system may not retrieve the right source material.

**Actionable guidance:**

- Rephrase the question
- Make the question clearer
- Ask more specific questions

### 3. Information is not in the sources

NotebookLM is designed to answer based on the information provided in uploaded or available sources.

If the answer is not present in the sources, NotebookLM may not be able to answer.

### Key excerpt

> NotebookLM is designed to answer questions based on the information provided in your uploaded sources.

---

## Data Protection and Privacy

NotebookLM’s data handling depends on the type of account used.

### Applicable terms

Depending on account type, use of NotebookLM is governed by:

- **Google Workspace Terms of Service**  
  For users accessing NotebookLM through qualified Workspace editions
- **Google Workspace for Education Terms of Service**  
  For Workspace for Education accounts
- **Google Terms of Service**  
  For other users

# Document 264: Learn about prompts for Gemini in Docs, Sheets, Slides, Vids & Forms - Google Docs Editors Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/docs/answer/15013615
Product area: sheets
Original file: docs/learn-about-prompts-for-gemini-in-docs-sheets-slides-vids-forms-google-docs-editors-help-f0c8c538.md

# Learn about prompts for Gemini in Docs, Sheets, Slides, Vids & Forms

**Source:** [Google Docs Editors Help](https://support.google.com/docs/answer/15013615)

This guide provides tips and sample prompts for using Gemini in Google Docs, Sheets, Slides, Vids, and Forms to boost productivity.

## Feature Availability

- Requires an eligible Google Workspace or Google AI plan. [Learn about Gemini features and plans](https://support.google.com/docs/answer/13952129).
- [Supported languages for Google Workspace with Gemini](https://support.google.com/docs/answer/14925782).

---

## Tips for Writing Prompts for Gemini

Four key areas make an effective prompt. You don’t need all four, but combining them yields better results:

| Area | Description | Example |
|------|-------------|---------|
| **Persona** | Tell Gemini what role to play for context on behavior and tone. | *I am a customer service representative.* |
| **Task** | Specify what to do using an action verb (summarize, write, create, etc.). | *Help me craft an empathetic email response.* |
| **Context** | Provide relevant background information. Attach files using `@` followed by the file name to include more details. | *The customer ordered a pair of headphones that arrived damaged.* |
| **Format** | Be explicit about the desired structure (bullets, paragraphs, tables, etc.). | *Include a paragraph that acknowledges their frustration and 3 bullet points with potential resolutions.* |

**Combined prompt from the table:**

> I am a customer service representative. Help me craft an empathetic email response. The customer ordered a pair of headphones that arrived damaged. Include a paragraph that acknowledges their frustration and 3 bullet points with potential resolutions.

### Additional Best Practices

- **Use natural language:** Write as you speak, in full sentences.
- **Be specific and iterate:**
  - Provide as much context as possible.
  - Attach relevant documents by typing `@` to select from suggestions or type a file name.
  - Use descriptive prompts to clarify audience, purpose, and goals.
- **Make it a conversation:** Refine results with follow-up prompts. Build on previous outputs and use an iterative process of review and refinement.

---

## Sample Prompts by Application

Copy, paste, and customize these prompts to fit your needs. They are meant for illustration.

### Google Docs

- “Draft templates for 3 different types of customer communication. Create a template for apology emails, order confirmation messages, and thank you notes for loyal customers. Keep each template to one paragraph and use a friendly tone.”
- “Craft a list of customer communication best practices that can be used to train new team members. Outline 3 sections that include how to handle happy customer inquiries, neutral customer inquiries, and dissatisfied customer inquiries.”
- “Summarize this content to write a clear and concise product return policy.”
- “Draft an email template to a customer that highlights self-service resources [blog link] for [support issues]. Thank the customer for their business and assure them of our commitment to meet their needs.”
- “Write 3 engaging social media posts about [product or service or topic] that would appeal to [target audience]. Keep each social media post to 2 sentences and include a call to action to visit [our website].”

### Google Sheets

- “Create a table to track the progress and impact of different customer experience improvement tactics with relevant metrics that include support ticket volume and priority level (high, medium, low).”
- “Create a table that tracks weekly staffing. Create columns for date, name, shift (AM or PM), and notes.”
- ”Create a table that outlines a new employee’s first-week schedule, with key meetings, training sessions, and introductions. Provide a column for key contacts and priority level (low, medium, high) for each activity.”

### Google Slides

- “Create an image of a plane that flies above the clouds over mountains and rivers during sunrise that I can use in the marketing campaign to promote my travel company.”
- “Create a slide that outlines the next steps from @_[File name]_ and @_[File name]_.”
- “Create a slide about how to optimally train for a marathon.”
- ”Summarize this presentation in 3 sentences.”

### Google Vids

- “Explain our Q4 product roadmap for executives with @_[File name]_. This video informs company leadership of upcoming plans and strategy to achieve end of year goals.”
- “Remind employees to complete their annual self-assessments as explained in @_[File name]_. This process is important so this video will be shared with everyone in the company.”
- “Pitch video shared with internal customer service teams to use our latest product update with @_[File name]_. This video should convince potential customers of our product to try new features.”

### Google Forms

- “Design a form to collect data on exercise habits and fitness goals of adults.”
- “Design a post-event survey

# Document 265: Learn about smart features & controls for Google Workspace & other Google products - Computer - Workspace Studio Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/workspace-studio/answer/15604322
Product area: workspace-studio
Original file: docs/learn-about-smart-features-controls-for-google-workspace-other-google-products-c-4f5f5400.md

# Summary: Smart Features & Controls for Google Workspace and Other Google Products

**Source:** Google Workspace Studio Help — “Learn about smart features & controls for Google Workspace & other Google products”  
**URL:** https://support.google.com/workspace-studio/answer/15604322

---

## Key Takeaways

Google updated its **smart feature settings for Google Workspace** to give users more granular control over how their Workspace data is used. The previous control has been split into **two distinct settings** for different product experiences, while Gmail/Chat/Meet also retain their own smart feature setting.

> “These updates also give you more choice and control over your data, and do not alter our underlying data handling practices or our robust privacy commitments.”

Smart features can enable experiences such as:

- Using **Gemini in Google Workspace** to reference Drive files from Gmail
- Automatically adding events from Gmail, such as flights, to Calendar
- Compiling loyalty cards and tickets from Gmail into Google Wallet
- Tracking orders using Gmail summary cards

---

## Smart Feature Settings Overview

Each smart feature setting works independently and controls different types of personalization.

| Smart feature setting | Where to find it | What it controls | Example experiences |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Smart features in Gmail, Chat, and Meet** | Gmail | Use of Gmail, Chat, and Meet data to provide smart features within those products | Smart Compose in Gmail; summary cards |
| **Smart features in Google Workspace** | Gmail, Calendar*, Chat*, Drive*, Meet* | Use of Workspace data to provide smart features across Workspace products | Events from Gmail in Calendar; Google Workspace with Gemini |
| **Smart features in other Google products** | Gmail, Calendar*, Chat*, Drive*, Meet* | Use of Workspace data to provide smart features in non-Workspace Google products | Maps reservations; Wallet tickets, passes, loyalty cards |

\* These settings can only be turned on from a web browser.

---

## 1. Smart Features in Gmail, Chat & Meet

When enabled, Gmail, Chat, and Meet can use your content and activity in those products to personalize your experience within those apps.

### Experiences Enabled

- [Automatic email filtering/categorization](https://support.google.com/mail/answer/3094499), such as:
  - Primary
  - Social
  - Promotions
- [Smart Compose](https://support.google.com/mail/answer/9116836) in Gmail
- [Smart Reply](https://support.google.com/mail/answer/6585) in Gmail
- [Summary cards above emails](https://support.google.com/mail/answer/15195630), such as:
  - Travel
  - Package tracking
  - Other contextual cards

### Important Notes

- This setting controls **app-level smart features** in Gmail, Chat, and Meet.
- It does **not** affect the separate **Smart features in Google Workspace** setting.
- If turned off, the listed Gmail/Chat/Meet experiences become unavailable until the setting is turned back on.
- Some smart features with their own separate controls may still remain available, including:
  - [Conversation summaries in Chat](https://support.google.com/chat/answer/12918975)
  - [Smart Reply in Chat](https://support.google.com/chat/answer/7654374)

---

## 2. Smart Features in Google Workspace

When enabled, Google Workspace can use your Workspace content and activity to personalize your experience across Workspace apps.

Workspace includes apps such as:

- Calendar
- Gmail
- Chat
- Meet
- Drive
- Other business and school Workspace apps

### Experiences Enabled

- Showing events from Gmail in Calendar, such as:
  - Flight itineraries
  - Invitations
- Personalized search in products like Drive, including:
  - Keyword suggestions
  - File suggestions
  - More relevant results
- Google Workspace with Gemini features, such as asking Gemini to:
  - Summarize content
  - Create drafts
  - Find key information

### Important Notes

- Turning this setting off disables the listed Workspace-wide experiences.
- To use them again, the setting must be turned back on.
- This setting does **not** affect the separate Gmail/Chat/Meet smart features control.

---

## 3. Smart Features in Other Google Products

When enabled, Google can use your Workspace content and activity to personalize your experience in Google products outside Workspace.

### Experiences Enabled

For **personal accounts only**, examples include:

- [Restaurant reservations and to-go orders in Maps](https://support.google.com/maps/answer/3503748)
- [Suggested tickets, passes, and loyalty cards in Wallet](https://support.google.com/wallet/answer/12060038#zippy=%2Cadd-items-from-gmail)
- [Personal Intelligence in the Gemini app](https://support.google.com/gemini/answer/16598406)
- [Personal Intelligence in Search services](https://support.google.com/websearch/answer/16859283)

### Additional Controls May Apply

Data sharing and personalization in certain Google products may also depend on other settings, including:

- [Web & App Act

[... summary truncated for context management ...]

# Document 266: Learn about spaces - Google Chat Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/chat/answer/7659784
Product area: chat
Original file: docs/learn-about-spaces-google-chat-help-0d7280fe.md

# Learn about Spaces — Google Chat Help Summary

**Source:** https://support.google.com/chat/answer/7659784  
**Topic:** What Google Chat spaces are, how they differ from direct messages, and how access/roles work.

---

## Key Excerpts

> To communicate with a group of people or an organization about a topic, project, or shared interest, create a space in Google Chat.

> **Important:** If you use Chat with your personal Google Account, you can only create spaces for collaboration.

> After you create a space for announcements, you can’t change the type of space.

> In addition to the main conversation in the space, you can reply to any message and create a separate thread.

> In work or school accounts, you can make the space private or discoverable to everyone or a target audience in your organization.

---

## What Google Chat Spaces Are

Google Chat **spaces** are shared areas for group communication around a **topic, project, team, organization, or shared interest**.

Examples include:
- Discussing timelines for a marketing campaign
- Coordinating project or team updates
- Sharing announcements
- Connecting around interests like travel or cooking

With spaces, you can:

- Focus conversations around a shared topic or project
- Make organizational announcements
- Create threads for detailed discussions
- Share files
- Assign tasks to members
- Add apps to create workflows inside the space

---

## Types of Spaces

Google Chat supports two main types of spaces:

1. **Collaboration spaces**
2. **Announcement spaces**

### Collaboration Spaces

**Availability:**
- Personal Google Accounts
- Work accounts
- School accounts

**Use for:**
- Project updates
- Team discussions
- Shared interests
- Open collaboration among members

**Capabilities:**
- Everyone in the space can:
  - Post messages
  - React to messages
  - Reply to messages
- Members can message the entire space in the main conversation
- Members can reply directly to messages and create threads

**Important limitation:**

> If you use Chat with your personal Google Account, you can only create spaces for collaboration.

---

### Announcement Spaces

**Availability:**
- Work or school accounts only

**Use for:**
- Sharing important news
- Posting team or organization updates

**Space owner/manager capabilities:**
- Post announcements in the main conversation window
- Respond to member comments on announcements
- Turn off replies

**Space member capabilities:**
- React to announcements
- Reply directly to announcements

**Important restriction:**

> After you create a space for announcements, you can’t change the type of space.

---

## Threads in Spaces

Spaces include a **main conversation**, but members can also reply to individual messages to create separate **threads**.

Threads are useful for:
- Tracking focused discussions
- Keeping the main conversation uncluttered
- Organizing detailed conversations by topic

In a thread, you can:

- Send and reply to messages
- Follow or unfollow the thread
- Change notification settings for new messages in the thread

Related help article: [Use threads in a space](https://support.google.com/chat/answer/12176589)

---

## Space Access Settings

For **work or school accounts**, spaces can be configured as either:

- **Private**
- **Discoverable** to everyone or to a target audience in the organization

---

### Private Spaces

Private spaces are best for:
- Teams
- Projects
- Specific groups
- Discussions limited to certain people

Key behavior:
- You must be invited or added by an existing member to join
- If you are not a member or invited, you cannot browse for the space in Chat

---

### Discoverable Spaces

Discoverable spaces are best for:
- Topics not limited to specific people or teams
- Broad organizational discussions
- Open-interest spaces

Key behavior:
- Users can browse, preview, and join the space
- If someone has a link to the space, they can join through that link

---

## Spaces vs. Direct Messages

Google Chat supports both **spaces** and **direct messages**, but they serve different purposes.

### Core Difference

- **Spaces:** Central places where people can share files, assign tasks, and stay connected.
- **Direct messages:** Chats with a person or group, often for quick discussions.

Example use case for direct messages:
- A quick discussion after a meeting

---

## Comparison: Spaces vs. Direct Messages

| Feature | Spaces | Direct Messages |
|---|---|---|
| **Message history** | Admin determines whether history is on by default and whether users can change it. | Personal accounts can turn history on/off. Workspace account history depends on organization settings. |
| **Name** | In Google Workspace, the **“Who can modify space board and details”** permission controls who can edit the name and description. If created by a personal account, any space member can change the name. | Member names are listed. |
| **Description** | In Google Workspace, the **“Who can modify space board and d

[... summary truncated for context management ...]

---

# Document 267: Learn how to schedule events in Gmail - Google Calendar Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/calendar/answer/16865706
Product area: calendar

[Skip to main content](https://support.google.com/calendar/answer/16865706#search-form)

# Learn how to schedule events in Gmail

In Gmail, you can schedule Google Calendar events and negotiate times to meet directly from a message. There are different options available in Gmail based on your account type.

| **Feature** | **Description** | **Availability** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| [Suggest times to meet with Gemini in Gmail](https://support.google.com/calendar/answer/16865189) | Use Gemini in Gmail to coordinate and set up meetings with one or more people. If you have an appointment schedule, you can also insert a booking page.<br> This feature supports 1:1 and group meetings. | - Workspace Business Standard and Plus<br>- Workspace Enterprise Starter, Standard, and Plus<br>- Workspace Frontline Plus<br>- Google AI Pro for Education<br>- Google AI Pro and Ultra<br>- Gemini Business and Enterprise - Legacy |
| [Create an event from a Gmail message](https://support.google.com/mail/answer/13469141) | Use Gmail to convert an existing email thread into a Calendar invite. When you create the event, it automatically copies the email subject and body into the event details, and adds all email recipients as invitees. | All Gmail users |
| [Insert an event or booking page in a Gmail message](https://support.google.com/calendar/answer/16865501) | When you draft a reply or a new email, you can create an event and insert a summary of it in your email draft. If you have an appointment schedule, you can also insert a booking page. | - Workspace Business Starter<br>- Personal Gmail accounts |
| [Offer times to meet in a Gmail message](https://support.google.com/calendar/answer/16864784) | You can offer a list of times to meet from your calendar to your recipient. The event isn’t created until the recipient selects a time. Recipients get times in their own time zone on your booking page.<br>This feature supports 1:1 meetings only. | - Workspace Business Starter<br>- Personal Gmail accounts |

# Document 268: Learn more about the conversational AI tool when watching videos - Computer - YouTube Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/youtube/answer/14110396
Product area: youtube-ai
Original file: docs/learn-more-about-the-conversational-ai-tool-when-watching-videos-computer-youtube-he-207a2ed8.md

# YouTube Help: Conversational AI Tool When Watching Videos — Summary

**Source:** https://support.google.com/youtube/answer/14110396  
**Topic:** YouTube’s conversational AI tool for asking questions and getting suggestions while watching videos.

---

## Overview

YouTube’s conversational AI tool lets users interact with AI while watching select videos. Users can:

- Ask questions about the video they’re watching.
- Choose suggested prompts, such as **“recommend related content.”**
- Get answers or recommendations without leaving the video.

The feature uses generative AI and large language models (LLMs), and its availability depends on language, region, device, account type, and video eligibility.

---

## Key Excerpts

> “While you’re watching a video on YouTube, the conversational AI tool lets you interact with AI to learn more about the content.”

> “For example, you can ask questions about the video you’re watching or choose a suggested prompt like ‘recommend related content.’”

> “AI-generated responses do not reflect the views of YouTube. Quality and accuracy may vary.”

> “The conversational AI tool is for informational purposes only. Don’t rely on AI-generated responses as medical, legal, financial, or other professional advice.”

> “Conversations connected with your Google Account will be deleted automatically after 45 days.”

> “Please don’t give confidential info, or anything you wouldn’t want a reviewer to see, in your conversations.”

> “No, your conversations aren’t being used to show you ads. If this changes, we will clearly communicate the change to you.”

---

## Availability

The tool is currently available only in **select languages and regions** and appears on **select videos**.

**Important:** Availability may change in the future.

---

## Supported Languages

The conversational AI tool is currently available in:

- Arabic

The conversational AI tool is available to:

- Signed-in users
- Users who meet the minimum age requirement:
  - **13+**, or
  - the applicable age in the user’s country
- Users in supported countries and languages

The tool is **not available** for:

- Supervised kid accounts
- The YouTube Kids app

# Document 269: Learn to use Gemini in your Google Drive files (Workspace Experiments) - Google Drive Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/drive/answer/15095275?hl=en
Product area: drive
Original file: docs/learn-to-use-gemini-in-your-google-drive-files-workspace-experiments-google-driv-18c15e79.md

# Learn to Use Gemini in Google Drive Files (Workspace Experiments) — Summary

**Source:** Google Drive Help  
**URL:** https://support.google.com/drive/answer/15095275?hl=en

---

## Key Points

> **Important:**
>
> - Gemini in file viewer currently supports PDF and video file types.
> - Some Gemini features are only available in English.

Gemini can be used inside the Google Drive file viewer to interact with supported files, especially **PDFs** and **videos**. It can summarize content, answer questions, help create new content, combine information from other Drive files, and identify action items.

---

## What You Can Do with Gemini in Drive File Viewer

Gemini supports several workflows for supported file types:

- **Summarize:** Get a quick overview of PDFs and videos.
- **Ask questions:** Find specific information or insights from a PDF or video.
- **Create new content:** Use a PDF to make something new, such as:
  - A study plan
  - An email draft
- **Bring in more information:** Combine the PDF with other files in Google Drive by typing `@`.
- **Identify action items:** Use meeting recordings to check whether you missed important action items.

---

## Feature Availability

Gemini in Drive file viewer is not universally available.

Requirements and availability notes:

- Requires an eligible **Google Workspace** or **Google AI** plan.
- Supported languages vary by Gemini feature.
- Available through **Google Workspace Experiments**, a trusted tester program for users with personal accounts to try new AI features.

To check whether you have Workspace Experiments access:

1. Open a new presentation in **Google Slides**.
2. Look for a bottom bar indicating Workspace Experiments access.

Relevant help links from the article:

- [Learn about Gemini features and plans](https://support.google.com/drive/answer/13952129)
- [Learn about supported languages for Google Workspace with Gemini](https://support.google.com/docs/answer/14925782)
- [Learn how to get started with Google Workspace Experiments](https://support.google.com/docs/answer/13447104)

---

## Open Drive File Viewer

When you double-click a supported file in Drive, it opens in the **overlay file viewer**.

To open a PDF or video in a dedicated space:

1. Go to [drive.google.com](http://drive.google.com/) on your computer.
2. Double-click a **PDF** or **video**.
3. Click **More actions** → **Open with** → **Open in new tab**.

**Tip:**  
To adjust the default settings for opening a PDF, go to your [Drive settings](https://drive.google.com/drive/u/0/settings).

---

## Get Automatic Summaries

To open Gemini in the Drive file viewer:

- Click **Ask Gemini** at the top right of the navigation bar.

When Gemini opens in the file viewer, the side panel **automatically summarizes the file**.

The summary provides a quick overview of the main points in the open PDF or video.

---

## Ask Questions and Get Answers

You can ask Gemini questions about the PDF or video you are viewing.

Steps:

1. Enter a question in the side panel’s textbox.
   - You can also select a **starter tile** to use a template prompt.
2. Review Gemini’s answer in the side panel.
3. Give feedback by clicking **thumbs-up** or **thumbs-down**.

---

## Sources for Gemini’s Answers

Gemini uses relevant sources to generate answers. These may include:

- The open PDF
- The open video
- Other files in your Google Drive
- Information from the web
- A combination of these sources

To help users verify accuracy, Gemini provides links to the sources used.

Important source behavior:

- Sources are expanded by default.
- Clicking a source chip opens that source file in a new tab.

---

## Turn Off Gemini in Drive

To turn off Gemini or any Workspace Experiments feature, you must **exit Workspace Experiments**.

Important consequences:

- Exiting permanently removes access to **all Workspace Experiments features**.
- You cannot rejoin Workspace Experiments after exiting.

Related link:

- [Learn more about how to exit Workspace Experiments](https://support.google.com/docs/answer/13447104#labs-opt-out)

---

## Workspace Experiments Feature Suggestions and Warnings

Google provides several cautions about Workspace Experiments features:

- Workspace Experiments feature suggestions do **not** represent Google’s views and should not be attributed to Google.
- Do **not** rely on Workspace Experiments features as:
  - Medical advice
  - Legal advice
  - Financial advice
  - Other professional advice
- Features may suggest inaccurate or inappropriate information.
- User feedback helps make Workspace Experiments more helpful and safe.
- Do **not** include personal, confidential, or sensitive information.

---

## Data Collection and Use

Google uses Workspace Experiments data and metrics to provide, improve, and develop products, services, and machine learning technologies across Google.

When users interact with Workspace Experiments features, Google collects data including:

- Prompts and input
- 

[... summary truncated for context management ...]

# Document 270: Let Gemini in Google Calendar find times to meet - Google Calendar Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/calendar/answer/16690875?hl=en
Product area: calendar
Original file: docs/let-gemini-in-google-calendar-find-times-to-meet-google-calendar-help-22ea6989.md

[Skip to main content](https://support.google.com/calendar/answer/16690875?hl=en#search-form)

# Let Gemini in Google Calendar find times to meet

**Important:** This feature requires an eligible Google Workspace plan. [Learn about Gemini features and plans](https://support.google.com/docs/answer/13952129).

To save you time, you can use Gemini in Google Calendar to search for everyone's availability and suggest times to meet that work best for everyone.

## Get suggested times when you create an event

1. On your computer, open [Google Calendar](https://calendar.google.com/).
2. At the top left, click **Create**![and then](https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/3_l97rr0GvhSP2XV5OoCkV2ZDTIisAOczrSdzNCBxhIKWrjXjHucxNwocghoUa39gw=h36) **Event**.
3. Add guests to your event.
4. Click **Suggested times**.
5. Select a suggested time.
   - If you want to see other times, click **More suggestions**.

     - Or, you can directly edit on the grid.
     - If you edit on the grid, you can click Filter and view ![](https://storage.googleapis.com/support-kms-prod/2pLQFgB9rT4D5TjsXS5cO4dCviGcLT1dRbfi). This lets you toggle rooms and filter guests by required or optional status.

**Tip:** To collapse or expand the suggestions, on the left, click **Suggested times**.

## Get suggested times when you reschedule an event

1. On your computer, open [Google Calendar](https://calendar.google.com/).
2. Click the event you want to reschedule.
3. Click Edit event ![](https://storage.googleapis.com/support-kms-prod/YL1HyKGQPj5qXFcApz66xPy140gy7cwsF4iH).
4. Click **Suggested times**.

**Tip:** If you're the event organizer and several guests decline, a banner might appear at the top of the event with alternative schedule suggestions.

- To accept the suggestion, click **Reschedule to \[date and time\]**.
- To manually find a different time, click **More times**.

## Learn why you don't get suggested times

You can't get suggested times if:

- You use the Calendar app for Android, iPhone, or iPad.
- The event is longer than 8 hours.
- The meeting has too many attendees.
- The date range you are viewing is in the past.

## Related resources

- [Get started with Google Workspace with Gemini](https://support.google.com/docs/answer/13952129)
- [Change your event visibility settings](https://support.google.com/calendar/answer/34580)
- [Share your calendar with someone](https://support.google.com/calendar/answer/37082)
- [See someone's calendar availability](https://support.google.com/calendar/answer/6294878)
