# Document 352: Tips to use AI steps in flows - Workspace Studio Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/workspace-studio/answer/16431105?hl=en
Product area: workspace-studio
Original file: docs/tips-to-use-ai-steps-in-flows-workspace-studio-help-c99b5454.md

# Tips to Use AI Steps in Flows — Google Workspace Studio

**Source:** [Workspace Studio Help](https://support.google.com/workspace-studio/answer/16431105?hl=en)  
**Topic:** How to incorporate Gemini and AI-powered steps into Workspace Studio flows.

---

## Overview

Google Workspace Studio lets you incorporate **Gemini** into automated flows. Gemini can help flows:

- Analyze content
- Write or draft text
- Research information
- Determine whether a flow should run
- Summarize, extract, or recap information

The article explains when to use different types of AI steps:

- **General AI steps**
  - Ask Gemini
  - Ask a Gem
- **AI action steps**
  - Decide
  - Extract
  - Summarize
  - Recap unread emails

---

## Account Type Requirements

### Personal Accounts

> **Personal accounts:** If you have a Google AI Pro or Ultra plan, you can use flows in Workspace Studio. You can't share flows with others.

### School Accounts

> **School accounts:** If you use a school account and are under 18, you can't get AI features in Workspace Studio. If someone shares a flow with AI steps with you, those steps are removed when you open the flow.

---

## How to Use AI Steps Successfully

The article identifies **4 keys to success** when using any AI step.

---

### 1. Provide Context in Your Request

Gemini does **not** automatically know what happened in earlier steps of your flow.

> Gemini doesn’t know about the other steps in your flow. Prompts like “Write a professional reply to the email” won’t work because Gemini doesn’t know which email you mean.

#### Actionable Tips

- Reference data from earlier flow steps.
- Include variables from previous steps.
- Link to specific Google Drive files.
- Link to relevant websites.
- Add enough context for Gemini to understand the request.

#### For Open Prompt AI Steps

For steps such as **Ask Gemini** and **Decide**, add context directly into the prompt for better results.

> **Reference data from earlier steps and specific files**: For AI steps with open prompts, such as 'Ask Gemini' and 'Decide', add context to your prompt to get better results.

---

### 2. Control Data Sources Gemini Can Use

By default, Gemini can use:

- The web
- Workspace content you can access
- Connected integrations you can access

To limit access, choose **Web only**.

> **Allow Google Workspace data sources**: By default, Gemini can reference the web, as well as all content that you can access in Google Workspace and connected integrations. To prevent Gemini from accessing your personal or organization's data, choose **Web only**.

---

### 3. Use Gemini’s Response as a Variable Later

Gemini’s response becomes a variable tied to the AI step.

> **Access Gemini’s response by adding a variable in a later step**. The response from Gemini or a Gem is available as a variable associated with the AI-powered step.

You can reuse that output in later steps, such as:

- A message
- A document
- A spreadsheet
- Another Gemini request

---

### 4. Use Multiple AI Steps in One Flow

You can chain AI steps together by passing variables from one step to another.

Example workflow from the article:

> You could use the **Sales pitch ideator** Gem in **Ask a Gem** to analyze a form response, use an **Extract** step find any questions in an open-ended response, use a **Ask Gemini** step draft an email message using the Gem response and extracted questions for additional context, and use an **Ask Gemini** step to create a subject line for your message.

---

### 5. Use AI Action Steps When Appropriate

Although **Ask Gemini** can handle many requests, Workspace Studio includes specialized AI action steps optimized for common tasks.

> Though you can use Ask Gemini for almost all types of requests, Workspace Studio has AI steps that are optimized for certain actions, such as extracting information from text, summarizing ideas and insights, or writing text for specific scenarios.

---

## General AI Steps

General AI steps are used similarly to Gemini in Workspace.

| Step | Best For | Examples / Notes |
|---|---|---|
| **Ask Gemini** | General inquiries or text generation | Web research, summarizing project status, creative writing |
| **Ask a Gem** | Using the agentic expertise of a pre-built or custom Gem | Sales pitch ideation, custom file-grounded content generation |

---

### Ask Gemini

Best for **general inquiries** or **text generation** needs.

Examples:

- Do a quick web search on the company of a person who submitted a form or contacted you.
- Summarize the state of a project based on the latest meeting notes and tracking spreadsheet.
- Write a poem about the importance of updating a meeting agenda to send to attendees.

Important tip:

> **Tip:** For common drafting, summarizing, and extracting tasks, you can save time crafting prompts by using AI action steps instead.

---

### Ask a Gem

Use **Ask a Gem** when you need the expertise of a specific Gem, either pre-built or custom.

Examples:

- Have t

[... summary truncated for context management ...]

---

# Document 353: Tips to write prompts for Gemini - Google Workspace Learning Center

Source URL: https://support.google.com/a/users/answer/14200040
Product area: workspace-learning-center
Original file: docs/tips-to-write-prompts-for-gemini-google-workspace-learning-center-5f36d3bf.md

# Tips to Write Prompts for Gemini  
**Source:** Google Workspace Learning Center — “Tips to write prompts for Gemini”  
**Context:** Gemini for Google Workspace productivity guide

---

## Core Purpose

To get better results from **Gemini for Google Workspace**, users should follow basic prompt-writing best practices. Good prompts should be natural, specific, contextual, and broken into manageable tasks when needed.

---

## Key Prompt-Writing Best Practices

### 1. Use Natural Language

Write prompts conversationally, as if speaking to a friend or colleague.

| Instead of... | Try this... |
|---|---|
| `"Training plan."` | `"Write a training plan for the sales team for the launch of a brand new product."` |

**Takeaway:** Avoid short, vague fragments. Ask in complete, natural sentences.

---

### 2. Be Clear and Concise

Gemini can follow precise instructions, so avoid ambiguity.

| Instead of... | Try this... |
|---|---|
| `"Marketing talking points."` | `"Give me 12 thoughtful questions to ask a Chief Marketing Officer on their strategy for 2024."` |

**Takeaway:** Specify the exact output you want, including quantity, audience, and topic.

---

### 3. Provide Context

The more context you provide, the more useful Gemini’s response can be.

| Instead of... | Try this... |
|---|---|
| `"Write about a sales job."` | `"Write a job description for a _[job title]_, including the required skills and experience, as well as a summary of _[company name]_ and the position."` |

**Takeaway:** Include relevant background such as role, company, audience, goal, or constraints.

---

### 4. Use Specific and Relevant Keywords

Gemini can understand individual words and phrases, so using relevant keywords helps it generate more accurate and informative responses.

| Instead of... | Try this... |
|---|---|
| `"Create project plan."` | `"Create a project plan for the launch of a brand new product. The timeframe should be from now until June 2024."` |

**Takeaway:** Include keywords tied to the task, deliverable, subject, and timeline.

---

### 5. Break Down Complex Tasks into Separate Prompts

If a task involves several related actions, split it into multiple prompts.

**Reason:** This helps Gemini better understand each step and provide more useful responses.

**Example reference:** Google links to an example showing how to use Gemini to recruit and hire for a job opening.

---

## Important Usage Notes Before Using Gemini

Google includes several cautions and limitations for Gemini features:

- **Gemini suggestions do not represent Google’s views** and should not be attributed to Google.
- **Do not rely on Gemini features as professional advice**, including:
  - Medical advice
  - Legal advice
  - Financial advice
  - Other professional advice
- Gemini may suggest **inaccurate or inappropriate information**.
- User feedback helps make Gemini more helpful and safe.
- Enterprise end users can submit feedback about generative AI features.
- Before submitting feedback, users are informed that feedback data:
  - Should not contain personal, sensitive, or confidential information
  - May be read by humans

---

## Language Availability

Key language-related notes:

> _All Gemini features are available in English. Some AI features are available in other languages._

> _The Gemini app is available in 45+ languages._

---

## Actionable Prompt Formula

A strong Gemini prompt should usually include:

- **Task:** What you want Gemini to do  
- **Context:** Background information Gemini needs  
- **Audience:** Who the output is for  
- **Format:** Desired structure or output type  
- **Constraints:** Timeline, length, tone, number of items, or required details  

### Example Structure

```text
Write/Create/Summarize [specific deliverable] for [audience/purpose].
Include [required details].
Use [tone/format].
The timeframe/constraint is [specific requirement].
```

---

## Quick Do / Don’t Summary

| Do | Don’t |
|---|---|
| Use conversational, natural language | Use vague fragments |
| Be specific about the desired output | Assume Gemini knows your intent |
| Provide background context | Ask broad, context-free questions |
| Include relevant keywords | Use generic wording |
| Split complex tasks into smaller prompts | Combine many tasks into one unclear prompt |
| Review outputs carefully | Treat Gemini responses as guaranteed accurate |

---

## Related Google Workspace Learning Center Item

The page also promotes a related guide:

**“Print, save, or customize Learning Center guides”**  
Users can learn how to print Learning Center guides, save them as PDFs, or customize them for their organization.

---

# Document 354: Transforming HR workflows with AI

Source URL: https://workspace.google.com/blog/ai-and-machine-learning/turing-transforming-hr-workflows-ai?hl=en
Product area: workspace-site
Original file: docs/transforming-hr-workflows-with-ai-f8135b7d.md

# Transforming HR workflows with AI

Source: https://workspace.google.com/blog/ai-and-machine-learning/turing-transforming-hr-workflows-ai?hl=en

AI and Machine Learning

# Transforming HR workflows with AI

August 8, 2025

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/5492g_-_Hero_image_templates_-_AI_at_Work_.max-2500x2500.jpg](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/5492g_-_Hero_image_templates_-_AI_at_Work_.max-2500x2500.jpg)

##### Taylor Bradley

VP of Talent Strategy and Success, Turing

##### Google Workspace Newsletter

Keep up with the evolving future of work and collaboration with insights, trends, and product news.

[SIGN UP](https://workspace.google.com/?source=gafb-blog_article-body-en#newsletter)

Before joining Turing as VP of Talent Strategy and Success, Taylor Bradley led strategic HR programs at IPO-stage and high-growth companies. He’s also a recognized voice on the future of work, a CNBC Workforce Executive Council member, and host of the TroopHR podcast.

---

**“The biggest barrier to AI adoption isn’t technology — it’s the inertia of the status quo.”**

When [Turing](https://www.turing.com/) CEO, Jonathan Siddharth, said that we needed to onboard 800 new people in the span of a single week, I told him we would “AI our way out of this.” Looking back, this was the beginning of truly scaling HR operations with AI, not just to meet the immediate demands but to lay the foundation for creating an AI-forward culture. Below are five ways we’ve embraced this moment of HR transformation with AI.

### 1. Onboarding talent at scale with the help of AI

In early 2024, Turing needed to hire and train people more quickly to meet the demand for our services. That was when we designed Turing University, powered by AI, to route talent to multiple onboarding paths based on role, skill set, and the required speed of deployment. It’s a bit like the London Underground — multiple lines run under one central system.

To build this system, we created videos of engineering leaders onboarding new talent, used those videos to train a proprietary LLM, and then our model generated interactive learning modules for new hires. These included work simulations, sandbox environments for hands-on practice, and knowledge assessments for both formal exams and proficiency checks.

The new onboarding program took just three hours to create instead of the 2-5 weeks we used to spend putting this type of program together. Thanks to these AI-driven innovations, Turing onboarded over 5,000 people in the second half of 2024.

### 2. Empowering our talent with Gems

[Gems](https://gemini.google/overview/gems) are customized versions of Gemini that you can tailor to specific types of tasks, like coding, deep research, or data analysis. We’ve developed multiple career-focused AI assistants tailored to specific technical roles, which help upskill and validate technical proficiency for hundreds of team members.

For broader impact, in our Thrive Together program, we launched Google’s premade Gemini Career Builder Gem, which delivers highly personalized career guidance, motivation, and learning support. We paired it with training on how to use the Gem effectively, and alongside Turing’s curated library of self-paced technical courses, the Gem empowers team members to grow their skills in measurable ways without increasing HR headcount.

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/5492a_LI_Newsletter__Reshaping_HR_workflow.max-1500x1500.jpg](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/5492a_LI_Newsletter__Reshaping_HR_workflow.max-1500x1500.jpg)

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/5492a_LI_Newsletter__Reshaping_HR_workflow.max-1500x1500.jpg](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/5492a_LI_Newsletter__Reshaping_HR_workflow.max-1500x1500.jpg)

Thrive Together Gem delivers highly personalized career guidance and learning support.

### 3. Optimizing training programs

At Turing, we’re AI agnostic, so we always select the best AI tool for the job. To help us improve, we built a Gem to analyze feedback on how the training modules were working.

We discovered that some of our talent, which is spread around the globe, found it difficult to understand the accents of their trainers. That insight led us to embrace synthetic voices. With synthetic voices, we can create audio tracks with different accents, languages, and even dialects for each region. Now we can change the script, click publish, and launch the updated module in a matter of minutes.

### 4. Retaining talent

With the release of Gemini 2.5, we’ve been able to better personalize employee development programs and streamline staffing workflows. And the embedded experience within Google Workspace has helped increase adoption and daily usage among team members — from Gmail to Sheets.

All of these aspects contribute to higher employee satisfaction and retention. Team member satisfaction rose 32% in the first half of this year, setting a company record. Our top-scoring drivers: Turing University and manager effectiveness.

### 5. Automating HR support

Our people ops team saw support ticket volumes surge from 5,000 to 18,000 per quarter after 4,000 new team members joined in six months. Existing workflows broke down under the strain of such hypergrowth.

During a two-day hackathon, our team developed and launched a new AI model. The model was trained on internal knowledge to draft replies, which were then reviewed by our staff. This preserved oversight while speeding response times and supporting alignment with our policies and culture.

**After only two working days of development, the custom model** **reduced ticket processing time by 33%.** Since then, we’ve built Gems that are on track to automate 60% of the 52,000 annual tickets by year end. That frees up the rest of our team to create agentic workflows that automate other key HR tasks.

### Creating an AI-forward culture

All of these technology implementations created new efficiencies for HR operations. But the biggest barrier to AI adoption isn’t technology — it’s the inertia of the status quo. As an AI-native HR executive, I have two roles when it comes to AI: boosting workforce productivity and creating an AI-forward culture.

That’s why our people ops specialists receive targeted training to understand how AI can augment — not replace — their expertise. That gives them the confidence to lead this transformation. It’s critical for AI and human intelligence to work alongside each other. AI tools should complement, not replace, the work and expertise of an HR team. Having an “informed human in the loop” is at the foundation of our long-term plans for transforming HR workflows with AI.

# Document 355: Troubleshoot issues with flows - Workspace Studio Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/workspace-studio/answer/16430806
Product area: workspace-studio
Original file: docs/troubleshoot-issues-with-flows-workspace-studio-help-ec31a778.md

# Troubleshoot Issues with Flows — Workspace Studio Help

**Source:** Google Workspace Studio Help  
**URL:** https://support.google.com/workspace-studio/answer/16430806

---

## Important Eligibility Note

> **Important:**  
> If you're signed in with a school account and are under 18, you can’t use any AI features in Workspace Studio. If a flow containing AI steps is shared with you, those steps will be removed when you open the flow.

---

## Error Indicators

When a flow has an issue, Workspace Studio shows notifications in several places:

- A **red dot** on the **Google Workspace Studio icon** in Gmail.
- A message on the **My flows** tab in the Studio web app.
- A red dot in the **Activity** tab in the Studio web app.

### Common Causes of Flow Failures

Flows can fail because of:

- **Invalid data during a run**
  - Example: drafting an email to an invalid email address.
- **Step configuration errors**
  - Example: losing access to a Chat space or a deleted Google Doc used by a step.
- **Usage limits or proactive security measures**
  - Flows that run too often may hit usage limits.
  - Flows may stop if malicious content or a possible prompt injection attack is detected.

### How to Investigate

- Click the flow that had the problem.
- For **invalid data issues**:
  - Review the flow’s **activity feed** to find problematic data.
  - Add **“Check If”** steps to detect invalid or empty variables.
- For **configuration issues**:
  - Check the error message on the affected step.

> After you resolve the issue, dismiss the warning. If you don’t, the flow still counts as a flow with issues.

---

## General Troubleshooting Note

> **Note:** Workspace Studio is in active development and bugs are fixed regularly. If a flow stops working, review its set up and then try turning on the flow again to pick up any bug fixes.

---

# Common Issues and Fixes

## Issues When Turning On a Flow

| Error message | Reason and resolution |
|---|---|
| **The starter or a step isn't set up correctly** | The flow setup is missing a required field. Click each item to find and fix the issue. |
| **Couldn’t turn on flow. Please try again.** | Possible causes:<br><br>- You already have **100 flows turned on**. Turn off or delete an active flow to enable a new one.<br>- You already have **25 active flows that use the Gmail starter**. Turn off or delete an active Gmail-starter flow.<br>- Workspace Studio encountered an error. Wait a few minutes and try again. If it still fails, click **Help → Send feedback to Google** to report a bug. |

---

## Issues While Running a Flow

Errors during a flow run appear in the **activity log**.

| Error message | Reason and resolution |
|---|---|
| **After trying several times, your flow couldn't complete this run** | Possible causes:<br><br>- For work or school accounts, your Google Workspace administrator may have disabled the app or service used in the step.<br>- Make sure **Smart features** are turned on.<br>- The step may be restricted to the **Gemini Alpha program**.<br>- The app developer may have removed or ended support for the step.<br>- The step hit a bug and could not complete. |
| **Workspace Studio is missing permissions** | Workspace Studio needs permission to connect to other apps and services. Try turning the flow on again.<br><br>- **Work or school accounts:** If it still fails, your admin may have blocked access.<br>- **Personal accounts:** Check that you haven’t revoked Workspace Studio access in your Google Account security settings. |
| **The prompt, including content referenced with variables and links, exceeded Gemini limits. Consider editing your prompt, such as using fewer variables.** | Applies to AI steps. The prompt must be under approximately **144,000 characters**, or roughly **18,000 words**, including linked content and variables.<br><br>This often happens when a variable includes a long email body or thread. Reduce the number of variables in the prompt. |
| **Couldn't start because "Google Workspace smart features" in your Gmail settings are turned off** | Some flows require Google Workspace smart features. Ensure both **Smart features** and **Google Workspace smart features** are turned on. |
| **Content couldn’t be shared with Gemini** | **Work or school accounts:** Admin settings may prevent downloading or copying files, which also prevents Gemini access. Contact your admin.<br><br>**Personal accounts:** The file may have encryption or restrictive sharing settings. Ensure the file isn’t restricted. |
| **Flow was automatically turned off because it hasn't run in 6 months** | If a flow doesn’t run for **6 months**, it automatically turns off.<br><br>To turn it back on:<br>1. Go to **My flows**.<br>2. Click the flow.<br>3. Click **Turn on**. |
| **Couldn’t send message** | **Personal accounts:** This can happen if the flow has a Chat step but you haven’t used Google Chat before.<br><br>To fix:<br>1. In Gmail, click **Chat** to initialize the service.<br>2. Ru

[... summary truncated for context management ...]

## Deliver polished, consistent messaging with AI avatars

# Document 359: Turn access to Google Workspace with Gemini alpha on or off  |  Generative AI  |  Google Workspace Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/a/answer/14170809?hl=en-IE
Product area: admin-console
Original file: docs/turn-access-to-google-workspace-with-gemini-alpha-on-or-off-generative-ai-google-workspace-9c5013a0.md

# Summary: Turn Access to Google Workspace with Gemini Alpha On or Off

As a Google Workspace administrator, you can enable Gemini alpha features so users can try them before general availability.

---

## Important Notes About the Alpha Program

- **Default state**: Off by default; only admins can enable.
- **Scope**: Can be turned on for all users or specific organizational units/groups.
- **Data protection**: Same robust standards as all Workspace services.
- **Usage limits**: Alpha features may be subject to limits when they reach general availability.
- **Edition availability**: Not all alpha features are available in all Workspace editions (see features table below).
- **Feature list changes**: Google periodically adds or removes alpha features; this page reflects current supported features.

---

## Terms That Apply

Enabling Gemini alpha features means your organization agrees to the [Google Workspace Service Specific Terms](https://workspace.google.com/terms/service-terms/):

- **Business & Enterprise** and **Education** both reference:
  - Pre-General Availability Offering Terms (excluding Section 6.1(b))
  - Generative AI Services sections of the Service Specific Terms
- Data protection and processing obligations under the Cloud Data Processing Addendum (CDPA) apply.

---

## Features Available Today in Gemini Alpha

Checkmarks (✔) indicate availability. The table below summarizes editions:  
- **BS**: Business Starter  
- **BS+/BP**: Business Standard/Plus  
- **ES+/EP**: Enterprise Standard/Plus  
- **AI Pro Edu**: Google AI Pro for Education  
- **Voice**: Available to users with a Google Voice license  

| Feature | BS | BS+/BP | ES+/EP | AI Pro Edu | Notes |
|---------|----|--------|--------|------------|-------|
| **NotebookLM as context source in Docs** | | ✔ | ✔ | ✔ | Ground Gemini responses in curated NotebookLM sources |
| **Gemini-powered comment workflows in Docs** | | ✔ | ✔ | ✔ | Review, summarize, draft comment responses |
| **Generate and edit images with Gemini** (Docs) | | ✔ | ✔ | ✔ | Infographics, diagrams directly in Docs |
| **Formula Fixer** (Sheets) | | ✔ | ✔ | ✔ | Instant troubleshooting & explanations for formulas |
| **Help Me Create with Personalization** (Docs) | | ✔ | ✔ | ✔ | Drafts using Workspace data & templates |
| **AI Inbox** (Gmail) | | | EP only | | Suggested to-dos & topic summaries; **US only** |
| **AI overviews in Chat** | | ✔ | ✔ | ✔ | AI-powered answer card atop Chat search |
| **Sheets canvas** | | ✔ | ✔ | ✔ | Conversationally build interactive mini-apps from spreadsheets (single-table only) |
| **Ask Gemini in Chat** | | ✔ | ✔ | ✔ | Summarize topics, manage schedule from Workspace data |
| **Your instructions for Gemini in Workspace** | | ✔ | ✔ | ✔ | Saved preferences for Gemini behavior |
| **Take notes for in-person meetings** (Google Meet) | | ✔ | ✔ | ✔ | AI notes stored in Drive; rolling out Android first |
| **Take notes for me in Google Voice** | Voice | Voice | Voice | Voice | Recording, transcription, notes in Voice & email |
| **Help me Create in Docs** | | ✔ | ✔ | ✔ | Generate drafts using Workspace data & templates |
| **Help me Write in Docs** | | ✔ | ✔ | ✔ | Generate and format content with shared context |
| **AI overview in Drive** | | ✔ | ✔ | ✔ | Gemini summaries in Drive search results |
| **Ask Gemini in Drive** | | ✔ | ✔ | ✔ | Organize context sources into shareable projects |
| **Fill with Gemini in Sheets** | | ✔ | ✔ | ✔ | Promptless drag-and-drop column population |
| **Solve complex optimization problems in Sheets** | | ✔ | ✔ | ✔ | Natural-language multi-step workflows & dashboards |
| **Build spreadsheets with Gemini in Sheets** | | ✔ | ✔ | ✔ | Entire spreadsheet construction via natural language |
| **Help me Create in Slides** | | ✔ | ✔ | ✔ | Generate slides matching deck style |
| **Gemini in Workspace conversation history** | ✔ | ✔ | ✔ | ✔ | View prior prompts/responses; private to user |
| **New admin control for take notes for me** | | ✔ | ✔ | ✔ | Pre-configure AI note taking; auto-enable for meetings with 3+ participants |
| **AI overviews in Gmail** | ✔ | ✔ | ✔ | ✔ | Summaries & answers in Gmail search results |
| **Take notes for me decisions and concise summary** (Meet) | | ✔ | ✔ | ✔ | Customizable views with Decisions section & concise summary |
| **Personalized Help me Write in Gmail** | ✔ | ✔ | ✔ | | Drafts using your emails/Drive files; **English only** |
| **Gemini for Workspace admins** | ✔ | ✔ | | | Gemini assistant in Admin console |
| **Use third-party integrations in Gemini Apps & Workspace Studio** | ✔ | ✔ | ✔ | ✔ | Connect with Asana, Mailchimp, Slack, Salesforce, etc. |

---

## How to Enable Gemini Alpha Features

> **Note:** Enabling alpha turns on **all** available alpha features; you cannot control individual features.

1. Sign in to the [Google Admin console](https://admin.google.com/).
2. Navigate to **Menu** → **Generative AI** → **Gemini for Workspace**.  
   *(Requires the **Gemini Settings adminis

# Document 360: Turn Gemini Enterprise on or off for users  |  Google Workspace Help

Source URL: https://knowledge.workspace.google.com/admin/generative-ai/gemini-enterprise/turn-gemini-enterprise-on-or-off-for-users
Product area: admin-console
Original file: docs/turn-gemini-enterprise-on-or-off-for-users-google-workspace-help-353f8968.md

# Turn Gemini Enterprise on or off for users

**Source:** [Google Workspace Admin Help – Gemini Enterprise](https://knowledge.workspace.google.com/admin/generative-ai/gemini-enterprise/turn-gemini-enterprise-on-or-off-for-users)

## Overview
As an administrator, you control which users can use **Gemini Enterprise**. You can turn it on for:

- Everyone in your organization
- Specific organizational units (OUs)
- Specific access groups

Users with access can use the **Gemini Enterprise side‑panel agents**, **Gemini Enterprise assistant**, and **Gemini CLI**.

> **Important:** Gemini Enterprise is **not included** with Google Workspace subscriptions. You must purchase separate licenses.

## Get licenses
- **Business edition** – Contact a Google sales representative or Google Cloud partner.
- **All editions** – You can also sign up directly and purchase licenses.  
  → [Get subscriptions and assign licenses for Gemini Enterprise](https://docs.cloud.google.com/gemini/enterprise/docs/licenses)

---

## Turn the service on or off

Before you begin, place users’ accounts in an [organizational unit](https://knowledge.workspace.google.com/admin/users/advanced/how-the-organizational-structure-works) (department) or an [access group](https://knowledge.workspace.google.com/admin/users/advanced/customize-service-access-using-access-groups) (cross‑department access).

### Steps
1. In the **Google Admin console**, go to **Menu** → **Generative AI** → **Gemini Enterprise**.  
   *(Requires the* **Service Settings administrator** *privilege.)*
2. Click **Service status**.
3. Choose **On for everyone** or **Off for everyone**, then click **Save**.
4. (Optional) To apply the setting only to specific users, select an **organizational unit** or an **access group** at the side.  
   **Group settings override organizational units.** [Learn more](https://knowledge.workspace.google.com/admin/users/advanced/customize-service-settings-using-configuration-groups#how-groups-work)

> Changes can take **up to 24 hours** but typically happen more quickly. [Learn more](https://knowledge.workspace.google.com/admin/support/troubleshooting/how-changes-propagate-to-google-services)

---

## Share chats externally (Business edition only)
*Applies only to **Gemini Enterprise – Business edition**.*

Control whether users can share Gemini chats outside your organization.

### Steps
1. Go to **Menu** → **Generative AI** → **Gemini Enterprise** → **Business edition**.  
   *(Requires the* **Service administrator** *privilege.)*
2. Click **Sharing options**.
3. (Optional) Apply to an OU or access group.
4. Choose one:
   - **Off** – Share only with domains associated with your Google Workspace organization (primary + secondary domains). No external sharing.
   - **Share with allowlisted domains** – Share with accounts in your allowlisted domains. Click **Edit** to modify the list.  
     → [Allow sharing with only trusted domains](https://knowledge.workspace.google.com/admin/domains/allow-external-sharing-with-only-trusted-domains)
   - **On** – Share outside your organization to any domain.
5. Click **Save**.

---

## Control access to Workspace data
Gemini Enterprise – Business, Standard, and Plus editions **can independently access your organization’s Google Workspace data** (e.g., Gmail, Drive, Calendar). You decide whether each edition can read this data.

### Business edition access
1. Go to **Menu** → **Generative AI** → **Gemini Enterprise** → **Business Edition**.
2. Check or uncheck **Allow Gemini Enterprise to access Google Workspace data**.
3. (Optional) Apply to an OU or access group.
4. Click **Save**.

### Standard and Plus edition access
1. Go to **Menu** → **Generative AI** → **Gemini Enterprise** → **Standard, Plus, Frontline**.
2. Check or uncheck **Allow Gemini Enterprise to access Google Workspace data**.
3. (Optional) Apply to an OU or access group.
4. Click **Save**.

Again, **group settings override OUs**, and changes may take up to 24 hours to propagate.

---

## Legal & data ownership
- **Frontline, Standard, Plus editions:** Governed by [GCP Terms of Service](https://cloud.google.com/terms/) and Google Cloud Privacy Notice.
- **Business edition:** Governed by [Google ToS](https://policies.google.com/terms), Google Privacy Policy, and [Business Edition Additional Terms of Service](https://cloud.google.com/terms/gemini-enterprise/business).

Across all editions for Workspace customers, the following promises apply:

> - **You own your data**, not Google.  
> - Your data (prompts, outputs, and training) **isn’t used to train Google models** or models for any other customer.  
> - Google **never sells** customer data to third parties.

---

## Next steps
- [Gemini Enterprise overview (Google Cloud)](https://cloud.google.com/gemini-enterprise)

*Page last updated 2026-06-11 UTC.*

---

# Document 361: Turn the Gemini App on or off  |  Google Workspace Help

Source URL: https://knowledge.workspace.google.com/admin/generative-ai/gemini-app/turn-the-gemini-app-on-or-off
Product area: admin-console
Original file: docs/turn-the-gemini-app-on-or-off-google-workspace-help-0b9f06a6.md

# Turn the Gemini App On or Off — Comprehensive Summary

**Source:** [Google Workspace Admin Help](https://knowledge.workspace.google.com/admin/generative-ai/gemini-app/turn-the-gemini-app-on-or-off)  
**Last Updated:** 2026-06-11 UTC

---

## 1. What is the Gemini App?

The Gemini app includes:

- Web app at [gemini.google.com](https://gemini.google.com/)
- Mobile apps for Android and iOS
- Gemini in Chrome
- Gemini on Mac

It helps users brainstorm, summarize complex topics, create first drafts of outlines/emails/posts, and more.

> **Note:** “Gemini Apps” are also referred to as “Gemini app” or “Gemini.” When used with a work or school Google Account, different data handling terms may apply.

---

## 2. How to Get the Gemini App

### 2.1 As a Core Service (Enterprise‑grade data protections)

Available with most Google Workspace editions:

- **AI Expanded Access** / **AI Ultra Access**
- Business Starter\*, Standard\*, Plus\*
- Enterprise Starter, Standard, Plus
- Essentials, Enterprise Essentials, Enterprise Essentials Plus
- Frontline Starter, Standard, Plus
- Nonprofits

Users under these editions are subject to the **Google Workspace Terms of Service** – content is **not** human‑reviewed and **not** used for generative AI model training outside the domain without permission.

> \* Business edition users must sign up with a business email address or verify their domain to get the Gemini mobile app.

### 2.2 As an Additional Google Service

Users **without** a qualifying Workspace edition are subject to the **Google Terms of Service** and the **Gemini Apps Privacy Notice**.  
Their chats **may be reviewed by human reviewers** and used to improve Google’s products, services, and machine‑learning technologies.

> **Important:** Tell these users to avoid entering confidential or sensitive information in the Gemini app.

---

## 3. Feature & Model Comparison – Business/Enterprise Editions

| **Access level** | Additional Service (Android Enterprise, Cloud Identity, Chrome Enterprise, Business Base, Essentials Starter, G Suite, Workspace Individual) | Core Service – Basic (Business Starter, Essentials, Enterprise Essentials, Enterprise Essentials Plus, Frontline Starter/Standard/Plus, Nonprofits) | Core Service – Pro (Business Plus, Business Standard, Enterprise Plus, Enterprise Standard, Gemini Enterprise/Business – Legacy) | Core Service – AI Expanded Access | Core Service – AI Ultra Access |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Models** | | | | | |
| Pro | Basic access – limits may change frequently | Up to 25 prompts / 4 hours | Up to 200 prompts / day | Up to 500 prompts / day | Up to 500 prompts / day |
| Thinking | Basic access – limits may change frequently | Up to 300 prompts / day | Up to 600 prompts / day | Up to 1500 prompts / day | Up to 1500 prompts / day |
| Fast | General access | | | | |
| **Context window** | 32 thousand tokens | | 1 million tokens | 1 million tokens | 1 million tokens |
| **Features** | | | | | |

# Document 362: Understanding ‘How this content was made’ disclosures on YouTube - YouTube Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/youtube/answer/15447836
Product area: youtube-ai
Original file: docs/understanding-how-this-content-was-made-disclosures-on-youtube-youtube-help-95be95ed.md

# Understanding “How this content was made” Disclosures on YouTube — Summary

**Source:** YouTube Help — <https://support.google.com/youtube/answer/15447836>

## Core Purpose

YouTube may show information explaining **how a video was made**. These disclosures can appear in the **video player** or **video description**, especially in the expanded description section labeled **“How this content was made.”**

These notices help viewers understand:

- The **origin** of the content
- Whether the content was **meaningfully altered**
- Whether the content was **synthetically generated**, including with AI
- Whether disclosure information came from creators, YouTube tools, or secure metadata such as **Content Credentials / C2PA**

---

## Key Excerpts

> While watching YouTube, you might find information about how content was made. This information, found in the video player or description, can offer insight into the content's origin and whether it has been meaningfully altered.

> The "How this content was made" section in the expanded video description provides further details.

> ‘Altered or synthetic content’: This indicates that a video contains content that has been meaningfully altered or synthetically generated.

> YouTube will carry forward disclosures from tools and creators available with secure Content Credentials (C2PA) 2.1 or higher that indicate the entire video was made with AI.

> Content Credential (C2PA) disclosures often include an attribution to the signing authority, which indicates the source that validated the information within the secure Content Credentials.

---

## Disclosure Type: “Altered or Synthetic Content”

The disclosure **“Altered or synthetic content”** means a video includes content that has been:

- **Meaningfully altered**, or
- **Synthetically generated**, such as by AI

This disclosure may appear in the **“How this content was made”** section of the expanded video description.

---

## When Viewers Should Expect This Disclosure

YouTube says viewers should expect to see an **altered or synthetic content** disclosure when one of the following applies:

1. **The creator manually discloses it**
   - The creator indicates in the **YouTube Studio workflow** that the video contains altered or synthetic content.
   - If creators do not disclose such content, YouTube may sometimes apply a label itself.

   > When content is undisclosed, in some cases, YouTube may take action to reduce the risk of harm to viewers by proactively applying a label that creators will not have the option to remove.

2. **The creator uses YouTube’s generative AI tools**
   - Example given: **DreamScreen**

3. **The video contains valid Content Credentials data**
   - Specifically, data indicating that **the entire video is made with AI**
   - YouTube references **Content Credentials / C2PA** as the standard involved.

---

## Content Credentials / C2PA

YouTube may use **Content Credentials**, also known as **C2PA**, to carry forward disclosure information.

### Important Details

- YouTube supports disclosures from secure Content Credentials using **C2PA 2.1 or higher**.
- These disclosures may come from:
  - External tools
  - Creators
  - Signing authorities that validate metadata
- The disclosure may appear:
  - As a **label on the video player**
  - As language in the **detailed / expanded video description**

### AI-Specific Use

YouTube carries forward disclosures when secure Content Credentials indicate:

- The **entire video was made with AI**

---

## “Info from” Attributions

Some C2PA-based disclosures include an **“Info from”** attribution.

This attribution identifies the **signing authority** — the source that validated the information inside the secure Content Credentials.

> Content Credential (C2PA) disclosures often include an attribution to the signing authority, which indicates the source that validated the information within the secure Content Credentials.

When this applies, viewers will see an **“Info from”** attribution beneath the disclosure.

---

## Related Disclosure: “Captured with a Camera”

YouTube notes that Content Credentials may also be used for other types of content-origin disclosures, including:

- **“Captured with a camera”**

This is a separate disclosure type from **altered or synthetic content**.

---

## Practical Takeaways

- YouTube’s **“How this content was made”** section helps viewers evaluate whether a video was AI-generated, synthetic, or meaningfully altered.
- The **“Altered or synthetic content”** label can come from:
  - Creator self-disclosure
  - YouTube’s own AI tools
  - Secure Content Credentials metadata
  - YouTube-applied labels in some undisclosed cases
- Some labels may be **non-removable by creators** if YouTube applies them to reduce risk of harm.
- **C2PA 2.1+ Content Credentials** can allow disclosure information to persist across platforms and tools.
- **“Info from”** tells viewers who validated the disclosure information.

---

# Document 364: Upgrade NotebookLM - NotebookLM Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/notebooklm/answer/16206866?hl=en
Product area: notebooklm
Original file: docs/upgrade-notebooklm-notebooklm-help-36a88fae.md

# Upgrade NotebookLM — Comprehensive Summary

Source: [NotebookLM Help: Upgrade NotebookLM](https://support.google.com/notebooklm/answer/16206866?hl=en)

## Overview

NotebookLM can be upgraded for **higher usage limits** and **premium features** through:

- [Google AI Plans](https://one.google.com/about/ai-premium/)
- [Google Cloud / NotebookLM for Enterprise](https://cloud.google.com/resources/notebooklm-enterprise?sjid=4840678451071865222-NC&hl=en)
- Qualifying [Google Workspace plans](https://support.google.com/notebooklm/answer/16337734)

---

## Key Excerpts

> You can upgrade NotebookLM to get additional capabilities, including higher limits and more features through Google AI Plans, Google Cloud, or a qualifying Google Workspace plan.

> Your data is protected, and is not used to train NotebookLM unless you provide feedback.

> Google Workspace or Google Workspace for Education users. Your uploads, queries and the model's responses in NotebookLM will not be reviewed by human reviewers even when you provide thumbs up or down feedback, and will not be used to train AI models.

> Sharing a notebook does not change the source limit for any collaborator.

> Daily quotas are reset after 24 hours; monthly quotas are reset after 30 days.

> Some users may see a report, flashcards, infographic, slide deck, audio overview, or a video overview created automatically, when sources are first added to a notebook. These are generated once and do not contribute to limits.

> To confirm if you're using NotebookLM with upgraded plans, check to see if you have a distinct badge next to your user profile image in the user interface.

---

# Google AI Plans: NotebookLM Tiers

NotebookLM is available in several tiers:

1. **NotebookLM Standard**
2. **NotebookLM in Plus**
3. **NotebookLM in Pro**
4. **NotebookLM in Ultra — 20 TB Plan**
5. **NotebookLM in Ultra — 30 TB Plan**

---

## How to Upgrade

| Tier | How to Upgrade |
|---|---|
| **NotebookLM Standard** | Sign up free using a Gmail account, or use a qualifying Workspace / Workspace for Education license. |
| **NotebookLM in Plus** | Sign up for a [Google AI Plus](https://one.google.com/ai?utm_source=notebooklm&utm_medium=web&utm_campaign=upgrade_page) plan. |
| **NotebookLM in Pro** | Sign up for a [Google AI Pro](https://one.google.com/ai?utm_source=notebooklm&utm_medium=web&utm_campaign=upgrade_page) plan, or use a qualifying Workspace / Workspace for Education license. |
| **NotebookLM in Ultra** | Sign up for a [Google AI Ultra](https://one.google.com/ai?utm_source=notebooklm&utm_medium=web&utm_campaign=upgrade_page) plan, or use a qualifying Workspace / Workspace for Education license. |

Related plan-management help:

- [Manage Google One plans](https://support.google.com/googleone/answer/9003633?sjid=13573268693365001414-NA)
- [Manage Workspace plans](https://support.google.com/a/answer/15095586?sjid=13573268693365001414-NA)

---

## Supported Countries & Regions

### Standard

NotebookLM is available in browser to users above the age of consent, or the applicable age in their country, in [supported countries & territories](https://support.google.com/notebooklm/answer/16164461#notebooklmcountries).

### Plus

Available in browsers with Plus access where [Google AI Plus is available](https://support.google.com/googleone/answer/16548195?hl=en#zippy=).

### Pro

Available in browsers with Pro access where [Google AI Pro is available](https://support.google.com/googleone/answer/16476811?hl=en#zippy=%2Clist-of-countries-where-google-ai-pro-is-supported%2Clist-of-countries-regions-where-google-ai-pro-is-supported).

### Ultra

Available in browsers with Ultra access where [Google AI Ultra is available](https://support.google.com/gemini/answer/16275805?hl=en#ultra_countries&zippy=%2Cwhere-google-ai-pro-google-ai-ultra-are-available).

### Mobile App

For all tiers, see: [NotebookLM mobile app supported countries & territories](https://support.google.com/notebooklm/answer/16296687?sjid=15971369145674768503-NA#Supportedcountriesandterritories)

---

# Upgrade NotebookLM — Summary

**Source:** [NotebookLM Help: Upgrade NotebookLM](https://support.google.com/notebooklm/answer/16213268?hl=en)

NotebookLM can be upgraded for **higher usage limits**, **premium features**, and **expanded enterprise/security capabilities** through:

- **Google AI Plans**
- **Google Cloud**
- **Qualifying Google Workspace / Workspace for Education plans**

---

## Key Excerpts

> “You can upgrade NotebookLM to get additional capabilities, including higher limits and more features through Google AI Plans, Google Cloud, or a qualifying Google Workspace plan.”

> “Sharing a notebook does not change the source limit for any collaborator.”

> “Daily quotas are reset after 24 hours; monthly quotas are reset after 30 days.”

> “Your data is protected, and is not used to train NotebookLM unless you provide feedback.”

> “Google Workspace or Google Workspace for Education users. Your uploads, queries and the model's responses in NotebookLM will not be reviewed by human reviewers even when you provide thumbs up or down feedback, and will not be used to train AI models.”

> “Your uploaded files will remain within your GCP project. Data regionalization is honored.”

# Upgrade Options

## 1. Google AI Plans

NotebookLM offers multiple tiers through Google AI Plans:

| Plan | How to Upgrade |
|---|---|
| **NotebookLM Standard** | Free with a Gmail account, or with a qualifying Workspace / Workspace for Education license |
| **NotebookLM in Plus** | Sign up for a **Google AI Plus** plan |
| **NotebookLM in Pro** | Sign up for a **Google AI Pro** plan, or use a qualifying Workspace / Workspace for Education license |
| **NotebookLM in Ultra — 20 TB Plan** | Sign up for a **Google AI Ultra** plan, or use a qualifying Workspace / Workspace for Education license |
| **NotebookLM in Ultra — 30 TB Plan** | Same Ultra path; higher limits than 20 TB plan |

- Google One plan management
- Workspace plan management

# Premium Features by Google AI Plan

|---|---|
| **Custom Chat** | Available to everyone |
| **Analytics** | Available to everyone |
| **Advanced Sharing** | Available to paid users |
| **Gemini Model Access — Standard / Plus** | Access to Gemini models |
| **Gemini Model Access — Pro** | Higher access to Gemini models |
| **Gemini Model Access — Ultra** | Highest access to Gemini models |
| **Earlier Access to Key Features — Standard** | Standard |
| **Earlier Access to Key Features — Plus** | Early |
| **Earlier Access to Key Features — Pro / Ultra** | Priority |
| **Watermark Removal — Standard / Plus / Pro** | No |
| **Watermark Removal — Ultra 20 TB** | Yes |
| **Watermark Removal — Ultra 30 TB** | Yes, for Infographics and Slide Decks |

- **Daily quotas reset after 24 hours.**
- **Monthly quotas reset after 30 days.**

# Document 366: Usage limits  |  Google Chat  |  Google for Developers

Source URL: https://developers.google.com/workspace/chat/limits
Product area: chat-developers
Original file: docs/usage-limits-google-chat-google-for-developers-75b4339d.md

# Google Chat API Usage Limits — Summary

**Source:** Google for Developers — Google Chat API Usage Limits  
**Last updated:** **2026-05-01 UTC**

## Overview

Google Chat API enforces quotas and limits to ensure **fair usage** and protect **Google Workspace performance**.

> Because the Google Chat API is a shared service, we apply quotas and limitations to make sure that it's used fairly by all users and to protect the overall performance of Google Workspace.

If a quota is exceeded, the API returns:

```http
429: Too many requests
```

Google notes that additional backend rate-limit checks can also produce the same error.

**Recommended response:** use a **truncated exponential backoff** algorithm and retry later.

> As long as you stay within the per-minute quotas listed in the following tables, there's no limit to the number of requests you can make per day.

Multiple quota types can apply to a Chat API method:

- **Per-project quotas** — apply to a Google Cloud project / single Chat app.
- **Per-space quotas** — apply within a given Chat space and are shared by all Chat apps acting in that space.
- **Per-user quotas** — apply across all Chat apps calling API methods on behalf of a user using user authentication.

---

## Per-Project Quotas

Per-project quotas limit query rates for a **Google Cloud project**, typically applying to a single Chat app calling the listed Chat API methods.

These limits are also visible in the Google Cloud Console on the **Quotas** page.

| Per-project quota | Chat API methods | Limit |
|---|---|---:|
| **Message writes per minute** | `spaces.messages.create`<br>`spaces.messages.patch`<br>`spaces.messages.delete` | **3000 / 60 sec** |
| **Message reads per minute** | `spaces.messages.get`<br>`spaces.messages.list` | **3000 / 60 sec** |
| **Membership writes per minute** | `spaces.members.create`<br>`spaces.members.delete` | **300 / 60 sec** |
| **Membership reads per minute** | `spaces.members.get`<br>`spaces.members.list` | **3000 / 60 sec** |
| **Space writes per minute** | `spaces.setup`<br>`spaces.create`<br>`spaces.patch`<br>`spaces.delete` | **60 / 60 sec** |
| **Space reads per minute** | `spaces.get`<br>`spaces.list`<br>`spaces.findDirectMessage` | **3000 / 60 sec** |
| **Attachment writes per minute** | `media.upload` | **600 / 60 sec** |
| **Attachment reads per minute** | `spaces.messages.attachments.get`<br>`media.download` | **3000 / 60 sec** |
| **Reaction writes per minute** | `spaces.messages.reactions.create`<br>`spaces.messages.reactions.delete` | **600 / 60 sec** |
| **Reaction reads per minute** | `spaces.messages.reactions.list` | **3000 / 60 sec** |
| **CustomEmoji writes per minute** | `customEmojis.create`<br>`customEmojis.delete` | **600 / 60 sec** |
| **CustomEmoji reads per minute** | `customEmojis.get`<br>`customEmojis.list` | **3000 / 60 sec** |
| **Section writes per minute** | `users.sections.create`<br>`users.sections.delete`<br>`users.sections.patch`<br>`users.sections.position`<br>`users.sections.items.move` | **600 / 60 sec** |
| **Section reads per minute** | `users.sections.list`<br>`users.sections.items.list` | **3000 / 60 sec** |

---

## Per-Space Quotas

Per-space quotas limit query rates **inside a specific Chat space**.

These quotas are **shared among all Chat apps** acting in that space.

| Per-space quota | Chat API methods | Limit |
|---|---|---:|
| **Reads per second** | `media.download`<br>`spaces.get`<br>`spaces.members.get`<br>`spaces.members.list`<br>`spaces.messages.get`<br>`spaces.messages.list`<br>`spaces.messages.attachments.get`<br>`spaces.messages.reactions.list` | **15 / sec** |
| **Writes per second** | `media.upload`<br>`spaces.delete`<br>`spaces.patch`<br>`spaces.messages.create`<br>`spaces.messages.delete`<br>`spaces.messages.patch`<br>`spaces.messages.reactions.delete` | **1 / sec** |
| **Create Reaction writes per second** | `spaces.messages.reactions.create` | **5 / sec** |
| **Message writes per second while importing data to Google Chat** | `spaces.messages.create` | **10 / sec** |

**Important note:** additional limits apply to **incoming webhooks** for `spaces.messages.create`.

---

## Per-User Quotas

Per-user quotas limit query rates for a **Google Chat user**.

They apply to all Chat apps that call Chat API methods **on behalf of a user** using **user authentication**.

| Per-user quota | Chat API methods | Limit |
|---|---|---:|
| **CustomEmoji writes per second** | `customEmojis.create`<br>`customEmojis.delete` | **1 / sec** |
| **CustomEmoji reads per second** | `customEmojis.get`<br>`customEmojis.list` | **15 / sec** |
| **Section writes per second** | `users.sections.create`<br>`users.sections.delete`<br>`users.sections.patch`<br>`users.sections.position`<br>`users.sections.items.move` | **1 / sec** |
| **Section reads per second** | `users.sections.list`<br>`users.sections.items.list` | **15 / sec** |

---

## Additional Usage Limits

High traffic directed at the same Chat space can trigger **ad

[... summary truncated for context management ...]

# Document 367: Use AI Mode in Chrome - Computer - Google Chrome Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/chrome/answer/16704170
Product area: chrome-ai
Original file: docs/use-ai-mode-in-chrome-computer-google-chrome-help-c160ec4a.md

# Use AI Mode in Chrome — Summary

**Source:** Google Chrome Help — “Use AI Mode in Chrome”  
**URL:** https://support.google.com/chrome/answer/16704170

## Overview

AI Mode in Chrome lets users ask questions, receive AI-powered answers, ask follow-up questions, and get helpful links directly from Chrome. It improves search relevance by breaking a query into subtopics and searching for them simultaneously.

> “When you use AI Mode, it divides your questions into subtopics, then searches for each one simultaneously. This way, AI Mode finds more relevant content that matches your query.”

**Important caveat:**

> “AI responses may include mistakes.”

Google links to additional information about how data helps develop generative AI in Search.

---

## Availability & Requirements

### Availability

**Important:** AI Mode in Chrome is not available to everyone or on every device yet.

> “This feature is gradually coming to people outside the US and to non-English languages, so it might not be available to you right now.”

### Recommended setting

For the best experience, Google recommends enabling **Web & App Activity** in **My Activity**:

- Go to **My Activity**
- Turn on **Web & App Activity**

If this setting is off:

- You can still use AI Mode.
- You **can’t pick up where you left off** with previous searches.

---

## What AI Mode Can Do in Chrome

From Chrome’s search box or address bar, you can:

- Enter detailed AI-powered search queries
- Ask follow-up questions
- Access AI Mode results
- Add recent tabs
- Upload an image or file
- Use AI tools such as:
  - **Canvas**
  - **Create Images**
  - **Deep Search**
- Choose a model for your question
- Use voice input
- Ask about the current page you’re browsing
- View AI Mode history

Google also links to a related feature for using AI to search and chat about tabs.

---

## Get Started with AI Mode in Chrome

### From the New Tab Page

1. Open **Chrome** on your computer.
2. Open a **New Tab** page.
3. On the right side of the search box, select **AI Mode**.
   - Or use the keyboard shortcut:

     > **Tab** + **Enter**

4. On the left side of the search box, select the “add” / tools option. You can:
   - Add recent tabs
   - Upload an image or file
   - Apply tools, including:
     - Canvas
     - Create Images
     - Deep Search
   - Choose a model for your question

5. To select from previous activity, choose **View your AI Mode history**.
6. Enter a question. Chrome directs you to the **AI Mode results page**.

---

### From the Address Bar

1. Open **Chrome** on your computer.
2. On the right side of the address bar, select **AI Mode**.
   - Or use the keyboard shortcut:

     > **Tab** + **Enter**

3. Enter your question.

Optional actions:

- To use your voice, select the **Microphone** icon.
- To add media, tools, or models to your question, select the add/tools option.
- To ask about the current page you’re browsing, select the add/tools option.

### Address Bar Tips

- When you ask about the current page, the result opens in the **AI Mode side panel**.
- To hide **AI Mode** and **Add tabs and more** from the address bar:
  1. Right-click the address bar.
  2. Unselect **Always show AI Mode**.
- To check AI Mode history from the address bar, select **View your AI Mode history**.

---

## Delete AI Mode History Saved in Your Account

To delete AI Mode history:

1. Open **Chrome** on your computer.
2. Open a **New Tab** page.
3. On the right side of the search box, select **AI Mode**.
4. On the left, select **AI Mode history** to find past searches saved in your account.
5. Choose what to delete:

### Delete a Specific AI Mode Search

Next to the AI Mode history item:

1. Select **More**.
2. Select **Delete**.

### Delete All AI Mode History

1. Select **More**.
2. Select **Delete all**.

---

## Delete AI Mode Activity from Your Account

Google also notes that AI Mode activity saved in your account can be deleted through Search history:

1. Go to **Search history**: https://myactivity.google.com/product/search
2. Select **Delete**.

---

## What Happens When You Delete AI Mode History

Deleting AI Mode history does **not always immediately remove all related activity everywhere**.

Key points:

- If you delete searches from AI Mode history, they may still appear in **My Activity** for some time.
- These items are **auto-deleted within 24 hours**.
- You can also manually delete them from **My Activity**.

> “If you delete searches from AI Mode history, they may still appear in the ‘My Activity’ page for some time. These items are auto-deleted within 24 hours.”

### AI Overview to AI Mode interactions

If your search starts with an **AI Overview** and you select **“Dive deeper in AI Mode,”** you may see **2 entries** in Search history:

1. The original query
2. The transition to AI Mode for follow-up questions

### Search History vs. AI Mode History

Deleting a search from **AI Mode history** does not fully remove it from all Google history.

> “Whe

[... summary truncated for context management ...]

# Document 368: Use AI Mode to check local availability and pricing - Computer - Google Search Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/websearch/answer/17104441
Product area: search-ai
Original file: docs/use-ai-mode-to-check-local-availability-and-pricing-computer-google-search-help-23f3b3ec.md

# Use AI Mode to Check Local Availability and Pricing — Google Search Help Summary

**Source:** Google Search Help  
**URL:** https://support.google.com/websearch/answer/17104441  
**Platform covered:** Computer

---

## Key Notices & Warnings

> **Important:** We’re currently updating settings for Google Search services, which include Search, Maps, Shopping, Flights, Hotels, Translate and News. The settings described in this article are being updated so may not match your current experience. [Learn more about the new Search services settings](https://support.google.com/websearch/answer/17025248).

> **Important:** AI responses may include mistakes. [Learn about generative AI and its limitations](https://support.google.com/websearch/answer/13954172).

---

## What AI Mode Can Do

AI Mode in Google Search can help with time-consuming local tasks by gathering information from the web, filling out forms, or calling businesses directly when details are not available online.

### Supported use cases include:

- Finding local appointments, such as:
  - Haircuts
  - Auto repair
- Researching home services, such as:
  - Electricians
  - Tree cutting
  - Exterior cleaning
- Checking:
  - Real-time pricing
  - Shopping inventory
  - Dining reservations

AI Mode is designed to help users get specific details such as service costs, appointment availability, local inventory, and reservation options.

---

## Availability

- Available in **English**
- Available to **signed-in users over 18**
- Works on both:
  - Mobile Google Search
  - Desktop Google Search

### Dining Reservations Availability

Dining reservations are available in **English and Hindi** in:

- United States
- Anguilla
- Australia
- Canada
- Cook Islands
- Gibraltar
- Hong Kong
- India
- Isle of Man
- New Zealand
- Singapore
- South Africa
- United Kingdom

### Recommended Settings

For the best experience, Google recommends enabling:

- [Search history](https://myactivity.google.com/search-services/settings)
- [Personalized recommendations](https://www.google.com/search-personalization/)

---

## How to Use AI Mode on a Computer

### 1. Open AI Mode

Go to Google Search and select **AI Mode**, or visit:

> [google.com/ai](https://google.com/ai)

---

### 2. Enter a Request

Type your question or task in the **Ask anything** bar near the bottom of the screen.

Example prompts from the article:

> “Deep cleaning appointment for an apartment move out.”

> “Reservation for 6 on Friday with vegetarian options”

---

### 3. Select “Check for me”

Choose **“Check for me”** to let AI Mode help complete the request.

---

### 4. Answer Follow-Up Questions if Needed

AI Mode may ask clarifying questions to better understand the request.

> **Note:** If your initial request has enough detail, this step will be skipped automatically.

---

### 5. Google May Call Businesses on Your Behalf

If a business has limited information online, Google may call the business to gather the details you requested.

Important call-related details:

- Only the details you shared and confirmed are provided to businesses.
- Calls are used to get information not available online, such as:
  - Earliest appointment times
  - Current inventory
  - Pricing or availability

If you do **not** want Google to call businesses:

> type “don’t call” into the prompt box and then select **Enter**.

---

### 6. Review the Request

Before AI Mode proceeds:

- Review the request details.
- Choose whether to receive an email notification.
- If calls are made, the email will notify you when results are ready.

---

### 7. Confirm the Request

Select:

> **Looks good**

This allows AI Mode to proceed.

---

### 8. View Results

Timing depends on the type of request:

- **Web results:** usually take a few minutes
- **Call results:** can take up to **30 minutes**

You can leave AI Mode and return later.

A **blue dot** appears on the thread when results are ready in AI Mode history.

---

### 9. Email Notifications

If you opted into email notifications and calls were made, you’ll receive an email when your results are ready.

---

### 10. Stop the Request

To end all work on a request, select:

> **Stop**

---

## Important Behavior Details

> **Important:**
>
> - AI Mode is designed to provide the most accurate and up-to-date information. If a business does not have its information listed online, such as the earliest available appointment or current inventory in stock, Google will make a call to get those answers for you.
> - AI Mode can process multiple requests simultaneously. If you provide unrelated details during an active search, AI Mode will suggest starting a new thread so each task is handled independently.
> - Google only calls for the specific information you've requested, keeping interactions brief, professional, and focused. Business owners can learn more about managing [these automated calls](https://support.google.com/business/answer/16190256).

---

## Privacy, Data Use & AI Improvement



[... summary truncated for context management ...]

# Document 369: Use AI to create with Google Photos - Android - Google Photos Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/photos/answer/16763021

# Use AI to Create with Google Photos (Android) — Summary

**Source:** Google Photos Help — “Use AI to create with Google Photos”  
**URL:** https://support.google.com/photos/answer/16763021

---

## Key Excerpts / Important Notices

> **Important:**
>
> - These features aren't available in all regions at this time. Functionality can vary by location. Some features may have additional eligibility requirements.
> - The photos you want to edit must be backed up.
> - These features require network connection.
> - You can only generate a limited number of photos or videos per day. Purchasing a Google AI plan increases your number of generations.

> You can transform your photos with Generative AI through **Photo to video**, and **Remix**. These features may give unexpected or inaccurate results.

> **Important:** Videos generated through the Photo to video tool may now contain audio.

> **Tip:** The video you created saves to the day you created it and not on the original date the photo was taken.

---

## Overview

Google Photos offers generative AI creation tools on Android, mainly:

- **Remix** — Reimagines photos using AI templates.
- **Photo to video** — Turns a still photo into a short dynamic video using effects or text prompts.

These tools are found in the **Create** tab of the Google Photos app and require eligible accounts, backed-up photos, and an internet connection.

---

## Availability & Usage Limits

- Features are **not available in all regions**.
- Functionality may vary depending on location.
- Some features may have extra eligibility requirements.
- Photos must be **backed up** before editing.
- A **network connection** is required.
- Users can generate only a **limited number of photos or videos per day**.
- Buying a **Google AI plan** increases the number of available generations.

---

## Eligibility Requirements

To use these AI creation tools:

- You must be **at least 13 years old**.
- You need a **personal Google Account**.
- These features are **not available** for:
  - Google Workspace accounts
  - School accounts

Additional age restrictions:

- **Remix** is available only to users **18 years old and over**.
- The **text prompt** feature in Photo to video is initially available only to users **18 years old and over**.

---

## Terms of Service

Use of **Photo to video** and **Remix** is subject to:

- [Google Terms of Service](https://policies.google.com/terms)
- [Generative AI Prohibited Use Policy](https://policies.google.com/terms/generative-ai/use-policy)

---

# Remix Your Photos

## What Remix Does

**Remix** is a tool in the Google Photos **Create** tab that uses AI templates to turn photos into unique, shareable creations.

It allows users to:

- Browse featured or full template selections
- Apply creative styles or visual effects
- Generate personalized or template-based AI creations
- Save, share, regenerate, or send feedback on results

---

## How to Use Remix on Android

1. Open the **Google Photos** app on your Android device.
2. At the bottom, tap **Create**.
3. Open **Remix** in one of two ways:
   - Under the **“Remix your photos”** header, select a template.
   - Tap **Remix** to view available templates.
4. If using the feature for the first time, an acknowledgement screen appears.
   - Tap **Try now** to continue.
5. Choose a template.
6. Tap **Generate**.
7. After generation, you can:
   - Tap **Save** to save the result.
   - Tap **Share** to share it.
   - Tap **Regenerate** to get a different result using the same template.
   - Tap **Send feedback** to share your experience and help improve the feature.

---

## Browsing Remix Templates

You can start from featured templates or browse all options.

### Start with a Featured Template

1. Under **“Remix your photos”**, find the featured template cards.
2. Scroll through available templates.

### Browse All Templates

1. Tap **View all**.
2. Scroll through every template option.

### Filter Templates

1. Tap specific category labels.
2. Scroll through the filtered template options.
3. To try another creative direction, tap a new category at any time.

---

## Remix Template Types

### Personalized Templates

Personalized templates are marked with a **badge**.

Key details:

- They use insights from your life to create unique and relevant outputs.
- Some personalized templates do **not require uploading an image**.
- For some templates, generation starts immediately after selection.
- To use badged templates, you must meet the **eligibility requirements for Gemini features in Photos**.

### Other Templates

Other templates apply specific creative styles or visual effects to generate a photo of you.

These templates usually require a **reference photo** before generation.

#### Upload a Reference Photo

To upload from your photo library:

1. Tap **Upload your own reference photo**.
2. Tap **Add photo**.
3. Choose from suggested photos or scroll through your library.
4. To change the uploaded photo, tap **Edit**

# Document 370: Use AI to generate video clips in Google Vids - Google Docs Editors Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/docs/answer/16143507
Product area: google-vids
Original file: docs/use-ai-to-generate-video-clips-in-google-vids-google-docs-editors-help-716b664a.md

# AI Video Clip Generation in Google Vids – Comprehensive Summary

## Feature Availability & Requirements

- **Eligible subscription**: Requires an eligible Google Workspace subscription with Gemini features.  
  [Learn about Gemini features and plans](https://support.google.com/docs/answer/13952129)
- **Supported languages**: [See Gemini language support](https://support.google.com/docs/answer/14925782)

## Generated Video Specifications

- **Frame rate**: 24 fps
- **Resolution**: 720p
- **Aspect ratio**:
  - **Landscape (Widescreen)**: 16:9
  - **Portrait (Vertical)**: 9:16
- **Monthly generation limit**: Up to 50 videos per month; resets at 12 AM PT on the 1st day of each month.  
  For Google AI Pro / AI Ultra with family sharing, the limit is shared.  
  [Learn more about generation limits](https://support.google.com/docs/answer/15609411)

---

## 1. Generate a Video Clip from Scratch (Text Prompt)

**Important**: Generated clips are only available in landscape or portrait orientation.

1. Open [Google Vids](https://docs.google.com/videos/create?usp=hc) on your computer.
2. Select **Veo 3.1** from the start menu – or, in an existing video, click the Veo icon on the right.
3. In the side panel dropdown, choose **Create from Scratch**.
4. (Optional) Choose **Landscape** or **Portrait** aspect ratio.
5. Enter a **text prompt** with details: subject, location, action, camera, lighting, dialogue, sounds, tone.  
   **Example prompts**:
   - “A cat walking down a fashion week catwalk wearing designer clothes with electronic music in the background.”
   - “A dog driving a race car in Italy at sunset with loud engine roars.”
6. (Optional) Set a clip aspect ratio different from your canvas using the option at the bottom of the prompt window.
7. Click **Generate**.
8. To refine, edit the text prompt and regenerate.
9. Hover over the generated clip and click **Insert** to add it to your Vid.
10. The clip appears on its own timeline track at the bottom – you can control it there.

> **Tip**: Always insert the clip into your Vid to keep it accessible for later editing within the session.

---

## 2. Animate an Image into an 8‑Second Video Clip

**Important**: Only **Landscape (16:9)** or **Portrait (9:16)** images are accepted. If the uploaded image has a different ratio, Vids automatically adds **black bars**.

### Steps

1. Open [Google Vids](https://docs.google.com/videos/create?usp=hc).
2. Select **Veo 3.1** (as above).
3. In the side panel dropdown, choose **Animate an image**.
4. (Optional) Select **Landscape** or **Portrait**.
5. Click **Add image** and upload a **.jpg** or **.png** image.
   - **Best practice**: Use a high‑quality, sharp, clear source image – its composition and style set the visual direction.
6. Enter a motion‑focused description (do **not** re‑describe the scene). Describe how elements move or how the camera moves.  
   **Example** (from an elephant image): “The elephant moves around naturally.”
7. Click **Create**. The clip appears in the “Generate video” panel when ready.
8. To adjust, click **Edit prompt** and refine.
9. Hover and click **Insert** to add the clip to your Vid; it gets its own timeline track.

---

## 3. Generate a Video with Reference Images (Ingredients)

**Important**: This feature only supports **Landscape (16:9)** aspect ratio.

- Upload **up to 3 reference images** to define subjects, styles, and locations.
- Re‑use the same references across multiple clips to maintain **consistent characters, design, and objects**.

**Example**: Provide images of a woman, a yellow mug, and an office; prompt:  
“Create a video of a woman drinking coffee from this mug, in this office.”

### Steps

1. Open [Google Vids](https://docs.google.com/videos/create?usp=hc) and select **Veo 3.1**.
2. From the side panel dropdown, choose **Create from scratch**.
3. Click **Ingredients** (image icon).
4. Select up to 3 images.
5. (Optional) Click **Avatar** to use an avatar (including its voice) as an ingredient.  
   **Note**: Avatar ingredients are limited to English and certain account types (see below).
6. Write a prompt that explains how to incorporate the ingredients – reference the objects, styles, or people.
7. Click **Generate**.

#### Avatar Availability (for Ingredients)
Avatars can only be used as ingredients in **English** and with these accounts:
- Business Starter, Business Standard, Business Plus
- Enterprise Starter, Enterprise Standard, Enterprise Plus
- Essentials Starter, Enterprise Essentials, Enterprise Essentials Plus
- Nonprofits
- Education Plus and Education Teaching and Learning add‑on
- Google AI Pro and Google AI Ultra (**USA only**)
- Google AI Pro for Education add‑on

---

## Tips for Best Results

- **Be specific** – include subject and tone.
- **Use precise, cinematic language**: Instead of “A car on a road,” try:  
  > “Wide shot, eye‑level, static camera focused on a red race convertible driving along with the camera down the center of an empty, paved road at

[... summary truncated for context management ...]

# Document 371: Use AI to search & ask about your tabs - Google Chrome Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/chrome/answer/17025061
Product area: chrome-ai
Original file: docs/use-ai-to-search-ask-about-your-tabs-google-chrome-help-af2b1bfd.md

# Use AI to Search & Ask About Your Tabs — Google Chrome Help

**Source:** https://support.google.com/chrome/answer/17025061  
**Feature:** AI Mode side-by-side in Google Chrome

---

## Overview

Chrome’s **AI Mode side-by-side** lets you browse while asking AI questions about your **open tabs** or **files** from either:

- A **side panel**
- A **full tab view**

You can start AI Mode from the **New Tab page**, add context such as recent tabs, images, or files, and use tools like **Canvas**, **Create Images**, and **Deep Search**.

---

## Key Availability & Requirements

> **Important:** AI Mode in Chrome isn't available to everyone or on every device at this time. This feature is gradually coming to people outside the US and to non-English languages, so it might not be available to you right now.

To use AI Mode in Chrome, you must be:

- **Signed in to Chrome**
- **Not browsing in Incognito mode**

---

## Start a Thread from the New Tab Page

1. Open **Chrome** on your computer.
2. Open a **New Tab** page.
3. On the right side of the search box, select **AI Mode**.
   - Or use the keyboard shortcut: **Tab** + **Enter**
4. On the left side of the search box, select the add/context icon. You can:
   - Add recent tabs
   - Upload an image or file
   - Apply tools, such as:
     - **Canvas**
     - **Create Images**
     - **Deep Search**
   - Choose a model for your question
5. To select from previous conversations, choose **View your AI Mode history**.
6. Enter a question to be taken to the **AI Mode results page**.

### Tips

- To expand the AI Mode panel into a full tab, **close the link you opened**.
- When you select a link from a full-page search, it automatically opens in the panel and adds that tab to your follow-up question.

---

## Add Context and Tools in AI Mode

While in the AI Mode panel:

1. Below the **“Ask anything”** text box, select the add/context icon.
2. From the menu, you can choose:

### Available Context Options

- **Find most recent tabs to add to your search:** Find all tabs currently open in the window.
- **Show more:** Find more tabs to add to your search.
- **Add images:** Add images to your search.
- **Add file:** Attach files to your search.

### Available AI Tools

- **Create images:** Describe the image you want to create.
- **Deep search:** Research anything.
- **Canvas:** Build visual content.

---

## AI Mode Panel Toolbar Options

From the toolbar of the AI Mode panel, you can:

- **Search history or thread:** Select **AI Mode history**
- **Create a new thread:** Select **Create**

### Limitations

> Internal Chrome URLs, Gemini chats, or certain workspaces subject to controls will not work with AI Mode side-by-side.

---

## Data Privacy for AI Mode Side-by-Side

Google describes several privacy and storage behaviors for AI Mode side-by-side.

### Temporary Data

Temporary data such as:

- Screenshots
- Lens selections
- PDF captures

stays available only during your session and **won’t be saved**.

### Saved Tab Information

To help users revisit conversations later:

- The **titles and URLs** of tabs added to a thread are saved for a specific number of months.
- These sources can still be referenced even if:
  - The original tabs are closed
  - Temporary screenshots have expired

### Confidentiality of Raw Data

> All raw data from both public and private URLs are treated with strict confidentiality.

This raw data is **not used for**:

- Human review
- Personalizing the experience
- Ad targeting
- Training AI models

---

## Related Resources

- [Use AI mode in Chrome](https://support.google.com/chrome/answer/16704170)
- [Get AI-powered responses with AI Mode in Google Search](https://support.google.com/websearch/answer/16011537)

---

## Help & Feedback Options

The page includes options to:

- Give feedback on whether the article was helpful
- Report issues such as:
  - Inaccurate information
  - Hard-to-understand content
  - Missing information
  - Irrelevant content
  - Minor errors
  - Other suggestions
- Post to the Chrome Help Community for additional support

It also mentions an **AI support assistant for Google Chrome** that can help with Chrome features, settings, and related questions.
