# Document 372: Use an AI Overview in Google Chat - Google Chat Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/chat/answer/16944767
Product area: chat
Original file: docs/use-an-ai-overview-in-google-chat-google-chat-help-fed08585.md

# Use an AI Overview in Google Chat — Summary

**Source:** Google Chat Help — <https://support.google.com/chat/answer/16944767>

## Feature Availability

- AI Overview in Google Chat is **rolling out gradually** as part of the **Gemini Alpha**.
- Only **some Gemini Alpha users** have access.
- Users may not have access to some or all related features.
- Access is controlled by the organization’s administrator.
  - If the feature is missing, the admin may not have enabled **Alpha features** for the organization.

> **Feature availability**
>
> - This feature is rolling out gradually as part of the Gemini Alpha. Only some Gemini Alpha users will have access to this feature.
> - You may not have access to some or all of these features.
> - Your administrator controls access to the Gemini Alpha.

## What AI Overview in Google Chat Does

AI Overview helps users quickly find information from Google Chat conversations by asking questions or entering keywords in the Chat search bar.

It can help:

- Locate specific information or messages across the user’s full Workspace profile.
- Understand past discussions or outcomes mentioned in chats.
- Find attachments without extensive manual searching.

## Search Google Chat Conversations with AI Overview

### Important Limitations

> **Important:**
>
> - If you use search operators, like `is:unread` or `from:`, you won’t get an AI Overview.
> - Search within any conversation or space doesn’t provide an AI Overview.

Key points:

- AI Overview appears only when searching from the main Chat search bar.
- It does **not** appear when:
  - Using search operators such as `is:unread` or `from:`
  - Searching within a specific conversation or space

### How to Get an AI Overview

1. On a computer, open:
   - [Google Chat](https://chat.google.com/), or
   - [Gmail](https://mail.google.com/)
     - In Gmail, click **Chat** on the left.
2. In the search bar, enter keywords or a natural-language query.

Examples:

> - "What was the deadline for project X?"
> - "Find messages from Alice about the Q3 review."
> - "Show me attachments from the design sprint space."

3. Click **Search** or press **Enter**.
4. Review the **AI Overview** at the top of the search results.

**Tip:** Provide as much detail as possible in the search description to get the best AI Overview.

## Expand or Collapse an AI Overview

When an AI Overview summary appears:

- Click **Show more** to expand and view the full summary.
- Click **Collapse** to hide the expanded overview.

## Give Feedback About an AI Overview

Google notes that Workspace with Gemini is continually learning and may not always support a request accurately.

If an AI Overview is inaccurate or feels unsafe, users can submit feedback to help improve AI-assisted Workspace features and Google’s broader AI efforts.

### Privacy Warning for Feedback

> Because feedback may be human readable, do not submit data that contains personal, confidential, or sensitive information.

### Feedback Steps

1. At the top right of the generated output, select:
   - **Good summary**, or
   - **Bad summary**
2. In the pop-up window, choose what data to share with the feedback.
   - Users can uncheck data they do not want to share.
   - If selecting **Bad summary**, click **Next** to choose the issue and add more feedback.
3. Select **Submit**.

### General Feedback

To provide general feedback about the feature:

- Select **Help**
- Then select **Send feedback to Google**

Google also links to instructions for sending feedback in Google Chat.

### Legal Issues

To report a legal issue, users should create a request through Google’s legal support process.

## Gemini Feature Suggestions: Important Disclaimers

Google provides several cautions about Gemini-powered suggestions:

> - Gemini feature suggestions don’t represent Google’s views, and should not be attributed to Google.
> - Don’t rely on Gemini features as medical, legal, financial or other professional advice.
> - Gemini features may suggest inaccurate or inappropriate information. Your feedback makes Gemini more helpful and safe.
> - Enterprise end users can submit feedback about their experience using this feature. End users are informed before they submit feedback that the feedback data shouldn’t contain personal, sensitive, or confidential information.

Key takeaway:

- Gemini outputs may be inaccurate or inappropriate.
- Users should not treat them as professional advice.
- Feedback may be reviewed, so users should avoid including sensitive information.

## Related Resources

- [Use Gemini conversation summaries for unread messages](https://support.google.com/chat/answer/16358670)
- [Summarize chats in Google Chat](https://support.google.com/chat/answer/12918975)

Available options include:

- **Hard to understand

# Document 373: Use apps connected to Gemini with a work or school Google Account - Computer - Gemini Apps Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/gemini/answer/14959807?hl=en
Product area: gemini-apps
Original file: docs/use-apps-connected-to-gemini-with-a-work-or-school-google-account-computer-gemini-apps-hel-30bd1957.md

# Use Apps Connected to Gemini with a Work or School Google Account — Summary

Source: Google Gemini Apps Help  
URL: https://support.google.com/gemini/answer/14959807?hl=en

## Overview

Gemini Apps can connect with other services, called **apps**, to provide more useful responses for work or school Google Accounts.

With connected apps, Gemini can help you:

- Find information from **Gmail**
- Summarize documents from **Google Drive**
- Find and summarize information from **Google Chat** in English
- Add and show tasks from **Google Tasks**
- Create and show notes and lists in **Google Keep**
- Create, find, and edit events in **Google Calendar**
- Attach a **GitHub repository** to better understand a codebase and debug issues

> **Important:** If you’re using Gemini Apps with a personal account, see Google’s separate guidance on using Connected Apps in Gemini.

---

## Requirements

To connect apps to Gemini with a work or school Google Account, all of the following must be true:

- You have a **qualifying edition of Google Workspace**.
- Your **Keep Activity** setting is turned on.
  - Only your organization’s Google Workspace administrator can manage this setting.
- Your Google Workspace administrator has enabled the ability to connect apps to Gemini.
  - Workspace administrators can manage access to apps in Gemini through the admin settings.

---

## How to Use Apps in Gemini

> **Important:** Gemini Apps can hallucinate responses or provide outdated information, such as referencing an older email when a newer one exists. To verify information, click and review the sources listed after Gemini’s response.

### Steps

1. Go to [gemini.google.com](https://gemini.google.com/).
   - If connecting to a Google Workspace app, make sure you’re signed in to the same Google Account you use with Google Workspace.

2. In the text box, enter:

   ```text
   @
   ```

   Then select the app you want to use.

   - Tip: Enter `@`, select **Google Drive**, and reference Workspace content using keywords from the content.

3. Ask Gemini to get information from the selected app or service.

4. Click **Submit**.

If the app has been enabled by your Workspace administrator, Gemini Apps will automatically use it.

---

## Example Prompts

### Find information

```text
What dates did Erik propose in the email about the team offsite?
```

### Summarize a document

```text
Find notes from the latest team meeting titled “Team Meeting June 2023” from my drive and summarize it to a short paragraph
```

### Simplify and organize information

```text
Find the doc from Ashley about the website clean-up project and summarize the proposal for me in 5 bullets
```

---

## Available Connected Apps

> **Important:** Availability of Connected Apps varies by account type, Google Workspace edition, location, language, device, and Gemini app.

When signed in with a work or school account, Gemini can interact with services through the **Google Workspace app**, including:

- **Gmail**
- **Google Calendar**
- **Google Chat**
  - Currently available in English if you have Gemini Apps as a core service
- **Google Docs**
- **Google Drive**
- **Google Keep**
- **Google Tasks**

On the **Gemini web app on a computer**, you can also import a **GitHub repository** into your chat and ask Gemini questions about its code using the **GitHub app**.

---

## Troubleshooting: Why You Can’t Use an App

### You need to explicitly mention the app

For a new conversation, you must explicitly ask Gemini to use the app or service.

Example workflow:

1. Enter:

   ```text
   @
   ```

2. Select **Google Drive**.
3. Use keywords from the content you want Gemini to reference in your prompt.

You do **not** need to repeat this for follow-up questions in the same conversation.

---

### The app may not be enabled by your organization

For work or school accounts, app access must be enabled by your Google Workspace administrator.

If your prompt includes multiple actions requiring separate apps or services, and one or more of those services are not enabled, **none of the related actions will be completed**.

Example:

- You ask Gemini to:
  - Create a calendar event
  - Create a reminder for that event
- But the **Tasks app** is not enabled

Result:

- The calendar event will **not** be added.
- The reminder will **not** be created.

Workspace administrators can manage access to apps in Gemini through admin controls.

---

## What Gemini Can’t Do with Connected Apps

> **Important:** Your Google Workspace administrator can enable or disable access to apps. If you can’t access a certain app in Gemini, contact your administrator.

Gemini Apps **can’t**:

- Access attachments, comments, or images in **Docs** or **Gmail**
- Access folders, presentations, spreadsheets, pictures, or videos in **Drive**
  - This includes any content that isn’t in a document or PDF
  - To access multiple documents in one prompt, mention all desired files in the prompt
- Create email drafts or d

[... summary truncated for context management ...]

# Document 374: Use apps in Google Chat - Computer - Google Chat Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/chat/answer/7655820
Product area: chat
Original file: docs/use-apps-in-google-chat-computer-google-chat-help-81d44226.md

# Use Apps in Google Chat — Summary

**Source:** Google Chat Help — “Use apps in Google Chat”  
**Platform covered:** Computer  
**URL:** https://support.google.com/chat/answer/7655820

---

## Overview

Apps in Google Chat are special accounts you can message to connect Chat with other services. They can help you:

- Look up information
- Schedule meetings
- Complete tasks

Google Chat apps may be built and maintained by Google or by third-party software vendors.

---

## Key Excerpts

> Apps are special accounts you can message that connect you to services in Chat

Apps can help you:

- Look up information.
- Schedule a meeting.
- Do tasks.

When you interact with an app in Chat, the app can see your:

- Email address
- Avatar
- Basic information, such as your name

> The app can see other’s basic information in a chat, but it can't see their email address or avatar unless they interact directly with the app.

**Important:**

- On your work or school accounts, your admin can install apps for you. Afterwards, the apps can send you direct messages. You can't uninstall the apps but you can turn off notifications for conversations with the app.
- If you're an external member of a Google Chat space created by a work or school account, you can't add, remove, or interact with any apps.

App commands use:

```text
/
```

> The app commands you use in a conversation are private. The app's responses appear in the conversation.

---

## How Apps Work in Google Chat

When you interact with a Chat app, it may access certain user details.

### Information an App Can See About You

An app can see your:

- Email address
- Avatar
- Basic profile information, such as your name

### Information an App Can See About Others

In a chat, the app can see other users’ basic information, but it **cannot** see their:

- Email address
- Avatar

unless those users interact directly with the app.

### Google vs. Third-Party Apps

Google creates and maintains some apps, including:

- Google Drive app
- Poll app

Other apps are created and maintained by third-party vendors.

If you use a third-party app, Google recommends reviewing the vendor’s:

- Terms of Service
- Privacy policy

to understand how your data is used.

---

## Work or School Account Limitations

If you use a work or school Google account:

- Your organization controls which apps are available.
- Your administrator can install apps for you.
- Admin-installed apps can send you direct messages.
- You cannot uninstall admin-installed apps.
- You can turn off notifications for conversations with those apps.

If you are an **external member** of a Google Chat space created by a work or school account:

- You cannot add apps.
- You cannot remove apps.
- You cannot interact with apps.

---

## Find & Install a Chat App

**Important:** If you have a work or school account, your organization controls which apps are available to you.

### Steps

1. On your computer, open:
   - [Google Chat](https://chat.google.com/), or
   - [Gmail](https://mail.google.com/)
2. Choose one of the following:
   - Click **New chat** → **Find apps**
   - In the Chat sidebar, click **Get Add-ons**
3. Search or browse for an app.
4. Select an app.
5. Choose an installation option:
   - Click **Install** to create a dedicated 1:1 direct message with the app and use its features individually.
   - Click **Add to Space** to add the app to a space so all space members can use it.

### Tips

- You can also find and install apps in the Google Workspace Marketplace.
- If an app needs to perform tasks on your behalf, it will ask for permission to access your Google Account.
  - To grant access, click **Allow** in the permission prompt.
- You can remove third-party app access from your Google Account settings.
- To uninstall an app from a direct message:
  1. Open the app conversation.
  2. Click the app name at the top.
  3. Click **Uninstall**.
- For work or school accounts, you cannot uninstall apps installed by your administrator.

---

## Add Apps to a Conversation or Space

Installed apps can be used in conversations and spaces.

When you add an app to a space, you grant it permission to:

- Fetch basic information about the space
- Create messages in the space

### Steps

1. Open [Google Chat](https://chat.google.com/) or [Gmail](https://mail.google.com/) on your computer.
2. Select a conversation or space.
3. At the top, click the space name or conversation participants.
4. Click **Apps & integration** → **Add apps**.
5. Enter the app name.
6. Select one or more apps.
7. Click **Add**.

### Important Access Notes

- Adding an app to a space gives it access to messages that trigger the app.
- It does **not** automatically allow the app to read every message in the space.
- Apps that need full message context can only access it with explicit permission.

### Additional Notes

When you add an app to a space:

- The app is installed in your **Apps** section.
- The app becomes available in every other space 

[... summary truncated for context management ...]

# Document 375: Use Ask Photos for photos, information & assistance - Computer - Google Photos Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/photos/answer/15318661
Product area: photos-ai
Original file: docs/use-ask-photos-for-photos-information-assistance-computer-google-photos-help-eac24854.md

[Skip to main content](https://support.google.com/photos/answer/15318661#search-form)

# Use Ask Photos for photos, information & assistance

You can ask Google Photos for photos, information, and assistance with a conversational agent.

When you search with Ask Photos, it starts by showing a page of relevant results. For simple searches like “beach” or “dog,” this is usually enough. But if you ask something complex like “Photos that’d make great phone backgrounds,” or “What did I eat on my trip to Barcelona?,” Gemini models continue to work in the background to narrow down the results set and highlight the most relevant results or information based on your search.

This feature is still experimental, so it may give unexpected or inaccurate results. When you leave feedback, you can help improve Ask Photos for everyone.

Edit your pictures in Google Photos just by asking. Describe the edits you want and watch the changes appear in seconds. Google Photos uses advanced Gemini model capabilities to understand, interpret, and apply your edits, so you don’t have to be a professional to get the picture you want. [Learn how to ask Photos to edit your pictures](https://support.google.com/photos/answer/14674995#ask_edits).

## Before you start

**Important:** This feature isn't available in all regions at this time.

Currently, Ask Photos is rolling out to a select number of countries and regions, and is available in several languages, with plans to continue expanding. Functionality can vary by location.

To use Ask Photos, you must meet the [Gemini features in Photos requirements](https://support.google.com/photos/answer/15344015#eligibility_for_gemini).

**Tip:** If you don’t have access to Ask Photos, you can still [use classic Search](https://support.google.com/photos/answer/15235862).

### Terms of Service

You agree that your use of Ask Photos is subject to the [Google Terms of Service](https://policies.google.com/terms) and the [Generative AI Prohibited Use Policy](https://policies.google.com/terms/generative-ai/use-policy).

We’re committed to protecting your privacy. [Learn how Ask Photos keeps your data private](https://support.google.com/photos/answer/15344015).

# Document 376: Use automatic dubbing - Computer - YouTube Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/youtube/answer/15569972
Product area: youtube-ai
Original file: docs/use-automatic-dubbing-computer-youtube-help-9b7bed89.md

# YouTube Help: Use Automatic Dubbing — Summary

**Source:** <https://support.google.com/youtube/answer/15569972>  
**Platform:** Computer / YouTube Studio

---

## Overview

YouTube’s **automatic dubbing** feature generates translated audio tracks for videos to make them more accessible globally.

> “Automatic dubbing generates translated audio tracks in different languages to make your videos more accessible to viewers around the world.”

Videos with automatic dubs are labeled as **“auto-dubbed”** in the video description.

Viewers can:
- Set preferred languages for **audio, titles, and descriptions**
- Switch between the **original audio** and **dubbed audio tracks** on individual videos

For eligible creators, dubs are generated automatically when a new video is uploaded. YouTube may also generate dubs for older, previously published videos over time.

---

## Key Behavior & Publication

- New uploads may receive automatic dubs.
- Dubs are published based on your **channel publication settings**.
- Creators may choose to:
  - Publish dubs automatically
  - Manually review dubs before publication
  - Review all dubs
  - Review only experimental-language dubs
- Dubs may be regenerated over time to improve quality.

---

## Quality, Limitations & Eligibility

Automatic dubbing is improving, but quality can vary by language and source audio.

> “We cannot yet successfully and accurately dub all videos, but we’re continually working on improvements.”

Automatic dubs may contain errors due to:
- Mispronunciations
- Accents or dialects
- Background noise
- Translation challenges with:
  - Proper nouns
  - Idioms
  - Jargon
- Speech recognition issues
- Difficulty matching dubbed voice to the original voice

Some languages support **expressive speech**, which:

> “replicates the original audio's pitch and intonation to produce more natural-sounding dubs.”

---

## Reasons a Video May Be Ineligible

A video may not qualify for automatic dubbing for these reasons:

- **Video Length:** The video exceeds **120 minutes**
- **Audio Content:**
  - No speech
  - Only music
  - Very little spoken content
  - Original audio is in an unsupported language
- **Source Language Detection:** YouTube cannot automatically detect the source language
- **Speech Pacing:** Speech is too fast, which would create an unlistenable sped-up dub
- **Content ID Claims:** The video contains copyrighted content

---

## Best Practices

To improve dub accuracy:

- Set the **original video language** correctly when uploading.
- Review dubs before publishing if quality control is important.
- Ask someone who speaks the target language to review dubs if needed.

Important limitation:

> “You cannot edit automatic dubs.”

Creators can only preview, review, publish, unpublish, or delete dubs.

---

## Access to Automatic Dubbing

Automatic dubbing is **enabled by default** for eligible creators.

If it is not enabled, creators may use early access by enabling it in:

**YouTube Studio → Settings → Channel → Advanced settings**

Then click or check **Enable / Allow automatic dubbing**, depending on availability.

---

## Available Languages

YouTube is gradually expanding supported languages and improving quality.

> “Some channels might have access to new languages sooner.”

Some languages may appear in YouTube Studio as **“experimental.”** For a specific video, available and experimental languages can be viewed on the **Languages** page in YouTube Studio.

Languages marked with `*` support **expressive speech**.

### Dubbing Into English

Supported source languages for dubbing into English:

- Arabic*
- Bengali*
- Chinese
- Chinese (Traditional)
- Dutch
- Farsi
- French*
- German*
- Hebrew
- Hindi*
- Indonesian*
- Italian*
- Japanese
- Korean*
- Malayalam
- Polish
- Portuguese*
- Punjabi
- Romanian
- Russian*
- Spanish*
- Swahili
- Tamil
- Telugu*
- Thai
- Turkish
- Ukrainian*
- Urdu
- Vietnamese

### Dubbing From English

Supported target languages for dubbing from English:

- Arabic
- Bengali
- Dutch
- French*
- German*
- Hebrew
- Hindi*
- Indonesian*
- Italian*
- Japanese
- Korean
- Malayalam
- Polish
- Portuguese*
- Punjabi
- Russian
- Spanish*
- Tamil
- Telugu
- Ukrainian

---

## Turn Off Automatic Dubbing for Your Channel

To disable automatic dubbing:

1. Sign in to [YouTube Studio](https://studio.youtube.com/).
2. Go to **Settings → Channel → Advanced settings**.
3. Uncheck **Allow automatic dubbing**.
4. Click **Save**.

---

## Turn On Automatic Dubbing for Your Channel

If you previously disabled automatic dubbing:

1. Sign in to [YouTube Studio](https://studio.youtube.com/).
2. Go to **Settings → Channel → Advanced settings**.
3. Check **Allow automatic dubbing**.
4. Optional: Check **Manually review dubs before publishing**.
5. Choose one:
   - **Review dubs in all languages**
   - **Review dubs in experimental languages only**
6. Click **Save**.

---

## Adjust Automatic Dubbing Publication Settings

To review generated dubs before t

[... summary truncated for context management ...]

# Document 377: Use Deep Research in Gemini Apps - Computer - Gemini Apps Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/gemini/answer/15719111?hl=en
Product area: gemini-apps
Original file: docs/use-deep-research-in-gemini-apps-computer-gemini-apps-help-b6a84e67.md

# Use Deep Research in Gemini Apps — Summary

**Source:** Google Gemini Apps Help  
**URL:** https://support.google.com/gemini/answer/15719111?hl=en  
**Platform covered:** Computer

---

## Overview

Deep Research in Gemini Apps lets users conduct **in-depth, real-time research** on almost any topic. Gemini uses **Google Search by default**, but users can choose or add other sources, including:

- Google Search
- Personal Gmail
- Google Drive
- Uploaded files/images
- NotebookLM notebooks

> “You can conduct in-depth and real-time research on almost any subject with Deep Research in Gemini Apps.”

Deep Research generates a research plan first, then creates a report after the user starts the research.

---

## Requirements

To use Deep Research, you must:

- **Be 18 or over**
- **Be signed in to the Gemini app**

---

## Models & Usage Limits

### Available Models

- **All users** can use **Thinking** for Deep Research reports.
- **Google AI Pro** and **Google AI Ultra** users can generate reports using **Pro** for higher quality.

> “Google AI Pro and Google AI Ultra users can generate reports using Pro for even higher quality.”

### Limits

Gemini Apps limits:

- The number of **daily research requests**
- The number of research requests that can run **at the same time**

If you are close to your limit, Gemini notifies you how many research requests remain for the day.

**Google AI Pro** and **Google AI Ultra** users receive higher limits.

---

## How to Start a Deep Research Report on Computer

1. Go to [gemini.google.com](https://gemini.google.com/).
2. In the text box, click **Add Files** → **Deep Research**.
3. Optional: To upload files or an image with your prompt, click **Add Files** → **Files**.
4. Optional: To choose research sources, click **Sources** and select sources such as Gmail or Drive.
   - Gmail, Drive, and other Google services are available only if the **Google Workspace app is connected to Gemini Apps**.
   - **Google Search is included by default**.
   - To limit research to selected sources only, **deselect Google Search**.
5. Enter a question or prompt describing what you want researched.
6. Click **Submit**.
   - Gemini creates a research plan.
   - To revise it first, click **Edit plan**.
7. Click **Start research**.
8. When the report is ready, click **Open**.

---

## Report Generation Time & Notifications

Deep Research reports usually take:

- **About 5–10 minutes**
- Longer for more complex reports

> “It usually takes about 5-10 minutes to generate the report since Gemini analyzes many sources.”

You can leave the chat while the report is generating. Gemini will notify you when it is ready:

- **Web app:** Notification appears next to the completed report’s chat thread.
- **Mobile app:** Device notification may appear, depending on notification and lock-screen settings.

---

## Visuals & Animations in Reports

**Availability:** Google AI Ultra plan only.

> **Important:** For now, this feature isn’t available if you include any Google Workspace services, like Gmail and Drive, as a source.

With a **Google AI Ultra** plan, Deep Research reports may include visuals and animations to make complex information easier to understand.

Possible visuals include:

- Charts
- Diagrams
- Interactive simulators

---

## Find Past Research Reports

> **Important:** You can only find past research reports if your Keep Activity setting is on.

To find a previous report:

1. Go to [gemini.google.com](https://gemini.google.com/).
2. If chats are hidden, click **Menu** at the top.
3. Under **Recent**, find the research chat.

---

## Listen to a Research Report

To generate an audio version:

1. Go to [gemini.google.com](https://gemini.google.com/).
2. If chats are hidden, click **Menu**.
3. Under **Recent**, select the chat with the research report.
4. In the Canvas panel on the right, click **Create** → **Audio Overview**.

---

## Visualize a Research Report

To create a custom visualization from a report:

1. Go to [gemini.google.com](https://gemini.google.com/).
2. If chats are hidden, click **Menu**.
3. Under **Recent**, select the research report chat.
4. In the Canvas panel on the right, click **Create**.
5. Enter a prompt describing the visualization you want.

---

## Share, Export, or Copy a Research Report

1. Go to [gemini.google.com](https://gemini.google.com/).
2. If chats are hidden, click **Menu**.
3. Under **Recent**, select the chat with your research report.
4. In the Canvas panel on the right, click **Share & export**.
5. Choose an action:
   - **Share Canvas** — share the report
   - **Export to Docs** — export the report to Google Docs
   - **Copy Contents** — copy the report text

---

# Document 378: Use enhanced Smart Fill with AI in Google Sheets (Workspace Experiments) - Google Docs Editors Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/docs/answer/14001183
Product area: sheets
Original file: docs/use-enhanced-smart-fill-with-ai-in-google-sheets-workspace-experiments-google-docs-editors-5f4b1b17.md

# Use enhanced Smart Fill with AI in Google Sheets (Workspace Experiments)

> **Important:** This feature is part of the Google Workspace Experiments trusted tester program, currently available only to selected users in US English. Availability may vary by country. To check access, open a new sheet and look for the **`Help me organize`** sidebar on the right.

## How It Works

Enhanced Smart Fill uses AI to detect relationships between partially filled column pairs and instantly predicts the remaining values. It works with text columns only (not numeric/date columns) and requires:

- At least **3 example rows** with **2 distinct column values** to be filled.
- **English** text values.

### Triggering Suggestions

- Suggestions appear after an edit or **live while you type** (as soon as a prediction is available).
- A colored outline shows the range that Sheets can predict; hover over the suggestion icon to see the fill preview.

### Accepting or Rejecting

- **Accept:** Click the suggestion bubble.
- **Reject:** Click the cancel icon on the expanded suggestion pill.

## Example Use Cases

- Categorize feedback by theme
- Organize news articles by topic
- Convert addresses to a consistent format
- Extract phone numbers from text fields

## Giving Feedback

1. Click the three-dot menu on any suggestion.
2. Select **`Send feedback`**.

For general feedback: go to **Help** → **`Help Sheets improve`**.  
For legal issues: use [this form](https://support.google.com/legal/answer/3110420) or **`Send feedback`** → **`Report an issue`** / **`Suggest an idea`** → click **`Legal Help`** at the bottom of the sidebar.

## Turning Off Enhanced Smart Fill

You can disable only the AI-enhanced suggestions while keeping standard Smart Fill active.

### From a cell suggestion
1. Click the three-dot menu on any suggestion.
2. Uncheck **`Enable enhanced Smart Fill suggestions`**.

### From the menu
1. Go to **Tools** → **`Suggestion controls`**.
2. Uncheck **`Enable enhanced Smart Fill suggestions`**.

To re-enable, follow the same path and re-check the option.

> **Note:** When turned off, all undecided suggestions are removed from all sheets. This **does not** disable standard [Smart Fill](https://support.google.com/docs/answer/9914525) (e.g., extracting first names from full names).  
> Exiting the Labs program permanently revokes access to all Labs features and cannot be reversed. Learn about [exiting Labs](https://support.google.com/docs/answer/13447104#labs-opt-out).

## Important Limitations & Data Practices

- **Only English** text values are supported.
- Suggestions are not professional advice and may be inaccurate or inappropriate.
- Google processes data according to the [Workspace Experiments Privacy Notice & Terms](https://support.google.com/docs/answer/13447401). Data may be read by human reviewers, but if Google-selected input is used to generate output, the content is **aggregated/pseudonymized** before review (unless provided directly via feedback).
- Avoid entering personal, confidential, or sensitive information.
- Your feedback helps improve the feature and broader AI efforts.

## Related Resources

- [Get started with Google Workspace Experiments](https://support.google.com/docs/answer/13447104)
- [Organize with AI in Google Sheets](https://support.google.com/docs/answer/13676332)
- [Write with AI in Google Docs](https://support.google.com/docs/answer/13447609)
- [Create images with AI in Google Slides](https://support.google.com/docs/answer/13635180)
- [Draft emails with AI](https://support.google.com/mail/answer/13384326)
- [Google Workspace Experiments Privacy Notice and Terms](https://support.google.com/docs/answer/13447401)

---

# Document 379: Use enhanced Smart Fill with Gemini in Google Sheets - Google Docs Editors Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/docs/answer/14226603
Product area: sheets
Original file: docs/use-enhanced-smart-fill-with-gemini-in-google-sheets-google-docs-editors-help-30ac209e.md

# Use Enhanced Smart Fill with Gemini in Google Sheets — Summary

**Source:** Google Docs Editors Help  
**URL:** https://support.google.com/docs/answer/14226603  
**Feature availability:** Desktop Google Sheets only

---

## Key Excerpts

> **Important:** This feature requires an eligible Google Workspace or Google AI plan.

> This feature is also available through our early access testing program, **Google Workspace Experiments**.

> On Google Sheets, enhanced Smart Fill suggestions makes manual text processing tasks easier. It automatically detects the relationship between incomplete column pairs. It also uses AI to predict the remaining values, which allows them to be instantly filled.

> **Important:** The outline indicates the range Sheets can predict and you can hover over the icon to see the fill suggestions.

> Enhanced Smart Fill can only detect and support **English values**.

> To trigger enhanced Smart Fill detection, a table must have at least **3 example rows** with **2 distinct column values** to be filled with suggested values.

> Enhanced Smart Fill can only be triggered on **text columns**. It will not run on **numeric and date columns**.

---

## What Enhanced Smart Fill Does

Enhanced Smart Fill with Gemini in Google Sheets uses AI to help automate manual text-processing tasks. It detects relationships between incomplete column pairs and predicts missing values so users can fill them instantly.

It is designed for spreadsheet tasks where Google Sheets can infer a pattern from examples, especially text-based transformations, classifications, or extractions.

---

## Availability and Requirements

- Requires an eligible:
  - **Google Workspace plan**, or
  - **Google AI plan**
- Also available through:
  - **Google Workspace Experiments**, Google’s early access testing program
- Available on:
  - **Desktop only**
- Supported data:
  - **English text values only**
- Not supported for:
  - Numeric columns
  - Date columns

---

## How Enhanced Smart Fill Suggestions Appear

Enhanced Smart Fill suggestions can appear in two ways:

1. **After an edit is made** in the file.
2. **Live while entering data manually**, once Sheets detects that it has a prediction available.

When suggestions appear:

- Google Sheets outlines the predicted fill range.
- Users can hover over the Smart Fill icon to preview fill suggestions.

---

## How to Accept or Reject Suggestions

When enhanced Smart Fill suggestions appear, you can:

1. **Accept the suggestion**
   - Click the suggestion bubble.

2. **Reject the suggestion**
   - Click the cancel icon on the expanded suggestion pill.
   - This rejects the suggestion for the selected range.

---

## Example Use Cases

Enhanced Smart Fill can help with tasks such as:

- Categorizing feedback by theme
- Organizing news articles by topic
- Converting address data into a consistent format
- Extracting phone numbers from text fields

---

## Triggering Enhanced Smart Fill

To trigger Enhanced Smart Fill detection:

- The table must include at least:
  - **3 example rows**
  - **2 distinct column values** to be filled with suggested values
- The columns must contain:
  - **Text data**
- The feature will not trigger on:
  - Numeric columns
  - Date columns

---

## Giving Feedback on Generated Suggestions

Google notes that Workspace with Gemini is still learning and may not always support a request correctly.

If a suggestion is inaccurate, unsafe, or otherwise problematic, users can submit feedback to help improve Gemini-powered Workspace features and Google’s broader AI efforts.

### Submit Feedback on a Specific Suggestion

1. Click the **three-dot menu** in any suggestion.
2. Click **Send feedback**.

### Submit General Feedback on the Feature

In Google Sheets:

1. Go to **Help**.
2. Select **Help Sheets improve**.

### Report a Legal Issue

To report a legal issue, users can either:

- Create a request through Google Legal Help, or
- Go to **Send feedback** → **Report an issue** or **Suggest an idea** → click **Legal Help** at the bottom of the sidebar.

---

## Important Notes About Gemini Suggestions

Google provides several cautions about Gemini-generated suggestions:

- Gemini feature suggestions **do not represent Google’s views**.
- Gemini suggestions **should not be attributed to Google**.
- Users should **not rely on Gemini features as medical, legal, financial, or other professional advice**.
- Gemini features may generate:
  - Inaccurate information
  - Inappropriate information
- Feedback helps improve Gemini’s helpfulness and safety.
- Enterprise end users can submit feedback about their experience.
- Before submitting feedback, users are informed that feedback data should not include:
  - Personal information
  - Sensitive information
  - Confidential information

---

## Related Help Resources

Google suggests additional support options:

- **Post to the Help Community**  
  Get answers from community members.

- **Visit the Prompt Guide**  
  Lear

# Use Gemini — Android Help Summary

**Source:** Google Android Help — “Use Gemini”  
**URL:** https://support.google.com/android/answer/15729107  
**Context:** Part of “Get the most out of your Android device” / Android Learning Center.

---

## Key Excerpts

> You can use Gemini to:
>
> - Learn in new ways through text, voice, images, and your camera.
> - Write thank you notes, plan events, and more.
> - Connect with Google apps, like Gmail, Maps, and YouTube.
> - Create lists, set up reminders, manage phone settings, and create YouTube Music playlists.

> The Gemini mobile app may not be available for all languages, devices, accounts, or locations.

> You can conveniently get quick answers, contextual help, and assistance from Gemini without leaving the screen you were on.

> When you say “Hey Google” or activate Gemini by touch, like long pressing the power button, Gemini will start listening to you even if you have another app open.

---

## What You Can Do with Gemini on Android

Gemini can help Android users with a wide range of tasks, including:

- **Learning and exploring** using:
  - Text
  - Voice
  - Images
  - Camera input
- **Writing and planning**, such as:
  - Thank-you notes
  - Event plans
  - Other everyday writing tasks
- **Using Google app integrations**, including:
  - Gmail
  - Maps
  - YouTube
  - Tasks
  - YouTube Music
- **Productivity and device tasks**, such as:
  - Creating lists
  - Setting reminders
  - Managing phone settings
  - Creating YouTube Music playlists based on a desired vibe

**Availability note:** Gemini mobile app availability may vary by **language, device, account type, and location**.

---

## Chat with Gemini Live

Gemini Live supports real-time voice conversations with Gemini.

### Gemini Live Can Help You

- Start a conversation with **real-time spoken responses**
- Have more natural voice conversations that **ebb and flow at your own pace**
- Brainstorm ideas
- Rehearse for interviews
- Handle many other conversational tasks

### How to Start Gemini Live

1. On your Android device, open **Gemini**.
   - If available, you can also say: **“Hey Google.”**
2. At the bottom right, tap **Live**.
   - If this is your first time, follow the on-screen instructions.
3. Start talking.

### Microphone Control Tip

To turn off the microphone:

- Tap **Hold**
- Tap **End**
- Or say: **“stop”**

---

## Use Gemini as Your Built-in AI Assistant

Gemini can work as an integrated assistant on Android, allowing you to get help **without leaving your current screen**.

### Ways to Activate Gemini

You can activate Gemini by:

- Saying **“Hey Google”**
- Using a touch shortcut, such as **long pressing the power button**

Once activated, Gemini starts listening even if another app is currently open.

### Built-in Assistant Tips

- You can generate custom images and **drag and drop them directly into apps**, such as Gmail.
- In some apps, opening the on-screen overlay can help you find key information in content, such as **YouTube videos**.

---

## Use Gemini Extensions

Gemini extensions let Gemini interact with supported apps and services.

### Supported / Mentioned Apps and Services

Gemini can access or work with apps such as:

- Gmail
- Maps
- Tasks
- YouTube
- YouTube Music
- Phone settings and other supported services

### What Extensions Enable

With extensions, Gemini can:

- Create lists
- Set reminders
- Manage phone settings
- Create YouTube Music playlists matching a requested mood or “vibe”
- Use Google apps to complete relevant tasks

### How to Use Gemini Extensions

1. On your Android device, open **Gemini**.
   - If available, you can also say: **“Hey Google.”**
2. Enter your question or prompt.
3. Tap **Submit**.
   - If an extension is available, the Gemini app automatically uses it.

---

# Document 381: Use Gemini Apps - Computer - Android Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/android/answer/13275745
Product area: android-ai
Original file: docs/use-gemini-apps-computer-android-help-f60bbd3e.md

# Use Gemini Apps — Computer (Android Help)

**Source:** <https://support.google.com/android/answer/13275745>

## Overview

Gemini Apps help users “supercharge” ideas by assisting with tasks such as:

- Brainstorming ideas, developing plans, and finding ways to get things done
- Summarizing complex topics in an easy-to-understand way
- Creating first drafts of outlines, emails, blog posts, poems, and more

> “Gemini Apps can make mistakes. When using Gemini Apps, double-check responses and don’t rely on responses from Gemini Apps for professional advice.”

---

## Requirements

You can use some Gemini Apps features without signing in, but signing in is required for additional features and to save Gemini Apps activity.

### Sign-in options

To sign in to Gemini Apps, you need one of the following:

- **A personal Google Account**
  - Family Link managers can turn off Gemini Apps access for users under 13, or the applicable age in their country, in **Google Family Link** settings.
- **A work Google Account with a qualifying Workspace edition**
  - You must be **18 or over**.
- **A school Google Account**
  - The service must be enabled by your institution’s administrator.

### Availability and browser support

- Supported languages and countries vary for:
  - Gemini web app
  - Gemini mobile app
- Work or school Google Account usage has additional guidance.
- To use **gemini.google.com**, you need a supported browser:
  - Chrome
  - Safari
  - Firefox
  - Opera
  - Edgium

---

## Start a Conversation in Chat

1. On your computer, go to [gemini.google.com](https://gemini.google.com/).
2. In the text box at the bottom, enter your question or prompt.
3. Optionally, add a file or image by clicking **Add files**.
   - Follow the on-screen instructions.
4. Click **Submit**.

### Tips

- To start a new chat:
  - Click **New chat** at the top left.
  - If you don’t see it, click **Menu** first.
- Depending on your location and device type, some features may be available without signing in.
- To access additional features or save activity, sign in with a Google Account.

---

## Start a Temporary Chat

A **temporary chat** does not appear in:

- Your Gemini Apps Activity
- Your recent chats

### Requirements

To start a temporary chat, you need:

- To be signed in to Gemini Apps
- A **personal Google Account**
  - Not available for work or school Google Accounts
- Access to the Gemini web or mobile app
  - Not available for Gemini in Chrome or Gemini in Google Messages

### How to start a temporary chat

1. Go to [gemini.google.com](https://gemini.google.com/).
2. Next to **New chat**, click **Temporary chat**.
3. Enter your question or prompt in the text box.
4. Click **Submit**.

### Temporary chat tips

- Once you leave a temporary chat, you no longer have access to it.
- It won’t appear in recent chats.
- When using a temporary chat, the temporary chat icon appears at the top of the screen.

### What you can’t do in a temporary chat

Temporary chats do **not** support:

- Gems
- Connected Apps and integrated services that require **Keep Activity** to be turned on
- Personalized responses
- Saving information for future personalization
- Giving feedback about the response

---

## Choose the Gemini Model

> **Important:** To switch between models in Gemini Apps, you must be signed in.

### How to switch models

1. Go to [gemini.google.com](https://gemini.google.com/).
2. At the bottom, inside the text box, click the model name.
3. Select the model you want to use.

### Available models

- **Gemini Flash-Lite**
  - Efficient workhorse model designed for speed
  - Ideal for everyday tasks like summarization and brainstorming

- **Gemini Flash**
  - More powerful model that balances speed and reasoning
  - Suitable for a wide range of problems, from simple to complex

- **Gemini Pro**
  - Most advanced model
  - Excels at complex math and coding prompts
  - Offers high performance and reasoning
  - Has deeper understanding across text, files, images, and videos
  - Includes major upgrades for coding and studying
  - Responses generally take longer than other models

### Subscription note

If you have a Google AI subscription plan and hit your limit, you can continue chats with **Flash-Lite**.

---

## Example Prompts

### Core examples

- `Outline my blog post about summer mocktail recipes.`
- `Debug this error message: "FileNotFoundError: [Errno 2] No such file or directory: 'data.csv'"`
- `I want to learn how to code in Python. How should I start? Create a learning plan for me, and recommend some videos.`
- `I love video games and drawing. What are some jobs that I might like when I grow up?`
- `Show my monthly expenses in a pie chart: Rent 1200, Groceries 300, Gas 60, Electricity 80, and Internet 50.`

### More examples

- `Help me finish my art studio tagline: craft, create, and ...`
- `Draft a packing list for my weekend fishing and camping trip.`
- `I want to write a novel. How can I get started?`
- `Help me un

# Document 382: Use Gemini Apps with a work or school Google Account - Business / Enterprise - Gemini Apps Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/gemini/answer/14620100?hl=en
Product area: gemini-apps
Original file: docs/use-gemini-apps-with-a-work-or-school-google-account-business-enterprise-gemini--0bbaef42.md

# Use Gemini Apps with a Work or School Google Account — Business / Enterprise Summary

**Source:** Google Gemini Apps Help  
**URL:** https://support.google.com/gemini/answer/14620100?hl=en

---

## Core Takeaways

- Most users with a **work or school Google Account** can access Gemini Apps.
- **Feature availability, model access, usage limits, terms, and data handling** depend on the user’s **Google Workspace license**.
- Gemini Apps may be available either as:
  - an **additional service**, or
  - a **core Google Workspace service** with **enterprise-grade security and privacy**.
- To use Gemini Apps with a work account, users must be **18 or older**.
- Usage limits apply across devices and Gemini entry points, including:
  - [gemini.google.com](https://gemini.google.com/)
  - Gemini mobile app
  - Gemini in Chrome

> **Important:** “Gemini is AI and can make mistakes, including about people. Some content may not be suitable for everyone.”

---

## Gemini Apps by Workspace Edition

### License Categories

Gemini Apps access is grouped into five broad categories:

| Category | Example Workspace Editions | Access Level |
|---|---|---|
| **Additional service** | Android Enterprise, Cloud Identity, Chrome Enterprise, Business Base, Essentials Starter, G Suite, Workspace Individual | Standard access to features & models |
| **Core service — standard** | Business Starter, Essentials, Enterprise Essentials, Enterprise Essentials Plus, Frontline editions, Nonprofits | Standard access with enterprise-grade security & privacy |
| **Core service — Pro** | Business Plus, Business Standard, Enterprise Plus, Enterprise Standard, Gemini Enterprise Legacy, Gemini Business Legacy | Pro access with enterprise-grade security & privacy |
| **Core service — AI Expanded Access** | AI Expanded Access | Higher access with enterprise-grade security & privacy |
| **Core service — AI Ultra Access** | AI Ultra Access | Highest access with enterprise-grade security & privacy |

---

## Key Model Limits by Access Level

### Pro Model

| Access Level | Pro Model Limit |
|---|---|
| Additional service | Basic access; access varies; daily limits may change frequently |
| Core standard | Basic access; access varies; daily limits may change frequently |
| Pro | Up to **25 prompts per 4 hours** |
| AI Expanded Access | Up to **200 prompts/day** |
| AI Ultra Access | Up to **500 prompts/day** |

### Thinking Model

| Access Level | Thinking Model Limit |
|---|---|
| Additional service | Basic access; access varies; daily limits may change frequently |
| Core standard | Basic access; access varies; daily limits may change frequently |
| Pro | Up to **300 prompts/day** |
| AI Expanded Access | Up to **600 prompts/day** |
| AI Ultra Access | Up to **1,500 prompts/day** |

### Fast Model

- **General access** is available.

---

## Context Window

| Access Level | Context Size |
|---|---|
| Standard / Pro levels | **32 thousand** |
| Higher tiers | **1 million** |

### What a Context Window Means

A context window is Gemini’s “reading capacity” when processing prompts, files, and conversations.

> “The context window is like the reading capacity. It determines how much content can be understood together at one time.”

### Why a Larger Context Window Matters

A larger context window allows Gemini to process more content at once.

Example:

> “With a 1M token context window, Gemini can understand up to 1,500 pages of text or 30,000 lines of code.”

If the context window is exceeded, responses may fail to account for all uploaded or provided content, especially in large files with details spread throughout.

---

## Feature Limits by Tier

### Audio Overviews

- Up to **20 audio overviews/day**

### Connect to Google Workspace Apps

- Available as **general access** for eligible core-service users.
- Not available in some additional-service tiers.

### Connect to GitHub & Upload Code Folders

- Available as **general access** for eligible higher-tier users.
- Not available in lower tiers.

### Deep Research

| Access Level | Limit |
|---|---|
| Lower tiers | **5 reports/month** |
| Standard / Pro levels | **20 reports/day** |
| AI Expanded Access | **30 reports/day** |
| AI Ultra Access | **120 reports/day** |

### Deep Think 3.1

Available only with **AI Ultra Access**:

> “Up to 10 prompts / day with a 192 thousand token context window”

### Image Generation & Editing with Nano Banana 2

| Access Level | Limit |
|---|---|
| Lower tiers | Up to **20 images/day** |
| Higher tiers | Up to **100 images/day** |

### Redo Images with Nano Banana Pro

| Access Level | Limit |
|---|---|
| Lower tiers | **3 images/day** |
| Pro | Up to **100 images/day** |
| AI Expanded Access | Up to **200 images/day** |
| AI Ultra Access | Up to **1,000 images/day** |

> **Note:** Image generation and editing are in high demand. Limits may change frequently and reset daily.

### Scheduled Actions

- Higher-tier users may have up to **10 active actions at a time**.

###

[... summary truncated for context management ...]

# Document 383: Use Gemini for human resources - Google Workspace Learning Center

Source URL: https://support.google.com/a/users/answer/14171678

# Use Gemini for Human Resources — Google Workspace Learning Center Summary

**Source:** Google Workspace Learning Center  
**Article:** “Use Gemini for human resources”  
**URL:** https://support.google.com/a/users/answer/14171678  
**Context:** Gemini for Google Workspace productivity guide for HR use cases.

---

## Availability & Important Warnings

This feature is only available if the user’s organization supports Gemini for Google Workspace.

> _You can use this feature only if your organization supports it. For help, contact your administrator._

Before using Google Workspace with Gemini:

- Gemini feature suggestions **don’t represent Google’s views** and should not be attributed to Google.
- Gemini should **not** be relied on for medical, legal, financial, or other professional advice.
- Gemini may suggest **inaccurate or inappropriate information**.
- User feedback helps make Gemini more helpful and safe.
- Enterprise end users can submit feedback about generative AI features, but:
  - Feedback should **not contain personal, sensitive, or confidential information**.
  - Feedback may be read by humans.

**Tip from the guide:**

> **Tip:** If you don't like Gemini's first suggestion for any given prompt, ask it to try again before inserting its suggestion

---

## Apps & Gemini Features Used

The guide focuses on using Gemini in:

- **Google Docs** — Help me Write
- **Gmail** — Help me Write
- **Google Sheets** — Ask Gemini
- **Google Slides** — Create image

Related resources:

- [Gemini cheat sheet](https://support.google.com/a/users/answer/14143478)
- [Tips for writing prompts](https://support.google.com/a/users/answer/14200040)

---

# 1. Recruit & Hire for a Job Opening

Gemini can support the full hiring pipeline, including:

- Drafting job descriptions
- Creating job postings
- Writing candidate outreach emails
- Preparing interview questions
- Tracking candidates in Sheets

---

## 1.1 Draft a Job Description

Use Gemini in **Google Docs** to draft a job description from scratch based on a job title. The generated draft can then be reviewed and refined collaboratively with the hiring team.

**Setup:**

- Open a **Google Doc**
- Use **Help me Write** to generate new text

**Prompt:**

> "Write a job description for a \[ _job title_\], including the required skills and experience, as well as a summary of \[ _company name_\] and the position."

**Example job titles:**

- “Senior Product Manager”
- “Regional Sales Representative”

---

## 1.2 Draft a Job Posting

After creating a job description, Gemini can generate multiple job post options and hashtags tailored to a specific target audience.

**Setup:**

- In a **Google Doc**, select the job description
- Use **Help me Write** to ask Gemini about the selected text

**Prompt:**

> "Using this job description, provide 2 options for a job post directed at \[ _target audience_\]. Include 3 hashtags for each option."

**Example target audiences:**

- “mid-level manager”
- “someone with international experience”

**Recommended use:** Refine the generated posting and publish it to a job listing site.

---

## 1.3 Send an Outreach Email to a Candidate

Gemini can draft personalized recruiting emails in **Gmail**, starting from a job title and optionally referencing a candidate’s project or achievement.

**Setup:**

- Open a **Gmail compose window**
- Use **Help me Write** to draft a new email

**Prompt:**

> "Write an email to a job candidate about a job opening for \[ _insert job title_\]. Mention something about \[ _specific project or achievement_\]."

**Personalization source:**

> To find a _specific project or achievement_ for that candidate, refer to their resume or public job profile.

---

## 1.4 Prepare Interview Questions

Gemini can create interview questions tailored to a specific role and audience, helping interviewers avoid starting from scratch.

**Setup:**

- Open a **Google Doc**
- Use **Help me Write** to create new text

**Prompt:**

> "Create a list of interview questions for the job role \[ _job role_\]. Tailor the questions for a \[ _audience_\]."

**Example audiences:**

- “VP”
- “CFO”
- “individual contributor”
- “manager”

---

## 1.5 Track the Interview Pipeline

Gemini in **Google Sheets** can generate a table to track, monitor, and report on candidate interview status in one central spreadsheet.

**Setup:**

- Open a blank **Google Sheet**
- Use **Ask Gemini** to build a new table

**Prompt:**

> "Create a table for tracking interview candidates for a job position we're trying to fill."

---

Gemini can assist with onboarding by generating checklists, artwork for training materials, and rewritten training content.

## 2.1 Draft an Onboarding Checklist

Use Gemini in **Google Sheets** to create an initial checklist of tasks for employees hired into a specific role.

**Setup:**

- Open a blank **Google Sheet**
- Use **Ask Gemini** to build a new table

**Prompt:**

> "Create an onboarding che

# Marketing Use Cases & Prompt Examples

---

## 1. Generate Ideas for Blog Posts

### What Gemini Can Help With

Use Gemini in **Google Docs** to generate a blog post or social media content around a topic such as:

- A new product
- A webinar
- A specific customer segment
- A specific industry

Gemini is intended to help users:

- Get started
- Generate ideas
- Develop and evolve a pitch
- Adjust tone through revisions

### Setup

Use **Help me Write** in a **Google Doc** to write new text.

### Key Prompt Examples

```text
"Write a blog post promoting our webinar on [ topic] targeting [ audience]."
```

Example values:

- `topic`: “classroom management”
- `audience`: “teachers”

```text
"Write 3 social media posts targeted at [ audience] telling them about our new product."
```

Example audiences:

- “tech enthusiasts”
- “home owners”

---

## 2. Draft Corporate Profiles

### What Gemini Can Help With

When a new executive joins the company, Gemini can help quickly draft a corporate profile for the company website.

It can draft a profile based on:

- The executive’s job role
- Optional personal information, such as a hobby or public accomplishment

The guide notes that Gemini may create **placeholder information** that should be reviewed, replaced, and refined.

### Setup

Use **Help me Write** in a **Google Doc** to write new text.

### Key Prompt Example

```text
"Help me write a corporate profile for our company web site about a new [ job role] joining the company. Mention [ something personal about the person]."
```

Example values:

- `job role`: “CFO” or “VP of Marketing”
- `something personal`: a hobby or past accomplishment from the person’s public job profile

---

## 3. Rewrite Content as a Press Release

### What Gemini Can Help With

After creating a blog post, corporate profile, or other public announcement content, Gemini can help transform it into a **press release**.

Gemini can provide:

- A typical press release framework
- Placeholder information
- A draft to refine before publishing

### Setup

In a **Google Doc**:

1. Insert the information you want to summarize.
2. Select that information.
3. Use **Help me Write** to ask Gemini about the selected text.

### Key Prompt Example

```text
"Rewrite this corporate profile as a press release announcing that this executive is joining our company."
```

---

## 4. Email a Company Announcement

### What Gemini Can Help With

Gemini can help draft internal announcement emails for the entire company.

Users should provide:

- A brief description of the announcement
- Desired tone

The Gemini output should be used as a **framework** and revised as needed.

### Setup

Use **Help me Write** in a **Gmail compose window** to write a new email.

### Key Prompt Example

```text
"Email the entire company notifying them that we acquired [ company name]. Use an excited tone."
```

---

## 5. Organize a Marketing Campaign

### What Gemini Can Help With

In **Google Sheets**, Gemini can help outline a new marketing campaign, such as for:

- A product launch
- A new customer segment

It can generate structured campaign planning t

[... summary truncated for context management ...]

# Use Gemini for Nonprofits — Google Workspace Productivity Guide

**Source:** Google Workspace Learning Center  
**URL:** https://support.google.com/a/users/answer/14571258  
**Purpose:** Shows nonprofit teams how to use **Gemini for Google Workspace** across **Docs, Gmail, Sheets, and Slides** for fundraising, grants, donor engagement, and volunteer management.

---

## Availability & Important Usage Notes

> _You can use this feature only if your organization supports it. For help, contact your administrator._

Before using Google Workspace with Gemini:

- **Gemini suggestions don’t represent Google’s views** and should not be attributed to Google.
- **Do not rely on Gemini** for medical, legal, financial, or other professional advice.
- Gemini may suggest **inaccurate or inappropriate information**; user feedback helps improve safety and usefulness.
- Enterprise users may submit feedback about generative AI features, but:
  - Feedback should **not contain personal, sensitive, or confidential information**.
  - Feedback may be **read by humans**.

**Tip:**

> If you don't like Gemini's first suggestion for any given prompt, ask it to try again before inserting its suggestion

Helpful resources referenced:

- [Gemini cheat sheet](https://support.google.com/a/users/answer/14143478)

# Main Use Cases Covered

The guide provides nonprofit-specific examples for using Gemini to:

1. **Brainstorm marketing & fundraising campaigns**
2. **Write grant applications & proposals**
3. **Manage & engage donors**
4. **Recruit & train volunteers**

---

# 1. Brainstorm Marketing & Fundraising Campaigns

Gemini can help nonprofits create marketing materials, fundraising messages, visuals, and event plans.

---

## Draft Social Media & Blog Posts That Drive Empathy

Use Gemini in **Google Docs** to draft compelling marketing content that raises awareness and encourages donations.

**Recommended uses:**

- Social media posts
- Blog posts
- Email newsletters
- Ad copy
- Website content

You can target messaging by audience interests, demographics, or geography, then revise Gemini’s output for tone and accuracy.

**Setup:**  
In a **Google Doc**, use **Help me Write** to write new text.

**Prompt:**

> "Write a blog post that generates empathy about \[ _your cause_\] and encourages donations to \[ _your organization_\]. Target your language at \[ _audience_\]."

**Audience examples:**

- “outdoor enthusiasts”
- “parent”
- “people in your community”

---

## Create Attention-Grabbing Email Campaigns

Use Gemini in **Gmail** to draft donation request emails and subject lines.

**Recommended uses:**

- Donation request emails
- Personalized donor outreach
- Calls to action
- Subject line brainstorming

**Setup:**  
In a **Gmail compose window**, use **Help me Write** to write a new email.

**Prompts:**

> "Write 3 compelling subject lines for an email requesting donations to help \[ _your cause_\]."

> "Write an email requesting a donation from a person who has contributed to your cause in the past. Add a personal touch by mentioning how \[ _something personal about the person_\] is relevant to \[ _your cause_\]. Emphasize how to make the donation."

**Personalization examples:**

- Profession
- Hobby
- Location
- Other public-profile details

---

## Create Visuals That Raise Awareness

Use Gemini in **Google Slides** to generate custom images for nonprofit outreach.

**Recommended uses:**

- Newsletter images
- Website graphics
- Stakeholder presentations
- Educational outreach materials

**Setup:**  
In **Google Slides**, use **Create image** to generate new artwork.

**Prompts:**

> "Create an image for a newsletter that raises awareness about \[ _the problem your nonprofit is trying to solve_\]."

> "Create a conceptual image for a presentation to board members of a nonprofit that shows effective money management. The nonprofit supports \[ _your cause_\]."

---

## Plan a Fundraising Campaign or Event

Use Gemini in **Google Sheets** to create a marketing plan and budget table.

Gemini can generate a table with placeholder values, activities, timelines, and budget categories that your team can later customize.

**Setup:**  
In a blank **Google Sheet**, use **Ask Gemini** to build a new table.

**Prompt:**

> "Create a table outlining a marketing plan and budget for a fundraising campaign for \[ _your cause or event_\]. Include a 2 month timeline. There will be live music and lunch from a local caterer."

---

**Recommended uses:**

- Extract eligibility requirements
- Clarif

# Use Gemini for Sales — Google Workspace Learning Center Summary

**Source:** Google Workspace Learning Center

## Core Purpose

This guide shows sales professionals how to use **Gemini for Google Workspace** to speed up common sales workflows, including:

- Preparing customer/prospect research
- Brainstorming leadership questions

- Writing social posts
- Summarizing technical information
- Planning webinars
- Outlining presentations

  - Medical
  - Legal
  - Financial
  - Other professional advice

- User feedback helps improve Gemini’s helpfulness and safety.

# Sales Use Cases & Prompts

## 1. Save Time Writing Email

**Goal:** Draft a personalized customer email asking for a meeting.

**Where to use Gemini:** Gmail  
**Tool:** Help me Write in a Gmail compose window

**How it helps:**

Gemini can draft an email in seconds. Users can ask it to include a personal detail, such as the customer’s city or a shared interest, then refine and send the result.

### Original Prompt

> "Email my customer asking for a meeting. I saw on their public job profile that they live in Charleston, South Carolina, so make a personal reference to that city."

---

**Goal:** Prepare for a customer or prospect meeting.

**Where to use Gemini:** Google Sheets  
**Tool:** Ask Gemini in a blank Sheet to build a new table

Gemini can generate a structured research framework, including:

- Questions to research
- Possible information sources
- Ideas for organizing meeting preparation

> "Create a framework for researching a sales prospect or their company, to prepare for a customer meeting."

**How it helps:**

Gemini can generate questions tailored to a specific leadership audience and topic, such as company strategy or product direction.

### Original Prompt

> "Give me 12 thoughtful questions to ask a Chief Marketing Officer on their strategy for 2024."

---

## 4. Create a Customer Contact Tracker

**Goal:** Organize sales outreach tasks.

**Where to use Gemini:** Google Sheets  
**Tool:** Ask Gemini in a blank Sheet to build a new table

**How it helps:**

Gemini can quickly create a table for tracking customers to call, using placeholder data that can be replaced with real customer details.

### Original Prompt

> "Create a tracker for all the customers I need to call this week."

---

## 5. Create Personalized Social Media Posts

**Goal:** Draft posts for customer-facing promotion, such as webinar invitations.

**Where to use Gemini:** Google Docs  
**Tool:** Help me Write to create new text

**How it helps:**

Users can briefly describe the audience and topic, and Gemini generates a first draft that can be refined before posting.

### Original Prompt

> "Create a social media post inviting my customers to a webinar on [ _webinar topic_]."

---

## 6. Summarize Technical Information

**Goal:** Turn complex technical material into customer-friendly content.

**Where to use Gemini:** Google Docs  
**Tool:** Insert and select the source information, then use Help me Write to prompt Gemini about the selection

**How it helps:**

Gemini can process large amounts of technical information and produce a shorter, more digestible customer-facing summary, such as a one-pager based on:

- Product specifications
- Tech

# Document 387: Use Gemini in AppSheet - AppSheet Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/appsheet/answer/16106353?hl=en
Product area: appsheet
Original file: docs/use-gemini-in-appsheet-appsheet-help-20c004c5.md

# Use Gemini in AppSheet — Summary

**Source:** [AppSheet Help: Use Gemini in AppSheet](https://support.google.com/appsheet/answer/16106353?hl=en)

## Overview

Gemini in AppSheet brings Gemini-powered capabilities to AppSheet through two main feature areas:

- **Gemini for App Creation**
- **Gemini in AppSheet Solutions**

These features are governed by different terms depending on how AppSheet is purchased:

> For AppSheet users purchasing AppSheet as part of Google Workspace, Gemini in AppSheet is considered to be a "Workspace Generative AI Service," subject to the "Workspace Generative AI Services" section of the [Google Workspace Service Specific Terms](https://workspace.google.com/terms/service-terms/).

> For AppSheet users purchasing AppSheet as a standalone offering, Gemini in AppSheet is considered to be an "AppSheet Generative AI Service," subject to the "AppSheet Generative AI Services" section of the [AppSheet Terms of Service](https://www.appsheet.com/Home/Terms).

---

## Gemini for App Creation

### Availability & Licensing

Gemini for App Creation is included with paid AppSheet licenses:

- **AppSheet Publisher Pro**
- **AppSheet Starter**
- **AppSheet Core**
- **AppSheet Enterprise Plus**

You may also have access to **AppSheet Core** through certain Google Workspace licenses.

> You don't need Gemini Enterprise or Gemini Workspace to use this feature.

### What It Does

Gemini for App Creation lets app creators build apps by describing a business process or idea in natural language.

AppSheet can then generate:

- Suggested **tables**
- Suggested **columns**
- A data schema
- Some pre-built **views**
- Some pre-built **actions**

Creators can choose to create the app based on the suggested schema.

Related help article:

- [Create apps using Gemini for App Creation](https://support.google.com/appsheet/answer/14699210)

### Admin Controls

Admins can disable AI-assisted app creation.

Key details:

- The setting is applied at the **team level**
- If an organization has multiple teams, it must be disabled separately for each relevant team
- Turning it off affects only Gemini-powered **app creation**
- It does **not** affect:
  - AI in automations
  - Gemini features in Google Workspace

Related help article:

- [Turn Gemini for App Creation on or off](https://support.google.com/appsheet/answer/11918568#gemini-for-app-creation)

---

## Gemini in AppSheet Solutions

### Availability & Licensing

Gemini in AppSheet Solutions is included with:

- **AppSheet Enterprise Plus**

> You don't need Gemini Enterprise or Gemini Workspace to use this feature.

### What It Does

Gemini in AppSheet Solutions enables Gemini-powered AI tasks in AppSheet automations.

Current AI task capabilities include:

- Extract information from an **image**
- Extract information from a **PDF file**
- Extract information from **text**
- Categorize information

The article notes that more AI features are planned:

> Add Gemini-powered AI tasks in automations to extract information from an image, PDF file, or text; or categorize information--with more AI features to come.

Related help article:

- [Overview of AI in automations](https://support.google.com/appsheet/answer/16045284)

### Governance Controls

Organizations can define a governance policy to control who can use AI in automations.

Related help article:

- [Control which app creators can use AI in automations](https://support.google.com/appsheet/answer/16052764)

### Credit Usage

Using Gemini in AppSheet Solutions consumes **credits**.

Credits are based on:

- The number of AI tasks run
- The complexity of those AI tasks
- Usage by users of apps owned by creators in your organization

> When you use Gemini in AppSheet Solutions, you consume _credits_ based on the number and complexity of the AI tasks that are run by users of the apps owned by creators in your organization.

Related help article:

- [Manage credits for Gemini usage](https://support.google.com/appsheet/answer/16274771)

---

## Key Takeaways

- Gemini in AppSheet includes both **AI-assisted app creation** and **AI-powered automation tasks**.
- **Gemini for App Creation** is available with several paid AppSheet licenses, including AppSheet Core and above.
- **Gemini in AppSheet Solutions** requires **AppSheet Enterprise Plus**.
- Neither feature requires a separate **Gemini Enterprise** or **Gemini Workspace** license.
- Admins can disable Gemini for App Creation at the **team level**.
- AI in automations can be governed through policy controls.
- Gemini automation usage consumes **credits** based on task quantity and complexity.

---

# Document 388: Use Gemini in Chrome - Computer - Google Chrome Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/chrome/answer/16283624
Product area: chrome-ai
Original file: docs/use-gemini-in-chrome-computer-google-chrome-help-908b6b84.md

# Use Gemini in Chrome — Google Chrome Help Summary

**Source:** <https://support.google.com/chrome/answer/16283624>  
**Platform covered:** Computer

## Overview

Gemini in Chrome provides AI assistance directly in the browser. It can help users get key takeaways, clarify concepts, find answers, compare information across pages, and more.

> “To provide the most relevant responses, Gemini in Chrome uses content from your current browser tab.”

On computers, users can also share **up to 10 open tabs** with Gemini.

---

## Availability & Requirements

> **Important**: Gemini in Chrome is not available to all users at this time. We’re gradually releasing Gemini in Chrome, so it might not be available to you just yet.

To use Gemini in Chrome on a computer, you must:

- Be **18 or over**
- Be in a **supported region**
- Use a **Chromebook Plus, Mac, or Windows computer**
- Use the **latest version of Chrome**
- Be **signed in to Chrome**
  - Not available in **Incognito mode**
- Set your device to a **supported language**

For work or school Google Accounts:

- Access must be enabled by your administrator.
- Gemini Apps usage depends on organizational settings.

---

## How to Chat with Gemini in Chrome

1. Open **Chrome** on your computer.
2. At the top of the browser, click **Ask Gemini**.
3. If using it for the first time, follow the opt-in instructions.
4. Enter your question or request in the text box.

### Tab context behavior

By default:

- Gemini uses content from your **current tab** to respond.
- Tabs opened from a chat or shared tab are also shared with Gemini.
- In Gemini Live, the current tab is shared by default.

You can add tabs directly in a prompt by typing:

```text
@
```

Then select the tab.

You can share **up to 10 open tabs**.

**Tip:** After opting in, Gemini in Chrome is added to the **Mac menu bar** or **Windows system tray**, and keyboard shortcuts become available.

---

## What You Can Do with Gemini in Chrome

Gemini in Chrome can help you:

- Summarize key takeaways from an article
- Explain complex topics differently
- Test your knowledge of a subject
- Modify recipes for dietary needs
- Compare or consolidate information across pages
- Make recommendations based on your preferences
- Complete multi-step actions on your behalf
- Draft Gmail messages
- Look up past photos and albums

---

## Managing Gemini Chats

Inside Gemini in Chrome, you can:

### Go Live

Click **Go Live** at the bottom to talk while browsing.

### Share or stop sharing tabs

Use **Update tabs** in the text box to add or remove shared tabs.

- Shared tabs show a **check mark**.
- Tabs being used by Gemini show a **glowing underline**.

### Pop out or dock Gemini

Use:

- **Pop-out chat**
- **Dock to original tab**

### Start a new chat

Click **Start new chat**.

If it is not visible:

1. Click **More options**
2. Select **Start new chat**

When starting a new chat, your current tab is shared by default unless you disable:

> **Share current tab by default**

### Switch Gemini models

Click the model name in the text box, then choose another model.

### Find recent chats

Click **More options** at the top of Gemini in Chrome.

---

## Managing Tabs Shared with Gemini

When Gemini opens, your current tab’s page content is shared by default.

### Stop sharing a tab

1. In Gemini in Chrome, above the text box, click **Show shared tabs**.
2. Next to the tab, click **Remove tab**.

When a tab is no longer shared:

- Its glowing underline disappears.

### Share additional tabs

You can share up to **10 recent open tabs** across browser windows.

Steps:

1. Click **Ask Gemini** at the top of Chrome.
2. In the text box, click **Add tabs**.
3. Select the tabs to add.

Effects:

- Shared tabs show a **check mark**.
- Gemini uses shared page content to respond.
- Tabs Gemini is using show a **glowing underline**.

**Tip:** You can also search for and add tabs by entering:

```text
@
```

---

## Workspace Webpages

When sharing a Google Workspace page, such as:

```text
docs.google.com
```

Gemini may directly access your Workspace account to respond about the shared Workspace webpage.

This allows Gemini to refer to an entire Google Doc rather than only what is visible in the browser tab.

To use more personal Workspace content, connect the Workspace app to Gemini Apps.

---

## Gemini in Chrome Settings

Settings path:

1. Open Chrome.
2. Click **More** → **Settings**.
3. Go to **AI innovations** → **Gemini in Chrome**.
4. If the menu is hidden, click **Menu** first.

---

## Hide or Show Gemini Icon

Under **Preferences**, you can turn these settings on or off:

- **Show Gemini at the top of the browser**
- **Show Gemini in Mac menu bar and turn on keyboard shortcut** on Mac
- **Show Gemini in system tray and turn on keyboard shortcut** on Windows

**Tip:** You can also right-click the top of Chrome and select:

- **Unpin Gemini**
- **Pin Gemini**

Under **Permissions**, you ca
