# Document 389: Use Gemini in Drive for research & analysis - Computer - Google Drive Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/drive/answer/16963068?co=GENIE.Platform%3DDesktop&hl=en-en-4
Product area: drive
Original file: docs/use-gemini-in-drive-for-research-analysis-computer-google-drive-help-03566b9c.md

# Use Gemini in Drive for research & analysis

**Important**: To use Gemini in Drive, you must have an eligible Google Workspace or Google AI plan. [Learn more about Gemini features and plans](https://support.google.com/drive/answer/13952129?hl=en&co=DASHER._Family%3DBusiness-Enterprise).

## Overview
- Group related files, folders, or emails (e.g., financial reports, pitch decks) as sources.
- Ask Gemini questions to connect the dots across your data.
- Capabilities:
  - Analyze insights across multiple folders, shared drives, Google Docs, Sheets, Slides, PDFs, Gmail, Google Chat, and Google Calendar.
  - Identify patterns, compare file versions, verify compliance against internal policies.
  - Get answers with direct citations to original documents.
  - Save grouped sources as a **Project** – a focused knowledge base.
  - Share a project with others.
  - Create new Google documents and images from Gemini outputs.

## Examples of Prompts
> "Synthesize the client feedback from Q1 meeting notes and write a list of specific requirements I should bring up on today's call based on our latest product deck."

> "Identify the top 3 risks mentioned across the Q2 operational updates," or "Compare the Q3 budget forecast sheet against our Q4 projected spend presentations."

> "Read the notes from Tuesday's marketing meeting sync and summarize all open action items that pertain to the social team."

## How to Start an “Ask Gemini” Session

### From a specific file or folder
1. Go to [drive.google.com](https://drive.google.com/).
2. Navigate to the folder you want to analyze.
3. In the toolbar at the top, click **Ask Gemini** (sparkle icon).  
   → Opens a full-screen workspace with the selected files listed as your sources.

### From scratch
1. Go to [drive.google.com](https://drive.google.com/).
2. At the top right, click **Ask Gemini** (sparkle icon).  
   → Full-screen workspace opens.
   - **Default settings**: Search across other Workspace apps (Drive, Gmail, Chat, Calendar) is turned **on**. Web search is **off**.

## Resuming Conversations
Gemini automatically saves your conversation history.

1. Click **Ask Gemini** at the top right.
2. In the left panel, click **History**.
3. Your history view depends on how you opened Gemini:
   - **From a Folder or Project**: previous conversations related to those sources appear under **Suggested**.
   - **From anywhere else in Drive**: conversations appear under **All**, sorted by most recent.
4. Continue where you left off.

## Drive Projects – Save & Share Sources
- Projects group all chats about specific sources, making it easy to find conversations.
- You can have multiple unique conversations using the same foundational documents.
- Share a project with your team so everyone can use the same set of documents.
- [Learn how to share your Gemini research with Drive projects](https://support.google.com/drive/answer/16686465).

## Managing Sources and Search Settings
You can control which sources Gemini uses when answering.

### Active Sources
- **Add sources**: In the left panel, under “Your sources” or “Project sources,” click **Add**.
  - **Tip**: You can add specific Gmail emails as sources.
- **Remove sources**: Hover over a source and click **Remove** (X icon). This removes the source from the analysis.
- **Temporarily exclude a source**: Uncheck the box next to the source. Gemini won’t use it, but it stays in the list.

### Suggested Sources
Gemini may suggest additional files based on your current sources or project name.  
**Important**: Suggested files are **not used** until you explicitly add them.

- To view suggestions: click **Suggested** in the left panel to expand the section.
- To add a suggestion: click **Add** next to the file to move it into active sources.

### Broadening Your Search
To let Gemini pull context from your entire Google Workspace:

1. In the left panel, toggle on **Let Gemini search for sources**.
2. Optionally, fine‑tune by checking/unchecking:
   - **Drive** – wider Drive content beyond pinned sources.
   - **Chat, Calendar, Gmail** – recent communications and meeting schedules.
   - **Web** – supplemental public internet data.
   - The primary toggle remains on; you only uncheck specific locations to limit.

[Learn more about search settings](https://support.google.com/drive/answer/16813283).

## Verifying Answers with Citations
- Hover over a citation number (e.g., `[1]`) at the end of a sentence to see which file provided the information.
- Click the file chip in the citation to open the original document in a new tab.

## Related Resources
- [Share your Gemini research using Drive projects](https://support.google.com/drive/answer/16684520)
- [Search & retrieve your files with Gemini](https://support.google.com/drive/answer/16685111)
- [Create Content, Images & Audio Overviews with Gemini in Drive](https://support.google.com/drive/answer/16685527)

---

# Document 390: Use Gemini while you navigate - Android - Google Maps Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/maps/answer/6041199
Product area: maps-ai
Original file: docs/use-gemini-while-you-navigate-android-google-maps-help-b6bfcae5.md

# Use Gemini While You Navigate — Android (Google Maps Help)

**Source:** Google Maps Help — “Use Gemini while you navigate”  
**Platform covered:** Android phones/tablets

## Overview

Gemini can be used inside Google Maps during navigation to help you get information and complete tasks while **driving, walking, or riding a two-wheeler**. You can ask questions or give commands without leaving navigation, helping you stay focused on the road.

## Important Notes

- **Availability:** Gemini is currently available in many countries and languages.
  - Related help: *Gemini mobile app availability*
- **Language and voice settings:** Google Maps uses the **Gemini language and voice preferences** for the signed-in Google account.
  - Related help: *Change Gemini’s language*
- **Location-based answers:** Gemini in Google Maps provides answers based on your **precise location preferences**.
  - Related help: *Manage precise location preferences*

---

## How to Talk to Gemini in Google Maps on Android

1. Open the **Google Maps app** on your Android phone or tablet.
   - If you haven’t used Gemini before, first get started with the **Gemini mobile app**.

2. While in **navigation mode**, activate Gemini by saying:

   > “Hey Google”

   Or tap the **Gemini** icon in Maps.

3. Ask Gemini for help with anything supported in Maps or connected apps.

### If “Hey Google” Doesn’t Work

Check that **“Hey Google” and Voice Match**, powered by Gemini, are turned on.

---

## What You Can Ask Gemini to Do in Maps

## 1. Get Help with Google Maps

### Basic Navigation Controls

Use Gemini to control navigation with commands such as:

> "Navigate home"  
> "Stop navigating"  
> "Add stop"  
> "Mute guidance"  
> "Avoid tolls"  
> "Show alternative routes"

### Basic Route Answers

Ask for information about your current route, including:

> "What's my next turn?"  
> "When will I arrive?"  
> “What’s the weather like at my destination?”  
> "What's my next stop?"

### Report Route Issues

Submit reports about conditions on your route, such as:

> "There’s traffic on the road."  
> "There’s construction up ahead."  
> "There's a car breakdown that’s causing congestion."

---

## 2. Search and Get Information About Places

Gemini can help you search for places while navigating.

### Search Capabilities

You can:

- Search along your route
- Find places near you
- Find places near your destination
- Refine search results
- Add stops to your route

Example:

> “Find me a cafe near my destination that serves tiramisu.”

### Place Information

Gemini can answer questions about destinations, search results, specific places, and landmarks.

Examples:

> “What's the most popular dish on the menu?”  
> "When does {Place Name} close?"

---

## 3. Call or Text Someone

You can ask Gemini to place calls or send messages:

> "Call Mom."  
> "Send a text to Larry”  
> "Send an SMS to Sergey.”

---

## 4. Play Music

Gemini can control music playback with commands like:

> "Play some jazz"  
> “Stop playing music.”

Related help: *Learn how to listen to music*

---

## 5. Use Other Apps with Gemini

**Important:** To use other apps with Gemini in Google Maps, the apps must be connected to the **same account signed into Google Maps**.

Related help: *Use and manage connected apps in Gemini*

Examples of supported requests:

> "Summarize my latest emails."  
> "When’s my next meeting?"  
> "Play music with Spotify."

---

## Additional Help Options

The Google Help page also includes:

- A feedback section for rating whether the article was helpful
- Options to report content issues such as:
  - Inaccurate information
  - Hard-to-understand content
  - Missing information
  - Irrelevant content
  - Minor errors
  - Other suggestions
- A link to the **Google Maps Help Community** for additional support from community members.

---

# Document 391: Use Gems in Gemini Apps - Computer - Gemini Apps Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/gemini/answer/15146780?hl=en
Product area: gemini-apps
Original file: docs/use-gems-in-gemini-apps-computer-gemini-apps-help-15af2d54.md

# Use Gems in Gemini Apps — Markdown Summary

**Source:** Google Gemini Apps Help — “Use Gems in Gemini Apps - Computer”  
**URL:** https://support.google.com/gemini/answer/15146780?hl=en

---

## Overview

Gems let you **create and save customized versions of Gemini** so Gemini Apps respond according to your specific needs, instructions, and optional reference files.

> “You can create and save Gems to customize responses in Gemini Apps to your specific needs.”

Gems can be created and managed in the Gemini web app and used across supported Gemini surfaces.

---

## Requirements

To create and use Gems:

- You must be signed in to Gemini Apps.
- With a **personal Google Account**, you must be:
  - **13 or older**, or
  - the applicable minimum age in your country.
- You must follow Google’s Terms of Service and Prohibited Use Policy.

**Important notice from Google:**

> **Important:** When you create or chat with a Gem, remember that you agreed to Google's Terms of Service, including the Prohibited Use Policy. Be sure not to violate others' rights. Use your discretion before you rely on, publish, or use content that Gemini Apps generate. Users with a work or school account may be subject to different terms.

Work or school accounts may have different terms and controls.

---

## Key Limitations

**Important:**

- Gems **can’t currently be used with Gemini Live**.
- The **Learning coach** premade Gem **doesn’t currently support language learning**.

---

## Create, Use & Manage Gems

### Create a Gem

**Important:**

> When you create a Gem in the Gemini web app, it will appear in the Gemini mobile app and the Gemini side panel in Google Workspace.

#### Steps

1. On your computer, go to [gemini.google.com](https://gemini.google.com/).
2. Click **Open Sidebar**.
3. Click **Gems**.
   - If you’ve never made a Gem before, first click **Settings and help** → **Gems**.
4. Click **New Gem**.
5. Enter:
   - a **name**
   - **instructions** for your Gem
6. Optional: under **Knowledge**, click **Add files** to add sources.
7. Click **Save**.

---

## Optional Ways to Improve a Gem

### Use Gemini to Rewrite Instructions

After typing a few words of instructions, you can use Gemini to make them more comprehensive.

> After a few words, you can click **Use Gemini to re-write instructions** to have Gemini Apps create more comprehensive instructions for most topics. It may take a few seconds for the generated instructions to appear.

---

### Upload Files as Gem Knowledge

You can upload files so the Gem has more context or can reference specific documents in chats.

> You can add files to give your Gem more context or to reference specific docs in your chats with the Gem.

To add files:

1. Add instructions to the Gem.
2. Under **Knowledge**, click **Add files**.

#### File Upload Options

- **Upload from your device:** click **Upload files**.
- **Add from Google Drive:** click **Add from Drive**.
- **Add a NotebookLM notebook:** click **More uploads** → **Notebooks**.

#### Google Drive Requirements

To add files from Google Drive:

- **Keep Activity** must be turned on.
- Google Workspace must be connected to Gemini Apps.
- If Google Workspace isn’t connected, Gemini will prompt you to connect it.

#### Google Drive File Version Behavior

> If you add a file from your Drive, Gemini will use the most recent version of the file. If you make changes to the file, these changes will be reflected in your Gem.

---

### Knowledge File Citations

By default, Gem responses may cite uploaded knowledge files.

To turn citations off:

> If you don’t want the Gem’s responses to include citations to your knowledge files, select **Disable knowledge citations**. This also disables citations for files uploaded in your Gem chats.

Additional tips:

- You may need to scroll down to find the **Knowledge** section.
- You can edit a Gem later to remove files or add more files.

---

## Preview a Gem

Before saving or finalizing edits, you can test the Gem.

1. In the text box on the right, enter a prompt to preview the Gem.
2. Review the response.
3. Edit the Gem instructions as needed.
4. Click **Submit**.

---

## Use a Gem in a Chat

1. Go to [gemini.google.com](https://gemini.google.com/).
2. Click **Open Sidebar**.
3. Click **Gems**.
   - If you’ve never made a Gem before, first click **Settings and help** → **Gems**.
4. Under **My Gems**, select the Gem you want to use.
5. Enter your question or prompt in the text box.
6. Click **Submit**.

---

## Pin and Unpin Gems

Recently used or created Gems appear near the top of the Gemini Apps menu. You can pin specific Gems to keep them at the top.

### Steps

1. Go to [gemini.google.com](https://gemini.google.com/).
2. On the left under **Gems**, find the Gem you want to pin or unpin.
3. Next to the Gem, click **More**.
4. Select **Pin** or **Unpin**.

---

## Edit a Gem

1. Go to [gemini.google.com](https://gemini.google.com/).
2. Click **Open Sidebar**.
3. Click **Gems**.
4. Under **M

[... summary truncated for context management ...]

---

# Document 392: Use generative AI at work - Google Workspace Learning Center

Source URL: https://support.google.com/a/users/answer/14628736?hl=en
Product area: workspace-learning-center
Original file: docs/use-generative-ai-at-work-google-workspace-learning-center-1e3ab82a.md

# Use Generative AI at Work — Google Workspace Learning Center Summary

**Source:** Google Workspace Learning Center  
**URL:** https://support.google.com/a/users/answer/14628736?hl=en  
**Tutorial type:** Google Workspace business tutorial  
**Platform note:** Instructions are **for computer only**.

---

## What You’ll Learn

How to use **Google Workspace with Gemini**, Google’s generative AI offering, at work across Workspace apps.

Covered use cases:

1. **Write & respond to email** in Gmail  
2. **Speed up & polish writing** in Docs  
3. **Brainstorm new ideas** in the Gemini app  
4. **Organize data** in Sheets  
5. **Illustrate presentations** in Slides  

---

## Requirements

To follow this tutorial, you need:

- A **Google Workspace account** with the **Google Workspace with Gemini add-on**
- A **Google Workspace with Gemini license** assigned by an administrator
- Computer access

Language availability notes:

- **All Gemini features are available in English**
- Some AI features are available in other languages
- The Gemini app is available in **45+ languages**

---

## Important Warnings Before Using Gemini

Google highlights several limitations and safety notes:

- **Gemini feature suggestions don’t represent Google’s views**, and should not be attributed to Google.
- **Don’t rely on Gemini features as medical, legal, financial, or other professional advice.**
- Gemini features may suggest **inaccurate or inappropriate information**.
- User feedback helps make Gemini more helpful and safe.
- Enterprise end users can submit feedback about generative AI features.
- Before submitting feedback, users are informed that feedback:
  - Should **not contain personal, sensitive, or confidential information**
  - **May be read by humans**

---

# 1. Write & Respond to Email in Gmail

Use **Help me write** in Gmail to draft emails quickly, such as meeting requests. Gemini can generate a draft in seconds, which you can then refine and send.

## Steps

### Step 1: Open Help me write in Gmail

1. Open [Gmail](https://mail.google.com/) on your computer.
2. Click **Compose**.
3. In the bottom-left of the compose window, click **Help me write**.

### Step 2: Enter a Prompt

Example prompt:

```text
Write an email asking for a meeting about [project name]. Include a bulleted list of discussion points including milestones, task status, and next steps.
```

Then click **Create**.

### Step 3: Review, Refine & Insert

1. Click **Refine**.
2. Choose how you want Gemini to change the content.
3. When finished, click **Insert**.

---

# 2. Speed Up & Polish Your Writing in Docs

Use Gemini in Google Docs to:

- Start from a blank page
- Create a first draft quickly
- Shorten existing text
- Elaborate on text
- Summarize content
- Adjust tone
- Transform text into another form, such as a **poem** or **script**

---

## Create a First Draft

### Step 1: Open Help me write in Docs

1. Open a [Google Docs file](https://docs.google.com/document/).
2. Click where you want to write.
3. On the left, click **Help me write**.

### Step 2: Tell Gemini What to Write

Example prompt:

```text
Write an outline of [project x]. Specify 4 phases: Research, Design, Testing, and Production. The project will kick off Q2 this year and wrap up in Q2 next year.
```

Then click **Create**.

### Step 3: Review, Refine & Insert

1. Review the response.
2. Click **Refine**.
3. Choose how Gemini should change the content.
4. Click **Insert** when finished.

---

## Polish Existing Writing

1. Open a [Google Docs file](https://docs.google.com/document/).
2. Select the text you want to polish.
3. On the left, click **Help me write**.
4. Choose how Gemini should change the content.
5. Click **Insert** when finished.

---

# 3. Brainstorm for New Ideas in the Gemini App

Use the Gemini app for brainstorming, writing, learning, and related tasks.

## Step 1: Start a Conversation

1. Go to [gemini.google.com](https://gemini.google.com/).
2. Sign in to your Google Account if needed.
3. Enter a question or prompt in the text box.

Example prompt:

```text
[Product name] is a product that will [describe what product will do]. Give me 10 things I can do to get customers excited about the launch of [product name].
```

4. Click **Submit**.

---

## Step 2: Review and Continue the Conversation

After Gemini responds, you can:

### View Other Drafts

For some prompts:

1. Click **View other drafts** or **Show drafts** above the response.
2. Select the draft you want to review.

### Revise Your Prompt

1. Click **Edit text** to the right of your prompt.
2. Edit the prompt.
3. Click **Update**.

### Modify the Response

Click **Modify response**, then choose an option:

- **Shorter** — reduce length
- **Longer** — expand the response
- **Simpler** — simplify language
- **More casual** — make tone more casual
- **More professional** — make tone more professional

### Create a Chart or Table

To generate a chart or table from data in the response, ask Gemini in a

[... summary truncated for context management ...]

---

# Use generative AI for customer service

Generative AI aids customer service by automating responses, helping to reduce agent burnout and enhance customer trust.

[Get started](https://workspace.google.com/business/signup/welcome?source=gafb-ai_customer_service-hero-en&hl=en&ga_region=amer&ga_country=us&ga_lang=en)[Contact sales](https://workspace.google.com/contact/?source=gafb-ai_customer_service-hero-en)

![Gemini generates a follow-up email, shown with woman on tablet](https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/HafEWngwsxwJrSbmK3O2NK6lcPVz2W91sYDdDK5yZgtfI0CfEQ8EfoL9bl0PdElRiogZMUGooF8Rr7AqnNO8YsktzF01Hh28sFLU=e365-pa-nu-rw-w528)

## How AI tools in Google Workspace benefits customer service

Gemini works across Google Workspace apps to help quickly make sense of large amounts of information and seamlessly generate first drafts. From building customer profiles to [assisting with messaging](https://workspace.google.com/resources/ai-writing/), Gemini can help provide faster service to customers and make greater use of your teamâs resources.

![Gemini summarizes an email and can suggest a reply](https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/UJvhw8XUU1K6r9Xr2axeR7tdW2UZ5mhLlDXHycuQjs1ykU6YPQIVLgqIFmEBzIJhIeqZ2HR52xaXK0uldUH1vUnv41yh8LdESB8=e365-pa-nu-rw-w608)

## Enhance customer satisfaction and improve client retention

Utilize Gemini to help transcribe and summarize customer interactions. Use this to help craft customer profiles and draft timely responses, which can help enhance satisfaction and retention.

## Reduce agent burnout

Gemini helps streamline customer service tasks by crafting effective responses to common customer questions in [Gmail](https://workspace.google.com/products/gmail/ai/) and Google Docs, reducing customer service agent burnout during periods of peak customer activity by getting answers in front of customers faster.

### Frequently asked questions

Expand all

- Generative AI is helping customer service teams respond faster and do more with fewer resources. From helping to craft timely, personalized responses to customer questions to helping reduce response times and customer agent burnout, generative AI has become a valuable tool to customer service teams.

  Beyond assisting with answering customer queries, generative AI can also be used to transcribe video calls, extract and summarize information from documents to help outline clear next steps, provide a record of service and build more accurate customer profiles to better anticipate current and future needs.
- There are a variety of ways AI is being used to provide faster, more efficient customer service. Gemini can be used as an intelligent virtual assistant, capable of helping customer support agents write answers to customers in a fast and precise way. This can help ease the burden on customer service agents, allowing them to focus their efforts on more personalized services.
- One of generative AIâs most impressive qualities is its ability to analyze large amounts of text and pull out relevant information and commonalities that can help improve processes. In customer service, generative AI can be used to analyze documents, job tickets, and transcripts to create accurate customer profiles that pinpoint their wants and needs. With this information, customer teams can proactively craft timely responses and action plans to streamline processes and increase customer satisfaction.
- Today, customer service teams are using generative AI to analyze large amounts of customer data to better predict behaviors and respond in ways that will help reduce agent burnout and increase customer satisfaction. Having fast, more accurate insights into customer wants and needs can allow service teams to boost customer engagement and serve them with more timely and personal responses to questions.

### Frequently asked questions

Expand all

- Generative AI is helping customer service teams respond faster and do more with fewer resources. From helping to craft timely, personalized responses to customer questions to helping reduce response times and customer agent burnout, generative AI has become a valuable tool to customer service teams.

  Beyond assisting with answering customer queries, generative AI can also be used to transcribe video calls, extract and summarize information from documents to help outline clear next steps, provide a record of service and build more accurate customer profiles to better anticipate current and future needs.
- There are a variety of ways AI is being used to provide faster, more efficient customer service. Gemini can be used as an intelligent virtual assistant, capable of helping customer support agents write answers to customers in a fast and precise way. This can help ease the burden on customer service agents, allowing them to focus their efforts on more personalized services.
- One of generative AIâs most impressive qualities is its ability to analyze large amounts of text and pull out relevant information and commonalities that can help improve processes. In customer service, generative AI can be used to analyze documents, job tickets, and transcripts to create accurate customer profiles that pinpoint their wants and needs. With this information, customer teams can proactively craft timely responses and action plans to streamline processes and increase customer satisfaction.
- Today, customer service teams are using generative AI to analyze large amounts of customer data to better predict behaviors and respond in ways that will help reduce agent burnout and increase customer satisfaction. Having fast, more accurate insights into customer wants and needs can allow service teams to boost customer engagement and serve them with more timely and personal responses to questions.

# Use generative AI for HR

## How AI tools in Google Workspace benefits human resources

HR teams can use Gemini to save time on a variety of tasks, including recruiting efforts, onboarding new hires, updating training materials for existing personnel, drafting company policies, and workforce planning.

## Save time on recruiting

Prompt Geminiâs [help me write](https://workspace.google.com/intl/en_ca/resources/ai-writing/) to create job descriptions for open roles within your organization. Gemini can also help streamline your hiring process by quickly analyzing resumes, providing summaries to help you pinpoint qualified candidates, and keep track of your interview pipeline.

![Gemini creates a job description using âhelp me writeâ, shown with woman on laptop](https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/HGdoJu1w8VevxE7SKUGgd6ceZlxSTGCe_ERJhFAEc6Wq-0ueGZ6kpJ9xh-2F8FxLCNFdn_GAR2LNrh8Gf548Cwyse27R7omteFc=e365-pa-nu-rw-w608)

## Automate the onboarding process

Use Gemini to create onboarding checklists to get new hires up-to-speed quickly. Use [Gemini in Sheets](https://workspace.google.com/intl/en_ca/resources/spreadsheet-ai/) to create a first draft of training tasks, then refine the list accordingly.

## Customize training

Use Gemini to update training materials or customize training documentation for specific roles, making them more engaging and relevant. In addition to updating training materials, Gemini can help you with original images to illustrate information.

![Gemini creates a slide using a prompt in Google Slides](https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/JjLDJS6uISX-mtPlMP_w_nzIgh1gFVCHYFyck8t8bq2gXwwLNP_T9SZkBU6lsZyestarIijOGy9ZZe_nrfZ19Nt-i4DqBJHKkMn0=e365-pa-nu-rw-w608)

# Use generative AI for HR

Use Gemini as your AI powered assistant to help transform HR: from drafting jobs to onboarding, speeding up hiring and enhancing policies.

[Get started](https://workspace.google.com/business/signup/welcome?source=gafb-ai_hr-hero-en&hl=en&ga_region=amer&ga_country=us&ga_lang=en)[Contact sales](https://workspace.google.com/contact/?source=gafb-ai_hr-hero-en)

![Gemini suggests email reply options about the hiring process](https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/AP8TrkfMNM5u3htIIiONfGvo6FjEoLC4l8f42Pl0nKbofW_S9pdDX1mh4sbJ6GasvZ7Aej0yUwHRU7zQ7SNJfg7mfZgfQJSWSfk=e365-pa-nu-rw-w528)

## How AI tools in Google Workspace benefits human resources

HR teams can use Gemini to save time on a variety of tasks, including recruiting efforts, onboarding new hires, updating training materials for existing personnel, drafting company policies, and workforce planning.

## Save time on recruiting

Prompt Geminiâs [help me write](https://workspace.google.com/resources/ai-writing/) to create job descriptions for open roles within your organization. Gemini can also help streamline your hiring process by quickly analyzing resumes, providing summaries to help you pinpoint qualified candidates, and keep track of your interview pipeline.

![Gemini creates a job description using âhelp me writeâ, shown with woman on laptop](https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/HGdoJu1w8VevxE7SKUGgd6ceZlxSTGCe_ERJhFAEc6Wq-0ueGZ6kpJ9xh-2F8FxLCNFdn_GAR2LNrh8Gf548Cwyse27R7omteFc=e365-pa-nu-rw-w608)

![Gemini creates an onboarding checklist with âhelp me organizeâ, shown with a man at laptop](https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/KFgfnLa9t44o8k4bdA99_asvZFk2iusIzAYiOeQyUcoFadBNAJ4B4ASvXnc4djQh_8N4KiGB0tgD5a61OcXC9N6npsEexNzHH-FV=e365-pa-nu-rw-w608)

## Automate the onboarding process

Use Gemini to create onboarding checklists to get new hires up-to-speed quickly. Use [Gemini in Sheets](https://workspace.google.com/resources/spreadsheet-ai/) to create a first draft of training tasks, then refine the list accordingly.

## Customize training

Use Gemini to update training materials or customize training documentation for specific roles, making them more engaging and relevant. In addition to updating training materials, Gemini can help you with original images to illustrate information.

![Gemini creates a slide using a prompt in Google Slides](https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/JjLDJS6uISX-mtPlMP_w_nzIgh1gFVCHYFyck8t8bq2gXwwLNP_T9SZkBU6lsZyestarIijOGy9ZZe_nrfZ19Nt-i4DqBJHKkMn0=e365-pa-nu-rw-w608)

# Use generative AI for marketing

AI is changing the marketing landscape, giving teams and individuals the tools that they need to get more done faster.

[Get Started Now](https://workspace.google.com/business/signup/welcome?hl=en-GB&source=gafb-ai_marketing-hero-en-GB&ga_region=emea&ga_country=uk&ga_lang=en)[Contact sales](https://workspace.google.com/intl/en_uk/contact/?source=gafb-ai_marketing-hero-en-GB)

![Person on laptop using Gemini to write a job description.](https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/JEvJlZXEHgRUpvCHtNLOQFb2gIf6xiu0c7Y2PlJQdI4fqDYNCbypWovbjwX9TJ36IP-j3S7QTJHT3XM4Kg6rgHwMQyTE2OstxUXm=e365-pa-nu-rw-w528)

## How AI tools in Google Workspace benefit marketing

With generative [AI tools in Workspace](https://workspace.google.com/intl/en_uk/solutions/ai/) you can quickly craft messaging, evaluate large amounts of information, summarise documents or emails, and automate regular tasks. This gives marketers time to focus on higher-value strategic and creative elements of their work.

![Person on laptop using Gemini to help write an email.](https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/u21xNJSwZn_MmPtq885AJPvXydos5MOwOdxLoWZfHrIdLfRlrIG1DmUM_3pqFUpwrUO3p_avTC9AUunOGuGL4l3Op2rmipRtfLuKDA=e365-pa-nu-rw-w608)

## Helps you write

With Gemini, you can quickly create first drafts of emails, blog posts, business proposals, ad copy and more.

[Gemini in Gmail](https://workspace.google.com/intl/en_uk/products/gmail/ai/) can also quickly summarise emails and craft replies.

## Helps you create images

'Help me visualise' in [Google Slides](https://workspace.google.com/intl/en_uk/products/slides/) allows you to enter a simple prompt to generate a custom image. You can further customise by using 'Add a style' to create visuals that feel on-brand and complement your messaging in exciting new ways.

![Person on a laptop uses Gemini to generate an image of a giraffe in front of the Eiffel tower.](https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/39WpbADD0bWS42WpvmNQnCIE12GdnNhma5WDsagn2E05z2HeaBZHly_drES0Ccq-vd6TBJDS3aQ-9Lh09xHbojDm46PX7mxMLA9b=e365-pa-nu-rw-w608)

## Helps you organise

With just a few words, [Gemini in Sheets](https://workspace.google.com/intl/en_uk/resources/spreadsheet-ai/) can help you quickly create a project tracker for your upcoming campaign launch or any other project.

## Helps you connect

[Gemini in Meet](https://workspace.google.com/intl/en_uk/resources/ai-for-meetings/) helps you look and sound your best on customer or agency calls and can even help translate the conversation across multiple languages.

![A woman uses Gemini appearance settings to adjust lighting levels and video framing in Meet.](https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/jddkfvYLoH-7OYC1tRNHg5RyhC3rgHz70yY68kSjzwFkQ3wnZCS5faJrtyxURMj8HJFYDZMgmCQ6L-IAZv2AFl61dCUiVQmhkTT5=e365-pa-nu-rw-w608)

#### Curious about generative AI in marketing?

See our FAQ to learn more.

Expand all

- Generative AI is just like any other marketing tool in that it has benefits and limitations:Â

  - Generative AI can help marketers save time, automating tasks like generating meeting and email summaries, data analysis and content creation.Â
  - You'll still need to fact check AI-generated content, as a generative AI platform is only as knowledgeable as the data used to train it.Â
  - Generative AI can assist with understanding customer behaviours and improving ad targeting based on historical campaign data.Â
  - Generative AI is best used for repetitive tasks, giving marketers more time to exercise their creativity when crafting marketing campaigns and strategies.Â
  - Because generative AI collects and analyses large data sets based on customer behaviours, it's important to be transparent about data collection and protection measures to reassure customers that their information is safe.Â

  With GoogleÂ AI, we've reaffirmed [our commitment to protect user data and privacy](https://cloud.google.com/privacy?hl=en-GB). Your privacy is our priority. Your Google Workspace content â including emails, documents, recordings and more â will not be shared with other users without permission. We never sell, delete or export your data. Additionally, while you may use Google Workspace and Gemini to generate your own ad campaigns, Google does not collect, scan or use your content in Google Workspace services for advertising purposes.Â

  [Learn more about how Google protects your data in the age of generative AI](https://workspace.google.com/blog/identity-and-security/protecting-your-data-era-generative-ai?hl=en-GB).
- Generative AI can help sales and marketing professionals save time and be more productive at every stage of an engagement. Generative AI can be used to create RFPs and nurture leads with more personalised content. It can be used to create sales materials, [email marketing assets](https://workspace.google.com/intl/en_uk/resources/ai-email-marketing/) and summarise meeting notes with clear takeaways and identifying follow-up opportunities. It can also help generate long-form and short-form content for marketing campaigns, analyse data to inform customer segments, and help measure and improve campaign performance.
- Generative AI can be used to tackle a variety of marketing tasks, including content creation and personalisation, as well as analysing customer behaviours and campaign performance.

# Use generative AI for marketing

AI is changing the marketing landscape, giving teams and individuals the tools they need to get more done faster.

[Get started](https://workspace.google.com/business/signup/welcome?source=gafb-ai_marketing-hero-en-CA&hl=en-CA&ga_region=amer&ga_country=ca&ga_lang=en)[Contact sales](https://workspace.google.com/intl/en_ca/contact/?source=gafb-ai_marketing-hero-en-CA)

![Person on laptop using Gemini to write a job description.](https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/JEvJlZXEHgRUpvCHtNLOQFb2gIf6xiu0c7Y2PlJQdI4fqDYNCbypWovbjwX9TJ36IP-j3S7QTJHT3XM4Kg6rgHwMQyTE2OstxUXm=e365-pa-nu-rw-w528)

## How AI tools in Google Workspace benefits marketing

With generative [AI tools in Workspace](https://workspace.google.com/intl/en_ca/solutions/ai/) you can quickly craft messaging, evaluate large amounts of information, summarize documents or emails, and automate regular tasks. This gives marketers time to focus on higher value strategic and creative elements of their work.

![Person on laptop uses Gemini to help write an email.](https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/u21xNJSwZn_MmPtq885AJPvXydos5MOwOdxLoWZfHrIdLfRlrIG1DmUM_3pqFUpwrUO3p_avTC9AUunOGuGL4l3Op2rmipRtfLuKDA=e365-pa-nu-rw-w608)

## Helps you write

With Gemini, you can quickly create first drafts of emails, blog posts, business proposals, ad copy and more.

[Gemini in Gmail](https://workspace.google.com/intl/en_ca/products/gmail/ai/) can also quickly summarize emails and craft replies.

## Helps you create images

âHelp me visualizeâ in [Google Slides](https://workspace.google.com/intl/en_ca/products/slides/) allows you to enter a simple prompt to generate a custom image. You can further customize by using âAdd a styleâ to create visuals that feel on-brand and complement your messaging in exciting new ways.

## Helps you organize

With just a few words, [Gemini in Sheets](https://workspace.google.com/intl/en_ca/resources/spreadsheet-ai/) can help you quickly create a project tracker for your upcoming campaign launch or any other project.

## Helps you connect

[Gemini in Meet](https://workspace.google.com/intl/en_ca/resources/ai-for-meetings/) helps you look and sound your best on customer or agency calls and can even help translate the conversation across multiple languages.

![A woman uses Gemini appearance settings to adjust lighting levels and video framing in Meet.](https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/jddkfvYLoH-7OYC1tRNHg5RyhC3rgHz70yY68kSjzwFkQ3wnZCS5faJrtyxURMj8HJFYDZMgmCQ6L-IAZv2AFl61dCUiVQmhkTT5=e365-pa-nu-rw-w608)

See our FAQs to learn more.

Expand all

- Generative AI is just like any other marketing tool in that it has benefits and limitations:Â

  - Generative AI can help marketers save time, automating tasks like generating meeting and email summaries, data analysis, and content creation.Â
  - Youâll still need to fact check AI-generated content, as a generative AI platform is only as knowledgeable as the data used to train it.Â
  - Generative AI can assist with understanding customer behaviors and improving ad targeting based on historical campaign data.Â
  - Generative AI is best used for repetitive tasks, giving marketers more time to exercise their creativity when crafting marketing campaigns and strategies.Â
  - Because generative AI collects and analyzes large data sets based on customer behaviors, itâs important to be transparent about data collection and protection measures to reassure customers their information is safe.Â

  With Google AI, weâve reaffirmed [our commitment to protect user data and privacy](https://cloud.google.com/privacy?hl=en-CA). Your privacy is our priority. Your Google Workspace contentâincluding emails, documents, recordings, and moreâ will not be shared with other users without permission. We never sell, delete, or export your data. Additionally, while you may use Google Workspace and Gemini to generate your own ad campaigns, Google does not collect, scan, or use your content in Google Workspace services for advertising purposes.Â

  [Learn more about how Google protects your data in the age of generative AI](https://workspace.google.com/blog/identity-and-security/protecting-your-data-era-generative-ai?hl=en-CA).
- Generative AI can help sales and marketing professionals save time and be more productive at every stage of an engagement. Generative AI can be used to create RFPs and nurture leads with more personalized content. It can be used to create sales materials, [email marketing assets](https://workspace.google.com/intl/en_ca/resources/ai-email-marketing/), and summarize meeting notes with clear takeaways and identifying follow-up opportunities. It can also help generate long-form and short-form content for marketing campaigns, analyze data to inform customer segments, and help to measure and improve campaign performance.
- Generative AI can be used to tackle a variety of marketing tasks, including content creation and personalization, as well as analyzing customer behaviors and campaign performance.

It can also help generate long-form and short-form content for marketing campaigns, analyze data to inform customer segments, and help to measure and improve campaign performance.
- Generative AI can be used to tackle a variety of marketing tasks, including content creation and personalization, as well as analyzing customer behaviors and campaign performance.

# Use generative AI for sales

# Use generative AI for sales

Generative AI boosts sales efficiency. AI tools in Google Workspace can help assist your sales teams to focus on selling more.

[Get Started Now](https://workspace.google.com/business/signup/welcome?hl=en-GB&source=gafb-ai_sales-hero-en-GB&ga_region=emea&ga_country=uk&ga_lang=en)[Contact sales](https://workspace.google.com/intl/en_uk/contact/?source=gafb-ai_sales-hero-en-GB)

![Gemini creates a summary table from a prompt with 'Help me organise', shown with three people at a computer](https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/d9iDM10MPwH892qyzEsbfQTzBl4kMVfcWNxhcGooTTGLO9zGXn735uZU45HTxGv_7Dgw06-eFYys6TJOAbtgiBpMJak-8rfUOhlB=e365-pa-nu-rw-w528)

## How AI tools in Google Workspace benefit sales

Gemini works across Workspace apps to help streamline sales by organising prospects, generating proposals and [marketing campaigns](https://workspace.google.com/intl/en_uk/resources/ai-email-marketing/), and crafting effective outreach for customer engagement.

![Gemini creates copy from a prompt with 'Help me write', shown with a man on phone](https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/J5Cxiivp6hREKZaLy__W_uFfMsigrSgv5PuZKmyaAvnN9YePLmEpyTWMW47YbVVs3AomUspwjs7uqJTIf-UHf6TX0oPERnrrVEs=e365-pa-nu-rw-w720)

## Improves efficiency in sales process

As your sales assistant, Gemini helps to tailor messaging for different prospects, expediting sales processes, from outreach to proposals, freeing time for increased focus on sales.

## Quickly create sales documents and materials

Generative AI is transforming sales by rapidly processing text. Gemini helps to extract useful information from documents to craft summaries and customises images for [presentations](https://workspace.google.com/intl/en_uk/resources/presentation-ai/), freeing up sales teams to focus on generating revenue.

![Gemini creates an image from a prompt with 'Help me visualise', shown with man at computer](https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/j6q_Mt52ko70kqW6St1BFGir8rrk3O7S75EEoi-ExcT7X_QPix7YkHv-wq7cLG7avo0ILOIbdUMq8MVRm1zjMLCMgNHR7v9i0wU=e365-pa-nu-rw-w720)

![Gemini writes a business proposal with prompt, shown with a man at computer](https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/kHpE9wek4kKIOOiWiiNsCxQ-X8p4iwRYzBEe1lrT15cozaEkX-04-qV1c2H81WPLpQNFgIvExKOqu6LBjAJqsfBUkgwgD8_8ZP1l=e365-pa-nu-rw-w720)

## Minimise repetitive work

Gemini can help you streamline repetitive, administrative tasks â giving your sales team more time to nurture qualified leads. Use Gemini to assist in crafting outreach messages, organising leads, outlining a business proposal and more.

### Effective use cases of generative artificial intelligence in sales

![Data-informed decision-making icon ](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-workspace-assets/uploads/7uffzv9dk4sn-qP2FW3zVIL7pxwAC8ioMq-596daa42f8e5fa243ed78e6ea0b053f6-AI_for_Sales_TemplateMulticolumn_Icon_Data_informed_decision_making.svg)

![Enhanced opportunity management icon ](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-workspace-assets/uploads/7uffzv9dk4sn-4BMsJ2K8ZBMEMyjQMQjioa-43802ccdc74ca552982aa1526be5e8ad-AI_for_Sales_TemplateMulticolumn_Icon_Enhanced_opportunities_management.svg)

Enhanced opportunities management

![Presentation icon ](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-workspace-assets/uploads/7uffzv9dk4sn-1wpjOUHjncW8UdpylVax5W-2b9302f8e2661c124c42e9f53ed822fa-AI_for_Sales_TemplateMulticolumn_Icon_Optimize_sales_engagement.svg)

### Frequently asked questions

Expand all

- Generative AI can help sales professionals streamline sales processes, helping them organise and track sales and customer information, conduct outreach with personalised messaging, and put together business proposals automatically and based on text prompts. Generative AI can also be used to help sales teams more accurately run sales forecasts, pinpointing inaccuracies and summarising information in an easily digestible way.
- Generative AI can help B2B sales professionals boost their productivity, assisting with creation of sales materials, organising and tracking contacts and leads, and even writing outreach and proposals tailored to prospects.
- Generative AI can help sales teams save time on more time-consuming administrative tasks, allowing them to focus on the more strategic elements of their job. Use Gemini to help accelerate research and help create sales materials so that you can spend less time keeping track of leads and analysing lengthy reports, and devote more time to nurturing leads and making meaningful connections with customers.

# Document 405: Use Google Workspace Studio with a screen reader - Workspace Studio Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/workspace-studio/answer/17119624
Product area: workspace-studio
Original file: docs/use-google-workspace-studio-with-a-screen-reader-workspace-studio-help-705189f1.md

# Use Google Workspace Studio with a Screen Reader — Summary

**Source:** Google Workspace Studio Help  
**URL:** https://support.google.com/workspace-studio/answer/17119624  
**Page title:** *Use Google Workspace Studio with a screen reader*

---

## Overview

Google Workspace Studio is a web-based automation tool for Google Workspace. It lets users create automated workflows, called **flows**, using Gemini, without programming.

> **Note**: A supported Workspace edition is required. Learn more about [access to Workspace Studio](https://support.google.com/workspace-studio/answer/16782648).

Workspace Studio supports:

- **Keyboard-only focus navigation**
- **Screen reader navigation**
- Web structure optimized for screen reader context
- Creating, editing, sharing, testing, reviewing, and managing flows

---

## Key Terms

### Flows

In Workspace Studio, automated tasks are called **flows**.

A flow has 3 main parts:

- **Starter**: The event or schedule that starts the flow.
  - Example: receiving an email from specific people
  - Example: every Monday at 9 AM
- **Steps**: Actions the flow performs after it starts.
  - Example: ask Gemini to extract action items
  - Example: draft a reply
  - Example: add content to a document
- **Variables**: Dynamic placeholders for information from earlier steps.
  - Example: Gemini’s response
  - Example: email sender
  - Example: form response

> Variables let you use information from one step in later steps.

---

## Before You Start: Screen Reader Mode Tips

Workspace Studio works best when screen readers are set to focus-oriented editing modes.

### ChromeVox

> Ensure sticky mode is turned off. To turn off, press the Search key twice until you hear “Sticky mode disabled.”

### JAWS

> For editing content, ensure that JAWS Virtual PC cursor is off. To turn off, press JAWS + z until you hear "Use virtual PC cursor off."

Additional note:

- Depending on Forms mode configuration, you may need to do this more than once.

### NVDA

> Focus mode generally provides a better experience for editing content than browse mode. To switch between focus and browse modes: Press NVDA + space.

### VoiceOver

- Turn off Quick Nav:

> Press the Left arrow and Right arrow keys at the same time until you hear “Quick Nav off.”

- If focus is not placed correctly, such as after a dialog opens:

> Press Escape to return your focus to the editing area.

- To return to the editing area:

> Press VoiceOver + Shift + Down arrow.

---

# Navigate the Workspace Studio UI

Go to:

```text
https://studio.workspace.google.com/
```

If the UI does not match the described layout, you may not have access to Workspace Studio.

> **Note:** the title of the main page is: “Discover - Google Workspace Studio”.

---

## Screen Reader Web Navigation Structure

Starting from the top in screen reader **web navigation mode**, users should find:

- **Heading / Banner landmark**
  - Link: “Workspace studio”
  - Button: “Send Feedback”
  - Button: “Help”
  - Button collapsed: “Google apps”
  - Button collapsed: “Google Account: <name>”
- Button: “New flow”
- “Flows Left navigation” navigation landmark, vertical menu bar
  - Menu item: “Discover”
  - Menu item: “My flows”
- Heading: “Automate your work with Gemini”
- Heading: “Streamline and manage tasks across Workspace”
- Edit field:
  - “prompt input field”
  - read-only
  - multiline
  - prompt: “Describe a task for Gemini”
- Button: “Create”

### Example Workflow Cards

Example 1 may include:

- Static text: “Streamline my follow-up”
- Static text: “Send me summaries & action items after meetings”
- Static text:

```text
Start after your meetings Have Gemini summarize the meeting and action items Get the summaries in Chat
```

- Graphics:
  - “Calendar”
  - “Gemini”
  - “Gemini”
  - “Chat”
- Static text: “+1”
- Button: “Turn on Workflow”

Other examples may include:

- Static text: “Review quickly”
- Button: “More details about this workflow”
- Static text: “Get prepared”
- Button: “More details about this workflow”

### Filter Toolbar

The toolbar can filter remaining examples:

- Toggle button not pressed: “Email boosters”
- Toggle button not pressed: “Better meetings”
- Toggle button not pressed: “Tasks and action items”

Additional headings and examples may include:

- “Email boosters”
- “Better meetings”
- “Tasks and action items”

---

## Keyboard Focus Navigation

Using screen reader **focus navigation mode**, tab order from the top includes:

1. Link: “Workspace studio”
2. Button: “Send Feedback”
3. Button: “Help”
4. Button collapsed: “Google apps”
5. Button collapsed: “Google Account: <name>”

Initial focus is on:

- “New flow” button

Then:

- Tab to “Flows Left navigation” vertical menu bar
  - Menu item current page: “Discover”
    - Activating it returns to initial focus
  - Down arrow to: “My flows”

Next tab opens a dialog:

- Initial focus is on:
  - “prompt input field” edit field
- If text is typed, focus moves to:
  - Create button
  - Ex

[... summary truncated for context management ...]

# Document 406: Use & manage Connected Apps in Gemini - Computer - Gemini Apps Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/gemini/answer/13695044
Product area: gemini-apps
Original file: docs/use-manage-connected-apps-in-gemini-computer-gemini-apps-help-7754448b.md

# Use & Manage Connected Apps in Gemini — Summary

**Source:** Google Gemini Apps Help — “Use & manage Connected Apps in Gemini”  
**URL:** https://support.google.com/gemini/answer/13695044  
**Platform focus:** Computer / Gemini web app, with notes for Android, iOS, and other devices.

---

## Core Idea

Gemini Apps can connect to other apps and services to complete user requests and provide more useful, personalized responses. With permission, Gemini can access information and content from connected apps, such as Gmail, Google Calendar, Spotify, Google Photos, WhatsApp, smart home tools, and more.

> “Gemini Apps can connect to other apps to complete your request and give you more helpful responses. With your permission, Gemini can also help you with your info and content in other apps.”

---

## What You Can Ask Gemini to Do with Connected Apps

Gemini can help across several categories:

### Productivity

- Summarize emails from Gmail.
- Create events in Google Calendar or third-party calendar apps.
- Help with code from GitHub.
- Interact with Google Workspace apps such as Docs, Drive, Keep, and Tasks.

### Media

- Play music from YouTube Music or Spotify.
- Search Google Photos or Samsung Gallery.

### Communication

- Make calls and send messages using default Android apps.
- Send messages through WhatsApp on Android devices.
- Find and manage details about people using Contacts.

### Device Control

- Manage smart home devices through Google Home.
- Use Android Utilities to:
  - Set alarms and timers.
  - Open websites, apps, and settings.
  - Check and control device features.

---

## Important Account and Availability Notes

### Personal vs. Work or School Accounts

This help page is for users accessing Gemini Apps with a **personal Google Account**.

> **Important for users with a work or school account:** The info below is for users who access Gemini Apps with a personal account. If you’re using a work or school account, learn how to use Connected Apps in Gemini with a work and school Google Account.

### Google Messages Limitation

> **Important:** For now, Gemini can’t use Connected Apps in Gemini in Google Messages.

### Availability Varies

Connected Apps availability depends on:

- Location
- Language
- Device
- Gemini app or platform
- Country
- Specific app support

> **Important:** Availability of Connected Apps vary by location, language, device, and Gemini app.

Apps available only on Android may not appear in Connected Apps settings on iOS or the Gemini web app.

---

## Getting Started with Connected Apps

To use Connected Apps:

1. **Sign in to Gemini Apps.**
   - You must be signed in to use Connected Apps.

2. **Check your Keep Activity setting.**
   - Connected Apps depend heavily on whether Gemini’s **Keep Activity** setting is on.

3. **Choose which apps Gemini connects to.**
   - Apps can be connected or disconnected anytime in Connected Apps settings.

### Keep Activity Behavior

> “When Keep Activity is off, Connected Apps won’t be available on gemini.google.com, iOS devices, or smart watches.”

On Android devices, if Keep Activity is off, only these apps remain available:

- Utilities
- Phone
- Messages
- WhatsApp

---

## How to Use Connected Apps on Computer

To use Connected Apps in Gemini web:

1. Go to [gemini.google.com](https://gemini.google.com/).
2. Enter your question or prompt in the text box.
3. To specify an app, type:

```text
@
```

Then select the app.

4. If the selected app is not connected:
   - Gemini may connect it automatically, or
   - Gemini may ask for permission.
5. Click Submit.
6. Follow any on-screen instructions.

> “If an app is available, Gemini automatically uses it.”

---

## Browsing Available Connected Apps

To view available apps:

1. Go to [gemini.google.com](https://gemini.google.com/).
2. At the bottom, click:
   - **Settings & help**
   - **Connected Apps**

If “Connected Apps” does not appear:

- Click **Personal Intelligence**
- Then click **Connected Apps**

You can also browse directly:

- [Browse Connected Apps](https://gemini.google.com/apps)

---

## Managing Which Apps Gemini Works With

You can connect or disconnect apps anytime.

### Steps on Computer

1. Go to [gemini.google.com](https://gemini.google.com/).
2. Click:
   - **Settings & help**
   - **Connected Apps**

   If not visible:
   - Click **Personal Intelligence**
   - Then **Connected Apps**

3. Find the app you want to manage.
4. Toggle the app:
   - Turn it on to connect.
   - Turn it off to disconnect.
5. Follow any on-screen instructions.

### Data and Privacy Notes

Google points users to the Gemini Apps Privacy Hub for:

- What happens when an app is disconnected.
- How data is handled when using Connected Apps.

---

## Apps Gemini Automatically Uses Public Information From

Gemini automatically uses **public information** from certain Google services, including:

- Google Search
- Google Flights
- Google Hotels
- Google Maps
- YouTube

> “Gemini won’t ac

[... summary truncated for context management ...]

# Document 407: Use Mind Maps in NotebookLM - NotebookLM Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/notebooklm/answer/16212283?hl=en
Product area: notebooklm
Original file: docs/use-mind-maps-in-notebooklm-notebooklm-help-db40ad20.md

# Use Mind Maps in NotebookLM — Summary

**Source:** [NotebookLM Help](https://support.google.com/notebooklm/answer/16212283?hl=en)  
**Topic:** How to generate, use, save/share, and provide feedback on Mind Maps in NotebookLM.

---

## Key Excerpts

> **Important:** The NotebookLM mobile app does not support this feature at this time.

> Mind Maps in NotebookLM visually summarize your uploaded sources, showing main topics and related ideas as a branching diagram.

> **Important:** NotebookLM can be inaccurate; please double check its responses.

---

## What Mind Maps Do

Mind Maps in NotebookLM create a **visual branching diagram** from your uploaded sources. They are designed to help users quickly understand source material by showing:

- Main topics
- Related ideas
- Subtopics
- Connections between concepts

---

## When to Use Mind Maps

Use Mind Maps to:

- **Get a quick overview**  
  Quickly understand the big picture and key themes in your sources.

- **Explore and learn new things easily**  
  Use the map as an entry point into unfamiliar topics before reading details.

- **Connect the dots**  
  Visually identify relationships, links, and associations that may not be obvious.

- **Organize your thoughts**  
  Structure information to improve understanding and retention.

---

## How to Generate a Mind Map

1. Open an existing notebook in [NotebookLM](https://notebooklm.google.com/).
   - Or create a new notebook and upload sources.
2. In the chat, select the **Mind Map** chip.
3. In the **Studio** panel, find the newly generated Mind Map note.
   - You can revisit the Mind Map at any time.
4. To regenerate a Mind Map:
   - Select **More** / the three-dot menu.
   - Choose **Delete note**.
   - This restarts the generation process.

---

## How to Interact with a Mind Map

Available interactions include:

- **Zoom in or out and scroll**  
  Navigate around the Mind Map and focus on specific areas.

- **Expand or collapse branches**  
  Expand branches to see detailed subtopics or collapse them for a higher-level overview.

- **Select nodes to ask questions**  
  Click a node to ask NotebookLM chat questions about that specific topic.

- **Use top-right controls**  
  Options in the top-right corner let you:
  - Expand or collapse the view
  - Download the Mind Map
  - Exit the Mind Map view

---

## How to Save and Share a Mind Map

There are two sharing options:

### Option 1: Share the Entire Notebook

Generate a Mind Map, then share the notebook with another user.  
They can load the same Mind Map from the **Studio** panel.

### Option 2: Download the Mind Map

Select **Download** inside the Mind Map window, then share the downloaded file.

---

## Mind Map Usage Limits

Usage limits apply to Mind Map creation and are subject to change:

| Plan | Daily Mind Map Limit |
|---|---:|
| **NotebookLM Standard** | 10/day |
| **NotebookLM in Plus** | 20/day |
| **NotebookLM in Pro** | 100/day |
| **NotebookLM in Ultra — 20 TB Plan** | 500/day |
| **NotebookLM in Ultra — 30 TB Plan** | 1,000/day |

---

## Feedback Options

Users can provide feedback on generated Mind Maps:

- Select **Good content** if the Mind Map was helpful.
- Select **Bad content** if the Mind Map was:
  - Unhelpful
  - Inaccurate
  - Biased
  - Otherwise problematic

When marking content as bad, users are encouraged to describe their experience and provide additional feedback.

---

## Important Limitations and Notes

- Mind Maps are **not currently supported in the NotebookLM mobile app**.
- NotebookLM-generated content may be inaccurate, so users should **double-check responses and outputs**.
- Mind Map usage limits vary by NotebookLM plan and may change over time.

---

## Related Resources

- [Create a notebook in NotebookLM](https://support.google.com/notebooklm/answer/16206563)
- [Add or discover new sources for your Notebook](https://support.google.com/notebooklm/answer/16215270)
- [Use chat in NotebookLM](https://support.google.com/notebooklm/answer/16179559)

---

# Document 408: Use NotebookLM with a work or school Google account - NotebookLM Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/notebooklm/answer/16337734?hl=en
Product area: notebooklm
Original file: docs/use-notebooklm-with-a-work-or-school-google-account-notebooklm-help-5af9d668.md

# Use NotebookLM with a Work or School Google Account — Summary

**Source:** [NotebookLM Help](https://support.google.com/notebooklm/answer/16337734?hl=en)

## Overview

Most users with a **work or school Google Account** can access NotebookLM. However, **features, usage limits, security/privacy protections, and data handling depend on the organization’s Google Workspace license**.

> For Workspace and Workspace for Education users, NotebookLM is now a **core service** — similar to Gmail, Google Calendar, and Docs.

---

## How to Upgrade NotebookLM Access

To get **higher usage limits** and **additional features** with a work or school account:

- Your organization must upgrade to a **Google Workspace plan that includes higher NotebookLM limits**.
- Users should contact their **Google Workspace administrator** to request an upgraded license.
- Google Workspace admins can learn how to upgrade their organization’s license via Google’s admin help documentation.

---

## NotebookLM Access Levels by Workspace Edition

NotebookLM availability and limits vary by edition.

### Standard Access to Features

NotebookLM is available as an **additional service** with:

- Business Base
- Essentials Starter
- Workspace Individual
- G Suite
- Cloud Identity
- Chrome Enterprise
- Android Enterprise

### Standard Access with Enterprise-Grade Security & Privacy

NotebookLM is available as a **core service** with:

- Business Starter
- Enterprise Essentials
- Enterprise Essentials Plus
- Frontline Starter
- Frontline Standard
- Frontline Plus
- Nonprofits
- Education Fundamentals
- Education Standard

### More Access with Enterprise-Grade Security & Privacy

NotebookLM is available as a **core service** with:

- Education Plus
- Teaching and Learning add-on

### Higher Level Access with Enterprise-Grade Security & Privacy

NotebookLM is available as a **core service** with:

- Business Plus
- Business Standard
- Enterprise Plus
- Enterprise Standard
- Gemini for Business / Enterprise *(no longer available for purchase)*
- Google AI Pro for Education

### Expanded Level Access with Enterprise-Grade Security & Privacy

NotebookLM is available as an **add-on** with:

- AI Expanded Access

### Highest Level Access with Enterprise-Grade Security & Privacy

NotebookLM is available as an **add-on** with:

- AI Ultra Access

---

## Terms of Service & Data Handling

### Terms of Service

Applicable terms vary by edition:

- **Standard Access**
  - [Google Terms of Service](https://policies.google.com/terms)

- **Education editions**
  - [Google Workspace for Education Terms of Service](https://workspace.google.com/terms/education_terms/)

- **Workspace editions**
  - [Google Cloud Terms of Service](https://workspace.google.com/terms/premier_terms/)

---

## Data Protections

### Standard Access

> Your data is protected, and is not used to train NotebookLM unless you provide feedback.

If users submit feedback:

> When you share feedback to help us improve, we may review the full context of that interaction, including your queries, uploads, and the model’s responses.

Google may also process data:

> To ensure safety and reliability, we may process your data to prevent fraud, abuse, and technical issues.

### Enterprise-Grade Security & Privacy

For editions with enterprise-grade protections:

> Your uploaded files, chats and model outputs won’t be reviewed by human reviewers or used to improve generative AI models.

Additional privacy references:

- **Workspace:** Google Cloud Privacy Policy describes how Google Cloud handles NotebookLM data.
- **Education:** Google Workspace for Education Privacy Notice and Privacy Center describe how Google Cloud handles NotebookLM data.

---

> Usage limits are **subject to change**.

| Feature / Limit | Standard Access | More Access | Higher Level Access | Expanded Level Access | Highest Level Access |

| **Flashcards** | 10/day | 20/day | 100/day | 200/day | 1K/day |
| **Quizzes** | 10/day | 20/day | 100/day | 200/day | 1K/day |
| **Mind Maps** | 10/day | 20/day | 100/day | 200/day | 1K/day |
| **Deep Research** | 10/month | 3/day | 20/day | 30/day | 200/day |
| **Data Tables** | Limited | More Limits | Higher Limits | Expanded Limits | Highest Limits |
| **Infographics** | Limited | More Limits | Higher Limits | Expanded Limits | Highest Limits |
| **Slide Decks and Revisions** | Limited | More Limits | Higher Limits | Expanded Limits | Highest Limits,
