# Document 428: Welcome to Beyond the prompt : Our new series on getting the most out of Gemini for Workspace

Source URL: https://workspace.google.com/blog/ai-and-machine-learning/welcome-to-beyond-the-prompt?hl=en
Product area: workspace-site
Original file: docs/welcome-to-beyond-the-prompt-our-new-series-on-getting-the-most-out-of-gemini-fo-adb5b38b.md

# Welcome to Beyond the prompt : Our new series on getting the most out of Gemini for Workspace

Source: https://workspace.google.com/blog/ai-and-machine-learning/welcome-to-beyond-the-prompt?hl=en

AI and Machine Learning

# Welcome to Beyond the prompt: Our new series on getting the most out of Gemini for Workspace

April 19, 2024

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Beyond_the_prompt_Image_1_Blog_header_2436.max-2500x2500.jpg](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Beyond_the_prompt_Image_1_Blog_header_2436.max-2500x2500.jpg)

##### The Google Workspace Team

##### Google Workspace Newsletter

Keep up with the evolving future of work and collaboration with insights, trends, and product news.

[SIGN UP](https://workspace.google.com/?source=gafb-blog_article-body-en#newsletter)

Embracing a new technology and a new way of working can feel both exciting and challenging. This new series is designed to help you get started — and inspired — with generative AI. We’ll regularly post short tips, tricks, and suggestions on how to improve your use of [Gemini for Google Workspace](https://workspace.google.com/blog/product-announcements/gemini-for-google-workspace) to increase your productivity, creativity, and quality of work. 

From the very beginning, Google Workspace was built with collaboration at its core. Whether it’s in a shared Google Doc or a quick sync over Google Meet, Workspace helps you collaborate in real time and asynchronously with other people so you can do your best work.

Now, you can also collaborate with AI using Gemini for Workspace to help you write, organize, visualize, accelerate workflows, have richer meetings, and much more — all while using your favorite apps like Gmail, Docs, Sheets, Meet, Slides, and soon, [Google Vids](https://youtu.be/4SCjXcBeW1E). So let’s get started with our first tip.

### What’s in a prompt?

To realize the full benefits of generative AI, you need to write effective prompts. But what exactly is a prompt? You can think of it as a conversation starter with your AI-powered assistant. Like any good conversation, you might have follow up questions, requests, and thoughts.

This in turn might lead you to write several prompts as the conversation progresses. While the possibilities are virtually endless, there are consistent best practices that you can put to use today.

There are four main areas to consider when writing an effective prompt. You don’t need to use all four, but using a few will help! When crafting prompts, aim to include the following:

- **Persona** — Who is asking for the information?
- **Task** — What do you need Gemini for Workspace to do for you?
- **Context**— Any additional information Gemini could use to generate a more specific response
- **Format** — How do you want Gemini to format its output (in bullet points, images in a specific style, etc.)?

With these four areas, the possibilities are virtually endless.

Let’s look at an example prompt.

Imagine you’re writing a travel blog and need an image to accompany your post. You open a new Google Slide and you prompt Gemini in Slides by selecting *Create image with Gemini*.

In Slides, go to: Insert→Image→Create image with Gemini.

Once you see the area below, type in your prompt:  
Create an image of a plane flying above the clouds over mountains and rivers during sunrise that I can use in a blog to promote my travel company.

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Screenshot_2024-04-16_at_2.48.26PM_OTWXuDA.max-600x600.png](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Screenshot_2024-04-16_at_2.48.26PM_OTWXuDA.max-600x600.png)

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Screenshot_2024-04-16_at_2.48.26PM_OTWXuDA.max-600x600.png](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Screenshot_2024-04-16_at_2.48.26PM_OTWXuDA.max-600x600.png)

Putting our prompt to work in Slides

Gemini generates images and now you can simply select your favorite and insert it into your Slide.

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/travel_blog_image_of_plane.max-600x600.jpeg](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/travel_blog_image_of_plane.max-600x600.jpeg)

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/travel_blog_image_of_plane.max-600x600.jpeg](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/travel_blog_image_of_plane.max-600x600.jpeg)

Here’s our favorite image for the travel blog.

Generative AI and all of its possibilities are exciting, but it’s still new. The example prompts in this series are meant for illustrative purposes. Remember that responses will vary from one user to another, and from one prompt to another.

If you're ready for the next post in the series, you can find Part 2 [here](https://workspace.google.com/blog/ai-and-machine-learning/beyond-prompt-4-tips-boost-collaboration-gemini). Or you can catch up on Parts [3](https://workspace.google.com/blog/ai-and-machine-learning/beyond-prompt-part-3-rethinking-prompt-engineering), [4](https://workspace.google.com/blog/ai-and-machine-learning/beyond-prompt-part-4-using-gemini-workspace-your-sales-research-assistant), [5](https://workspace.google.com/blog/ai-and-machine-learning/beyond-prompt-part-5-marketers-conquer-blank-page), and [6](https://workspace.google.com/blog/ai-and-machine-learning/beyond-prompt-part-6-personalize-customer-service-responses). Or consider downloading the [Prompting guide 101 ebook](https://inthecloud.withgoogle.com/gemini-for-google-workspace-prompt-guide/dl-cd.html) and [get started with Gemini for Workspace today](https://workspace.google.com/solutions/ai/).

# Document 429: Welcome to Gemini in Chrome - Chrome Enterprise and Education Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/chrome/a/answer/17031592
Product area: chrome-enterprise-ai
Original file: docs/welcome-to-gemini-in-chrome-chrome-enterprise-and-education-help-1264def9.md

[Skip to main content](https://support.google.com/chrome/a/answer/17031592#search-form)

## Get started with Gemini in Chrome

# Welcome to Gemini in Chrome

[Previous: Gemini in Chrome Learning Center](https://support.google.com/chrome/a/answer/17034491?hl=en&ref_topic=17031489)[Next: What you'll need](https://support.google.com/chrome/a/answer/17031593?hl=en&ref_topic=17031489)

![](https://storage.googleapis.com/support-kms-prod/87a2a960-8c17-4849-922e-e991ef3766f9)

Gemini in Chrome provides AI assistance directly in your Chrome browser. Based on the context of your open tabs, Gemini in Chrome can summarize content, extract insights from videos, help you troubleshoot issues in real time, and much more. With auto browse, Gemini in Chrome can help you complete complex multi-step tasks.

**Note:** The instructions in this guide are for computers—MacOS, Windows, or Chromebook Plus devices.

## Features

![](https://storage.googleapis.com/support-kms-prod/mxcfaH9F3EV18pEMyhFNrn5CDdRDL6OkQBr6)

#### Gemini in Chrome (Side panel)

Gemini in Chrome lives in a side panel within Chrome browser and provides context-aware help, based on your open tabs. It also connects with your Google Workspace—Gmail, Calendar, Drive, and so on.

**Useful for:** Summarizing long complex articles, summarizing trends from log data across up to 10 open tabs/reports, and drafting emails or documents using information from the page that you’re currently viewing.

![](https://storage.googleapis.com/support-kms-prod/4gByRAmJildHjKTxmOR3fDlMKrNHuwEa2j4Q)

#### Auto browse

Auto browse lets Gemini in Chrome directly navigate and interact with websites to carry out complex multi-step tasks. Gemini in Chrome can click buttons, fill forms, and navigate websites on your behalf.

**Useful for:** Conducting consumer research, finding and booking flights and accommodation for a conference, finding and saving trip expense receipts from your email as PDFs in a specific Drive folder.

# Document 430: What chess can teach leaders about AI enablement

Source URL: https://workspace.google.com/blog/ai-and-machine-learning/what-chess-can-teach-leaders-about-ai-enablement?hl=en
Product area: workspace-site
Original file: docs/what-chess-can-teach-leaders-about-ai-enablement-52c2a893.md

# What chess can teach leaders about AI enablement

Source: https://workspace.google.com/blog/ai-and-machine-learning/what-chess-can-teach-leaders-about-ai-enablement?hl=en

AI and Machine Learning

# What chess can teach leaders about AI enablement

April 10, 2026

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/5522f_GWS_What_chess_can_teach_leaders_Blo.max-2500x2500.jpg](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/5522f_GWS_What_chess_can_teach_leaders_Blo.max-2500x2500.jpg)

##### Pat McCarthy

VP of Google Workspace Sales

##### Try Google Workspace at No Cost

Get a business email, all the storage you need, video conferencing, and more.

[SIGN UP](https://workspace.google.com/business/signup/welcome?source=gafb-blog_article-body-en)

In business, as in chess, we’re taught that success comes down to thinking multiple moves ahead, anticipating your opponent’s next move, and seeing the whole board. For a long time, we’ve viewed the ability to master that board as the ultimate symbol of human intellect.

All of that changed in 1997 when early AI, dubbed Deep Blue, defeated Gary Kasparov, the reigning world champion of chess. A machine outplayed the best player in the world, causing many to predict that people would lose interest in chess.

Was this “checkmate” for human chess players? Not even close.

The opposite happened: Chess is more popular today than at any other time in history. World Chess Championships attract tens of millions of viewers, while AI vs. AI matches draw a tiny niche audience.

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/5522f_GWS_What_chess_can_teach_leaders_inl.max-2000x2000.png](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/5522f_GWS_What_chess_can_teach_leaders_inl.max-2000x2000.png)

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/5522f_GWS_What_chess_can_teach_leaders_inl.max-2000x2000.png](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/5522f_GWS_What_chess_can_teach_leaders_inl.max-2000x2000.png)

Not only that, but today’s chess players are playing at a higher level than 30 years ago, with more than three times as many grandmasters. This is largely thanks to the analysis and training methods made possible by AI.

What AI has done for chess — elevating the game without replacing the players — it’s now doing for business at scale. When leaders embrace AI thoughtfully and strategically across their organizations, they can help every employee outdo themselves, becoming grandmasters in their own right, whether they pull insights from a spreadsheet, talk to customers, or present to the board.

### **Developing a dual-track strategy for AI enablement**

To win at chess, you have to be good at the small, incremental gains as well as the sudden combinations that change the board. In chess we call the first capability “positional play” and the second “tactical play.” As a metaphor for AI enablement, this works well.

**Positional play** means equipping every employee with AI tools that make their daily tasks easier and faster. It’s about optimizing existing workflows and consolidating productivity gains. Examples of this are [all around us](https://workspace.google.com/blog/ai-and-machine-learning/how-our-customers-transform-work-with-ai?e=48754805). It might be a medical clinic using Gemini in Gmail to explain medical terms in patient emails, a global technology company using NotebookLM to make performance reviews 90% more efficient, or a fashion retailer reducing the time it takes to create engaging web product copy from 30+ hours to 30 minutes per month. All of these come from real-world use cases our customers have embraced.

These kinds of daily, incremental gains create a solid foundation for boosting productivity across the entire organization. They also boost employee confidence, satisfaction, and engagement, according to [our recent global survey](https://workspace.google.com/learning/report/content/beyond-ai-optimism?e=48754805#from-saving-time-to-expanding-potential-8) of knowledge workers and business decision makers. Organizations that are highly transformed by AI prove that the biggest gains from adopting AI aren’t about saving time — they’re about expanding potential.

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/5522f_GWS_What_chess_can_teach_leaders_inl.max-2000x2000_fVHuh5z.png](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/5522f_GWS_What_chess_can_teach_leaders_inl.max-2000x2000_fVHuh5z.png)

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/5522f_GWS_What_chess_can_teach_leaders_inl.max-2000x2000_fVHuh5z.png](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/5522f_GWS_What_chess_can_teach_leaders_inl.max-2000x2000_fVHuh5z.png)

**Tactical play** is about driving game-changing impact. It means reinventing how the company operates instead of optimizing existing workflows. A few years into the AI revolution, this is less common. In fact, in that same survey, only 3% of organizations have truly transformed through AI. But they’re seeing outsized results: they report more than double the innovation, creativity, and ability to accelerate new products and services to market.

So why aren’t there more tactical chess moves happening? Because that 3% figure reflects the limitations of the recent past, an era of siloed productivity gains driven by chatbots. We are now entering the era of the agentic workplace. Instead of just embedding AI into existing workflows — that email to a customer or slide deck for the CEO — entire workflows can be reimagined with agents.

### **Why shared context is key to agentic transformation**

AI agents pursue goals rather than just answering prompts. Imagine an agent that aggregates customer sentiment across all your channels, recommends solutions, builds the strategy deck for your review, and projects the bottom-line impact. This represents a true human-AI collaboration at scale, fundamentally changing how we move across the board.

However, this vision is often held back by a lack of shared context.

When agents bounce between dozens of disconnected applications to find relevant data and act on it, they often hit a "context wall." They’re unable to travel with the context they need, grinding productivity to a halt. This affects humans too: if your email can’t talk to your file system, your AI-generated insights and outputs remain limited. To break through these silos, enterprises need a unified, secure platform where humans and agents can collaborate across all application boundaries.

### **Winning at AI adoption**

To win at chess — and AI adoption — you have to be able to play positionally and tactically at the same time, and the two modes of play are deeply intertwined. It’s your daily, positional investments that actually create the opportunities for those tactical strikes down the line. Good tactics flow from a good position on the board. For the 3% of organizations that are truly transformed by AI, it’s no accident that 74% of them continuously refine AI frameworks to optimize workflows over time. Like any grandmaster, they’re constantly reassessing the board.

Posted in

- [AI and Machine Learning](https://workspace.google.com/blog/ai-and-machine-learning)
- [Productivity and Collaboration](https://workspace.google.com/blog/productivity-collaboration)

[![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/5522i_GWS_AI_small_business_advantage_Blog_h.max-700x700.jpg](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/5522i_GWS_AI_small_business_advantage_Blog_h.max-700x700.jpg)

### How AI is giving small businesses a major advantage

# Document 431: What you can do with Google Workspace Studio - Google Workspace Learning Center

Source URL: https://support.google.com/a/users/answer/16430812
Product area: workspace-studio
Original file: docs/what-you-can-do-with-google-workspace-studio-google-workspace-learning-center-984b5c8d.md

# What You Can Do with Google Workspace Studio — Summary

**Source:** Google Workspace Learning Center  
**Article:** “What you can do with Google Workspace Studio”  
**URL:** https://support.google.com/a/users/answer/16430812

---

## Core Description

> **Google Workspace Studio is an online app that lets you automate your everyday, routine tasks across Google Workspace services with the help of Gemini—no programming required.**

Google Workspace Studio helps users create automated workflows, called **flows**, across Google Workspace apps. It is designed for non-programmers and uses **Gemini** to assist with automation.

---

## Main Use Case

Google Workspace Studio is for automating **everyday, routine tasks** across Google Workspace services.

Key points:

- Works online through a web app.
- Uses **Gemini** to help create automations.
- Does **not** require programming.
- Automations are referred to as **flows**.
- Intended for use with a Google Workspace account.

---

## In This Guide

The article is part of the **Get started with Workspace Studio** guide and points users to the following next steps:

1. **Create your first flow**
   - Link: https://support.google.com/a/users/answer/16430397

2. **Share your flows**
   - Link: https://support.google.com/a/users/answer/16431116

3. **Edit and manage your flows**
   - Link: https://support.google.com/a/users/answer/16433729

The next recommended article is:

> **Next: Create your first flow in Google Workspace Studio**

---

## What You’ll Need

To use Google Workspace Studio, you need:

| Requirement | Details |
|---|---|
| **An idea for a flow** | You should have an idea for a useful automation or workflow. |
| **Google Workspace access** | You need access to Workspace Studio through a **Google Workspace account**. If you don’t have access, contact your administrator. |
| **Supported computer/browser** | You need a computer with a supported web browser. |
| **No mobile support currently** | Workspace Studio **isn't available on mobile devices at this time**. |
| **Gemini access for AI features** | To use generative AI features, your Google Account must be allowed access to **Gemini and its features**. |

---

## Important Access Notes

- Workspace Studio requires a **Google Workspace account**.
- Access may depend on your organization’s settings.
- If Workspace Studio is unavailable, users should contact their Google Workspace administrator.
- Generative AI features require the account to be permitted to use **Gemini**.

Relevant help link:

- **Contact your administrator:** https://support.google.com/a/users/answer/6208960

---

## Device and Browser Requirements

Workspace Studio is currently intended for desktop use.

Key limitation:

> **Workspace Studio isn't available on mobile devices at this time.**

Users need:

- A **computer**
- A **supported web browser**

Supported browser help link:

- https://support.google.com/docs/answer/2375082

---

## Related Topics

The article links to additional Workspace Studio resources:

- **Google Workspace Studio cheat sheet**  
  https://support.google.com/a/users/answer/16430708

- **Troubleshoot issues with flows**  
  https://support.google.com/a/users/answer/16430806

---

## Additional Learning Center Resource

Google also links to a guide for saving or customizing Learning Center content:

- **Print, save, or customize Learning Center guides**  
  https://support.google.com/a/users/answer/9343015

Description:

> Learn how to print Learning Center guides, save them as PDFs, or customize them for your organization.

---

## Trademark Notice

> _Google, Google Workspace, and related marks and logos are trademarks of Google LLC. All other company and product names are trademarks of the companies with which they are associated._

---

## Quick Takeaway

Google Workspace Studio lets Google Workspace users create automated workflows across Workspace apps using Gemini, without writing code. To use it, you need a Workspace account, desktop browser access, administrator-enabled availability, and Gemini access for generative AI features.

# Document 432: What you'll learn - Google Workspace Learning Center

Source URL: https://support.google.com/a/users/answer/14505994
Product area: workspace-learning-center
Original file: docs/what-you-ll-learn-google-workspace-learning-center-1be8abae.md

# Google Workspace Learning Center: “What you’ll learn” — Summary

**Source:** [Google Workspace Learning Center](https://support.google.com/a/users/answer/14505994)  
**Guide:** *Get started with Gemini for Google Workspace*  
**Article title:** *What you’ll learn*

---

## Key Excerpts

> _You can use this feature only if your organization supports it. For help, contact [your administrator](https://support.google.com/a/users/answer/6208960)._

> Learn how to use Google Workspace with Gemini to draft new content quickly, improve your writing, summarize content, create custom graphics, and more.

> **Note**: The instructions in this guide are for computer only.

> Google Workspace account **— [Need an account? Start your 14-day trial today.](https://workspace.google.com/business/signup/welcome?utm_source=gwslearningcenter&utm_medium=support&utm_campaign=14505994)**

> Want advanced Google Workspace features for your business?  
> [Try Google Workspace today!](https://workspace.google.com/business/signup/welcome?utm_source=gwslearningcenter&utm_medium=support&utm_campaign=14505994)

---

## Main Purpose of the Guide

This Google Workspace Learning Center guide introduces users to **Gemini for Google Workspace** and explains what they can learn to do with Gemini inside Workspace apps.

The guide focuses on using Gemini to:

- **Draft new content quickly**
- **Improve and polish writing**
- **Summarize content**
- **Organize data**
- **Create custom graphics**
- **Illustrate presentations**
- **Enhance meeting experiences**

---

## Availability & Requirements

### Feature Availability

Gemini for Google Workspace is only available if the user’s organization supports it.

- If Gemini is unavailable, users should contact their **Google Workspace administrator**.
- The guide is intended for organizations using supported Google Workspace plans/features.

### Platform Scope

- The instructions in this guide are for **computer only**.
- Mobile instructions are not covered in this specific guide.

### Required Account

Users need a:

- **Google Workspace account**

Google also promotes a business trial:

- **14-day Google Workspace trial** available via the linked signup page.

---

## Guide Sections / Learning Path

The article serves as an overview page for a Gemini onboarding guide. It links to the following lessons:

1. **[Start with a great prompt](https://support.google.com/a/users/answer/14590328)**
   - Learn how to write effective prompts for Gemini.

2. **[Write a quick first draft](https://support.google.com/a/users/answer/14747862)**
   - Use Gemini to quickly generate initial drafts of content.

3. **[Improve & polish your writing](https://support.google.com/a/users/answer/14512929)**
   - Refine, rewrite, or enhance existing writing.

4. **[Summarize content & organize data](https://support.google.com/a/users/answer/14506398)**
   - Use Gemini to condense information and help structure data.

5. **[Illustrate a presentation](https://support.google.com/a/users/answer/14514640)**
   - Create or enhance presentation visuals with Gemini.

6. **[Create a custom background & enhance your meeting experience](https://support.google.com/a/users/answer/14638683)**
   - Use Gemini-related features to improve meetings, including custom backgrounds.

The next article in the sequence is:

- **[Next: Start with a great prompt](https://support.google.com/a/users/answer/14590328?hl=en&ref_topic=14506902)**

---

# Document 436: Working at the speed of fashion trends with Duet AI

Source URL: https://workspace.google.com/blog/ai-and-machine-learning/working-speed-fashion-trends-duet-ai?hl=en
Product area: workspace-site
Original file: docs/working-at-the-speed-of-fashion-trends-with-duet-ai-6fc625d1.md

# Working at the speed of fashion trends with Duet AI

Source: https://workspace.google.com/blog/ai-and-machine-learning/working-speed-fashion-trends-duet-ai?hl=en

AI and Machine Learning

# Working at the speed of fashion trends with Duet AI

November 16, 2023

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Adore_Me_Ranjan_Roy_Blogheader_2436x1200.max-2500x2500.jpg](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Adore_Me_Ranjan_Roy_Blogheader_2436x1200.max-2500x2500.jpg)

##### Ranjan Roy

VP of Strategy, Adore Me

##### Google Workspace Newsletter

Keep up with the evolving future of work and collaboration with insights, trends, and product news.

[SIGN UP](https://workspace.google.com/?source=gafb-blog_article-body-en#newsletter)

Editorial note: Duet AI for Google Workspace is now Gemini for Google Workspace. [Learn more](https://workspace.google.com/blog/product-announcements/gemini-for-google-workspace). This post also appears in our LinkedIn newsletter [AI@Work with Google Workspace](https://www.linkedin.com/newsletters/ai-work-with-google-workspace-7087479673143656448/).

---

[Adore Me](https://www.adoreme.com/) is an inclusive intimate apparel company with a mission to celebrate women for who they are. Our global teams use Google Workspace to connect and collaborate in service of that mission, creating products and experiences that support women through every stage of life.

As the Vice President of Strategy, I’m responsible for streamlining operations and enabling our team to quickly bring products to market while never losing sight of our customer’s needs. When we joined the Trusted Tester program for Duet AI in Workspace, I was hopeful it would create efficiencies. Now I can say it has truly supercharged how we work.

Generative AI in Gmail, Google Docs, and Google Slides has not only accelerated our product launches, but has also enhanced customer service and made our internal communications more inclusive.

### Working at the speed of fashion trends — from 40 hours to one

One of Adore Me’s priorities is to consistently launch new products that reflect the diversity of our customers. We often release more than 80 items per month, and it’s important that each one has a unique product description.

Each of our copywriters used to spend 30-40 hours per month writing those descriptions, but AI cuts that time down to about an hour. We use [Vertex AI](https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai?hl=en) from Google Cloud to develop useful first drafts that our writers finesse before sending to our development team. This frees up our writers to spend more time on pie-in-the-sky ideation. Meanwhile, Duet AI helps us quickly update our website and mobile apps with new products and features by giving our developers and UX designers the ability to draft their own copy.

We've also been able to speed up the planning phase of our campaigns with [help me organize](https://support.google.com/docs/answer/13676332?hl=en&sjid=8564469467013962035-NA). By entering a simple prompt like, “Create a project plan for a full-funnel marketing campaign around a spring product launch,” we get a first draft of a plan in Sheets to kickstart the project. And the results get better with every prompt. If the initial Sheet is missing a variable, we add feedback and Duet AI makes the change for us. This collaborative process helps us work even faster next time.

### Exceed customer expectations

Adore Me is always looking for new ways to connect with women, both through our styles and our customer experience. In a recent panel at [Google Cloud Next](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=fp1t_SOw0sA), we highlighted our customer service team’s new initiative: using generative AI to craft personalized stylist notes based on individual preferences, purchase history, and product reviews. This helps our customers feel even more seen and valued.

### Make communication more inclusive

With employees worldwide, Adore Me uses Duet AI’s real-time [translations in Google Meet](https://workspace.google.com/blog/product-announcements/duet-ai-in-workspace-now-available) to break down language barriers and create a more inclusive workplace. That way, employees can focus on sharing their perspective instead of translating their thoughts. And even if someone can’t attend a meeting, they can stay informed. Meet automatically transcribes the conversation into a Doc, which Duet AI summarizes in just a few seconds.

### Making room for what matters

Adore Me is excited to continue using Duet AI to work smarter and create more time to prioritize what matters most to us: our customers. From operations and internal communication to marketing and customer service, Duet AI is permanently transforming the way we fulfill our mission.

Posted in

- [AI and Machine Learning](https://workspace.google.com/blog/ai-and-machine-learning)
- [Productivity and Collaboration](https://workspace.google.com/blog/productivity-collaboration)

# Document 437: Workspace Drop: New easy ways to create and share videos with AI in Google Vids

# Workspace Drop: New easy ways to create and share videos with AI in Google Vids

Source: https://workspace.google.com/blog/product-announcements/workspace-drops-create-and-share-videos-with-ai-in-google-vids?hl=en

Product Announcements

# Workspace Drop: New easy ways to create and share videos with AI in Google Vids

April 3, 2026

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Workspace_drops_hero_image.max-1300x1300_1.max-1300x1300.png](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Workspace_drops_hero_image.max-1300x1300_1.max-1300x1300.png)

##### The Google Workspace Team

##### Try Google Workspace at No Cost

Get a business email, all the storage you need, video conferencing, and more.

[SIGN UP](https://workspace.google.com/business/signup/welcome?source=gafb-blog_article-body-en)

Welcome to the latest edition of Workspace Drops. This month, we’re delivering enhancements to Gemini across the Workspace apps to help you:

- **Deliver on-brand videos at scale** with avatar updates and Lyria 3 in Vids.
- **Simplify meeting coordination** with group scheduling in Gmail.
- **Get in-meeting assistance in more languages** with Ask Gemini in Google Meet.
- **Resume past conversations** with Gemini in your Workspace apps.

### **Deliver on-brand videos at scale with avatar updates and Lyria 3 in Vids**

In December [we announced](https://workspaceupdates.googleblog.com/2025/12/veo-3-1-powered-avatars-google-vids.html) our most realistic and highest quality avatars yet, powered by Veo 3.1. Avatars in [Google Vids](https://docs.google.com/videos/create?usp=blog) let you scale your on-screen presence, delivering engaging and on-brand messaging without the hassle, time, or expense of traditional studio production.

**Customize avatars to match your brand:** Rolling out today, this major update lets you create custom avatars to better reflect your company’s identity and appeal to your audience. This includes fine-grained controls for your avatar’s appearance, apparel, and setting. Whether you need a distinguished avatar in a tuxedo for a formal gala invite or a relaxed speaker in a company-branded zip-up for a team all-hands, your avatars can now look the part.

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/GoogleVIds_CustomAvatars_Thumbnail.max-2000x2000.png](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/GoogleVIds_CustomAvatars_Thumbnail.max-2000x2000.png)

Create custom avatars in Google Vids.

**Direct avatars with precision:** Also rolling out today, you can now move beyond static narrators and take full directorial control to create more engaging clips of your avatar. Using Veo 3.1, you can place avatars into specific scenes and have them interact directly with objects. Upload an image of your product and a relevant backdrop to showcase your brand while maintaining a consistent face and voice across every frame. Starting today, users on Google AI Ultra or AI Ultra Access have access to [1,000 Veo generations per month](https://support.google.com/docs/answer/15609411).

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/GoogleVIds_DirectAvatars_Thumbnail_2.max-1500x1500.png](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/GoogleVIds_DirectAvatars_Thumbnail_2.max-1500x1500.png)

Direct your avatars in Google Vids.

**Connect with cartoon avatars:** Engage your audience with our new [2D and 3D cartoon-styled avatars](https://workspaceupdates.googleblog.com/2026/02/new-cartoon-avatars-in-google-vids.html). These expressive options create an approachable and relatable speaker, making them perfect for delivering lighthearted project recaps or keeping learners focused on complex educational concepts.

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/GoogleVids_CartoonAvatars.max-2200x2200.png](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/GoogleVids_CartoonAvatars.max-2200x2200.png)

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/GoogleVids_CartoonAvatars.max-2200x2200.png](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/GoogleVids_CartoonAvatars.max-2200x2200.png)

Cartoon avatars in Google Vids.

**Bridge language barriers:** To help you reach a global audience, we’ve expanded [language support for AI avatars and voiceovers](https://workspaceupdates.googleblog.com/2026/02/vids-avatars-voiceovers-new-languages.html). You can now generate these in seven additional languages: French, German, Italian, Japanese, Korean, Portuguese, and Spanish. Pitch to global clients or update a worldwide workforce in their native language with complete consistency.

**Record your screen in a snap:** Quickly record your screen from any browser window using the new [Google Vids Screen Recorder Chrome extension](https://chromewebstore.google.com/detail/google-vids-screen-record/iklnnbgdcppplombffihcijanngoeifm). Instead of having to open Google Vids to access recording functionality, you can now initiate a recording with a single click right from the Chrome extensions toolbar.

**Create custom music to match the mood of your videos:** Give your videos more energy with custom music that perfectly matches your video’s tone. Powered by Google’s advanced music generation models, [Lyria 3 and Lyria 3 Pro](https://workspaceupdates.googleblog.com/2026/03/generate-custom-music-in-google-vids-powered-by-Lyria-3-and-Lyria-3-Pro.html), you can now generate everything from short clips (around 30 seconds) to longer tracks (up to three minutes) for your projects. Whether you need an upbeat intro for a product pitch or an ambient background for a marketing recap, you can easily create a track that hits the right note.

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/26698_WS_VIDS_Lyria3_3.max-2200x2200.png](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/26698_WS_VIDS_Lyria3_3.max-2200x2200.png)

Generate custom music with Lyria 3 Pro in Google Vids.

### **Simplify meeting coordination with group scheduling in Gmail**

Finding time to meet with groups requires a lot of coordination. Previously limited to one-on-one scheduling, *Help me schedule* in Gmail can now help schedule [meetings for groups](https://workspaceupdates.googleblog.com/2026/03/use-help-me-schedule-in-gmail-to-easily-set-up-a-meeting-time-with-multiple-guests.html). When Gemini detects you’re trying to set up a meeting, it surfaces a button in your toolbar to suggest times that work for you and your colleagues. With *Help me schedule*, you can focus on preparing for your meetings instead of managing logistics.

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Copy_of_YouTubeThumbnail.max-2000x2000.png](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Copy_of_YouTubeThumbnail.max-2000x2000.png)

Coordinate meetings with multiple guests with “Help me schedule” in Gmail.

### **Get in-meeting assistance in more languages with Ask Gemini in Google Meet**

Ask Gemini in Meet is expanding [language support](https://workspaceupdates.googleblog.com/2026/01/ask-gemini-google-meet-expansion-business-standard.html) to include French, German, Italian, Japanese, Korean, Portuguese, and Spanish. Now, even more users can rely on Gemini as their personal in-meeting assistant. Whether you need to catch up on what you missed in the conversation or grab action items, Gemini answers your questions privately in your preferred language — so you can stay focused on the meeting without interrupting the flow of the conversation.

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/original_images/Gemini_in_Meet_-_Smaller_file.gif](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/original_images/Gemini_in_Meet_-_Smaller_file.gif)

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/original_images/Gemini_in_Meet_-_Smaller_file.gif](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/original_images/Gemini_in_Meet_-_Smaller_file.gif)

Use Ask Gemini in Meet in your preferred language.

### **Resume past conversations with Gemini in your Workspace apps**

Now, Gemini in the side panel of Gmail, Docs, Sheets, Slides, Drive, and Chat includes [conversation history](https://workspaceupdates.googleblog.com/2026/02/gemini-conversation-history-is-coming-to-side-panel-in-google-workspace.html), letting you easily review previous responses and pick up where you left off. Administrators retain full control to ensure data management aligns with your organization’s policies, but your chat history will remain entirely private to you within each app.

### **Get started today**

These updates are rolling out to Google Workspace customers now. As always, you can [learn more about unlocking the power of generative AI with Workspace](https://workspace.google.com/blog/).

# Document 438: Write & edit with Gemini in Docs - Google Docs Editors Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/docs/answer/13951448?hl=en
Product area: docs-editors
Original file: docs/write-edit-with-gemini-in-docs-google-docs-editors-help-4f45466b.md

# Write & Edit with Gemini in Google Docs — Summary

**Source:** Google Docs Editors Help — “Write & edit with Gemini in Docs”  
**URL:** https://support.google.com/docs/answer/13951448?hl=en

---

## Feature Availability

Gemini writing and editing in Google Docs requires an eligible plan or program:

- Eligible **Google Workspace** or **Google AI** plan  
  - See: *Get started with Google Workspace with Gemini*
- Supported languages for Google Workspace with Gemini
- **Google AI Ultra** and **Google AI Pro** personal accounts
- **Google Workspace Experiments**, a trusted tester program for personal accounts

> **This feature is currently available on desktop and in English only.**

If you don’t have access to the features described, your experience may look different.

---

## What Gemini Can Do in Docs

Gemini can help draft, edit, and refine content in Google Docs using context from:

- Your current document
- Files in Google Drive
- Google Chat
- Gmail
- The web

Key capabilities include:

- **Edit with a prompt**
  - Example:  
    > “Make the introduction more concise and professional.”

- **Refine with quick actions**
  - Highlight text and choose:
    - **Rephrase**
    - **Shorten**
    - **Elaborate**
    - **Bulletize**
    - **Summarize**
    - **More formal**
    - **More casual**

- **Edit using context from another file**
  - Use your own documents as sources to add data, evidence, and citations.

- **Use the writing style of an existing file**
  - Gemini can mirror the writing style of existing documents.

---

## Write, Edit, or Refine Text with Gemini

Gemini can understand the context of your document, so you don’t always need to select text before prompting it.

### Steps

1. Open a document in [Google Docs](https://docs.google.com/document/u/0/).
2. Click the **Gemini** icon.
3. Enter a prompt in the bottom bar.
4. To target a specific section, mention it in the prompt.

Example:

> “Add evidence to my Research section.”

To refine the entire document, use a broader prompt, such as:

> “Suggest places where I can better defend my argument.”

5. Optional: Add sources for Gemini to reference.
6. Review Gemini’s suggestions directly in the document.

### Applying or Rejecting Suggestions

- To apply changes individually, click **Accept suggestion**.
- To apply all changes, click **Accept all**.
- To reject all changes, click **Reject all**.

You can also manage Gemini conversation history in Workspace.

---

## Edit Text with One Click

Gemini offers quick editing actions through the floating toolbar.

### Steps

1. Open a document in Google Docs.
2. Select the text you want to rewrite.
3. On the floating bar, click **Refine**.
4. Choose one of the suggested actions:
   - **Rephrase**
   - **Shorten**
   - **Elaborate**
   - **More formal**
   - **More casual**
   - **Bulletize**
   - **Summarize**

### Review Options

After Gemini suggests changes:

- Click **Accept suggestion** to apply changes individually.
- Click **Accept all** to apply all changes.
- Click **Reject all** to discard them.

---

## Reference Other Files to Create Content

You can ask Gemini to use specific files when writing, editing, or refining content.

Example:

> “Update the project timeline based on the latest updates from our meeting notes doc.”

### Add Files as Sources

1. In the bottom bar, click **Sources**.
2. Choose **Add from Drive** or another location.
   - Alternatively, type `@` and search/select a file from the menu.
3. Select one or more files.
4. Click **Add**.

### Select Where Gemini Can Search

You can choose the locations where Gemini can proactively search for content related to your prompt.

---

## Example Gemini Prompts and Use Cases

| Your goal | You should use |
|---|---|
| Update a project status | “Update the timeline in this document based on the latest project email from yesterday.” |
| Change document structure | “Convert the bulleted list in the 'To do' section into a three-column table.” |
| Incorporate external data | “Add a section about our Q3 goals using information from the 'Annual Strategy' file in my Drive.” |
| Summarize a long document | Click **Refine** → **More** → **Summarize**. |
| Change the document formatting | “Make all the headers use the font Inter, 24pt, bold, in navy blue.” |
| Improve your document | “Act as a sophisticated Director of Product Management and suggest ways I can improve this doc.” |

---

## Minimize or Hide the Bottom Bar

By default, the Gemini bar automatically minimizes to clear your screen and help you focus.

To show it again:

- Hover over the **Gemini** icon at the bottom of the document.

---

## Move Gemini from Bottom Bar to Side Panel

You can move Gemini to the side panel to keep it open while working. The side panel provides a larger response display and better readability.

### Steps

1. Open a document in Google Docs.
2. Enter a prompt in the bottom bar.

Examples:

> “Generate FAQs based on recent email conversations”

> “Add 

[... summary truncated for context management ...]

---

# Document 439: Your data and using AI features on YouTube - YouTube Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/youtube/answer/14772770
Product area: youtube-ai
Original file: docs/your-data-and-using-ai-features-on-youtube-youtube-help-5ec65c95.md

# Your Data and Using AI Features on YouTube — Summary

**Source:** YouTube Help — “Your data and using AI features on YouTube”  
**URL:** https://support.google.com/youtube/answer/14772770

---

## Scope: Covered YouTube AI Features

This notice applies to use of YouTube AI features, including:

- **Veo and Gemini Omni in Shorts**
- **Nano Banana and Gemini Omni in YouTube Create**
- **Dream Track in YouTube Shorts**
- **Edit with AI**
- **Photo to video**
- **Make me move in Shorts**
- **AI Image and AI Video in Effect Maker**
- **Posts or AI Stickers**

Collectively, these are referred to as **“YouTube AI Feature(s)”**.

Feature availability may vary by **country** and **language**.

---

## Key Excerpts

> Your use of YouTube AI features [...] must comply with YouTube’s Terms of Service and Community Guidelines, and shouldn’t violate the copyrights or privacy rights of others.

> You're responsible for the content that you interact with, create and publish.

> If the content that you interact with, create and publish violates Community Guidelines, it will be removed and you may get a strike.

> Don't include confidential or personal information when creating content or interacting with YouTube AI Features and don't rely on the feature for medical, financial or legal advice.

> Nevertheless, please don’t enter confidential information (or other data you wouldn’t want a reviewer to see) in prompts.

---

## User Responsibility and Policy Compliance

When using YouTube AI Features:

- You must follow:
  - **YouTube Terms of Service**
  - **YouTube Community Guidelines**
- You must not violate:
  - Others’ **copyrights**
  - Others’ **privacy rights**
- You are responsible for:
  - Content you **interact with**
  - Content you **create**
  - Content you **publish**
- Violating Community Guidelines can result in:
  - Content removal
  - A YouTube **strike**

YouTube also warns users not to rely on these AI features for **medical, financial, or legal advice**.

---

## C2PA Manifest and Content Provenance

AI-generated content may include a **valid, signed C2PA manifest**.

If you create content by selecting the referenced AI/content provenance icon and then reuse or share that content on an external platform, the external platform may be able to access the embedded metadata.

### C2PA Standard

> This technology is based on an open standard for content origin and authenticity developed by the Coalition for Content Provenance and Authenticity (C2PA).

The C2PA standard helps users understand the **authenticity** and **origin** of media, including:

- Images
- Videos
- Audio

---

## Privacy Notice: Who Provides YouTube AI Features

YouTube AI Features are provided by:

- **Google Ireland Limited** in the **European Economic Area and Switzerland**
- **Google LLC** everywhere else

The article refers to both entities collectively as **Google**.

This privacy notice works together with Google’s broader **Privacy Policy**.

---

## What Data Google Collects

Google may collect the following when you use YouTube AI Features:

- **Prompts**
- **Outputs**
- Content you share with AI features, such as:
  - Images
  - Videos
- **Related content**
- **Feedback**

---

## How Google Uses the Data

Google may use collected AI Feature data to:

- Provide Google products and services
- Improve Google products and services
- Develop Google products and services
- Improve and develop **machine-learning technologies**

---

## Reviewing and Deleting AI Feature Activity

You can review and delete past AI Feature data in **My Activity**, including:

- Prompts
- Outputs
- Related content

If certain content cannot be managed through My Activity:

- It is handled according to Google’s Privacy Policy
- It is deleted in **up to 60 days**

---

## YouTube AI Feature Activity Retention

**YouTube AI Feature Activity is on by default.**

### Default Retention

YouTube stores AI Feature data with your YouTube Account for:

- **Up to 18 months** for:
  - Prompts
  - Outputs
  - Related content

Prompts and outputs **not connected to your Google Account** are stored for:

- **Up to 3 years**

### Data Collected Before May 8, 2025

For YouTube AI Feature prompts, outputs, and related content collected by Google **before May 8, 2025**:

- Data connected with your Google Account is saved for:
  - **Up to 60 days**
- Outputs not connected to your Google Account are stored for:
  - **Up to 1 year**

---

## Avatar Data: Additional Collection, Use, and Storage Details

YouTube provides extra details for avatar-related AI features involving recordings of your **face** and **voice**.

### What Avatar Data Includes

Avatar-related data may include:

- Recordings of your face and voice, referred to as **“your recordings”**
- Data derived from your recordings
- Data generated from your recordings

Some face and voice data, including derived or generated data, may be considered **biometric data** in certain jurisdictions.

---

## Avatar Data Uses



[... summary truncated for context management ...]

# Document 440: Zoppas Industries adopts Google Workspace with Gemini to boost global collaboration and efficiency

Source URL: https://workspace.google.com/blog/customer-stories/zoppas-industries-workspace-with-gemini-global-collaboration-and-efficiency?hl=en
Product area: workspace-site
Original file: docs/zoppas-industries-adopts-google-workspace-with-gemini-to-boost-global-collaborat-acc10e9a.md

# Zoppas Industries adopts Google Workspace with Gemini to boost global collaboration and efficiency

Source: https://workspace.google.com/blog/customer-stories/zoppas-industries-workspace-with-gemini-global-collaboration-and-efficiency?hl=en

Customer Stories

# Zoppas Industries adopts Google Workspace with Gemini to boost global collaboration and efficiency

September 16, 2025

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Zoppas_Thumbnail.max-2500x2500.jpg](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Zoppas_Thumbnail.max-2500x2500.jpg)

##### Alvise Fontana

IT Practice Lead, Zoppas Industries

##### Google Workspace Newsletter

Keep up with the evolving future of work and collaboration with insights, trends, and product news.

[SIGN UP](https://workspace.google.com/?source=gafb-blog_article-body-en#newsletter)

“Having a tool for every daily task is the kind of improvement we could not have made without Google Workspace. It makes everything easy. We can communicate with colleagues, schedule meetings, search for files, and more, all in one place.”

**Snapshot:** Zoppas Industries, a leader in thermal management solutions, uses Google Workspace with Gemini to increase employee collaboration and drive faster, more consistent client service. Learn how Zoppas Industries:

- **Successfully rolled out Google Workspace with Gemini** across the organization, automating repetitive workflows to improve productivity
- Improved communication, reduced friction, and **enabled faster knowledge sharing for 8,000 global employees**
- Supports **faster time-to-market** for its clients

---

### **The mission and the challenge**

From coffee makers and vending machines to electric cars and satellites, [Zoppas Industries](https://zoppasindustries.com/en/) is a key player in electrical heating and thermal management applications that are part of everyday life. Founded in 1963, the company now operates 15 production sites across Europe, Asia, and the Americas, providing solutions for domestic appliances, industrial applications, and the commercial sector. We attribute our growth to our constant transformation over the years and our ability to bring sustainable innovation to our customers.

One of our biggest transformations began in 2015 when we wanted to address concerns with our existing email provider, including spam, phishing, and ransomware attacks. This led us to Gmail, which opened the door to Google Workspace, a platform that allowed us to reimagine how we collaborate globally. Google Workspace gave us a comprehensive solution that went beyond our initial security challenges to meet our goals for boosting productivity, improving collaboration, and unifying our tools. Now, ten years later, Workspace has enabled digital literacy within our company so we can innovate faster.

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Zoppas_Thumbnail.max-2200x2200.jpg](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/Zoppas_Thumbnail.max-2200x2200.jpg)

Zoppas Industries uses Google Workspace with Gemini to empower teams with seamless solutions to do their jobs and meet customer needs.

### **The switch to Google Workspace and Gemini**

We first replaced our existing email system with Gmail to increase security and improve the way we communicate. We then identified another pain point: fragmented tools for managing documents and data, and for collaborating with global teammates. Since we were already using Gmail, Google Drive was a natural next step into the Workspace ecosystem. It enhances our security with access controls and simplified document management so everyone can find the files they need with ease. We also expanded our solutions to include Google Meet and Chat to enable face-to-face and real-time collaboration from anywhere. We recently brought AI into the fold with [Gemini](https://deepmind.google/models/gemini/) to supercharge productivity.

To ensure consistency in using Workspace with Gemini, we first trained our team managers, bringing digital transformation from the top down. The increase in productivity and improved communication led to a companywide rollout. The [Gemini App](https://deepmind.google/models/gemini/) is already accelerating key processes for our 8,000 employees, including supplier research, training, generating meeting summaries, and creating and reviewing content. We believe that Gemini is helping us enhance the way our employees collaborate.

We’re now exploring [Agentspace](https://cloud.google.com/products/agentspace), a new agentic AI platform for unlocking expertise and efficiencies across workflows. This solution understands natural language, provides comprehensive answers, and automates tasks, helping us work smarter, faster, and more consistently. Since our customers prioritize time to market, we believe this will be key in our ability to meet their needs for the future.

Overall, having a tool for daily tasks and workflows is the kind of improvement we could not have made without Google Workspace. We can easily communicate with colleagues, schedule meetings, search for files, and more — all in one place. The accessibility of the Workspace tools — available at the click of a button in your browser or email — also contributes to faster innovation.

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/People_1.max-2200x2200.jpg](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/People_1.max-2200x2200.jpg)

![https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/People_1.max-2200x2200.jpg](https://storage.googleapis.com/gweb-cloudblog-publish/images/People_1.max-2200x2200.jpg)

Zoppas Industries applies its technical expertise across industries, using Google Workspace to collaborate on a global scale.

### **Looking ahead**

As a long-time Google Workspace customer, we’re excited to fully embrace new tools like Gemini and Agentspace to serve our clients on the timelines they expect. Integrating these new features and products into our creative processes will support our commitment to innovation and our goal to embrace the next generation of AI-powered productivity.

Decades ago, the Internet was a doorway to a new way of working. Workspace has built a new doorway — one that gives everyone access to the tools they need to do their jobs, collaborate seamlessly, and helps us all create a stronger company culture.

### Google Workspace is the “new default” for The Carbon Group's fractional C-suite services

# Document 441: Simplify your move to Google Workspace: New Migration & Interoperability Features

Source URL: https://workspace.google.com/blog/productivity-collaboration/simplify-the-move-to-google-workspace-with-new-migration-and-interoperability-features
Product area: workspace-site
Original file: docs/google-workspace-blog-simplify-move-migration-interoperability-june-2026.md

# Simplify Your Move to Google Workspace: New Migration & Interoperability Features

**Source:** Google Workspace Blog, June 10, 2026
**Authors:** Ashish Gupta (Sr. PM) and Luke Camery (Director of PM, Google Workspace)
**Announced at:** Google Cloud Next '26

---

## Key Highlights

Google Workspace introduced powerful new features that make data migration faster and less expensive, while also supporting smoother cross-platform collaboration for productivity apps including Microsoft Teams and Office.

---

## 1. Data Migration

### Data Import (New Service)
- Cloud-native, no installation or cloud infrastructure setup needed.
- Built into the Workspace admin console.
- No cloud hosting fees.
- Migrates entire org from Microsoft 365 up to 5x faster.
- Supports Teams migrations, encrypted files and emails, all metadata (Outlook rules, calendar settings, sensitivity labels).

### Migration Planner
- Client-side utility runs locally, no data visible to Google until migration proceeds.
- Provides clear migration timelines before closing the deal.
- Enables quick, privacy-safe environment scans during pre-deal phase.
- Generates one-click migration plan when ready.

---

## 2. Microsoft 365 Interoperability

### Office Editing Mode in Gmail
- Available in beta in June '26.
- Edit Office files directly within Gmail without leaving inbox.

### Advanced Word Compatibility
- Beta in June '26.
- New supported native features: captions, cross-references, small caps.
- Benefits legal and engineering workflows.
- Ongoing 2026 improvements: character spacing, fixing missing content.

### Redlining in Docs
- Q4 '26 release.
- Refined comment/suggestion workflows for contract redlining.

### Google Sheets Enhancements
- Performance improvements (GA): Open 1M+ cell sheets 30% faster; filter 60% faster; conditional formatting 60% faster.
- Doubled cell limits: from 10M to 20 million cells (beta).
- Expanded charts and pivot tables (Q4 '26).
- Solve optimization problems with Gemini (GA).
- Sheets canvas (Alpha): create dashboards, heat maps, Kanban boards from data.
- Macro converter (Alpha Q2 '26): Gemini converts legacy VBA scripts to Apps Script.

### Google Slides Enhancements
- Embedded audio and video (Beta June '26). Import and play directly in Slides.
- Slides performance (Q3 '26): import/export slides up to 5x the size with high-resolution media.

### Workflow and Automation
- Workflow approvals (GA): lightweight approval tools and API for cross-app processes.
- Workspace Studio (GA): replace Microsoft Power Automate; automate across Workspace suite.

### Meetings and External Collaboration
- Google Meet hardware interoperability (GA): one-touch join for Teams, Zoom, etc.
- Guest accounts (GA): invite external users with full Workspace functionality, governed like internal users.

---

## Summary of Key Dates (2026)

| When | What |
|------|------|
| June '26 | Office editing in Gmail (beta), Advanced Word compatibility (beta), Embedded audio/video in Slides (beta) |
| Q2 '26 | Macro converter (alpha) |
| Q3 '26 | Slides performance improvements |
| Q4 '26 | Redlining in Docs, expanded charts/pivot tables in Sheets |
| Now (GA) | Data import, Sheets performance, optimization with Gemini, workflow approvals, Workspace Studio |

---

# Document 442: New ways to create and get stuff done in Google Workspace

Source URL: https://blog.google/products-and-platforms/products/workspace/workspace-updates/
Product area: workspace-site
Original file: docs/google-blog-workspace-updates-may-2026.md

# New Ways to Create and Get Things Done in Google Workspace

**Source:** Google Blog - May 19, 2026
**Author:** Yulie Kwon Kim, VP Product, Google Workspace
**Audience:** Google AI subscribers (Pro, Ultra, Plus) and Google Workspace business customers

More than 4 billion users rely on Google Workspace apps. This announcement covers four major updates.

**Four major announcements:**
- Voice capabilities (Gmail Live, Docs Live, Talk to Keep)
- Google Pics - new image creation/editing tool
- AI Inbox - expanded to Google AI Plus and Pro subscribers
- Gemini Spark - 24/7 personal AI agent

---

## Voice Capabilities

### Gmail Live
- Voice-activated inbox search: ask about flights, school events, etc. - gets synthesized answers instantly.

### Docs Live
- Thought partner and co-writer: dictate stream-of-consciousness, structure outlines, refine tone hands-free.
- Pulls relevant details from Gmail, Drive, Chat and the web (with permission).

### Talk to Keep
- Record a brain dump - Keep automatically turns rambles into organized notes and lists.

Rolling out to Google AI Pro and Ultra subscribers and in preview to Google Workspace business customers this summer.

---

## Google Pics

- Built on the latest Nano Banana model.
- Object segmentation: select and edit specific elements without affecting the rest of the image.
- Text editing and translation: modify text directly inside a photo or translate it while preserving original design.
- Workspace integrations: edit images right in Slides and Drive.
- Collaboration: shareable canvases for real-time co-editing.

**Availability:** Launching to limited Trusted Testers, then globally this summer to Google AI Pro and Ultra subscribers and in preview to Google Workspace business customers.

---

## AI Inbox

New capabilities:
- Personalized draft replies - AI generates contextual drafts.
- Instant file access - relevant Google Doc/Sheet/Slide links surfaced next to to-do items.
- Streamlined task management - mark tasks as done, dismiss suggestions, or mark all emails in a topic as read with one click.

**Availability:** Now rolling out to all Google AI Plus and Pro subscribers in the U.S.

---

## Gemini Spark

- 24/7 personal AI agent that takes action on your behalf, under your direction.
- Shift from answering questions to performing actions (e.g. sending emails, adding calendar events) with user consent.
- High-stakes actions require "ask you first" confirmation.
- Integrates with Workspace apps.

**Availability:** Soon in preview for business customers in the Gemini app.

---

## Summary

From brainstorming out loud with conversational voice features, to bringing ideas to life visually with Google Pics, to streamlining email management with AI Inbox, to navigating digital life with Gemini Spark - these updates help users save time so they can focus on the work that matters most.

---

# Document 443: How to create professional work videos with AI avatars in Google Vids

# Google Vids: Create Professional Videos with AI Avatars

**Source:** Google Workspace Blog
**Author:** David Nachum, Product Manager, Google Vids
**Date:** June 17, 2026

---

## Overview

Google Vids has launched major enhancements to AI avatars, voiceovers, and video generation. Anyone with a Google account in the US can now try AI avatars in Google Vids at no cost, with rollout to other regions in coming months.

---

## 1. Turn Static Presentations into Engaging Videos

- **Google Slides integration:** Convert presentations into scalable videos with a few clicks.
- Vids automatically creates a storyboard and generates a script for an AI avatar.
- Avatars act as digital presenters; can be re-scripted anytime.
- When an avatar is not needed, select an AI voiceover instead.
- **Soon:** AI voiceovers will narrate Slides in 8 languages (Japanese, Korean, Spanish, French, and more).

---

## 2. Craft Winning Pitches That Scale Across Languages

- Create custom avatars with company logo and custom background.
- **Late June 2026:** Steer the emotion of avatars to fine-tune delivery and match intent.
- **Global scaling:** AI avatars and voiceovers from scripts written in 24 languages (Arabic, Hindi, Dutch, Vietnamese, and more).

---

## 3. Demo Your Product Without a Video Shoot

- Direct custom avatars to walk, talk, and interact with objects by adding them as ingredients and typing a prompt.
- **Now available:** High-fidelity video clips without duration limits — build complete demonstrations from start to finish.

---

## Availability

- Free AI avatar trial for US Google account holders (more regions soon).
- Over 7 million monthly active users on Google Vids.
- Get started at vids.new.

---

## Key Customer Quote

> *"I was blown away by how Vids turns our sales decks into short video walkthroughs for customers. It's completely changed how we personalize our outreach. Sales thinks it's magic!"*
> — Forest Donovan, Sr. Director of Strategic Alliances, Fullstory
