# Document 444: Use Gemini Spark to manage your tasks & workflows in Gemini Apps - Gemini Apps Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/gemini/answer/17094507
Product area: gemini-apps
Original file: docs/gemini-spark-tasks-workflows.md

# Use Gemini Spark to Manage Tasks & Workflows – Summary

**Source:** Google Gemini Apps Help — "Use Gemini Spark to manage your tasks & workflows in Gemini Apps"
**URL:** https://support.google.com/gemini/answer/17094507

---

## Overview

Gemini Spark is a personal AI agent that can automate complex workflows and manage schedules for ongoing tasks in Gemini Apps. Gemini can help handle tasks, using info from sources like Connected Apps, skills, chats, websites you're signed into, Personal Intelligence, location, and more. Just tell Gemini your specific goals and when you want tasks completed.

### Example Uses
- **Declutter inbox** – summarize/archive newsletters, unsubscribe
- **Custom news digest** – follow topics over time
- **Deep dive on topics** – research with cited sources

Tip: Schedules in Gemini Spark are different from scheduled actions in Gemini chats.

---

## Requirements

| Requirement | Detail |
|-------------|--------|
| **Age** | 18 or over |
| **Account** | Personal Google Account with a Google AI Ultra subscription |
| **Activity setting** | Keep Activity must be ON |
| **Availability** | Not available in: Australia, Canada, EEA, Hong Kong, India, Japan, Korea, Nigeria, Switzerland, UK |
| **Language** | English only |
| **Platforms** | Gemini mobile app & web app at gemini.google.com |

---

## What Gemini Spark Can Use

- Some Connected Apps (like Google Workspace)
- Remote browser with automatically saved info
- Remote computer with code execution data
- Personal Intelligence
- Skills
- Schedules

**Important:** When using Spark, Gemini accesses info from these sources and from websites it interacts with (including logged-in sites). Do NOT enter sign-in info, payment details, or sensitive data directly into the task thread. Instead, take control of Gemini's browser and enter info on the webpage.

---

## Creating a Task

1. Go to gemini.google.com (computer).
2. Click **Change mode** → **Switch to Spark** → **Tasks**.
3. Describe the task in the text box. You can:
   - Specify when to run (time or event-based)
   - Use `/` or `@` to select an existing skill
4. To add files: click **Add** → choose **Upload files**, **Add from Drive**, or **Add Notebook**.
5. Click **Submit**.
6. Monitor the task thread for inputs Gemini needs (e.g., taking over the browser).

---

## Monitoring & Managing Tasks

1. Go to gemini.google.com, switch to Spark → Tasks, click the desired thread.
2. To show the work panel, click **Close tasks panel** (left edge of thread panel).
3. The work panel shows:
   - **Progress:** completed (✅), current (▶), and planned (○) steps.
   - **Files:** review which files Gemini read/updated; click to open.
   - **Schedules:** hover → **More** → **Pause** or **Resume**.
   - **Monitor & take over:** Open work panel → Remote browser → Take over task.

---

## Gemini Spark Safety & Limits

- **Limits:** Gemini Apps have compute-based usage limits. Max 15 tasks running at once.
- **Risks:** Gemini can make mistakes and do unexpected things. Prompt injection from external data could cause unintended actions.
- **Data:** Remote browser data (cookies, auth info) can be deleted from Settings → Gemini Spark → Delete remote browser data.
- **Turning off:** Deletes remote browser and code execution data, pauses schedules, but does not delete tasks, threads, or files.

---

## What Workspace Actions Can Gemini Spark Perform?

Gemini Spark can take actions in Google Workspace such as:
- **Manage Your Schedule:** Check your calendar, RSVP to invitations, schedule new events.
- **Reschedule & Clean up:** Update meeting times, change locations, or cancel events.
- **Smart Scheduling:** Check your calendar and suggest open slots that fit everyone's availability.

---

# Document 445: Get Started with Gemini Go - Gemini Apps Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/gemini/answer/17079170
Product area: gemini-apps
Original file: docs/gemini-go-get-started.md

# Get Started with Gemini Go – Summary

**Source:** Google Gemini Apps Help — "Get Started with Gemini Go"
**URL:** https://support.google.com/gemini/answer/17079170

---

## Overview

Gemini Go is a streamlined version of Gemini designed for Android Go devices. It's efficient even on devices with lower storage. Gemini Go replaces Assistant Go, providing a new conversational experience.

### Eligibility and Device Requirements

- **Devices:** Android Go devices with 2GB of RAM or more
- **Account:** Signed in to a personal Google Account or work/school account with Gemini Apps access
- **Languages & regions:** Available in all languages and countries where Gemini is available

> **Important:** Gemini Go is rolling out gradually, so it may not be available to you yet.

---

## Getting Started

On Android Go devices, Gemini is available as part of the Google Search app. To check if you have the latest version:

1. Open the Google Play app on your Android Go device.
2. Search for "Google" or "Google Search."
3. If an "Update" button is available, tap it to install the latest version.
4. If the button says "Open," you already have the latest version.

### Start a Conversation

- Touch and hold the **Home** button.
- Press and hold the **Power** button, on supported devices.

---

## What You Can Ask Gemini Go

- **Stay connected:** Ask Gemini to call or send text messages to your contacts.
- **Find local info:** Check drive time to work, or ask complex questions like "Help me find a ramen restaurant open for lunch on Tuesday with an EV charger nearby."
- **Plan your day:** Set an alarm or create a calendar event.
- **Upload files:** Upload docs, photos, and other files to add more context to your chats.
- **Play media:** Ask Gemini to play music by mood or activity.

---

# Document 446: Create & manage skills for Gemini Apps - Gemini Apps Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/gemini/answer/17094296
Product area: gemini-apps
Original file: docs/gemini-skills-create-manage.md

# Create & Manage Skills for Gemini Apps – Summary

**Source:** Google Gemini Apps Help — "Create & manage skills for Gemini Apps"
**URL:** https://support.google.com/gemini/answer/17094296

---

## Overview

Skills are reusable custom instructions (plus optional files) that teach Gemini how to perform specific tasks or workflows. Instead of re-typing preferences or re-uploading files each time, you build a skill once and use it across tasks.

### Key Benefits
- **Save time & effort** – build once, reuse in any Gemini Spark task
- **Automate workflow** – Gemini automatically detects when a skill is relevant and applies it
- **Mix & match** – combine multiple skills for complex, multi-step tasks

> **Important:** Skills are currently available **only in Gemini Spark** (not in standard Gemini chat).

---

## Requirements

| Requirement | Detail |
|-------------|--------|
| **Age** | 18 or over |
| **Account** | Personal Google Account with Google AI Ultra subscription |
| **Activity setting** | Keep Activity must be ON |
| **Availability** | Not available in: Australia, Canada, EEA, Hong Kong, India, Japan, Korea, Nigeria, Switzerland, UK |
| **Language** | English only |
| **Platform** | Gemini mobile app + web app at gemini.google.com (Spark mode) |

---

## Creating a Skill

Access the **Skills page** from the sidebar in Gemini Spark mode at gemini.google.com.

### Methods

1. **Create with Gemini (Conversational):** Click **Create with Gemini** and follow the guided thread.
2. **Edit a Prefilled Template:** Under "Recommended," click **Add skill** on a template → edit → **Save**.
3. **Use a Blank Template:** Click **Create manually** → enter name, description, instructions → **Save**.
4. **Upload a Skill File:** Click **Upload** → select a `SKILL.md` file (or `.zip` containing one) → review → **Save**.

### Upload Requirements

**Supported file types** (plain-text only):
- Text & Docs: `.txt` `.md` `.rst` `.rtf` `.tex` `.log`
- Code: `.py` `.sh`
- Data & Config: `.json` `.yaml` `.csv` `.toml` `.xml` `.env` `.sql`
- Web & Build: `.html` `.css` `.svg` `Makefile` `Dockerfile`

**Not supported:** PDF, DOCX, DOC, XLSX, JPG, PNG, or any binary/rich-media formats.

**Naming:** Skill name inside `SKILL.md` must be all lowercase, words hyphen-separated (e.g., `my-new-skill`).
**Size limit:** Total upload must not exceed 100 MB.
**Security:** No scripts that perform external web actions.

### Ask Gemini in a Spark Task

In any Spark task thread, type: `Create a skill based on these instructions: <your instructions>` → Gemini creates the skill and saves it to your Skills page.

---

## Using Skills for a Task

- **Manual invocation:** In the task thread, type `/` or `@` and select the skill.
- **Automatic use:** Gemini automatically applies any active skill when relevant to your prompt.
- **Combine skills:** Include multiple skills in one task for complex workflows.

---

## Managing Your Skills

All management is done from the **Skills page** in Gemini Spark.

- **Activate / Deactivate:** Click the skill → **More** → **Deactivate**. If off and manually called, Gemini asks to turn it back on.
- **Edit:** Click the skill → edit fields directly, or **More** → **Edit with Gemini** for conversational editing, or **More** → **Replace skill** to upload a new file.
- **Delete:** Hover → **More** → **Delete** (permanent, cannot be undone).
- **Download:** Hover → **More** → **Download** (saved as `.zip`).

---

## Common Questions

### Task vs. Schedule vs. Skill

| Concept | Role | Example |
|---------|------|---------|
| **Task** | *What* you want to accomplish | "Plan my business trip to London" |
| **Schedule** | *When* it runs (time- or event-based) | "Every day at 8 AM, give me AI news" |
| **Skill** | *How* to do a specific sub-task | "Travel Booking" skill + "Gmail Writing" skill |

### What happens when I downgrade or cancel Google AI Ultra?
- In-progress Spark tasks finish, not deleted.
- Schedules are paused, not deleted.
- Skills are turned off, not deleted.
- To resume, you must upgrade or resubscribe.

---

# Document 447: Google Workspace standardized model for agent tools and APIs

Source URL: https://developers.google.com/workspace/tools-safety
Product area: workspace-developers
Original file: docs/workspace-standardized-model-agent-tools-apis.md

# Google Workspace Standardized Model for Agent Tools and APIs – Summary

**Source:** Google for Developers — "Google Workspace standardized model for agent tools and APIs"
**URL:** https://developers.google.com/workspace/tools-safety
**Last updated:** 2026-05-01 UTC

---

## Overview

Following the launch of new agent tools like the Google Workspace MCP server, Google is introducing a standardized tiering model for Google Workspace APIs (including MCP) to safeguard the ecosystem while fostering innovation. The model provides a predictable path to scale for high-impact applications and protects against risks such as API abuse and unintended large-scale data egress. Less than 1% of active developers are expected to need to move beyond the standard usage tier.

---

## Key Dates & Changes (starting May 1, 2026)

**Affected APIs:** Gmail API, Google Calendar API, Google Drive API.

Quota adjustments align with typical usage patterns to support safety and privacy.

| Existing projects (with API usage Nov 2025–Apr 2026) | New projects (created from May 1, 2026) |
| --- | --- |
| Existing quotas remain unchanged for at least 60 days. | New quotas documented for Gmail, Calendar, Drive roll out over several weeks. |
| Quota increase requests can be submitted for approval. | Same process – requests subject to approval. |

**Later in 2026 (after 90 days notice):**
- Quota increase requests will require Google Cloud billing to be enabled.
- API usage over standard daily thresholds will generate charges on your Google Cloud bill.
- New standard API quotas will apply unless an increased quota has already been approved and billing is enabled.

---

## Coming: Paid Option for Scaled Access

A billable usage quota increase option will be introduced for developers needing faster, scaled access. Developers can increase project quotas by paying for API usage above the standard daily limit. Additional details expected in the coming months with 90 days notice.

---

## Developer Policy

All usage remains subject to the [Google Workspace API developer policy](https://developers.google.com/workspace/workspace-api-user-data-developer-policy) and the [Google APIs Terms of Service](https://developers.google.com/terms). Google may require verification/audit of adherence to user data policies.

---

# Document 448: Write effective skills for Gemini Apps - Gemini Apps Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/gemini/answer/17102773?hl=en
Product area: gemini-apps
Original file: docs/write-effective-skills-gemini.md

# Write Effective Skills for Gemini Apps — Summary

**Important:** Skills are currently only available in **Gemini Spark**. [Learn more about Gemini Spark](https://support.google.com/gemini/answer/17094507).

## What is a Skill?

A skill is a set of instructions and preferences that teaches Gemini how to handle a specific type of task the way you would. Think of it as a cheat sheet that captures your expertise—your process, your preferences, and the details only you would know—so Gemini can do a recurring task correctly every time, without you explaining it again.

- Built for a **single job**; can be combined with other skills for larger workflows.
- Includes steps, formatting rules, templates, and common problems to avoid.

## How Skills Work

1. **Check relevance** – Gemini only checks the skill's **name and description** to decide if it's relevant.
2. **Read & follow instructions** – If relevant, Gemini reads the full instructions.
3. **Use other files as needed** – Only opens attached files (template, style guide, spreadsheet) when the step requires them.

## When to Create a Skill

Frequently do any of these? → Create a skill:

- **Repeat prompts** – Same complex instructions over and over.
- **Fix formats** – Repeatedly adjust layout or style.
- **Work across Connected Apps** – Manually combine steps in Gmail, Docs, Calendar.

### When to Skip

- **One-off job** – Not repeated.
- **Already handled well** – Gemini delivers expected results without guidance.
- **Constantly changing** – Steps or requirements change too often.

## Writing an Effective Skill — Best Practices

### 1. Name & Description (Critical for Relevance)

#### Skill Name
- Start with an **action verb** (focus on *what it does*, not what it is).
- Avoid vague words like `helper`, `tools`, `data`.
- Use **lowercase with hyphens** – e.g., `plan-meal-from-recipe`.

#### Skill Description (1–2 sentences, ≤1024 characters)
- **List specific usage examples** – Start with `Use when…`
- **Third-person capability statement** – e.g., "Categorizes recipes, scales ingredient portions, and generates grocery lists..."
- **Never use first or second person** – no "I can help you" or "You can use this to".

### 2. Optimize Instructions

#### Focus on the *type* of task, not a specific one
- ❌ *Don't:* Instructions for "Spaghetti on Tuesday"
- ✅ *Do:* Instructions for building any meal plan based on your preferences

#### Decide How Much Freedom to Give
- **Creative tasks** – General directions (e.g., "brainstorm blog post ideas").
- **Strict/critical tasks** – Clear, step-by-step instructions (e.g., "copy financial data in exact layout", "summarize meeting notes using only bullet points").

### 3. Use Formats & Output Templates

Include structural templates or clear examples in instructions.

### 4. Add a "Common Mistakes" Section

Warn Gemini about pitfalls. E.g., "Remove personal info: Make sure the report does not include any personal info (like names, emails, or phone numbers) unless the task specifically asks for it."

### 5. Use Workflows & Checklists

For long/complex tasks, provide a checklist to track progress.

### 6. Tell Gemini How to Handle Missing Information

Be explicit – prevents Gemini from guessing or skipping. E.g., "If an email doesn't have a date for the event, ask me for the date instead of guessing or leaving it out."

---

## Related Resources

- [Use Gemini Spark to manage your tasks & workflows in Gemini Apps](https://support.google.com/gemini/answer/17094507)
- [Create & manage skills for your workflows in Gemini Apps](https://support.google.com/gemini/answer/17094296)

---

# Document 449: Generate a Slide Deck in NotebookLM - NotebookLM Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/notebooklm/answer/16757456?hl=en
Product area: notebooklm
Original file: docs/notebooklm-slide-deck.md

# Generate a Slide Deck in NotebookLM — Summary

**Important:** Feature only available to users over 18. Slide Decks are available in the mobile app but may have differences.

Transform your sources into a polished presentation by generating a Slide Deck, perfect for sharing or reviewing key concepts. Present your deck instantly in NotebookLM or share it as a PDF.

## How to Generate a Slide Deck

1. Open an existing notebook or create a new one and upload sources.
   - To generate or delete a Slide Deck, you need **edit access** to a notebook.
2. In the **"Studio" panel**, select **"Slide Deck"** to start, or use the **pencil icon** to customize before generating. The presentation is generated in the background – you can continue working.
3. (Optional) In the customization panel, you can express preferences:

### Customization Options

- **Format**
  - **Detailed Deck** – comprehensive deck with full text and details, perfect for emailing or reading on its own.
  - **Presenter Slides** – clean, visual slide decks with key talking points to support you while you speak.
- **Output language** – select your language.
- **Length** – choose between short, default, or long.
- **Prompt** – describe the slide deck you want. Example: "Create a deck for beginners using a bold and playful style with a focus on step-by-step instructions."

**Important:**
- Slide Decks are AI-generated and may contain visual or factual inaccuracies.
- Generation may take multiple minutes; you can continue working in the meantime.

## Manage Your Slide Deck

- Use the **three dots menu** next to your generated Slide Deck to: Rename, Download as PDF, Share, or Delete.
- To view the prompt used, select **"View custom prompt"** from the three dots menu.
- To present in full-screen slideshow mode, select **"Start slideshow."**

## Revise Your Slide Deck

1. Open an existing notebook.
2. In **Studio** panel, navigate to the **"Slide Deck"** section.
3. Select the title of the Slide Deck you wish to load.
4. Choose **"Revise"** (pencil icon).
5. On each slide, input your revision instructions. You can change more than one slide; instructions are grouped under **"Pending Changes"**.
   - Adding/removing slides is **not yet supported**.
   - **Note:** Sources are **not taken into account** during revisions.
6. Select **"Generate revised deck"** to regenerate with revised slides.

**Tip:** Batch your slide revisions. Every revision creates an entirely new slide deck. No limit on individual slides per revision, but there are quota limits on total slide deck revisions.

## Share a Slide Deck

### Option 1: Share a Link
1. Generate a Slide Deck.
2. In the Slide Deck viewer, select **Share**.
3. Confirm the notebook is shared with the recipient or made public (**"Anyone with a link"**) and viewers have access to the **"full notebook."**
4. Copy link and share.

**Important:**
- Public notebook sharing is only enabled for consumer accounts. Disabled for Workspace Enterprise or Education accounts.
- Only notebook owners and editors can make a generated Slide Deck publicly available.
- Once a Slide Deck is deleted, any previously generated share link will no longer work.

### Option 2: Share the Entire Notebook
Share the notebook – other users can access the Slide Deck in the **"Studio" panel.**

### Option 3: Download & Share the Slide Deck
From the three dots menu, select **"Download PDF Document (.pdf)"** or **"Download PowerPoint (.pptx)."** Share the downloaded file.

---

## Related Resources

- [Create a notebook in NotebookLM](https://support.google.com/notebooklm/answer/16206563)
- [Add or discover new sources for your Notebook](https://support.google.com/notebooklm/answer/16215270)

# Document 450: Use public notebooks and featured notebooks in NotebookLM - NotebookLM Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/notebooklm/answer/16322204?hl=en
Product area: notebooklm
Original file: docs/notebooklm-public-notebooks.md

# Use Public Notebooks and Featured Notebooks in NotebookLM — Summary

**Important:** Public sharing is only enabled for consumer accounts. It's currently disabled for Workspace Enterprise or Education accounts.

## Public Notebooks

- **Any Google account holder** can view a public notebook via a share link.
- **Owners and editors** can manage public access; viewers cannot.

### How to Share a Public Notebook
1. Open or create a notebook.
2. Click **Share** (top-right).
3. Set access to **"Anyone with a link"**.
4. Copy the link (or use **"Copy link to chat view"** for a focused chat-only experience).

> **Note:** Chat View hides sources and artifacts visually but does not completely revoke underlying access; viewers may still navigate to hidden materials.

- Owners with **paid subscriptions** can view usage analytics in the Analytics tab.
- Changes made by owners/editors appear **without regenerating the link**.
- **Deleting or making a notebook private** breaks all previously shared links.
- A globe icon next to the Share button confirms the notebook is public.

### How to Visit a Public Notebook
- Open the URL → notebook appears on your homepage.
- You can ask questions, view Audio Overviews, FAQs, briefing documents, etc.
- To remove from homepage: use the **three-dots menu → Remove notebook**.

### How to Disable Public Sharing
1. Owner or editor opens the notebook.
2. Click **Share**.
3. Set access back to **Restricted**.
Effect: New visitors get blocked; previous visitors lose access from their shared notebooks page.

## Featured Notebooks

- **Enabled only for consumer accounts.** Workspace Enterprise/Education accounts can only access via direct URLs.
- Pre-loaded with **curated, high-quality sources** from authors, researchers, nonprofits, etc.

### Browsing a Featured Notebook
- Access via the **"Featured notebooks"** tab.
- As a viewer you can: Read original sources, Ask questions in chat, Consume pre-generated artifacts (Audio Overviews, Study Guides).
- **You cannot generate your own artifacts** or add sources.
- You can **select/deselect sources** in the left source panel.
- NotebookLM answers use only those materials + your current chat history.

## Accepted Use & Policies

- **Copyright:** Do not share copyrighted content without rights. Google responds to infringement notices; repeat infringers face account termination.
- **Ownership:** Google does not claim ownership over content you generate.
- **AI-generated content:** Do not rely on NotebookLM for medical, legal, financial, or professional advice. It is informational only.
- **Report violations** in public/featured notebooks via abuse report form.

---

# Document 451: Gemini Apps limits & upgrades for Google AI subscribers - Gemini Apps Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/gemini/answer/16275805?hl=en
Product area: gemini-apps
Original file: docs/gemini-apps-limits-upgrades.md

# Gemini Apps Limits & Upgrades for Google AI Subscribers — Summary

**Notification:** Starting **May 17, 2026** changes to usage limits apply.

## Age Requirements

- **Most countries:** Must be 13+ (or applicable age in your country).
- **EEA, Switzerland, UK:** Must be 18+.

## Usage Limits

| Plan | Limit |
|---|---|
| Without AI plan | Standard limits |
| AI Plus | 2x higher than standard |
| AI Pro | 4x higher than standard |
| AI Ultra | 5x or 20x higher than AI Pro (depends on subscription) |

- Limits refresh every **5 hours** until weekly limit is reached.

## Gemini 3 Models

| Model | Description |
|---|---|
| **Gemini Flash-Lite** | Efficient workhorse – fast, ideal for everyday tasks (summarization, brainstorming). |
| **Gemini Flash** | Balances speed & reasoning – solves a large variety of problems. |
| **Gemini Pro** | Most advanced – excels at complex math & coding, deeper understanding across text, files, images, videos. Slower response time. |

## Thinking Levels

| Level | Available Plans | Best For |
|---|---|---|
| **Standard thinking** | All (default) | Most questions – faster responses |
| **Extended thinking** | All | Complex problem solving – model reasons longer |
| **Deep think** | AI Ultra only | Maximum parallel reasoning – requires Pro model, takes a few minutes |

## Context Windows

| Plan | Context Window |
|---|---|
| Without AI plan | 32k tokens |
| AI Plus | 128k tokens |
| AI Pro & AI Ultra | 1 million tokens |

With 1M tokens, Gemini can understand up to 1,500 pages of text or 30,000 lines of code.

## Feature Availability by Plan

| Feature | No Plan | AI Plus | AI Pro | AI Ultra |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Canvas | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Gems | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Connected Apps | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Deep Research | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Daily brief | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Gemini Spark | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ |
| Image generation | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Video generation | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Music generation | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Scheduled actions | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |

## More Info About Limits

- **With subscription when reaching limit:** Continue conversation using Flash-Lite.
- **Without subscription or after weekly limit:** Upgrade to higher plan or wait until limit refreshes.
- Limits may change without notice due to capacity constraints.
- During high activity, features (e.g., Deep Research) may be restricted for non-paying users before paid users are affected.

### How to check limits
1. Go to gemini.google.com.
2. At bottom left, select **Settings** → **Usage Limits**.

# Document 453: Introducing Workspace Intelligence

Source URL: https://workspace.google.com/blog/product-announcements/introducing-workspace-intelligence
Product area: workspace-site

# Introducing Workspace Intelligence

**Date:** April 22, 2026
**Author:** Yulie Kwon Kim, VP Product, Google Workspace

Workspace Intelligence delivers unified, real-time understanding to power agentic work. It is a secure, dynamic system that inherently understands complex semantic relationships within your Workspace apps (Docs, Slides, Gmail) content, your active projects, your collaborators, and your organization's domain knowledge.

## Three Core Capabilities

### 1. Information Gathering
- Breaks down context walls between apps
- Automates bridging between apps and projects
- Pulls the right information automatically

### 2. Situational Awareness
- Uses advanced Gemini reasoning
- Identifies what's most important right now
- Surfaces action items automatically

### 3. True Personalization
- Learns your work style, voice, and formatting preferences
- Adapts to individual communication patterns
- Makes outputs sound authentically like you

## Key Features by App

### Google Chat - A New Command Line for Work
- **Ask Gemini in Chat** - Unified command line accessible from Chat
- **Daily briefing** - Surfaces important tasks, unread threads, urgent action items
- **Skills in Workspace** - Generate Docs/Slides, schedule meetings, find files
- **Third-party connectors** (new) - Asana, Jira, Salesforce

### Sheets, Docs, and Slides
- **Sheets** - Build/edit entire spreadsheets using natural language; synthesizes data across files, emails, chats, and the web; state-of-the-art benchmark
- **Docs** - Generate infographics grounded in business data; edit multiple images; triage/respond to comments; edit based on feedback
- **Slides** - (Coming soon) Create full, editable slide decks in one shot, adhering to company templates

### Gmail - Break Through Information Overload
- **AI Inbox** - Streamlined view of what's most important
- **AI Overviews in Gmail search** - Synthesizes information from multiple email threads

### Google Drive - From Storage to Active Knowledge Base
- **AI Overviews** (GA) - Find information fast across files
- **Ask Gemini** (GA) - Detailed insights about all your files
- **Drive Projects** - Centrally organize files and emails for full context

## Security and Governance

Your data is your data. It is not reviewed by humans, used for ads, or used to train AI models outside of Workspace without your permission.

- Built on the same secure, compliant infrastructure as Workspace
- **Admin controls** - Manage Workspace Intelligence usage
- **Data processing regions** - Lock to US or EU (Germany and India coming soon)
- **Client-side encryption** - Ultimate shield; deny access from any agent or entity

## Availability

- Rolling out to Google Workspace customers
- Part of Gemini for Google Workspace add-on

# Document 457: Use Large Language Models (LLMs) to develop on Google Workspace

Source URL: https://developers.google.com/workspace/guides/build-with-llms
Product area: workspace-developers

# Use Large Language Models (LLMs) to Develop on Google Workspace

**Last updated:** 2026-06-25 UTC

## Overview

Best practices and recommended tools for working with Large Language Models (LLMs) to develop solutions for Google Workspace.

### Key Capabilities

- Generate or troubleshoot code for calling Google Workspace APIs.
- Build a solution based on the latest Google Workspace developer documentation.
- Access Google Workspace resources from the command line or your IDE.

## Recommended AI Code Assist Tools

- Google AI Studio: Generate code for your Google Workspace solutions, including Apps Script projects.
- Gemini Code Assist: AI-powered assistance in your IDE from the Gemini 2.5 model.

## Google Workspace MCP Server

An MCP server provides tools for LLMs to access Google Workspace developer documentation.
Supports retrieving up-to-date API information and fetching official documentation.

For Gemini Code Assist, add to settings.json:
  mcpServers: { workspace-developer: { httpUrl: https://workspace-developer.goog/mcp, trust: true } }

For Gemini CLI: gemini extensions install https://github.com/googleworkspace/developer-tools

## Related Topics
- Build with AI for Google Workspace
- Extend the Google Workspace UI
- Gemini for Google Cloud overview


# Document 458: Generate videos with Gemini Apps - Gemini Apps Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/gemini/answer/16126339
Product area: gemini-apps

# Generate Videos with Gemini Apps

**Source:** Google Gemini Help — Generate videos with Gemini Apps

## Overview
Create and edit videos in minutes using Gemini Omni, Google's latest AI video generation model. Blend text, images, and video in a conversational interface.

## Requirements
- **Personal account:** Must have a Google AI plan.
- **Work/school account:** Must have a qualifying Workspace license.
- **Age:** Not available for users under 18.
- Must be signed in to Gemini Apps.

You must comply with Google's Terms of Service and Prohibited Use Policy. Only upload content you have rights to.

## About Gemini Omni

- **Video to Video Edit:** Upload a video and apply edits (not available in EEA, Switzerland, UK, and some US states)
- **Multi-turn editing:** Continually edit a video in one conversation; each turn can include image/video references
- **Aspect Ratio Control:** Choose aspect ratio before creation. Default: landscape (text prompts) or matches uploaded content
- **Templates:** Pre-made curated styles for instant inspiration

## How to Generate a Video

1. Go to gemini.google.com on your computer.
2. Click Add Files → Create video.
3. (Optional) Select a template.
4. Enter a text prompt describing the video.
   - To use images/videos: click Add image (up to 5) or Add video (max 1).
   - To include your avatar: type @[your Google username] in the prompt.
5. Click Submit.

Brainstorm and refine prompts in the main chat before generating. Video generation may take a few minutes; you cannot interact with the same chat during generation. You can also ask Gemini to generate audio along with the video.

## Editing a Generated Video

After generation, edit with a simple prompt such as:
- Remove/replace objects or characters
- Change camera angles
- Modify the scene

## Exporting or Downloading a Video

Under the video, click Share → Share on YouTube or Download video.

## Why Videos Might Be Removed

Gemini Apps may remove videos if systems detect a possible violation of Google's Terms of Service or Prohibited Use Policy.

## Voice Edits & Avatar

You can create AI content with your own voice and likeness using an avatar.

# Google AI Announcements – June 2026

**Source:** Google Blog | Published: Jul 01, 2026

"This month was about creating a more unified environment where AI delivers help naturally throughout your day."

## Developer & Platform Updates

- **Gemma 4 12B (open model):** Runs locally on laptop with just 16GB memory. Combines unified architecture, vision, and native voice processing. Enables private, advanced reasoning on everyday hardware.
- **Computer use in Gemini 3.5 Flash:** Custom agents that see, reason, and take action across desktop, mobile, and browser. Improved for long-horizon and enterprise automation (e.g., continuous software testing).
- **Nano Banana 2 Lite:** Fastest and most cost-efficient Gemini image model – now available.
- **Gemini Omni Flash (public preview via API):** Natively multimodal model for enterprises – enables dynamic video workflows.

## Everyday Life & Devices

- **Android 17:** Floating app windows, Screen Reactions (picture-in-picture recording), foldable gaming layout, biometric remote lock for lost phone. Rolling out first to Pixel, then other Android devices in 2026.
- **June Pixel Drop:** Screen recording reactions, AI video/music creation, floating app bubbles, expanded real-time voice translation, custom voicemail greetings, automated emergency notifications.
- **Google Finance (out of beta):** Track personal portfolio, market intel, new Android app with AI research tool and "key moments" explaining stock moves.
- **Gemini 3.5 Live Translate:** Audio model for live speech-to-speech translation – auto-detects 70+ languages, preserves natural intonation, eliminates pauses. Rolling out in Gemini Live API, Google AI Studio, and Google Translate app.
- **New Google Home Speaker (built for Gemini):** Natural conversation without rigid commands; handles multiple requests, complex questions, remembers context.

## Learning & Education

- **NotebookLM upgrades:** Advanced reasoning, secure cloud code execution, chart/spreadsheet/slide deck generation. Organizes loose ideas from web sources into structured research repo. Available globally for Google AI Ultra subscribers and specific Workspace accounts.
- **Study notebooks in Gemini app:** Set goal → upload notes → baseline quiz → personalized lessons → progress dashboard.
- **Connected AI tools for classrooms:** Teachers use real class context securely to streamline tasks. Students get adaptive study notebooks, free standardized test prep.
- **Sierra Leone study & resources:** AI as pedagogical partner where teacher shortage is acute. Free teacher training guide and research playbook released.

## Culture & Art

- **Colonial Williamsburg digital collection** (for US 250th birthday): Explore 18th-century streets/artifacts; custom NotebookLM with 150+ primary sources.
- **Dataland:** World's first AI arts museum (with Refik Anadol). Powered by Google Cloud + Gemini. Uses neural networks to turn billions of data points into omni-sensory landscapes.

## Research & Societal Impact

- **Co-Scientist:** Structured hypothesis generation for life sciences. Used for infectious diseases, cellular aging, ALS.
- **UK planning prototype:** Gemini-powered council tool automates manual data extraction and policy cross-referencing. Aims to cut household planning applications by 50%.
- **Combating AI scams:** Civil lawsuit filed against "Outsider Enterprise" (China-based, coordinated via Telegram) for phishing kits mimicking Google. Advocating for 7 bipartisan bills.
- **Extreme weather:** 10 years of AI research: river floods predicted 7 days in advance, wildfire boundaries via satellite, cyclone paths with high confidence. Alerts on Search & Maps.
- **UK AI adoption study:** Workplace AI adoption doubled to 73% (from 34% in 2025). Top 15% of users see faster career progression, better reviews, promotions, pay rises.


# Document 460: Gemini can handle note-taking during Google Meet calls

Source URL: https://blog.google/products-and-platforms/products/workspace/take-notes-for-me/
Product area: workspace-meet

# Gemini Can Now Take Notes in Google Meet

**Source:** Google Blog | Published: Jun 29, 2026

Google AI Pro and Ultra subscribers can use the "Take notes for me" feature in Google Meet on web and mobile devices in select languages. The tool handles note-taking during calls with your permission, allowing you to stay focused on the discussion.

## How It Works

- **Real-time capturing:** Gemini transcribes the conversation and creates a meeting summary with key action items.
- **Seamless saving:** Your notes are automatically saved to a Google Doc in your Google Drive.
- **Instant recaps:** After the meeting, you'll get an email with the summary and action items.

## Availability

Available for Google AI Pro or Ultra subscribers — or eligible Workspace business customers — for meetings you host.

## How to Use

- **During a call:** Click the pencil icon at the top of your Meet window to start note-taking.
- **For all calls:** Enable the feature under Meeting records in your Google Meet settings.

All meeting participants will be notified when the feature is enabled.

# Document 462: Use chat in NotebookLM - NotebookLM Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/notebooklm/answer/16179559?hl=en
Product area: notebooklm
Original file: docs/use-chat-in-notebooklm-notebooklm-help-a1b2c3d4.md

## Chat Overview

- After uploading sources, you can ask questions about source material and give instructions to perform actions.
- Responses include direct citations (quotes, text, images) from your sources. Hover to see full quoted text; select to navigate to original context.
- Use checkboxes on sources to include/exclude which sources the model uses.

Important: Chat responses in NotebookLM only use data from your sources. If you explicitly ask the model to do something more creative, like "rewrite the end of my short story," you may receive "NotebookLM can't answer this question." Try to rephrase or ask a different question.

Mobile app may have limitations.

## Chat Settings

1. In the Chat panel, navigate to Configure Chat (gear icon).
2. Select a conversational style:
   - Default — general research and brainstorming
   - Learning Guide — educational content, grasping new concepts
   - Custom — specific style (e.g., "Respond like a PhD student") or role (e.g., "Pretend to be a role-playing game host")
3. Select a response length: Default, Longer, Shorter.
4. Click Save.

### Tips
- Pin responses to noteboard: select Save to note (preserves original format, including tables and clickable citations).
- Chat history is retained and private. Clear it via the three-dot menu → Delete Chat History.
- In chat, generate a note with Add a note, or access Audio Overview and Mind Maps.

## Additional Features for Google AI Ultra Subscribers

Important: Only available to Google AI Ultra subscribers on desktop. Experimental — requires supervision. NotebookLM can make mistakes and do unexpected things. Please double check it.

You get agentic capabilities and advanced reasoning in chat, enabling actions like:
- Searching the web
- Running code
- Creating downloadable files, charts, and images

Use this chat experience with or without sources.

### New Outputs You Can Generate

| Category | Formats |
|----------|---------|
| Data visualizations and charts | png, svg |
| Documents | PDF, Microsoft Word, Markdown, text files |
| Images | png, jpg, gif |
| Structured data | CSV, JSON |
| Spreadsheets | Microsoft Excel (xlsx) |
| Presentations | Microsoft PowerPoint (pptx) |

### Other Capabilities
- Make changes to uploaded charts
- Modify artifacts as versions
- Expand "thinking steps" to see what NotebookLM is doing
- Complete research from within chat — select and import research report and sources into your notebook

## Changing Your Chat Request (All Users)

While NotebookLM is processing:
1. Click Stop in the prompt box.
2. Choose an option:
   - Stop — to revise your prompt
   - Keep generating — to continue with the existing prompt

---

- [Use Mind Maps in NotebookLM](https://support.google.com/notebooklm/answer/16212283)

- Priority access to Google Antigravity (agent-first development platform)
- 20 TB cloud storage
- YouTube Premium individual plan included

## Price Reduction: $200 AI Ultra Plan

Reduced from $250 to $200/month – same capabilities at lower cost.

**Key benefits (both $100 & $200 tiers):**
- **Gemini Spark** ($100 & $200; U.S. only) – 24/7 AI agent that navigates digital life, takes action on your behalf. Rolls out to trusted testers this week, Beta for U.S. Ultra subscribers next week.
- **Project Genie** ($200 only; global) – experimental research prototype to create/explore worlds. New Street View-powered capability.

## Access to Latest Models

| Model | Tiers | Description |
|-------|-------|-------------|
| **Gemini Omni** | AI Plus, Pro, Ultra | Creates anything from any input (starting with video). Available in Gemini app (text, images, video inputs) and Google Flow. |
| **Gemini 3.5 Flash** | AI Plus, Pro, Ultra | Frontier performance for agents and coding, excels at complex long-horizon tasks. |

## Productivity Tools

**AI Inbox in Gmail:** Surfaces critical to-dos, personalized draft replies, marks tasks done, links to relevant Docs/Sheets/Slides. Rolling out to AI Plus & Pro subscribers (U.S. only).

- **Additional voice capabilities** in Gmail, Docs, Keep

## Premium Benefits

- **YouTube Premium Lite** – coming to paid AI Pro subscribers (select countries) at no extra charge
- **YouTube Premium** already included in AI Ultra
- **Health Premium** and **Home Premium** included in AI Pro & Ultra

## Usage Limits: Compute-Based Model

Moving from daily prompt limits to a "compute-used" model. Compute factors: prompt complexity, features used, chat length. Limits refresh every 5 hours until weekly cap reached. If cap reached on biggest models, shifted to lightning-fast smaller models. AI Pro & Ultra can buy pay-as-you-go top-up AI credits for Google Antigravity, Google Flow, and coming soon Gemini app.

## Rollout Timeline

- Starting today: Many updates begin rolling out
- Next week: Gemini Spark Beta for U.S. Ultra subscribers
- This summer: Google Pics & additional voice capabilities for AI Pro & Ultra

# Document 466: June Workspace Drops: AI updates for Drive, Sheets, and Gmail

Source URL: https://workspace.google.com/blog/product-announcements/june-2026-workspace-feature-drop
Product area: workspace-site

## Overview

June 2026 Workspace Drop introduces three major AI-driven features plus expanded availability of existing tools:

- Drive: Organize my files — intelligently suggests or creates folders for loose files.
- Sheets: Gemini-powered troubleshooting of formula errors with a "fix" button.
- Gmail: Improved Help me write — now allows inline editing via the prompt bar after the first draft.

Additional expansions: AI Inbox now available to more subscribers, and Gemini in Sheets supports 28 more languages.

## New Features

### 1. Tidy up your Drive faster with Organize my files

Instead of manually dragging files into folders, let Drive do the work for you. The new Organize my files feature intelligently suggests the best folders for your loose files, or creates new ones to group related items.

Key details:
- You can review suggestions file-by-file or approve in bulk.
- Full control over what to accept.
- Now generally available.

### 2. Accelerate data analysis by resolving formula errors with Gemini in Sheets

Gemini in Google Sheets can now remove the frustration out of troubleshooting formula errors. When you encounter a formula error, click on the fix button, and Gemini will automatically analyze your spreadsheet's surrounding data structure to identify the logical or syntax fault.

Key details:
- Gemini explains the issue and fixes the formula inline where possible.
- Reduces troubleshooting time so users can focus on analysis and insights.
- Works for syntax typos, incorrect references, and logical faults.

### 3. Perfect your email drafts with an improved Help me write in Gmail

Just prompt Help me write as you normally would for your first draft. If the result is not perfect, you can now use the prompt bar to make specific edits. Simply type the instruction and Gmail will instantly update the draft for you.

Key details:
- First draft generated as usual.
- Prompt bar allows iterative edits (e.g., add a missing detail, include a deadline).
- Updates are instant, no need to rewrite the whole draft.
- Available on desktop.

## Additional Features with Expanded Availability

- AI Inbox — now available for most Google AI Ultra, Pro, and Plus subscribers, as well as Google Workspace Enterprise Plus customers on Gemini Alpha.
- Build and edit spreadsheets (Gemini in Sheets) — now supports 28 additional languages, including Spanish, Japanese, Portuguese, French, German, Italian, Korean, and more.

## Get Started Today

These updates are rolling out to Google Workspace customers now.

## Key Announcements

### 1. Interact with Your Data in Sheets

- Gemini in Sheets already handles advanced optimization problems that typically require complex formulas or third-party tools.

### 2. Streamline Work with Skills in Workspace
- Skills = agentic automation that can be orchestrated and deployed across teams and workflows.
- Example: automate invoice review by comparing new invoices against latest ones in your inbox — identifies discrepancies and reduces billing errors.
- Skills are built in Workspace Studio and can be shared like a Doc.
- Can be invoked anywhere you use Gemini in Workspace.

### 3. Scale Your Message with Avatars in Vids
- Avatars in Google Vids — most realistic yet, preferred 5:1 over other platforms (announced Dec 2025 with Veo 3.1).

- 24 languages supported for avatars.

- Over 7 million monthly active users on Vids.

### 4. Help from Gemini in Every Meeting
- Take Notes for Me in Google Meet: Over 110 million attendees used it in the last month. 8.5x growth year-over-year.
- Now works for in-person meetings or meetings on other platforms (Zoom, Teams) — simply tap Take Notes for me on the Meet home screen (mobile or desktop). Gemini captures summary and action items in a Google Doc.

- Gemini Enterprise already integrates with Workspace data.

### 6. Auto Browse with Gemini in Chrome (Enterprise-Grade Protection)
- Available now for US Workspace customers.
- Auto browse in Chrome Enterprise handles multi-step tasks across the web and your apps, with checkpoints for user control.
- Existing Workspace enterprise-grade protections (security, data confidentiality) remain in place.

### 7. Bring Workspace to Other AI Apps with the New MCP Server
- Workspace MCP Server (preview) — standardized, open framework for developers to bring Workspace capabilities into AI applications and agents.
- Capabilities include: synthesizing Drive documents, drafting Gmail responses, managing Calendar and Chat logic.
- Official Workspace CLI coming soon for direct management and interaction from agents.

### 8. Maintain Control with Simplified Agent Governance
- New controls: AI control center, agent management, and Workspace Studio controls.
- Helps monitor, control, and audit agent access to Workspace data, reducing risks of indirect prompt injection, oversharing, and data loss.

### 9. New Sovereign Controls and Client-Side Encryption
- Lock data processing and storage to the US and EU — more countries (e.g., Germany, India) in future.
- Client-side encryption for most sensitive data — provides authoritative denial of access to Google.

### 10. Choose a Subscription That Fits Your Business
- AI Pro: $20/user/month — includes Gemini in Workspace apps, AI Inbox, and Gemini Enterprise features.
- AI Ultra: $40/user/month — includes all Pro features plus agentic capabilities, Studio Pro, Vids, NotebookLM Enterprise, and auto browse.

# Document 468: Use Gemini Spark to manage your tasks and workflows in Gemini Apps

Source URL: https://support.google.com/gemini/answer/17094507
Product area: gemini-apps

## Overview

Gemini Spark is your personal AI agent that can automate complex workflows and manage schedules for ongoing tasks in Gemini Apps. Gemini can help handle tasks, using info from sources like Connected Apps, skills, chats, websites you are signed into, Personal Intelligence, location, and more. Just tell Gemini your specific goals and when you want tasks completed.

## Requirements and Availability

- Age: 18 or over
- Account: Personal Google Account with a Google AI Ultra subscription
- Activity Control: Keep Activity must be ON
- Availability: Everywhere Gemini Apps are supported except Australia, Canada, EEA, Hong Kong, India, Japan, Nigeria, South Korea, Switzerland, UK
- Language: English only
- Platforms: Gemini mobile app and web app (gemini.google.com)

## Important Safety and Privacy Notes

- Do not enter sign-in info, payment details, or sensitive data directly into the task thread. Instead, take control of Gemini browser and enter them on the webpage.
- Avoid scheduling sensitive tasks — Gemini can make mistakes. If you are offline when a schedule runs, you may not be able to stop unintended actions.
- Prompt injection risk: Malicious instructions in websites, emails, or documents could mislead the agent to leak data or perform harmful actions.

How Spark helps keep you safe:
- Prompts for user confirmation before sending communications, modifying data, making purchases, or submitting forms.
- Prohibited task recognition (blocks harmful requests per Generative AI Prohibited Use Policy).
- Take control mode — pauses to let you enter passwords or payment details.
- Shows planned progress, skills, connected apps, and files used.

## Creating a Task

On the Web (gemini.google.com):
1. Click Switch to Spark on the sidebar.
2. Describe the task — include when to run it (time or event).
3. Optionally specify a skill (slash to select) or a custom connected app.
4. Upload files, add from Drive, or attach a Notebook.
5. Click Submit.
6. Monitor the task for any input needed (e.g., take over the browser).

On Mac (Gemini app):
1. Open Gemini, click Spark then Tasks on the sidebar.
2. Same description and file attachment options.
3. Submit and monitor.

## Monitoring and Managing Tasks

Open the Work Panel: Click the task thread, then open its work panel (left edge or progress chip).

Work Panel Features:
- Checkmark: Completed step
- Refresh/Spinner: Current step
- Empty square: Planned step

Files: Review files Gemini read or updated.
Schedules: Hover over a schedule to pause/resume.
Remote browser: Click to view Gemini browser; take over by hovering and clicking Take over task. Return control with Go back to Gemini.

Delete Remote Data:
- Delete remote browser data (cookies, sign-in info) — Settings then Gemini Spark Settings then Delete.
- Delete remote code execution data — same path.

## Explore Recommended Tasks and Skills

- Web/Sidebar: Switch to Spark — see Trending tasks; click Skills for recommended skills.
- Mac: Open Gemini then Spark — same sections.

## Turning Off Gemini Spark

Effects:
- Remote browser and code execution data is deleted.
- Schedules stop running.
- Tasks, threads, schedules, and files are not deleted.

Steps (Web): Settings and help then Gemini Spark Settings then Turn off.
Steps (Mac): Gemini menu then Settings then Gemini Spark Settings then Turn off.

To turn back on: click Switch to Spark (web) or Spark (Mac) then Use Gemini Spark. Schedules and tasks are automatically resumed.

## Limits

- Compute-based usage limits — based on number and complexity of requests.
- Maximum 15 tasks running at once. You must wait for a task to finish before starting another. Schedules will not run if 15 tasks are already active.

## Common Questions

Task vs. Schedule vs. Skill:
- Task: Your high-level goal (e.g., Plan my business trip to London).
- Schedule: When to run the task (time or event-based, e.g., Every day at 8AM).
- Skill: Reusable instructions plus tools (e.g., Travel Booking skill).

What if I cancel my Google AI Ultra subscription?
- Tasks finish but are not deleted.
- Schedules paused (not deleted).
- Skills turned off (not deleted).


# Document 469: Google Voice AI Note-Taking and New Standalone Plans

Source URL: https://workspace.google.com/blog/product-announcements/google-voice-offers-ai-note-taking-and-new-subscriptions-to-help-scale-your-business
Product area: workspace-site

## AI Note-Taking ("Take notes for me") in Google Voice

Google Voice introduces AI-powered note-taking during phone calls, bringing the same functionality from Google Meet to voice calls.

**How it works:**
- During an active call, tap "Notes" to start AI capture, audio recording, and transcription.
- Participants hear an audio disclosure: "This call is being recorded and captured by AI."
- After the call, Gemini delivers an organized summary with key points, action items, full transcript, and audio recording inside the Voice app.
- A link to the notes is also sent via email.
- Available in English for Voice Standard or Premier subscribers.

**Privacy:** Notes, recording, and transcripts are visible only to the user who activated the feature. Multiple participants can each get their own copy.

## New Standalone Plans (No Workspace Required)

For solopreneurs and small businesses (US only at launch, more countries coming):

- **Voice Starter** — Unlimited calls and text, professional features for individuals.
- **Voice Standard** — $20/user/month (regular), includes all Starter features plus AI note-taking.

**Limited-time promotion:** 50% off Voice Standard standalone plan for the first 6 months ($10/user/month), including unlimited access to "Take notes for me" and all advanced features. Sign up at the Voice homepage.

## Availability

- "Take notes for me" in Voice is available now for eligible Voice Standard/Premier subscribers.
- Standalone plans available to anyone with a Gmail account.
- Published July 7, 2026 by Mark Ewing, Director of Product Management, Google Voice and Google Meet hardware.

Desktop app with local file support. Gemini Spark and voice features coming later summer 2026.

# Document 474: What's new for Gemini Spark - Gemini Apps Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/gemini/answer/17171264?hl=en
Product area: gemini-apps

Stay up to date with the latest improvements, feature releases, and fixes for Gemini Spark.

## July 14, 2026
- **Expanded country and language support:** Rolling out Gemini Spark in all Gemini Apps languages to Google AI Ultra subscribers wherever Gemini Apps are supported, except in the European Economic Area, Nigeria, Switzerland, and the United Kingdom.
- **More Google Workspace actions:** Gemini Spark can now edit private spreadsheets and presentations; read comments on spreadsheets and presentations; edit shared documents, spreadsheets, and presentations; add images to documents and presentations; and edit and refine documents (including shared ones) using the Canvas panel.

## July 13, 2026
- **Smarter notifications:** Enhanced notifications with more details whenever input is needed; skips sending mobile notifications if you are actively present in that task's thread in the Gemini web app.
- **Smarter sourcing:** For complex tasks involving multiple sources, Spark can now retrieve and review sources in parallel for faster processing.

## July 7, 2026
- **Third-party Connected Apps:** Rolled out support for Dropbox and Zillow Connected Apps in Gemini Spark.

## July 1, 2026
- Third-party Connected Apps: We have started to roll out support for Canva, Instacart, and OpenTable Connected Apps to work with Gemini Spark.

## June 30, 2026
- Topic monitoring: Gemini Spark schedules are now better at monitoring topics across news, finance, sports, local events, and much more.
- Gemini app for Mac: Gemini Spark is now available with Schedules and Skills in the Gemini app for Mac.

## June 29, 2026
- Google Tasks and Google Keep: Gemini Spark can now connect to your content in Google Keep and Google Tasks.
- Comments in Google Docs files: Gemini Spark can now read comments in your Google Docs files.
- Custom Connected Apps: Connect your personal or third-party apps to Gemini Spark with their MCP server URLs.

## June 17, 2026
- Expanded availability: Gemini Spark starts rolling out everywhere Gemini Apps are supported, except in Australia, Canada, the EEA, Hong Kong, India, Japan, Nigeria, South Korea, Switzerland, and the UK.

## May 19, 2026
- Initial launch: Gemini Spark starts rolling out with Schedules and Skills in the United States.

# Document 475: Create and manage schedules for tasks in Gemini Spark - Gemini Apps Help

Source URL: https://support.google.com/gemini/answer/17094710?hl=en
Product area: gemini-apps

You can put your workflows on autopilot by adding schedules to your tasks. A schedule acts as the automated trigger for a task, telling Gemini Spark exactly when to step in and execute your instructions.

## What you need
- Be 18 or over.
- Sign in with a personal Google Account with a Google AI Ultra subscription.
- Have Keep Activity on.
- Gemini Spark is available wherever Gemini Apps are supported (except Australia, Canada, EEA, Hong Kong, India, Japan, Nigeria, South Korea, Switzerland, UK), currently English only, on mobile and gemini.google.com.

## Types of schedules
- Time-based: Run once, hourly, daily, weekly, monthly, or yearly.
- Gmail monitors: Triggered when an email satisfies a Gmail filter.
- Topic monitors: Monitor news, finance, sports, local events, and more.

## Create a schedule
Create conversationally in a Spark task thread by describing the task and schedule.
Create manually from the Schedules page using a blank template (time-based only).

## Manage schedules
- Find schedules in the task thread work panel or on the Schedules page.
- Edit with Gemini conversationally or directly from the Schedules page.
- Pause, resume, or delete schedules as needed.

## Limits and troubleshooting
- Active schedules limit: 50
- Concurrent task limit: 15
- Schedule run times are approximate; may be delayed during high traffic.

## Related resources
- Use Gemini Spark to manage your tasks and workflows in Gemini Apps
- Find, manage and share your Gemini Spark tasks
- Create and manage skills for your workflows in Gemini Apps

# Document 476: Introducing Gemini Omni Flash in Google Vids — Google Workspace Blog

Source URL: https://workspace.google.com/blog/product-announcements/introducing-gemini-omni-flash-in-google-vids
Product area: workspace-site
Tag: google-vids

Introducing Gemini Omni Flash in Google Vids. July 16, 2026. By Justin Luk, Product Manager, Google Vids.

Google is launching Gemini Omni Flash in Google Vids — Google's latest media generation model integrated into the video creation app. This enables users to edit videos using simple text prompts and generate new video clips with personal avatars that look and sound like the user.

Video editing through natural conversation:
- Rescue less-than-perfect footage by updating poor lighting or erasing messy background clutter.
- Refresh ad creative by swapping out backgrounds, updating product colors, or changing a character's appearance.
- Test different creative concepts by changing camera angles or visual style.
- Keep explainer videos and demos fresh by updating outdated visuals and revising voiceovers and on-screen text.

Personal avatars:
- Using Gemini Omni's world understanding and multimodality, users can generate a personal avatar that looks and sounds like them in videos.
- To get started, record face and voice in a secure capture process connected to the Google account.
- Avatars can produce videos for websites, social media clips, or welcome messages for customers.
- For marketers and creatives, this enables rapid video storyboarding — testing commercials and pitches with realistic avatars before scheduling a live shoot.

Safety:
- Personal avatar access is limited to users 18+ with English as their primary language preference.
- Every video generated with Gemini Omni includes an imperceptible SynthID digital watermark identifying AI-generated content.
- Workspace admins can manage access via the Help Center.

More than 7 million users use Google Vids monthly. Available to paid Workspace accounts at vids.new.

# Document 477: Notebooks in Gemini Apps — Gemini Notebook Help
Source URL: https://support.google.com/notebooklm/answer/17003757
Product area: notebooklm

Notebooks in Gemini Apps makes it easy to chat with your notebooks across Google products. This integration gives users more ways to interact with their personal knowledge base.

Your notebooks appear in Gemini: Notebooks created in Gemini Notebook automatically appear in Gemini's navigation. Users can view, edit, and chat with notebooks from either Gemini Notebook or Gemini.

Changes sync across apps: When renaming a notebook, adding sources, or updating custom instructions in one app, those changes sync to the other app. If updates don't appear right away, refreshing helps.

Gemini chats can appear as context: Users can opt in to include chats with a notebook in Gemini as shared context across notebooks in both Gemini and Gemini Notebook, helping build the notebook's knowledge base over time.

Sharing behavior: Shared notebooks created in Gemini Notebook, even those the user owns, are not visible in Gemini. They remain accessible in Gemini Notebook as usual. Additionally, notebooks containing Gemini chats are private and cannot be shared.

### Gemini Chats in Gemini Notebook

When chatting with a notebook in Gemini, those conversations appear as context in the Gemini Notebook sources panel. These Gemini chat threads are read-only in Gemini Notebook (they can't be edited, renamed, or removed directly from Gemini Notebook; they must be managed in Gemini Apps). They contribute to the notebook's knowledge base, allowing Gemini Notebook to reference past Gemini conversations when answering questions. They are visible to collaborators if the notebook is shared.

Tip: Turning off Keep Activity or deleting Gemini Apps activity in Gemini Apps does not delete data in Gemini Notebook.

### Custom instructions

The notebook uses a single set of custom instructions across both Gemini Notebook and Gemini. Any changes made to the notebook's instructions in one app apply in the other app as well.

### Key differences between apps

Source grounding: Even though notebooks are available in both apps, chat responses are grounded differently depending on the app used. In Gemini Notebook, responses are grounded exclusively in notebook sources. In Gemini, responses are grounded in notebook sources but may also include web search and other tools.

Artifacts: Gemini cannot generate items from the Gemini Notebook Studio panel. This includes Audio Overviews, Video Overviews, Infographics, Slide Decks, etc. — these features are only available in Gemini Notebook.

### Deleting Notebooks and Gemini chats

Deleting a notebook in Gemini Notebook removes the notebook and its sources, but Gemini conversations return to the main Gemini chat list.

### Availability

The Notebooks in Gemini integration is available to Gemini and Gemini Notebook users in supported regions. Some features may not be available in all regions.

### Data use and retention

Files added to notebooks and chats in Gemini Notebook are not used directly to train foundational generative AI models, unless feedback is provided.

Chats with notebooks in Gemini Apps are saved according to Keep Activity settings and used as described in the Gemini Apps Privacy Hub.
